The inventory of ices in particular, via the observations of background sources, is accessible for hundreds of lines of sight (LOSs) per molecular-cloud region, opening the possibility to add strong constraints on the solid phase chemistry in a vast domain of cloud densities. SynthIceSpec is a synthetic infrared (IR) spectrum generator that has been designed as a tool to support observing proposals and to test the outcome of chemical models. It is based on laboratory measurements of pure and mixed ices, where each vibrational component is fitted by a sum of Gaussian profiles. Given an initial ice chemical composition (either set by the user or the outputs of a chemical model), a full JWST spectrum is generated, to which the contribution of silicates; continuum, stellar photospheric absorption bands; and extinction law can be added. For the continuum, stellar photospheric models for a wide range of spectral types can be selected by the program, or, Spectral Energy Distribution (SEDs). We present a few use cases of SynthIceSpec: we probed the impact of dust temperature on CO_2 ice formation using IR data and gas-grain modelling. Next, we used SynthIceSpec to explore the detectability of the main feature of CH_3CN at 4.45 um in a cold core environment with the JWST, which was previously tentatively detected in YSOs. The detection thresholds we derive are reasonably low and observable, but identification is directly impacted by the photosphere absorptions that can greatly hinder identification. For some photostellar types, it could remain feasible. Coupled with the Estimated Time Calculator of the Space Telescope Science Institute, SynthIceSpec can be used to find the optimum observational setup for new observations.

title: Adaptive Subspace Modeling With Functional Tucker Decomposition
authors: Noah Steidle, Joppe De Jonghe, Mariya Ishteva
abstract: Tensors provide a structured representation for multidimensional data, yet discretization can obscure important information when such data originates from continuous processes. We address this limitation by introducing a functional Tucker decomposition (FTD) that embeds mode-wise continuity constraints directly into the decomposition. The FTD employs reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHS) to model continuous modes without requiring an a-priori basis, while preserving the multi-linear subspace structure of the Tucker model. Through RKHS-driven representation, the model yields adaptive and expressive factor descriptions that enable targeted modeling of subspaces. The value of this approach is demonstrated in domain-variant tensor classification. In particular, we illustrate its effectiveness with classification tasks in hyperspectral imaging and multivariate time series analysis, highlighting the benefits of combining structural decomposition with functional adaptability.

title: Measurement-induced non-commutativity in adaptive fermionic linear optics
authors: Chenfeng Cao, Yifan Tang, Jens Eisert
abstract: Fermionic linear optics (FLO) with Gaussian resources is efficiently classically simulable. We show that this is no longer the case for such quantum circuits for fermions with internal degrees of freedom, equipped with mid-circuit number monitoring and classical feedforward. In our architecture, the measurement record routes the selected blocks into a fixed-order Bell-fusion pairing geometry. On the level of classical description, this implies realizing a situation in which the permutation sum no longer collapses to a single determinant or Pfaffian. Each post-selected branch expands as a signed sum of path-ordered products of typically non-commuting dressed blocks, and branch amplitudes are matrix elements of the resulting non-commutative trace polynomials. Numerically, we observe Porter-Thomas statistics as the output distribution and a rapid growth of the minimal order-respecting matrix product operator bond dimension. These results thus establish mid-circuit measurement-induced non-commutativity as a route to sampling hardness for noninteracting fermions under reasonable complexity assumptions, without introducing coherent two-body interactions into the FLO evolution.

title: A Gait Foundation Model Predicts Multi-System Health Phenotypes from 3D Skeletal Motion
authors: Adam Gabet, Sarah Kohn, Guy Lutsker, Shira Gelman, Anastasia Godneva, Gil Sasson, Arad Zulti, David Krongauz, Rotem Shaulitch, Assaf Rotem, Ohad Doron, Yuval Brodsky, Adina Weinberger, Eran Segal
abstract: Gait is increasingly recognized as a vital sign, yet current approaches treat it as a symptom of specific pathologies rather than a systemic biomarker. We developed a gait foundation model for 3D skeletal motion from 3,414 deeply phenotyped adults, recorded via a depth camera during five motor tasks. Learned embeddings outperformed engineered features, predicting age (Pearson r = 0.69), BMI (r = 0.90), and visceral adipose tissue area (r = 0.82). Embeddings significantly predicted 1,980 of 3,210 phenotypic targets; after adjustment for age, BMI, VAT, and height, gait provided independent gains in all 18 body systems in males and 17 of 18 in females, and improved prediction of clinical diagnoses and medication use. Anatomical ablation revealed that legs dominated metabolic and frailty predictions while torso encoded sleep and lifestyle phenotypes. These findings establish gait as an independent multi-system biosignal, motivating translation to consumer-grade video and its integration as a scalable, passive vital sign.

title: On the Importance of Tactile Sensing for Imitation Learning: A Case Study on Robotic Match Lighting_supp1-3677716.mp4
authors: Niklas Funk
abstract: The field of robotic manipulation has advanced significantly in recent years. At the sensing level, several novel tactile sensors have been developed, capable of providing accurate contact information. On a methodological level, learning from demonstrations has proven an efficient paradigm to obtain performant robotic manipulation policies. The combination of both holds the promise to extract crucial contact-related information from the demonstration data and actively exploit it during policy rollouts. However, this integration has so far been underexplored, most notably in dynamic, contact-rich manipulation tasks where precision and reactivity are essential. This work therefore proposes a multimodal, visuotactile imitation learning framework that integrates a modular transformer architecture with a flow-based generative model, enabling efficient learning of fast and dexterous manipulation policies. We evaluate our framework on the dynamic, contact-rich task of robotic match lighting - a task in which tactile feedback influences human manipulation performance. The experimental results highlight the effectiveness of our approach and show that adding tactile information improves policy performance, thereby underlining their combined potential for learning dynamic manipulation from few demonstrations.

title: Sampling Augmented Bilevel Trajectory Optimization for Constrained Quadrotor Flights_supp1-3677754.mp4
authors: Qiang Li
abstract: Planning smooth trajectories through a sequence of waypoints under nonconvex constraints is challenging due to the coupling between coefficient optimization and time allocation. Existing gradient-based spline trajectory optimization methods tend to be susceptible to local minima and poor initializations, or restrained by complicated gradient computations. We propose a sampling augmented bilevel optimization (SABO) approach that integrates gradient-based optimization with correlated spatio-temporal sampling for improved robustness and optimality. Through temporal normalization, the closed-form solution of coefficient optimization becomes an explicit function of segment durations, while the Hessian becomes linear in their powers, enabling analytic bilevel gradient computation without using finite differences or linearized constraints. Correlated mutations are subsequently performed around the gradient-induced solution to further explore the constrained spatio-temporal space, with sample projection and covariance matrix adaptation to guide sampling towards low-cost, feasible regions. Simulations show that SABO outperforms existing methods in terms of optimality and robustness. We validate SABO in flight experiments conducted on a quadrotor.

title: What do I Know about AI Beyond Everyday Knowledge?
authors: Xingyao Xiao, Yihong Cheng
abstract: None

title: SightBySound: Real-Time Sonification of Stereo Depth Maps Using Hilbert Curves for Assistive Navigation Supported by a Virtual Training Environment
authors: Milian Ingco, Sejong Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Navigating unfamiliar indoor environments poses a significant challenge for people with visual impairments, especially in areas with unpredictable or overhead obstacles that cannot be detected by traditional aids such as canes or guide dogs. In this paper, we present a low-cost portable sensory substitution device (SSD) that enables users to perceive spatial depth using audio. Our method uses a novel real-time sonification technique based on the Hilbert curve, a space-filling curve that maps twodimensional depth images into a one-dimensional audio signal. Unlike prior approaches that sonify images through sequential scanning, our system represents entire scenes simultaneously by assigning frequencies and amplitudes to pixels based on position and depth. We show that this approach enables real-time audio rendering on resource-constrained hardware such as the Raspberry Pi 5 through an efficient multithreaded implementation. Our system fills a critical perception gap between obstacles at the waist and head level, offering a new form of spatial awareness to visually impaired users. In addition, we propose a virtual training environment to ease the steep learning curve of smart assistive devices. With a user study, we show that the virtual training environment may reduce the barriers to adoption. Our contributions lay the groundwork for more intuitive and efficient SSDs that can enhance independent navigation without costly hardware.

title: Towards Augmented Reality Authoring Tools That Ensure Cognitive Accessibility of Instructions
authors: Valentin Knoben, Jonas Blattgerste, Thies Pfeiffer, Björn Hein, Christian Wurl
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Current Augmented Reality (AR) authoring tool abstractions, while simplifying content creation, force trade-offs that often neglect cognitive accessibility as a result. To address this gap, we conducted a Hierarchical Task Analysis (HTA) of an exemplary AR-guided task created in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Guides and mapped it to the WHO's International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health (ICF) to identify specific cognitive barriers. Based on these findings, we introduce a framework where multimodal AI shifts the accessibility burden from the author to the authoring tool through a combination of authoring-time guidance and adaptive runtime support. We discuss specific leverage points for AI-driven auditing and outline a research agenda of design opportunities. This approach could empower authors to produce inclusive instructions by default without needing specialized expertise.

title: Automatic Dusk Flipper Machine (ADFM): A Mechatronic Solution for Enhancing Poultry Farm Health and Efficiency
authors: Nibha Shamuktha T, Gobinath V K, Raja G, Ritharshanaa G T, Narmatha V, Varshana G
abstract: The Automatic Dusk Flipper Machine (ADFM) is a mechatronics-based solution designed to reduce poultry mortality and labor through automation. Unlike existing litterturning systems, the ADFM employs a dual-motor DC architecture and simplified mechanical drive to achieve consistent flipping efficiency, operating at 230 RPM via a rear-mounted plough. This helps manage heat in the bedding, promoting flock health. The system emphasizes energy efficient motor control, allowing for operation on DC mains or battery power, making it suitable for rural poultry farms. Speed and power consumption analysis supports future integration of intelligent control and environmental sensing.

title: Finite-Element Analysis of Raindrop-Induced Electric-Field Distortion on AC Conductors and Suppression by Twin-Bundle Configuration
authors: Yuehong Liu, Hai Jiang, Hutao Ma, Chao Tang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Overhead alternating current transmission lines are prone to corona discharge under high voltage, generating audible noise. Under rainfall conditions, water droplets on conductor surfaces induce localised tip effects, leading to surface electric field distortion and peak enhancement, thereby promoting corona development. To elucidate the microscopic mechanism of dropletinduced electric field distortion and evaluate the suppression effect of split conductors, this study established a finite element electric field model based on a laboratory-scale circular three-section corona cage. Numerical simulations were conducted for dry single conductors, dry twin-bundle conductors, and conductors with adhering raindrops. Through parametric scans of droplet quantity, spacing, and radius, peak field strength and spatial field distribution patterns on the conductor surface were quantitatively compared. Results indicate that water droplets significantly alter surface field distribution, with peak field strength concentrated at droplet apexes. Under identical applied voltage, increased droplet count slightly elevates peak field strength, while greater droplet spacing slightly reduces it. Larger droplet radii attenuate the tip effect and lower peak field strength. Twin-bundle conductors effectively reduce the maximum surface electric field and improve field uniformity, thereby increasing the corona inception margin. These findings provide interpretable electric field mechanisms and structural parameter references for mitigating AC line corona noise during rainfall conditions.

title: Magnetostrictive Vibration Analysis of a 500 kV Transformer Core via Coupled Electromagnetic-Structural Simulation
authors: Chengxiang Liu, Caiyong Ye, Zhanyang Yu, Bing Xue, Jinwen Luo, Zhuoyan Zhong, Chenguang Yan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: A coupled electromagnetic-structural finite element (FE) model was established to investigate the magnetostrictive vibration behavior of a 500 kV power transformer core under noload conditions. The model integrates the core geometry, material properties of laminated silicon steel, and a field-circuit coupled approach. Simulation results indicate a maximum magnetic flux density of 1.78 T occurring at the inner corners of the core window, accompanied by a magnetostrictive force density of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.78 \times 10^{7} \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> concentrated primarily in the yoke-limb overlap regions. This excitation leads to a peak core vibration displacement of 2.812 mm, with localized stress reaching 1.1 MPa at the limb-yoke junctions. The study offers valuable insights into the spatial patterns of core excitation and vibrational response, contributing to the development of strategies for mitigating transformer noise and vibration.

title: Comparative Analysis of Attention Architectures for Multimodal AI-Proctoring
authors: Aleksei Samarin, Artem Nazarenko, Egor Kotenko, Anastasia Mamaeva, Alexander Savelev, Elena Mikhailova, Valentin Malykh, Alexander Motyko
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This article presents a comparative study of crossmodal attention architectures for intelligent AI-based proctoring. Our microservices-oriented multimodal system integrates video inputs (gaze, facial dynamics, head position) and audio features (paralinguistic anomaly signals) to detect cheating during online exams. Six attention mechanisms are evaluated: baseline concatenation, scalar product attention, joint attention, cross-modal transformer (CMT), extended memory attention, and gated crossattention, using a curated dataset of remote exam recordings. The CMT model achieves the highest macro F1 score of 0.935, outperforming the baseline concatenation approach (0.872) and other attention variants (0.905-0.928), with an absolute improvement of 0.063. Visualization of attention weights offers interpretable evidence by highlighting key temporal segments and modalities that trigger alerts, enhancing system transparency (XAI) and auditability. The findings demonstrate that explicitly modeling intermodal dependencies through cross-modal attention leads to higher accuracy and fewer false positives than unimodal or latefusion methods, confirming its practical value for scalable and secure remote assessment systems.

title: Alternating Optimization of Power, Beamforming, and Phase Shift for Maximizing Weighted Sum Rate in UAV-RIS Assisted NOMA Systems
authors: Kangqi Sheng, Hong Zhao, Ling Zhan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To solve the problem of poor information transmission in a non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) system, the authors proposed an uplink wireless sensor (SN) network assisted by an UAV mounted reconfigurable intelligent surface (UAV-RIS). An alternating iterative algorithm including fractional programming (FP) and successive convex approximation (SCA) was designed to maximize the system's weighted sum rate. The sensor power allocation, receiver beamforming, and RIS phase shift matrix were collaboratively optimized. The simulation results showed that the proposed algorithm significantly improves the system's weighted sum rate and had relatively low computational complexity.

title: Delayed Ai Access: Mitigating the Homogenising Effects of Generative Ai in Collaborative Problemsolving
authors: Margarida Romero
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The rapid integration of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) into collaborative learning environments raises a critical question about its impact on team creativity and idea diversity. This study investigates whether delaying the use of AI tools in collaborative problem-solving can mitigate the “homogenising effect” associated with immediate AI access. Using a quasi-experimental design, 36 students were assigned to three conditions (immediate AI access, delayed AI access, or no AI access) to solve a real-world problem-solving task. The results show a homogenising effect in the immediate AI access group, which produced significantly more similar solutions compared to the other groups, suggesting that continuous, early reliance on AI stifles idea diversity. In contrast, the No AI group achieved the lowest homogenisation score and the highest level of creativity. The Delayed AI group also showed a significantly lower homogenisation score than the immediate access group, supporting the argument that a period of unassisted, humancentric ideation is crucial for fostering novel and diverse solutions before using AI for refinement. These findings suggest that timing is critical for AI integration in education. By delaying AI access, educators can empower students to first engage in the creative struggle and socio-cognitive conflict essential for collaborative problem-solving, ensuring that AI serves as a tool for intentional augmentation rather than a replacement for original human thought. This approach promotes higher levels of learning and preserves human agency.

title: Reliability Analysis of AI-Enhanced Multimodal Assistive Systems for in-Home Elderly Care
authors: Sinan Chen, Masahide Nakamura
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the reliability challenges of AIassisted elderly care systems within smart home environments. By integrating multimodal inputs, including automatic speech recognition from instructional videos and optical character recognition from caregiving materials with context-aware processing, we develop a novel question answering system tailored for in-home care scenarios. A sliding context window mechanism prioritizes temporally relevant information, enhancing response accuracy. We propose three reliability metrics, Answer Accuracy Rate (AAR), Context Consistency Rate (CCR), and Modal Agreement Rate (MAR), to systematically assess system performance across four levels of care complexity, where MAR is reported separately as an auxiliary indicator of cross-modal consistency. Experimental results demonstrate optimal reliability at intermediate complexity levels, where procedures are well-structured and queries are clearly articulated. The findings contribute to the development of reliable AI-enhanced assistive systems, bridging advances in electrical engineering, automation technologies, and artificial intelligence for practical healthcare applications.

title: AgriOneAI: An Integrated AI-Powered Agricultural Advisory and Market Linkage Platform for Sustainable Farming
authors: Vijay M, Asokan Vasudevan, Dheeraj Reddy Byreddy, Chalagundla Haneesha, M Parvatham Sai Sreeja, Sanganagari Karthik Reddy
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Crop diseases, poor yields, marketing, and unpredictable weather conditions are some of the major problems that farmers have to grapple with in most parts of the world. AgriOneAI is an intelligent platform that is based in the cloud and has been created to solve these issues with the help of smart solutions in artificial intelligence. The system is comprised of several AI-based modules such as plant disease detection, yield prediction, fertilizer recommendation, weather prediction, and buyer-seller market connection. A neural network that has been trained on approximately 88,000 images of plants is able to identify <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 8}$</tex> diseases in plants with an accuracy of more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 9}$</tex> per cent. Moreover, the ensemble-based crop prediction system has an accuracy of 99.3 percent and a multi-crop yield prediction model helps to predict 22 crops. The platform is built on a modern cloud architecture powered by FastAPI for backend services, Next.js for frontend deployment, and Firebase for real-time synchronization and data management. Beyond diagnostics, AgriOneAI provides advisory support, community collaboration, and direct market connectivity for farmers. System evaluation shows disease detection completed within 4 seconds and robust scalability with 50 concurrent users achieving a 98.7 % success rate. Designed especially for small and medium-scale farmers, AgriOneAI serves as a complete, end-to-end digital farming ecosystem enabling informed decision-making from crop selection to market sale.

title: Advanced Fire and Smoke Detection System Using Enhanced YOLOv12 Architecture
authors: Niqi Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Early fire detection minimizes casualties and property damage. This paper presents a novel fire detection framework built upon YOLOv12 architecture with Content-Adaptive Area Attention (CAAA) and Cross-Scale Consistent Attention Fusion (CCAF). These mechanisms address limitations in dynamic scene adaptation and multi-scale feature consistency. Our approach achieves mAP@50 of 78.6% and mAP@50-95 of 46.9% while maintaining 57.6 FPS inference speed. The system demonstrates robustness across diverse conditions including varying lighting and weather. Ablation studies show CAAA provides 2.1% mAP improvement and CCAF contributes 1.4% enhancement. Comprehensive comparisons with mainstream detectors including YOLOv11, Faster R-CNN, and DETR demonstrate superior accuracy-efficiency trade-offs. This research bridges theoretical advancements with practical fire safety applications for smart cities and forest fire prevention.

title: Broadband Quarter-Wavelength Impedance Matching for Microwave Bone Health Monitoring Applications
authors: Amra Mehboob, Bilal Amin, Martin O’Halloran, Adnan Elahi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: The performance of non-invasive microwave medical technologies such as microwave imaging, microwave hyperthermia, and microwave sensors is directly related to the penetration of microwave signals into the human body. The impedance mismatch at the skin surface causes most of the microwave signals to be reflected, hence reducing the penetration depth. This study presents the implementation of a quarter-wavelength impedance-matching dielectric layer to reduce the reflection at the antenna-skin interface, thereby increasing the penetration depth of microwave signals for bone health monitoring applications. The analysis is performed over the frequency band of 1–12 GHz, proposing the values of relative permittivity and thickness of the impedance matching dielectric layer across the considered frequency band. The full-wave near-field simulation results showed an average decrease of 2.8 dB (47.5 % reduction) in reflected power at the antenna-skin interface and an average increase of 6.9 dB (390 % increase) in transmitted power to the trabecular bone compared to the case when an impedance-matching layer is not used. These results suggest that implementing a quarter-wavelength impedance-matching dielectric layer enhances signal penetration depth in the calcaneus, thereby improving the effectiveness of microwave-based bone health monitoring.

title: Enhanced Diabetes Prediction Using Deep Learning Framework: Enhanced Diabetes Classification with CNN and Bidirectional LSTM Hybrid Approach
authors: V. Abinaya, K. Chitra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: One of the main global health problems is diabetes, which frequently results in serious conditions such kidney damage, cardiovascular illnesses, and vision loss. Reducing these effects and enhancing patients' health outcomes are largely dependent on early diabetes identification. A mixed deep learning architecture is utilized in the proposed study to accurately forecast diabetes. The model combines Bidirectional Long ShortTerm Memory (BiLSTM) networks for containing temporal and sequential dependencies with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for identifying spatial patterns in medical data. This combination makes it possible for the system to detect time-related and spatial characteristics that are essential for predicting the onset of diabetes. Additionally, the most important features are chosen using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), an optimization method inspired by nature, which improves the model's accuracy and generalization. The proposed framework is calculated using standard metrics, representing its high predictive reliability. This study highlights the prospective of integrating sophisticated AI methods to build an efficient tool for early detection of diabetes, facilitating healthcare providers to formulate timely and informed decisions.

title: A Hybrid Spatiotemporal 3D Phase Unwrapping Method for Dynamic DSPI
authors: Jinhua Hao, Han Li, Yi Wei
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Dynamic deformation measurement using digital speckle pattern interferometry (DSPI) is often challenged by phase discontinuities caused by shadowing or object boundaries, as well as temporal under-sampling in regions with high deformation rates. Under such conditions, conventional spatialonly or temporal-only phase unwrapping methods tend to fail due to error propagation or phase aliasing. To address this problem, a hybrid spatiotemporal three-dimensional phase unwrapping method is proposed. The method first segments the phase map into independent regions based on phase quality and local spatial frequency. For each region, a reference point with low temporal phase variation is selected to establish an absolute phase anchor via temporal unwrapping, followed by spatial unwrapping within the region and inter-region phase alignment. Experiments on a vibrating cantilever beam demonstrate that the proposed method enables stable and accurate recovery of dynamic absolute phase in the presence of phase discontinuities, outperforming conventional single-domain unwrapping approaches.

title: Comparative Analysis of Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Deepfake Detection
authors: Shital B. Pawar, Kamini C. Nalavade
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Over the past decades, the generation of Deepfakes has made rapid progress, affecting real-world media authenticity and security. To recognize such manipulations, a strong model for both spatial and temporal information should account. In this paper, we compare three hybrid deep learning structures, RNN+LSTM, ResNet-50+LSTM and EfficientNet-B4+LSTM, in the task of deepfake video detection. The experiments are performed on the standard FaceForensics++ (FF++) dataset of 6, 450 tampered and real videos. All models are trained using single training setting, at 22 epochs, with batch size 32, ADAM optimizer, and hyper parameters (but the sampling) learning rate 0.000251, hidden size 307 and 3 LSTM layers. Generalization is improved by the use of standard preprocessing methods, such as rescale, zoom, shear and horizontal flip. The experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid CNN-RNN architectures significantly outperform the standalone ones integrating the spatial and temporal dependencies in a proper way. As to the compared models, EfficientNet-B4+LSTM achieves the superior performances in accuracy, robustness and efficiency. In the comparison, its pros and cons have been identified, which can be exploited to enhance reliability and scalability of deepfake detection for digital trust.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Virtual Power Plants Considering Green Certificate-Carbon Interaction Mechanisms and Electricity-to-Gas Synergy
authors: Kai Liu, Yang Zou, KaiYu Fu, ZhiCheng Zheng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the “dual carbon” goals, to enhance energy utilization efficiency, optimize operational flexibility of equipment, and further reduce system carbon emissions, this study proposes an optimized dispatch strategy for virtual power plant that integrates the green certificate-carbon interaction mechanism with electricity-to-gas conversion coordination. First, considering the participation of all virtual power plant members in both the carbon trading market and the green certificate market, a tiered carbon trading mechanism and a green certificate-carbon interaction mechanism are introduced to control carbon emissions and renewable energy consumption. Subsequently, the two-stage operation process of electricity-to-gas conversion is refined, proposing cogeneration with adjustable heat-to-power ratios to further enhance the system's low-carbon performance and economic efficiency. The proposed strategy is validated through simulation examples using the CPLEX commercial solver.

title: Deep Neural Network with A* Based on Floyd Search for Quadrupedal Robot Navigation
authors: Rui Liu, Wenyou Huang, Mingxia Fu, Xingyu Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Robust quadrupedal navigation in unstructured environments necessitates the simultaneous exploitation of geometric precision and semantic awareness within the search domain. To address this challenge, we introduce a hybridized framework that integrates a deep neural network with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{\star}$</tex> architecture augmented through Floyd's all-pairs search strategy. The neural module encodes latent geometric descriptors as weighted transitions while embedding semantic regularities of terrain and obstacle configurations as structured priors. In addition to this enriched representation, the Floyd-enhanced <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{\star}$</tex> algorithm computes globally consistent path estimates, ensuring both the locality of feasible transitions and the coherence of longhorizon connectivity. We establish the theoretical soundness of the method by proving convergence under bounded uncertainty and show that its search trajectories exhibit improved resilience to topological perturbations compared with conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{\star}$</tex> formulations. Extensive validation on quadrupedal robotic platforms across 2D terrain navigation, 3D kinematic constraints, and high-dimensional locomotion manifolds demonstrates that the proposed framework consistently prioritizes stable, lowuncertainty trajectories. Comparative analysis against baseline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{\star}$</tex> and deep-RL planners reveals that our approach yields superior path optimality and uncertainty attenuation, thereby enabling reliable autonomy for legged robots in complex, partially observable environments.

title: Digital Twin-Enabled Modeling and Operational Optimization for Power-Electronics-Rich Distribution Networks
authors: Zhiyang Liu, Baoxia Liu, Lichuan Yu, Yongxin Li, Yuanhui Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The rapid growth of inverter-based distributed energy resources, solid-state transformers, and fast electricvehicle chargers is turning distribution networks into power-electronics-dominated cyber-physical systems. In practice, the controller-rich dynamics, parameter drift, and incomplete observability make offline models brittle, while day-ahead setpoints cannot track minute-level changes. This paper develops a digital-twin-enabled framework that couples (i) a physicsinformed, continuously calibrated hybrid twin built in MATLAB/Simulink and (ii) a multi-objective operational optimizer that co-designs converter setpoints with distribution power flow constraints. A streaming synchronization pipeline aligns SCADA/AMI/μPMU measurements, performs event tagging, and updates key parameters via recursive identification and distribution-system state estimation. The twin then drives a closed-loop optimizer (OPF + MPC) for voltage regulation, frequency support, and loss suppression under inverter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}-\mathbf{Q}$</tex> capability limits, thermal constraints, and feeder voltage bounds. Case studies on an IEEE 33-bus feeder and a 10 kV urban feeder with PV/BESS inverters and STATCOM devices show that the proposed twin reduces modeling error to below 3 % across fast transients, improves dynamic response speed by over 20 % compared with static models, and cuts daily energy loss by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}-\mathbf{8 \%}$</tex> while reducing voltage fluctuation under irradiance and load ramps.

title: ICCE 2026 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)

title: Research on Lightweight Electricity Abnormal Detection Model for Low Carbon Parks and Its Collaborative Optimization for Energy Storage Scheduling
authors: Xiuliang Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: In response to the increasingly prominent abnormal electricity risk in low-carbon parks due to complex loads and high proportion of new energy access, this paper proposes a lightweight time-series anomaly detection model and its collaborative optimization framework with energy storage systems. Traditional methods have response lag and fail to effectively link energy storage systems, resulting in operational risks and economic losses. This study first constructs a lightweight neural network that integrates multisource electricity consumption data and equipment status data to achieve accurate identification and minute level warning of power anomalies (such as sudden increases and overloads). Furthermore, by incorporating warning information as forward-looking constraints and embedding it into the energy storage optimization scheduling model, a closed-loop collaborative mechanism of “detection warning scheduling” is formed. Experiments on real park datasets show that our model significantly outperforms traditional baseline methods in both anomaly detection accuracy and early warning time, with an accuracy rate of $95.2 \%$ and an average early warning time of 35 minutes. The case analysis further confirms that the collaborative optimization framework can effectively utilize the warning window, proactively adjust the energy storage charging and discharging strategy, smooth out load fluctuations, and reduce the daily electricity consumption cost of the park by about $7.3 \%$. This study provides an effective solution to enhance the autonomy, safety, and economy of low-carbon park energy systems.

title: Edge-Driven Semantic Fusion for Real-Time Image Captioning in IoT Environments
authors: Deema Abdal Hafeth, Mohammed Al-Khafajiy, Thar Baker
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Edge computing greatly enhances image processing in IoT applications, particularly for image captioning in smart monitoring systems. While encoder-decoder and attention-based models have improved caption quality, they often neglect semantic representations crucial for deeper visual understanding. To address this gap, we propose EdgeEye, an Edge-driven semantic fusion framework that performs image analysis and semantic feature extraction directly at the data source. EdgeEye combines visual and semantic features to generate richer and more accurate image captions. Its architecture is designed to i) learn distinctive features from image regions and ii) embed visual attention and semantic attributes together in a shared space for effective feature fusion. EdgeEye generates richer and more accurate captions. Experiments on the Flickr8k dataset show notable gains, achieving a CIDEr score of 44.6% and a ROUGE-L score of 46.9%, demonstrating its effectiveness and competitiveness with state-of-the- art for semantic image understanding in IoT environments.

title: Research on Online Monitoring System for Distribution Network Equipment Based on Differentiated Image Semantic Segmentation and Encoding
authors: An Chen, Xiangfeng Zhou, Yijiang Wu, Jianyi Li, Wenwei Lu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: This paper presents an online monitoring system for distribution network equipment, integrating differentiated image semantic segmentation and encoding, edge computing, multi-channel data fusion, and intelligent computing orchestration. The system is designed to optimize the real-time monitoring of equipment status, improve data transmission efficiency, and enhance system responsiveness. By employing deep learning-based image segmentation and differentiated encoding techniques, the system ensures efficient image processing while maintaining the quality of critical regions. The edge computing and device-side collaboration strategy reduces data transmission delay and enhances overall system performance. Furthermore, the multi-channel data fusion module integrates data from various sensors, increasing the accuracy and reliability of equipment status monitoring. The intelligent computing orchestration module dynamically allocates computing resources based on task complexity and node load, achieving efficient load balancing and optimal system performance. The proposed system provides a comprehensive solution for the intelligent and efficient monitoring and management of distribution network equipment.

title: Stacking Ensemble-Based Framework for Intrusion Detection in Internet-of-Energy (IoE) Systems
authors: Abdallah Moubayed
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Internet-of-Energy (IoE) development has been the result of the rapid growth of ICT coupled with the increase in demand for power systems. Such systems integrate IoT devices with ICTs which enable real-time energy management. Crucial components of IoE systems are Distributed energy resources (DERs) and battery energy storage systems (BESS). However, this makes IoE systems vulnerable to a variety of cyber attacks that can have dire consequences such as DDoS, SQL injection, and false data injection. Machine learning (ML) approaches have shown great promise in detecting attacks in networked environments, but may sometimes have bias/variance. To that end, this work proposes an ensemble-based framework that considers three different stacking ensemble approaches built using tree-dependent models. This choice aims to improve the overall performance and generalize well while simultaneously having reduced model bias/variance. Experimental results illustrate that the proposed stacking ensemble framework achieves better performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score while having comparable training and prediction speeds. This demonstrates the framework’s advantage from both a detection capability and computational/inference efficiency perspective.

title: Repeatability in Medical Microwave Imaging: Mapping Current Approaches
authors: Henrique V. Lopes, Daniela M. Godinho, Raquel C. Conceição
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: Medical microwave imaging (MMWI) has been proposed for several medical applications. However, for its successful integration into clinical practice, it is crucial to ensure the repeatability of the signal or imaging outputs. This work synthesised the current approaches used to assess repeatability in MMWI. The findings underscore the need for standardised protocols and metrics to evaluate repeatability in MMWI, as well as a broader exploration of applications beyond breast imaging.

title: Construction Garbage Classification Detection Using an Enhanced YOLOv12 Framework
authors: Hang Chen, Chunlin Liu, Shengfang Qiao, Jianfei Lei, Tianqi Yu, Wenshuai Lin
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Construction waste detection is challenged by mixed material composition, significant scale variation, complex backgrounds, and the prevalence of small targets. To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhanced construction waste classification detection model, termed BWM-YOLO, based on the YOLOv12 framework. A weighted bidirectional feature pyramid network (BiFPN) is introduced in the neck to enable efficient crossscale feature fusion through fast normalized weighting, thereby improving multi-scale feature representation. In addition, the original CIoU loss is replaced with Wise-IoU v3, which employs a dynamic non-monotonic focusing strategy to suppress low-quality anchor interference and enhance small-object localization accuracy. Furthermore, Mixup data augmentation is adopted to expand the training data distribution and mitigate overfitting under complex construction waste scenarios. Experimental results on the CODD dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves mAP50 and mAP50-95 values of 89.5% and 85.3%, respectively, outperforming the baseline YOLOv12 by 3.4 and 1.9 percentage points. Notably, these improvements are obtained with only a marginal increase in model parameters and no additional computational cost. Comparative and ablation experiments further verify the effectiveness of the proposed modules, indicating that the model satisfies the accuracy and real-time requirements of intelligent construction waste sorting applications.

title: An Ethical AI Framework for Identifying, Auditing and Mitigating Bias in AI Models
authors: Aryan Tony Johnson, Sydel Wivel Afonso, Vighnesha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence systems are extensively used across many domains such as healthcare, finance, and recruitment. Many of these systems are trained on sensitive or biased datasets, that results in unethical and unfair decisions taken by the AI models. Additionally, this makes user data accessible and puts privacy at risk. In order to overcome these challenges in AI systems, this study proposes an interactive and open-source platform that identifies, audits and mitigates the biases detected and gives a clear and fair understanding about the problems that have been resolved. Furthermore, the platform delivers an explainable scorecard and a community crowdsourcing option along with regeneration of synthesized data, privacy risk evaluation, proxy bias detection that is implemented using Cramér's V correlation measure, fairness audits, and retraining capabilities. The system provides a clear visual report with suggestions and is user-friendly. The system allows users to clean their datasets ethically, which in turn fosters the development of ethical and fair AI solutions.

title: Invited: Electromigration Avoidance Strategies in Infineon
authors: Shanthi Siemes
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: Hardware-Trojan Detection Using GraphGPS
authors: Sho Yoshimi, Yuka Ikegami, Nozomu Togawa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: With the growing demand for integrated circuits (ICs), design and manufacturing is increasingly outsourced to external vendors, raising the risk of hardware Trojans (HTs) being maliciously inserted by untrusted third parties. An HT is a small, stealthy circuit that can alter functionality, degrade performance, or leak confidential information, making it difficult to detect. This paper proposes an HT detection method based on GraphGPS, a Graph Transformer that integrates MPNN (Message Passing Neural Network) and Transformer layers to capture both local and global structural features of circuit graphs. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, achieving the highest F-score of 0.8322 at 2000 training epochs.

title: Smart Parking Lot Navigation: A Heuristic A* Search Approach for Design of Entry and Exit Algorithms
authors: Anirban Sen, Sayantan Pal, Tuhina Samanta
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Efficient parking management remains critical due to traffic congestion caused by inefficient slot assignments and exit routing in a parking zone. Increasing volume of traffic generates the need for intelligent and smart parking lot allocation for optimal resource utilization and smart urban planning. This paper presents an intelligent parking navigation framework leveraging a three-stage pathfinding strategy. Initially, synthetic parking layouts are generated using a scripting language and validated for connectivity. A multi-target Dijkstra algorithm assigns vehicles to the nearest available parking slots for vehicle entry process. Upon exit, the system employs heuristic-driven A* search (HD-A*) to find optimal departure paths from restricted exit routes. A bidirectional search procedure enhances the computational complexity of the proposed method. Comparative experiments conducted on synthetic datasets generated on some real world scenario demonstrate that our approach achieves up to 50% reduction in planning time compared to conventional algorithms, while guaranteeing route optimality.

title: OpenOCD-Compliant Radiation-Hardened JTAG Interface for Optically Reconfigurable Gate Array VLSIs
authors: Naoki Nagamine, Minoru Watanabe, Nobuya Watanabe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: We have been developing radiation-hardened optically reconfigurable gate array (ORGA) VLSIs. The ORGA- VLSIs allow the programmable gate array to be broken partly. In this case, radiation-damaged regions are avoided and non-damaged regions are only used for applications. However, to exploit the advantage point of ORGAs, the faulty regions must be identified prior to the use. Although JTAG interface is useful for identifying the faulty region, current JTAG interface is vulnerable to radiation. To solve this problem, we have developed a new radiation-hardened JTAG interface that is compliant with IEEE 1149.1, by applying triple modular redundancy to current JTAG interface. The designed JTAG interface was implemented onto an FPGA to test an optically reconfigurable gate array VLSI. The ORGA operation was verified using the open-source debugging tool OpenOCD and SVF files. At that time, boundary scan tests were successfully completed for the ORGA-VLSI. This paper presents the experimental results.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A

title: A Novel Hybrid Conical-Linear Laser Registration Method for Mixed Reality Navigation
authors: Ziyu Qi, Shiyu Zhang, Jiashu Zhang, Xiaolei Chen, Christopher Nimsky, Miriam H. A. Bopp
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Mixed Reality Navigation (MRN) has been proposed as a low-cost and intuitive alternative to conventional navigation systems, yet its adoption remains limited by registration inaccuracy and strong user dependence. This work presents a Hybrid Conical-Linear Laser Registration (HCLR) method that combines a conical projection onto five coplanar fiducials with a single linear-laser constraint to recover all six degrees of freedom analytically. A systematic evaluation of 57 marker layouts identified three robust configurations, which were tested in simulated scenarios using clinical CT and MRI datasets from 19 patients. Under realistic noise conditions, HCLR achieved a mean target registration error (TRE) of 1.06 mm compared with 2.57 mm for standard fiducial-based registration, and nearly eliminated rotation errors. These findings demonstrate that HCLR can improve registration accuracy, reduce operator dependence, and provide a compact, hardware-light solution for MRN. With further physical and clinical validation, HCLR has the potential to advance cost-effective neurosurgical navigation.

title: Research on Decision Logic Reasoning Technology in the Power Sector Based on Chain-Of-Thought
authors: Jiaqi Yuan, Xiaonan Gao, Di Wang, Zhanjie Liu, Lei Guo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the challenge of uncertainty brought by the high proportion of renewable energy in the new power system, this study discusses the intelligent demand of short-term load forecasting and dispatching decision-making. The traditional serial prediction-decision model has the problems of error propagation and black box, which restrict the application of artificial intelligence in critical tasks. Therefore, this paper proposes an integrated framework that combines the Transformer model optimized by PSO and the reasoning mechanism of the Chain-of-Thought, which automatically searches the transformer hyperparameters through the PSO algorithm to improve the accuracy of load forecasting, and decomposes the complex decision-making task into structured reasoning steps by using the Chain-of-Thought. Transparent conversion from data input to instruction output. Based on the actual data of provincial power grid, the experiment shows that the RMSE, MAE and MAPE of PSO-Transformer model in the test set are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 1. 0 6 ~ M W}, \mathbf{5 4. 6 1 ~ M W}$</tex> and 1.02 %, respectively, which are better than comparison model. Although the average decision-making time of the Chain-ofThought decision-making framework is increased to 22.7 seconds, the total power generation cost is only 12.518 million yuan, which is close to the traditional method, and the interpretability score is significantly increased to 4.5 points. This study confirms the effectiveness of Chain-of-Thought technology in enhancing the traceability of decision-making process and the reliability of human-computer collaboration, and provides a practical path for intelligent decision-making in the field of electric power that takes into account both accuracy and transparency.

title: Progress in the Application of Optical Fiber in Quench Detection on CFETR CSMC
authors: Qicai Ni, Jincheng Jiang, Longgui Zheng, Yanlan Hu, Yu Chen, Yezheng Xiao, Huaichao Liu, Qing Yan, Jilin Zhang, Xinxin Zhu
abstract: The CFETR project is a key node in China's efforts to achieve nuclear fusion to solve the energy crisis. CSMC is a large-scale hybrid (Nb3Sn/NbTi) superconducting coil designed for the CFETR project's central solenoid magnet. It is used to obtain the scientific goals and physical characteristics of CFETR, laying a solid foundation for the future development of CFETR CS superconducting magnets. At present, the quench detection system of the superconducting magnet system is mainly based on the detection of terminal voltage changes, combined with the temperature data of the thermometer as an auxiliary means. Although this solution can meet the basic quench detection needs, it has significant defects in the quench warning and positioning functions, and the high-voltage penetrating thermometer can only be installed at the back of the insulator, resulting in a deviation between the temperature measurement data and the actual value. This paper selects the distributed optical fiber based on Rayleigh scattering and the fiber Bragg grating (FBG) temperature measurement technology as a countermeasure. This technology has the advantages of naturally avoiding electromagnetic interference, fast temperature response speed, and continuous detection of temperature changes in space. Seamless steel tubes are used to encapsulate distributed optical fibers to improve their mechanical properties. We arrange distributed optical fibers in the coil at the highest magnetic field and the helium tube at the thermal disturbance trigger point to detect the cooling of CSMC. The trend of the spectral offset obtained from the curve is consistent with that of the thermometer, with some deviations in details. The distributed optical fiber successfully captured a 3 K temperature fluctuation based on the 20 K cooling baseline at the 14th turn of the inner Nb3Sn coil (high-field region) around the 80 h mark, demonstrating its sensitivity to thermal anomalies near the warning threshold. For the ultra-low temperature range where CSMC works normally, the temperature rise of 3 K in the 20 K range serves as a high-confidence indicator for thermal runaway confirmation and fault localization, which complements the instantaneous but non-localized voltage-based detection. The distributed optical fiber responds to this more obviously, providing a reliable basis for optical fiber quench detection. In order to improve the response of the optical fiber to the temperature details around 20 K, we use a Bragg grating packaged with PEEK with higher sensitivity at low temperatures for corresponding supplementation.

title: Optimal Experimental Design for Fuel Cell Model Parameter Identification
authors: Luis M. Winkler, Michael A. Danzer, Herbert Palm
abstract: Model-based development provides a systematic approach for investigating fuel cell (FC) and fuel cell system (FCS) operation, with particular emphasis on understanding reaction kinetics, transport phenomena, and aging mechanisms as functions of operating conditions. Characterizing these processes by physical models requires effective and efficient parameter identification, i.e., the derivation of precise parameter information with minimal experimental effort. Conventional strategies for parameter identification in FC models have so far suffered in particular from high time and resource requirements, leading to considerable bottlenecks in FC model development and system characterization. Therefore, this paper proposes a generic, systematic workflow for equally effective and efficient parameter identification in nonlinear, parametric models through structural and practical identifiability analysis based on the Fisher information matrix. The approach takes into account existing prior knowledge about parameter values, thereby enabling the calculation of optimal experimental designs. The methodology is universally applicable to differentiable parametric models. We demonstrate its practical applicability for a proton exchange membrane FC under realistic measurement conditions using a zero-dimensional model with six unknown parameters. Compared to conventional Latin Hypercube Sampling, the proposed approach achieves parameter identification with a 74% reduction in uncertainty or, conversely, saves 84% of the experimental effort required to achieve the same accuracy. The framework enables experimenters to conduct measurement campaigns that are both maximally effective and efficient. All methodological implementations, data, and demonstration results are made publicly available to facilitate reproducibility and community adoption.

title: Automated Incident Management using Infrastructure-as-Code
authors: Niyazi Gökberk Gündüz, Florian Hofer, Eduardo Guerra, Nabil El Ioini, Claus Pahl
abstract: Enterprises that manage their cloud systems through Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) often push dozens of changes every day. Human operators create a bottleneck in managing incidents in these rapidly changing systems: incident post-mortems reveal detection latencies of tens of minutes and manual recoveries that stretch into hours. An autonomous controller is presented that provides reliable incident self-healing in a Development and Operations (DevOps) environment. This self-healing controller architecture instantiates a common autonomic computing pattern. It provides end-to-end, rule-driven self-healing for an IaC pipeline without the need for specialized hardware or proprietary IT operation platforms. It contributes to handling infrastructure incidents in a reliable and automated fashion.

title: Efficient Cluster-Based Knowledge Distillation for Deep Face Recognition
authors: Xianwei Lv, Haibo Mi, Xin Niu, Wang Chen, Kun Wang, Chen Yu
abstract: Knowledge distillation has been widely used to improve the performance of small compact models for face recognition. However, selecting key knowledge and effectively transferring it from teacher to student remains a challenging problem. In this work, we propose an efficient Cluster-based Knowledge Distillation (CKD) dedicated to aligning the student model with the teacher model in terms of both sample relations and class centers. Specifically, CKD first determines the key sample relations based on the similarities between the sample features extracted by the teacher and their cluster centers generated by existing clustering algorithms. Then, CKD effectively transfers the knowledge of the above relations from the teacher to the student by designing a cluster-based relation distillation loss. Finally, CKD further improves the quality of the student's class centers by constructing a center loss between the above representative cluster centers and the student's class centers. We validate the proposed CKD on multiple face benchmarks. For example, CKD improves the baseline student performance from 91.95% to 94.20% on MegaFace and consistently outperforms recent competitive distillation methods on multiple benchmarks. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of CKD.

title: A multi-domain spatiotemporal deep convolutional autoencoder for health indicator construction in rotating machinery
authors: Yong Duan, Tao Wei, Man Li, Xiangang Cao
abstract: Rotating machinery is widely used in industrial production. Constructing an accurate health indicator (HI) for it can reveal the degradation process and capture the health status, which is of great significance. However, most existing HI construction methods have problems such as a single form of sample input, poor nonlinear expression ability, difficulty in extracting degenerative features and capturing early signs of degradation. To overcome these issues, this paper proposes a multi-domain omni-dimensional spatiotemporal collaborative deep convolutional autoencoder (MOSCDCAE) for automatically constructing the HI of rotating machinery. The multi-domain two-dimensional weight samples of the vibration signal are introduced to enhance the expression of signal features. A HI construction deep convolutional autoencoder network integrating omni-dimensional dynamic convolution (ODC), bidirectional gated recurrent unit (BiGRU), and attention mechanism (AM) is proposed to generate high-performance HI. This network incorporates newly proposed spatial, temporal, and fusion modules, which can deeply explore the internal deep relationships of the data. On this basis, an exponential function that fits the root mean square (RMS) is proposed, and a new loss function is constructed to enhance the HI’s comprehensive performance. The validity of the method is verified by the standard bearing dataset, and the proposed method improves the comprehensive score by an average of 0.1253 compared with other methods. On the reducer accelerated degradation dataset, this method improves the comprehensive score by an average of 0.1465 compared with other methods. It is also able to detect early degradation points earlier and predict remaining useful life more precisely.

title: Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio: A Unified Indicator for Green Evaluation of Evolutionary Algorithms
authors: Mingyang Yu, Haorui Yang, Xinjian Wei, Shengwei Fu, Jing Xu, Jun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Evolutionary algorithms (EAs) are widely adopted for black-box optimization, yet evaluation protocols remain largely accuracy-centric and often omit computational energy use, hindering carbon-aware deployment in green computing settings. We propose the Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio (CFER) to quantify optimization benefit per unit carbon emission and enable a unified assessment of carbon efficiency. To compute CFER without dedicated power-monitoring hardware, we develop a device-independent energy estimation model based on the Multimodal Energy Consumption Factor (MECF). Using a dataset constructed from 13 benchmark functions and 8 classical EAs, MECF is identified via outlier removal and Huber regression, achieving a mean estimation error of 1.80% and a maximum error of 2.51%. On the CEC2020 test suite, CFER-based comparisons reveal rankings that differ substantially from accuracy-only evaluations, highlighting algorithms that improve carbon efficiency with only marginal accuracy loss. Beyond metric-based assessment, we introduce a CFER-aware green optimization mechanism as an algorithm-agnostic plug-in control layer. Driven by real-time carbon estimation and marginal CFER feedback, it adaptively budgets computation through carbon-budget termination and population shrinking under low marginal carbon efficiency. Integrated into GA, DE, and PSO for uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) three-dimensional path planning, the resulting CFER-aware variants reduce CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> emissions by 45.91%, 28.28%, and 59.14%, respectively, while increasing CFER by 78.59%, 32.45%, and 133.70%, respectively, yielding an improved performance–carbon trade-off with minor performance-score degradation. Overall, the proposed framework supports carbon-aware evaluation, algorithm selection, and adaptive computation budgeting for green AI systems.

title: Instance-Conditioned Adaptation for Large-Scale Generalization of Neural Routing Solver
authors: Changliang Zhou, Xi Lin, Zhenkun Wang, Xialiang Tong, Mingxuan Yuan, Qingfu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In modern intelligent transportation systems (ITS), particularly in freight transportation and logistics, real-time route planning is crucial. It presents unique challenges driven by high uncertainty in service requests, where the number of service customers can vary drastically, ranging from hundreds to thousands. Existing neural methods struggle to maintain performance under such significant variations, which severely limits their practical applicability. To address this crucial shortcoming, this work proposes a novel Instance-Conditioned Adaptation Model (ICAM) designed for better large-scale generalization. In particular, we design a simple yet efficient instance-conditioned adaptation function that adjusts the policy based on the specific geometry and density of the current traffic scenario to improve model adaptability with minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, we propose a powerful yet low-complexity instance-conditioned adaptation module to generate better solutions for instances across various scales. Extensive experiments on synthetic, benchmark, and real-world instances demonstrate that ICAM can consistently achieve promising generalization performance across four widely studied large-scale route planning scenarios. Notably, our proposed method delivers high-quality solutions with remarkably fast inference speed, providing a scalable and efficient solution for real-time intelligent transportation operations. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CIAM-Group/ICAM</uri>

title: Flying Vehicle Detection Under Complex Conditions With RGB–Infrared Imagery: A Large-Scale Open-Source Suite and Benchmark Approach
authors: Xunkuai Zhou, Yijun Huang, Li Li, Jie Chen, Ben M. Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: While coordination among multiple flying vehicles improves aerial logistics efficiency, safe and orderly operation requires robust detection and collision-avoidance capabilities. These requirements apply to passenger aircraft as well as to urban traffic and maritime environments, including emerging underwater flying vehicles. However, existing detection methods often fail under challenging conditions such as low illumination or cluttered backgrounds. Their progress is further constrained by the lack of large-scale benchmarks and the high computational and memory costs required to achieve high accuracy, which limits their deployment in resource-constrained scenarios, such as air-to-air collision avoidance in aerial vehicles. To address this gap, we introduce FT55k, an open-source benchmark comprising over 55,000 annotated RGB and infrared images across diverse environments. We further provide baseline approaches tailored for platforms with different computational demands. Extensive experiments on FT55k and three public datasets demonstrate the superior accuracy and efficiency of our methods compared with state-of-the-art approaches. Notably, our approach is the first flying vehicle detection method with a computational cost below 0.5 BFLOPs, achieving real-time performance at 62.3 FPS on an edge-computing device. This work presents the first comprehensive benchmark for flying vehicle detection in complex environments, establishing a practical and scalable foundation for future research and deployment in intelligent transportation safety. Our datasets is publicly accessible at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chriszxk/Flying-Vehicle-Detection</uri>

title: A Digital Compressed Sensing Framework with Optimized Measurement, Quantization, and Sparse Recovery for IoT-Enabled Wearable Edge ECG Health Monitoring Devices
authors: Sajeev K. Jose, Narendra Kumar Reddy Gangireddy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wearable ECG monitoring devices, often limited by power constraints, require efficient methods to reduce energy consumption and extend battery life for continuous health tracking. This work proposes a digital compressed sensing approach for ECG (DCS-ECG), featuring a suitable sensing matrix, effective sparse signal recovery algorithms, and an optimized number of bits for quantization (BM). The framework is designed to achieve high compression ratios (CR) with minimal signal distortion and parameter extraction without the reconstruction of the original signal. The following five sensing matrices are examined: deterministic block binary diagonal (DBBD) matrix, random binary (RBin) matrix, random Bernoulli (RBen) matrix, random Gaussian (RG) matrix, and random sparse fixed binary (RSB) matrix. These are paired with a sparsifying matrix (ψ), a hybrid of identity matrix (I), discrete sine transform matrix (S), and discrete cosine transform matrix (C) (i.e. ψ = [I S C]). The following five sparse reconstruction algorithms are evaluated: iterative hard thresholding (IHT) algorithm, orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) algorithm, smoothed ℓ0 (SL0) algorithm, ℓ1 least square (L1LS) algorithm, and subspace matching pursuit (SMP) algorithm. The proposed DCS-ECG system was tested on ECG signals from the MIT-BIH arrhythmia database. The configuration using the DBBD sensing matrix and OMP recovery algorithm with the sparsifying matrix achieved a low average percentage of root mean square difference (PRD) of 3.75 ± 1.43% at CR = 12.5%, which also offers fast reconstruction. For CR = 12.5% and BM = 8, the CS measurement generation on the Arduino Due consumes 2.02 mJ, while reconstruction on the Raspberry Pi requires 1.13 J. The wireless transmission energy is 1.2973 J. Overall, the proposed CS-based system achieves an 86.24% total energy saving compared to the without CS approach, demonstrating its suitability for low-power IoT-enabled wearable edge ECG health monitoring applications.

title: Doeblin Curves
authors: Dongmin Lee, William Lu, Anuran Makur, Japneet Singh
abstract: Recent research on Doeblin coefficients has shed light on their usefulness as a multi-way generalization of the Dobrushin contraction coefficient for TV distance, in a separate vein from their classic role in the theory of Markov chain ergodicity. However, strong conditions, such as being bounded away from 0, are typically necessary for Doeblin coefficients to establish the existence of information contraction. Building on recently formulated concepts of nonlinear information contraction, we aim to propose a finer-grained Doeblin-based characterization of multi-way contraction behavior which yields non-vacuous contraction guarantees even for channels whose Doeblin coefficient is 0. To this end, we introduce the notion of a Doeblin curve—a nonlinear function which quantifies the contraction behavior of a Markov kernel on collections of input distributions at specific levels of divergence and power. Through the course of our analysis, we develop a new variational characterization of Doeblin coefficients, present several properties of Doeblin curves, define several versions of power-constrained Doeblin curves, and derive upper and lower bounds using our aforementioned variational characterization. We then utilize these results in diverse areas, including generalization bounds for noisy iterative optimization, error bounds for reliable computation with noisy circuits, and differential privacy guarantees for online iterative algorithms. In particular, we extend results in these areas to broader domains or group settings, leveraging Doeblin curves to reveal finer-grained contraction phenomena than Doeblin coefficients.

title: Evolution of the Model: Optimizing League of Legends Win Rate Prediction Neural Networks through Symmetry Feature Selection
authors: Fuxing Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Predict the mid - game winning rate of League of Legends matches. Also, propose a method for neural network modeling and optimization based on symmetric feature engineering. First, construct an initial deep neural network model with 39 features, and the prediction accuracy is 76.37%. Analyze the importance of features and find that the economic ratio and difference features have the highest predictive power, and the model has asymmetric dependence on the features of the red and blue sides. Based on this situation, design a symmetry - based feature selection algorithm to maintain the balance between the red and blue sides and reduce the number of features to 23. It retains the prediction performance (accuracy is 76.47%, ROC AUC is 0.8435), and greatly improves the model efficiency. The number of parameters is reduced by 74.3%, and the inference speed is increased by about 44%. The experimental results show that the proposed feature selection and model optimization methods effectively transform the "verification" model into a "practical" system, and provide a solution for real-time e-sports data classification combining accuracy, efficiency and fairness.

title: Sustainable Conversion Process of Eggshell Waste Into Piezoelectric Nanocomposite for Windmill-based Energy Harvesting Systems
authors: M Mary Linda, KR Nishalini, S Aadarsh Prada, V Aravinth
abstract: This study proposes an eco-friendly strategy for energy harvesting by employing eggshell based piezoelectric materials to generate electrical energy from vibrational sources, particularly wind turbine blade oscillations. Utilizing the inherent properties of calcium carbonate in eggshells, nanostructured composites are fabricated to function as sustainable piezoelectric harvesters. These nanocomposites successfully transform mechanical stress to electrical signals with output levels to power low power electronic systems like wireless sensor networks and wearable devices. By comparing the conventional ceramic and polymer piezoelectrics with the proposed materials, it provides advantages like biodegradability, low environmental impact, mechanical durability, and cost-effective processing methods. Experimental reports indicate that similar bio-derived materials can achieve output voltages ranging from 10 V to 100 V, demonstrating their potential as competitive alternatives. This framework offers a sustainable pathway for integrating piezoelectric energy harvesting into applications such as self-powered biomedical devices, structural health monitoring, and renewable energy systems. The research is aimed at the synthesis and characterization of piezoelectric nanocomposites from eggshell and coconut husk for the fabrication of a cost-effective, ecofriendly, and mechanically robust platform capable of generating sufficient electrical output to power future low-power electronics and sensor networks.

title: Evaluating Augmentation Strategies for Large Language Models: A Comparative Study of RAG, CAG, and KAG
authors: Sindhu M, Mohan Kumar J, Vignesh P, Senthil Kumar K, Monishaa N
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The present study explores how three approaches improve large language models: We studied the performance of three augmentation methods: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Context Aware Generation (CAG), and Knowledge Augmented Generation (KAG). Our point was to examine how various augmentation techniques perform in problems that require intricate and factual details and specific knowledge. Our appraisement of the quality of the response, factual accuracy, and computational efficiency was based on ROUGE, BLEU and human metrics. As our work reveals, RAG excels in fact-retrieval tasks with a compelling 23 % improvement in factual correctness, while KAG is strong for complex reasoning given its ability to combine structured knowledge. Our results provide concrete evidence in guiding the deployment of augmented language models in different fields.

title: EEG-Based Epileptic Seizure Prediction Using Attention-Based Temporal Convolutional Recurrent Neural Networks for Cross-Patient Generalization
authors: Danyang Lv, Cai Chen, Jiazheng Sun, Oluwarotimi Williams Samuel, Ningling Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Epilepsy affects approximately 1% of the global population, with timely seizure prediction being critical for mitigating life-threatening risks. This paper introduces a novel deep learning framework that integrates attention mechanisms with temporal convolutional and recurrent neural networks (ATC-RNN) for cross-patient seizure prediction using scalp EEG signals. We synergistically combine handcrafted feature extraction capturing time-frequency characteristics through maximum cross-correlation, phase-locking synchrony (SPLV), and statistical moments with automatic high-dimensional pattern mining via dilated causal convolutions. The architecture employs layer normalization and parametric ReLU activations to model long-range dependencies in EEG streams while an attention gate dynamically filters redundancies and amplifies discriminative preictal signatures. Evaluated comprehensively on the full CHB-MIT dataset (24 cases, ≈975 hours), our model achieves 0.988 AUC, 96.24% sensitivity, and 0.0053/h false prediction rate, surpassing state-of-the-art methods by >1.9% AUC. Implementation of a "5-of-10" post-processing rule further reduces false alarms by 68%, as validated in challenging cases like chb06 where false prediction rates decreased from 0.22/h to 0.07/h. These results demonstrate unprecedented cross-patient generalization capabilities essential for clinical deployment, though architectural simplification remains necessary for real-time implementation at bedside monitoring settings.

title:
Optimization of Voltage Windows for Aqueous Zn– MnO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Batteries to Suppress Mn
                    <sup>2+</sup>
                    Dissolution
authors: Rongsheng Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aqueous zinc-ion batteries are considered as promising candidates for large-scale energy storage technologies due to their high safety, low cost, and environmental friendliness.
