Herein, a purely decentralized version of MEMQ is proposed wherein the state space is augmented to include statistics whose values are coupled between agents, thus better capturing inter-agent relationships. The proposed approach is applied to an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) wireless system for an air-to-ground (A2G) communication setting. The goal is to design a policy that optimizes a performance metric based on throughput, energy efficiency, and fairness. The proposed approach DMEMQ offers performance improvements over prior methods, balancing complexity, communication costs, and learning speed.

title: Research on Scaled and Flexible Resource P2P Transactions Based on ADMM
authors: Yaoyu Qiu, Haoyong Chen
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of distributed energy systems, balancing supply and demand has become increasingly challenging due to fluctuations in renewable energy generation. Peer-to-Peer (P2P) energy trading, as a decentralized model, can fully leverage the flexibility of prosumers in resource regulation, thereby effectively promoting renewable energy integration and enhancing the economic efficiency and flexibility of the overall system. This paper proposes a P2P energy trading framework based on the adaptive Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) for prosumers in smart parks. First, we develop a prosumer model incorporating multiple flexible resources and establish an operational cost function that includes grid interaction costs. Subsequently, to minimize the park's total operating cost, we determine the optimal power quantities for P2P transactions. Furthermore, an asymmetric bargaining-based revenue allocation model is designed to determine the trading price, considering both fairness and incentives by accounting for the contributions of individual prosumers. The adaptive ADMM algorithm is employed to achieve distributed solving and protect user privacy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively reduces the park's operating costs, increases the revenue for all participants, and exhibits good scalability.

title: Hybrid EfficientNet–BiLSTM Model for Psoriasis Classification
authors: Parthasarathi Pattnayak, Arpeeta Mohanty, Tulip Das, Sanghamitra Patnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: In dermatological disease diagnosis, the precise classification of psoriasis is important, as it can have a variety of clinical presentation and severity. The similarity of multiple subtypes of psoriasis to other skin diseases complicate the diagnostic process, and expert medical opinion is not always available. However, it is important to recognize psoriasis and its subtypes to avoid delay in treatment for the patient. The proposed hybrid deep learning model combines EfficientNet (a convolutional neural network) with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to automate the classification of psoriasis derived from dermoscopic imaging. The hybrid representation allows the model to learn spatial features using EfficientNet while also capturing sequential or contextual information via the LSTM units for improved classification accuracy. The model was trained and evaluated on a public benchmark dataset of dermoscopic images with 7 classes. According to the results the suggested approach outperforms existing models VGG16 and ResNet50 with an accuracy of 89.8%, an F 1 -score of 88.4% and an ROC of 97.6%. Therefore, due to the smaller size and number of parameters of the model architecture, the model has lower computational costs, low memory usage and would work well using mobile or portable devices for dermatological or other applications. This advancement brings us closer to reliable, on-the-go skin disease diagnosis using deep learning technologies.

title: Accuracy Improvement of CO2 Gas Sensor using Neural Network for Remote Sensing Application
authors: Alfin, Sevia Indah Purnama, Mas Aly Afandi
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Climate change and environmental degradation are driving the urgency of improving the carbon dioxide measurement. Several sensors for measuring CO2 have already develop but still has high errors compared with standard measurement device. This issue occurs due to materials from the sensor materials, the calibration technique, and sensors manufacturing process. This study introduces a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network architecture for improving the accuracy of $\mathbf{C O}_{\mathbf{2}}$ measurement using sensors. The architecture is train on $\mathbf{3 0 0}$ data samples and validated with $\mathbf{1 8 6}$ test samples. The model achieved a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 167.51 ppm and coefficient of determination $\left(R^{2}\right)$ of 0.98. This parameter led to indicating strong predictive performance close to standard CO2 measurement device. The trained model was implemented directly on the real device with microcontrollers. The result of implementation shows the average error was significantly reduced from 19.71% to 3.54% and increasing accuracy from 80.29% to 96.46%. These findings demonstrate that architecture proposed in this research effectively improve the CO2 sensors performance. Future work is recommended to explore multivariable measurement and outdoor testing to assess long-term system stability.

title: Predictive Modeling Approach for Risk Classification of Cervical Cancer
authors: Somil Jain, Rajesh Yadav, Anand Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The burden of cervical cancer on women’s health continues to be a major global health concern. In the prevention, identification at earliest and treatment of cervical cancer along with the stratification is crucial. This study will showcase the causes of cervical cancer and how classification approaches of machine learning are helpful in dealing with it. Main risk factor for cervical cancer is getting infected with HPV i.e. Human Pappilomavirus. HPV genotyping and viral load analysis are frequently the first steps in risk assessment, which makes it easier to identify those who are at higher risk and needed a close observation or treatment. The risk analysis or the risk profiles are greatly influenced by socioeconomic differences, which lead to unequal access to resources and a proper treatment. The risk classification landscape is always changing, new methods of risk assessment such as liquid-based cytology and biomarker analysis holding remarkable outcomes as far as risk assessment is concerned. For preventing this type of cancer accurate risk assessment and customized interventions are needed, which in turns will improve the outcomes of women’s health and reduces the happening of cervical cancer globally.

title: Multi-View Fusion Transient Stability Assessment Algorithm Based on Transfer Learning
authors: Chuangye Guo, Tingting Liu, Zichun Zhang, Kang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Traditional transient stability assessment methods rely on the mechanism model, which leads to the timeconsuming calculation, and the model generalization challenge and the cost limitation of simulation data acquisition caused by the dynamic change of power grid topology. To solve these problems, this paper proposes a multi-view fusion transient stability assessment algorithm based on transfer learning. In this method, the mechanism features are extracted by the maximum Lyapunov exponent, and the dynamic features are automatically learned by the deep neural network. A dual-channel weight sharing network is constructed to realize multi-view feature fusion. The multi-kernel maximum mean difference is used for domain adaptation, and the knowledge transfer mechanism between the source domain and the target domain is established. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified in the simulation of the 197-node system.

title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning with Low-Cost Secure Aggregation for IoT Devices
authors: Walla Khalaifat, Jalal Khlaifat, Wael Elmedany, Haroun Alryalat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has led to the generation of massive volumes of distributed data, making traditional centralized machine learning approaches inefficient due to high communication costs and serious privacy concerns. Federated learning (FL) addresses these challenges by enabling local model training on devices while sharing only model updates with a central server. However, securing these updates during aggregation remains a critical challenge, particularly for resource-constrained IoT devices. Existing secure aggregation techniques often rely on computationally intensive cryptographic primitives or differential privacy mechanisms that either introduce significant overhead or degrade model accuracy. This paper proposes a lightweight secure aggregation framework for federated learning that combines Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key exchange with a multilayer weight masking scheme. The proposed method enables privacy-preserving aggregation without revealing individual client updates, while remaining efficient enough for constrained IoT environments. Experimental evaluation demonstrates linear computational complexity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(n m)$</tex>, with cryptographic overhead limited to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-5 \%$</tex> for small-scale deployments and 16.6% at larger scales. Communication overhead remains constant at 8.5%, regardless of client count. The framework provides 128-bit equivalent security, achieves approximately four times higher computational efficiency than RSA-based approaches, and is resilient against honest-but-curious servers and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n-1)$</tex> client collusion. These results indicate that the proposed approach offers a practical and scalable solution for secure and privacy-preserving federated learning in IoT systems.

title: A Novel Piezoelectric-Driven Small Bionic Underwater Robot_supp3-3672852.mp4
authors: Liang Wang
abstract: In deep-sea exploration, compact robotic systems encounter considerable difficulties in sustaining effective propulsion under tremendous hydrostatic pressure exceeding 100 MPa. Conventional electric drive systems suffer significant energy losses due to the requirement for large pressure-resistant enclosures, whereas current actuators utilizing functional materials frequently display constraints such as low thrust-to-weight ratios, elevated drive voltages, or insufficient pressure resistance. This work produced a unique seal-free small bionic underwater robot (SBUR) powered by piezoelectric actuation, inspired by anguilliform locomotion. A dual-SBUR system was employed to enhance the design of an underwater mobile platform (UMP), facilitating combined forward propulsion and steering capabilities. First, the structure and design of the SBUR and UMP are elucidated. Subsequently, modal features in air were examined by the transfer matrix method (TMM), while hydrodynamic responses in water were explored utilizing computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations. Performance evaluations conducted under standard pressure settings revealed a consistent SBUR forward speed of 152 mm/s. The UMP achieved a forward speed of 43.5 mm/s, generated a thrust of 20 mN, and performed rotations with a minimum radius of 130 mm. Experiments validated the practical feasibility of the SBUR at 110 MPa. The performance decline under elevated hydrostatic pressure did not surpass 3.3%, and it demonstrated negligible temperature sensitivity. Practical validation confirmed the SBUR’s ability to transport payloads of up to 10 kg, highlighting its suitability for deep-sea applications. The proposed robot offers advantages such as miniaturization, low operating voltage, and excellent adaptability to extreme hydrostatic pressure.

title: Model-Driven Engineering
authors: N/A
abstract: None

title: A Simulation Platform for MARL Training and Evaluation in Swarm Confrontation_supp1-3677714.pdf
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: In swarm confrontation, robots must swiftly formulate strategies in transient environments, a challenge well-suited for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL). However, existing platforms suffer from the lack of comprehensive confrontation scenario modeling and scalable frameworks, hindering MARL's widespread applications. We introduce a novel platform for training, simulating, and evaluating MARL algorithms in swarm confrontation tasks. It constructs a holistic simulation framework by integrating robot, environment, and rule models for complex confrontation scenarios. Equipped with a decentralized task allocator and path planner for each robot, the platform enables scalable cooperation across dynamic environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our platform simulates confrontations involving up to twenty agents per side, providing empirical guidance for algorithm selection in various settings.

title: Compile in Seconds and Run on an FPGA with DynaRapid
authors: Andrea Guerrieri, Isaac John Wetenkamp, Chris Lavin, Eddie Hung, Lana Josipović, Paolo Ienne
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: FPGAs are highly versatile devices, but their backend compilation process is significantly time-consuming. While software code compilation may take just a few seconds, FPGA compilation times can often span from several minutes to hours due to the complexity of the underlying toolchain and the ever-growing device capacity. DynaRapid <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> is a very fast compilation methodology that generates, in a matter of seconds, placed-and-routed kernel designs for AMD FPGAs [1] The DynaRapid workflow is illustrated in Figure 1.

title: Automated I.T Ops: Comprehensive Review of IaC Technology
authors: Yugansh Garg, Divya Jain, Shivangee Nagar, Riya Solanki
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) has changed how cloud infrastructure is built and maintained, moving away from manual setup toward automated processes defined in code. By writing infrastructure specifications in a codified form, teams can reduce configuration errors, speed up deployment, and make systems easier to reproduce. In this study, IaC is reviewed in the broader setting of cloud computing, with attention to its main principles, common tools, and the challenges that appear in real-world use. Both declarative and imperative styles are discussed, drawing on recent literature, alongside examples of popular tools such as Terraform, AWS CloudFormation, and Ansible. These are examined for provisioning resources and managing configurations. They also handle orchestration across multiple cloud platforms. The comparison considers scalability, DevOps workflow integration, and security. Results show Terraform's strength in multi-cloud deployments. They highlight why security is vital in IaC practices. This is especially important in a DevSecOps approach. It helps practitioners and researchers make infrastructure automated and secure.

title: Secure and Energy-Efficient Data Encryption Techniques for IoT Devices in Resource-Constrained Environments
authors: Rawan Nabeel, Muna Burshaid, Noor Almahmood, Amina Anwar, Wael Elmedany
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The rapid progression of the Internet of Things (IoT) has created challenges with respect to securing sensitive information transmitted over networks, especially on lowpowered devices. While previous research has explored various IoT security issues, this paper addresses the unique challenge of securing IoT communication using efficient data encryption techniques that do not compromise device performance. A mixed-methods approach is employed in this study to investigate the results of several encryption methods, including a comparative study of encryption algorithms such as Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), and lightweight encryption solutions, with insights drawn from pre-existing surveys referenced in the literature. These surveys capture perspectives on the usability challenges of securing IoT devices in resource-constrained environments. Furthermore, secure communication protocols such as Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Datagram TLS (DTLS) are explored. The anticipated results include encryption techniques and standards that will be capable of withstanding attacks without affecting overall responsiveness or battery operation of IoT devices. The findings will assist in improving the security of IoT devices, particularly in smart environments, healthcare, and industrial IoT. This research addresses critical data integrity and energy efficiency challenges in IoT networks, which in turn contribute to secure, scalable, and efficient IoT systems.

title: Out-of-Distribution Detection via Uncertainty Quantification in Diffusion Models
authors: Shirin Shoushtari, Yi Wang, Ulugbek S. Kamilov
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Distribution shift remains one of the most critical challenges in deep learning, often leading to unreliable predictions when test inputs deviate from the training distribution. Detecting such out-of-distribution (OOD) samples is therefore essential for deploying models safely and reliably. In this work, we propose a framework for OOD detection based on the spectral structure of the denoising posterior covariance in diffusion models. By analyzing the leading eigenvalues of this covariance, we observe that in-distribution samples exhibit compact, low-energy spectra, whereas OOD samples induce pronounced inflation in a few dominant modes. This spectral separation provides a principled and interpretable signal for OOD detection. Experiments across standard benchmark pairs show that our method consistently outperforms existing likelihood- and score-based baselines, offering a robust approach for detecting distribution shift at the sample level.

title: Design and Analysis of a Wideband Inverse Dual C-Slot Microstrip Antenna for 5G, WiMAX, WLAN, and Wi-Fi 6E Bands
authors: Ratnesh Sagar, Suryansh Pratap Singh Rajawat, Hemant Choubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A novel, compact microstrip patch antenna is introduced, featuring a dual C-slotted radiator and a Defected Ground Structure (DGS) to optimize impedance bandwidth and matching. With a miniature form factor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28 \times 26 \times 1.6 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex> on an FR-4 substrate, the design supports a wide impedance bandwidth from 2.8 GHz to 8.3 GHz. Simulation data confirms excellent dual-band performance, with deep nulls in the reflection coefficient at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.8 \text{GHz}(-24.3 ~\text{dB})$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.2 \text{GHz}(-49$</tex> dB). This makes the antenna a viable candidate for multiple services, including 5G NR bands n77/n78/n79, WiMAX, WLAN, and the new Wi-Fi 6E spectrum. Additional performance metrics include a maximum gain of 3.4 dBi, consistent 50Ω input impedance, and a VSWR maintained below 2.0 across the entire operating band.

title: CDAF-Net: Fusing Unpaired and Imbalanced Multi-Modal Data for Ocular Disease Classification
authors: Renu Fathima A J, Aafiathul Zuleha M, Ribu Hasini A, Mathivanan N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Deep learning models for medical tomography have shown great, really, promise but are often limited by the available data. Sometimes, while multiple imaging modalities like fundus photography and OCT are used for diagnosis, world datasets for these are typically unmatched, disjoint. What's more, severely imbalanced in size, in ophthalmology. This origin unbalance presents a critical, pretty much, barrier to developing reliable multimodal AI. Think about it this way: look, but here's what's interest: this paper introduces a novel two-part solution to this job. Let me put it this way: besides, clearly, naturally, first, we propose a Strategic Subset Creation algorithm, a data-centric methodology to curate a perfectly class-balanced, mixed-modality dataset from multiple disparate sources. Honestly, but here's what's interesting: here' s why this matters: second, we advise the Cross-Domain Attention Fusion, quite, Network (CDAF-Net), a dual-encoder architecture featuring a novel Gated Coalition mechanism. Indeed, the truth is: definitely, this block explicitly learns to integrate features from the unpaired domains, forcing the model to develop a modality-invariant understanding of disease. Surprisingly, using two public datasets, our approach achieves a high overall truth of 88.7% and a macro AUC of 0.962 on a 5 -class classification task. Let me put it this way: truth is, no doubt, you know, in fact, our preliminary results, including t-SNE visualizations, prove that our method successfully solves the source imbalance problem and acquires a unified feature space, presenting a viable path for training on real-world, imperfect clinical datum.

title: Predicting Customer Churn in Streaming Services Using Decision Tree Algorithms
authors: Monica Luthra, Shubham Sourav, Dwarkesh Mishra, Yash Verma, Mayank Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Customer churn has emerged as a dominant concern to streaming services that operate through subscriptions because it is sometimes less expensive to retain users rather than to attract them. Service providers can understand which clients are at risk and take proactive retention measures with the help of accurate prediction of the churn. The current paper proposes a churn prediction model that is implemented using decision tree algorithms to analyze activity patterns of customers and categorize subscribers as churn and non-churn. The research involves historical streaming data (demographics, subscription, and engagement measures) to train and test the model. The choice of decision trees is based on interpretability, efficiency in computation, and capability of working with heterogeneous data. The proposed model shows a good level of predictive performance, as it provides essential factors determining customer attrition including the frequency of viewing, 1, 3, and 6 months subscription, and the history of payments. The results justify the use of data in making decisions in customer relationship management (CRM) because they provide useful insights on how to decrease the rate of attrition and increase the lifetime value of clients. They can be further integrated with deep learning and ensemble models in future studies to be able to achieve better results in terms of accuracy and generalization.

title: State of Charge Distribution Estimation in Lithium-ion Battery Using Ultrasonic Scanning and GWO-MLP Model
authors: Ting Wang, Youyuan Wang, Dongning Huang, Zhiwei Shen
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: The development of electric vehicles and large-scale energy storage systems is advancing rapidly. This has made accurate monitoring of lithium-ion battery SOC a key technological requirement for BMS. Traditional SOC estimation techniques primarily depend on extrinsic parameters such as voltage and current, which are challenging to mirror the spatial heterogeneity within the battery and may inducing local performance deterioration and safety concerns. In this paper, a nondestructive SOC spatial distribution estimation method is proposed. This method is based on ultrasonic transmission scanning and a Gray Wolf Optimized Multi-Layer Perceptron (GWO-MLP) model, which achieves an accuracy of 3.88% RMSE,2.19% MAE, and 0.985 R2 in SOC estimation of a commercial lithium-ion pouch battery. Meanwhile, a successful reconstruction of the sub millimeter resolution SOC spatial distribution map has been achieved, which clearly reveals the existence of non-uniform phenomena inside the cell and localized deviation. This study demonstrates the significant potential of integrating ultrasonic detection with deep learning for the nondestructive testing of batteries.

title: Environment-Aware Intention Generation in Robots via Multistage Perception and Language Reasoning
authors: Shakil Arifeen, Ometh Perera, S. M. U. S. Samarakoon, C. N. Perera, H. M. K. K. M. B. Herath, S. L. P. Yasakethu
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time framework for generating humanoid intent designed for service-oriented robots operating across diverse environments. The system integrates visual perception, environment classification, and a hybrid large language model (LLM) reasoning to enable context-aware, human-like behavior. A YOLOv8x-based perception module detects 43 object classes with a mAP@0.5 of 0.89. In contrast, object distributions are processed by a lightweight artificial neural network (ANN) that classifies environments with high accuracy (≈99.4%) and near-perfect F1-scores across all classes. A hybrid LLM mechanism governs reasoning. When ANN confidence is high, Groq’s Llama 3.3 70B model provides rapid, low-latency (~36.3ms) intent generation; when confidence is low, OpenAI GPT-4o is invoked for vision-based inference and richer semantic reasoning. The framework operates on a cloud-edge deployment, with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) handling inference and a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B capturing live video streams. A Flask-based web interface visualizes object detections, environment predictions, and generates intentions with real-time audio-visual feedback. Experimental results demonstrate the system’s ability to maintain real-time performance while producing contextually appropriate, socially aligned responses, establishing its potential for adaptive and assistive humanoid robotic applications.

title: Multi-Task Hybrid Deep Learning with Cross-Domain Alignment for Real-Time Sentiment Analysis in Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Habibor R Rabby, Md Habibul Arif, Md Iftekhar Monzur Tanvir, Nusrat Yasmin Nadia, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in healthcare enables early detection of mental health risks, assessment of patient satisfaction, and support for clinical decision-making. However, existing approaches often address medical sentiment classification and mental health status detection as separate tasks, limiting their ability to leverage shared linguistic cues and adapt across heterogeneous data sources. This paper presents a Multi-Task Hybrid Deep Learning with Cross-Domain Alignment framework for realtime sentiment analysis in clinical decision support systems. The proposed architecture integrates a shared transformer-based encoder, a domain alignment module, and task-specific classification heads to jointly model two complementary datasets: a multi-class mental health status corpus and a medical sentiment dataset. Experiments on both datasets demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in accuracy, macro-F1, and other evaluation metrics while maintaining sub- 50 ms inference latency suitable for real-time deployment. These results highlight the potential of cross-domain multi-task learning to improve sentiment analysis performance in diverse healthcare contexts, enabling more responsive and context-aware clinical decision support.

title: Evaluation of AI Methods in Real-World Cloud Environments
authors: Giri Babu K, J Bhavana, B Rambabu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The increasing adoption of cloud computing has necessitated the deployment of intelligent and responsive security mechanisms capable of detecting and mitigating threats in real-time. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of recent AI-based intrusion detection methodologies in real-world cloud environments using actual log data and benchmark intrusion datasets. State-of-the-art machine learning and deep learning models, including ensemble-based classifiers and neural architectures, are assessed for their convergence speed, inference latency, retraining overhead, and threat detection accuracy. Real-world datasets, such as AWS CloudTrail logs and authentication logs, are combined with benchmark datasets like CIC-IDS2018 and UNSW-NB15 to simulate realistic threat scenarios in controlled cloud testbeds (OpenStack and AWS Educate). The experimentation replicates common attack vectors including DDoS, brute force, and privilege escalation. Evaluation focuses on the operational viability of AI models by measuring model adaptability, real-time performance, and detection robustness. The results provide key insights into model scalability and suitability for deployment in production-grade cloud infrastructures.

title: ReSage AI: An AI-Enhanced Resume Screening Framework with Intelligent Content Scoring and Suggestive Feedback Mechanism
authors: Mukil Ganesh C, Prasanna Sai S, Lokesh Raj J, K.Regin Bose
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: While recruiters are overloaded with applications candidates frequently find it difficult to modify their resumes to match employer expectations in today’s competitive job market. In order to evaluate and enhance resume quality this paper proposes an intelligent resume analysis and recommendation system that makes use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Based on industry- standard hiring criteria, the system extracts, evaluates and generates feedback from resumes (in PDF format) using sophisticated computer vision and natural language processing techniques. In order to contextualize the resume content and provide focused recommendations regarding grammar, formatting, content clarity and professional completeness, the suggested solution incorporates Google’s Gemini Application Programming Interface (API). In order to determine whether a profile photo is included in the resume, a deep learning- based image recognition module driven by OpenCV is also used.

title: AI-Driven Observability for Real-Time Incident Management
authors: Priyanka Verma, Shivam Ashokbhai Lalakiya, Rashmi Bharathan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The more complicated character of cloud-native and micro services-based infrastructures has led to the inability of the former-monitoring systems to respond to an incident in real-time. The contribution of an artificial intelligence (AI-based) observability framework, with multi-modal telemetry pipeline, machine learning-based anomaly detectors workflow, predictive incident correlation and machine learning-based automated remediation workflow will be presented to improve the reliability and effectiveness of the system in the current paper. The model was evaluated based on the benchmark data and enterprise observability space simulation. The results show that it had high level of performance with a lower Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) by 63.6 percent, lower Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) by 60 percent and higher detecting accuracy by 16 percent and reduction of false-positive rates by 72 percent compared to traditional monitoring. These results suggest that the possibility of the framework can be based on the provision of proactive information, the accelerated recovery of the incident and less alert fatigue. Recent progress in smart incident management systems development could become the future of the study itself regarding explainable AI models, multicloud architecture, and self-healing observability pipeline.

title: HRMSF-Net: A Hybrid Recurrent Network with Multi-Scale Fusion for Long-Term Time Series Forecasting
authors: Xianbing Wang, Jian Gong, Chenyu Tu, Yiran Qian, Shiyu Song, Jun Long
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Long-term sequence prediction is a pivotal task in various domains, yet existing models encounter significant challenges in capturing long-range dependencies. Although Transformer-based architectures have demonstrated notable performance in sequence modeling, they are prone to the loss of cross-segment context information when processing exceedingly long sequences, owing to their segmentation mechanism. To address this limitation, we introduce a novel model, the Hy brid Recurrent Network with Multi-Scale Fusion Architecture (HRMSF-Net). The central innovation of this model lies in the introduction of the Hybrid Recurrent Block (HR-Block), which efficiently propagates and accumulates context information across temporal segments through a recurrent state vector, thereby overcoming the information loss issue inherent in traditional Transformer models. Additionally, the model incorporates a Hybrid Frequency Attention (HFA) module, which facilitates the parallel processing of temporal and frequency features, enabling the model to concurrently capture both dominant trends and subtle high-frequency details in the data. Finally, the multiscale processing framework enables HRMSF-Net to process input sequences in parallel at multiple temporal resolutions, while a fusion module integrates these multi-scale features to generate robust and accurate final predictions. Extensive experiments on seven real-world public datasets demonstrate that HRMSF-Net achieves the lowest Mean Squared Error and Mean Absolute Error across all prediction horizons, underscoring its superior accuracy and robustness for long-term sequence prediction tasks. Furthermore, ablation studies validate the significant contributions of the HR-Block, HFA module, and multi-scale design to the overall performance of the model.

title: Pollen Quantification Using Bayesian Probability Mass Function Estimation with Automatic Rank Detection
authors: Alla Manina, Joseph K. Chege, Thomas Hornick, Susanne Dunker, Arie Yeredor, Martin Haardt
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Quantifying pollen concentrations from mixture samples is a challenging task, particularly when species signatures overlap in feature space. Traditional methods rely on manual microscopic annotation, often supported by machine learning classifiers. While effective in many settings, these approaches may be limited by label quality, subjectivity, and scalability in large or complex datasets. This work proposes a framework based on low-rank probability mass function (PMF) tensor estimation for automated, data-driven quantification of pollen proportions in a mixture with three pollen species. The approach is general and can be extended to identify more species. It uses the signatures of pure pollen types and a correlation-based matching strategy to associate mixture components with their sources. Experimental results on a real-measured pollen dataset show that the proposed method outperforms conventional techniques such as Gaussian mixture models and plain Bayesian PMF estimation, especially in cases with high interspecific similarity and varying mixture compositions.

title: Wi-Fi CSI Based Indoor Fire Detection System
authors: Johan Weckasinghe, Subramaniam Thayaparan, Mukunthan Tharmakulasingam
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Fire is a serious threat to both human life and property, and fire safety is crucial. Many traditional methods have been developed to detect fires. However, inspired by past research that used Wi-Fi signals for monitoring environments and recognizing activities, this approach aims to use Wi-Fi signals to detect fire that utilize existing wireless communication systems for non-intrusive sensing. The idea is that fire and smoke can interfere with Wi-Fi signals passing through an environment, then Wi-Fi CSI could provide detailed information about the wireless channel between a Wi-Fi transmitter and a receiver. This research demonstrates the effectiveness of using Wi-Fi for fire detection, utilizing Raspberry Pi and ESP32 devices with ESP32 CSI tool. It analyses the temporal shift in Wi-Fi CSI Amplitude before, during, and after the ignition of a flame. The results showed that fire causes a noticeable variation in the CSI amplitude and frequency, particularly due to the frequency-selective nature of subcarriers, where some subcarriers displayed larger changes in CSI amplitude into the presence of fire. The Wi-Fi fire sensing model was tested in an ideal setup using a gas flame where the fire detection accuracy reached 93.3%. This system complies with IEEE Wi-Fi 802.11n protocol, which is commercially available on routers, PCs and various electronic devices also, it’s a low cost, easy to install and efficient fire alarming prototype.

title: Smart Grid Compatible Multiport Converter System for Renewable-Integrated EV Charging with V2G
authors: Magesh T, Sabari L Umamaheswari, L. Annie Isabella, Supriya S, Monisha K, Nandhini R
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: There is the recent interest in all sectors of application in energy sources that have lesser effects on the environment. A Multi-port Bidirectional Converter to charge batteries in electric vehicles: design, topology and control1 A system implementing a design which is amenable to other energy sources such as batteries, fuel cells and solar panels to meet scalable power requirements. It is considered open-loop control, and therefore eliminates the complexity of closedloop controls that come with the traditional charging system and does not compromise the reliable functionality at low cost. To ensure that the generator is compatible to a large variety of charging, the generator adheres to an MPC scheme that can support the buck and boost modes. When in boost mode, it will amplify input voltages on low-input voltage renewables (including solar and fuel cells) up to 28 V to be able to charge a Li or LiPoly battery, and vice versa, 4.5 V to 5 V in buck mode. Apparently the solution is not very weak, and does require a few high-end equipment like high frequency transformers, MPPT controllers and MOSFET type switches that provide close control and reduce the losses of the energy.

title: Integration of Remote Sensing and GIS with Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process for Groundwater Potential Zone Mapping in Butuan City, Philippines
authors: Yvonne T. Olayvar, Joie D. Cataytay
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Urbanization and population growth have intensified groundwater extraction, leading to increasing stress on aquifers in Butuan City, Philippines. To support sustainable groundwater management, this study employed an integrated Remote Sensing and Geographic Information System (RS-GIS) framework combined with the Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (F-AHP) to map Groundwater Potential Zones (GWPZs). Nine parameters derived from conventional, satellitebased, and geospatial thematic layers, namely, drainage density, geology, geomorphology, land use/land cover (LULC), lineament density, Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), rainfall, slope, and soil, were prepared, reclassified, and weighted using fuzzy pairwise comparisons expressed as Triangular Fuzzy Numbers (TFNs). The defuzzified and normalized weights were integrated in GIS through a Weighted Sum Overlay to produce the GWPZ map. The results showed that the study area was categorized into very low (19.51%), low (30.15%), moderate (14.16%), high (17.11%), and very high (19.08%) potential zones. Validation using 34 well locations, including deep, shallow, and dug wells, as well as springs, revealed that $72-75 \%$ of wells coincide with high to very high potential zones. Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve analysis confirmed good model performance with an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.723. Among all parameters, geology and geomorphology exerted the strongest influence on groundwater occurrence. This combined RS-GIS and F-AHP approach provides a cost-effective and replicable method for preliminary groundwater potential assessment for sustainable water resource planning and management in Butuan City.

title: Pedestrian Navigation Using Spatial Audio Cues And Mobile Interaction
authors: B. Varasree, Gade Deekshitha, Muddana Harsha Vardhan, Yellanki Mallikarjun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This project presents a novel approach to pedestrian navigation by utilizing 3D spatial sound to provide direction-based real-time auditory cues. Traditional navigation systems like Google Maps rely heavily on visual interfaces, which can be distracting and accessible, particularly for users with visual impairments. In contrast, this system provides hands-free and eye-free guidance by simulating directional audio that intuitively indicates the user’s path. By integrating map-based navigation with spatial audio simulation, the system creates an immersive auditory environment where users can perceive navigational signals as if they originate from specific directions in physical space. This enhances situational awareness, reduces reliance on screens, and improves safety while walking in complex or unfamiliar environments. The project emphasizes inclusivity, offering significant benefits for the visually impaired and others who prefer or require non visual interfaces. Potential applications extend to assistive technology, smart city navigation systems, and indoor or AR/VR-based environments. This innovative solution aims to redefine the pedestrian navigation experience by making it more natural, accessible, and user-friendly.

title: Developing an Advanced Cross layer Distributed Model for Enhanced Attack Detection Model
authors: Smriti Singhatia, Madhavi Dhingra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The increasing interconnectivity of digital ecosystems such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Industry 5.0, and smart grids has led to a surge in sophisticated, distributed, and multi-vector cyber-attacks. Conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS), constrained by single-layer analysis and centralized architectures, are inadequate in addressing these evolving security challenges.This study presents a novel Advanced Cross-Layer Distributed Intrusion Detection Model (ACLD-IDM) that integrates federated learning with an attention-based deep learning mechanism to enable real-time, privacy-preserving, and cooperative threat detection. The proposed model introduces a cross-layer feature fusion strategy that correlates traffic data across the physical, network, and application layers, thereby uncovering hidden attack patterns that traditional single-layer systems often overlook. The ACLD-IDM employs hybrid artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, including recurrent neural networks (RNNs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, graph neural networks (GNNs), and Transformer architectures, to enhance detection accuracy, scalability, and resilience in heterogeneous and resource-constrained environments. Evaluation on benchmark datasets—NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017, and UNSW-NB15— demonstrates the model’s superior adaptability to zero-day and multi-vector attacks while maintaining strong privacy preservation through decentralized federated learning. The novelty of this research lies in its fusion of cross-layer analytics, decentralized intelligence, and attention-based adaptive learning, establishing a robust foundation for next-generation, intelligent, and self-sustaining cybersecurity frameworks capable of defending complex digital infrastructures against emerging threats

title: Automatic Solar Panel Tilting System Using ACS712 Current Sensor with Integration of IOT
authors: V Indumathi, M Eswaran, P Priya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This paper describes the design and implementation of an automated solar panel tilting system that utilizes an ESP32 microcontroller, an ACS712 current sensor, and Internet of Things connectivity to improve solar energy harvesting efficiency. The system dynamically adjusts the tilt angle of the solar panel to maintain optimal alignment with the sun, thereby maximizing energy output. Unlike traditional tracking systems that depend on light sensors or time-based algorithms, this method uses real-time current measurements to determine the most efficient tilt position based on actual energy performance. The ESP32's built-in Wi-Fi enables seamless IoT integration, allowing users to remotely monitor energy generation, panel orientation, and system performance. This approach offers a cost-effective and intelligent solution for enhancing solar power efficiency while providing valuable real-time data for performance analysis and future optimization.

title: FABLE: Florence-2–Assisted Behavioral Learning and Embedding for Multilabel Action Recognition
authors: Jonathan McGee, Soumee Guha, Lisa S. Berlizova, Peter Youngs, Scott T. Acton
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Understanding complex activities in a scene requires capturing subtle actor–actor and actor–object interactions, a problem made significantly harder when restricted to a single video frame. Generative Visual Language Models (VLMs) have a salient ability to construct coherent captions, identify object regions, or even identify objects from a given phrase. Florence2–Assisted Behavioral Learning and Embedding (FABLE) for Multilabel Action Recognition combines Florence-2’s regionbased visual grounding with discriminative text-embedding cues to understand the teacher-student interactions and individual activities within an elementary classroom environment. Analogous to CLIP, FABLE employs a cross-similarity mechanism to align visual and textual representations, generating a logit distribution that identifies the most probable actions within each frame. However, FABLE incorporates Florence-2’s image–prompt embeddings with text embeddings derived from label definitions, learning a one-to-one alignment between visual and semantic spaces that yields a coherent multilabel probability distribution. Across 23,000 training frames and 5,000 test frames of labeled elementary classroom data, FABLE was used to identify the Florence-2 pretrained tasks that fine-tune most effectively for classroom action recognition. The model achieved a micro F1 score of 0.74, micro mAP score of 0.74, macro F1 score of 0.77, and macro mAP score of 0.65, and we further report a detailed performance across individual classes.

title: Cross-Border Data Transfer and Cyber Risk: A Comparative Legal Exposure Framework for FinTech Transaction Security
authors: Md Omar Faruq, Md Rifat Al Amin Khan, Farhan Shakil, Md Nahian Suhaimee, Mumtahina Ahmed, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Cross-border financial transactions introduce complex cybersecurity risks due to inconsistent legal frameworks and regulatory enforcement across jurisdictions. Traditional fraud detection models often overlook this legal dimension, focusing instead on behavioral or transactional features. This paper proposes a novel deep learning-based framework that integrates a Legal Exposure Index (LEI), derived from global data breach records, into a supervised fraud detection pipeline. Using a synthetic credit card transaction dataset enriched with simulated country identifiers, we classify transactions as either domestic or cross-border and embed jurisdiction-specific LEI values into the model's feature space. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and a standard deep neural network, achieving an overall F1 score of 0.817 and AUROC of 0.912, with particularly strong gains in cross-border scenarios (F1 score of 0.782). Additionally, a country-pair fraud risk matrix reveals patterns of elevated cyber risk in weakly regulated corridors. This work highlights the importance of incorporating legal context into FinTech cybersecurity models and provides a scalable foundation for compliance-aware fraud detection in global financial systems.

title: 1D Hydraulic Modeling Assessment of Flood Control Structure in a Natural Drainage Channel at Caraga State University, Butuan City, Philippines
authors: Jezel Mae B. Bentula, Connie Emerald A. Durano, John Florenz P. Vasquez, Stephanie Mae Salcedo-Albores
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study conducts a one-dimensional steady- state hydraulic analysis of CSU Creek using HEC-RAS to evaluate the effectiveness of a flood control structure proposed by the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH). Two scenarios were analyzed: existing conditions (Scenario 1) and the implementation of the proposed structure (Scenario 2), under 5-, 25-, 50-, and 100year return periods. A detailed geometric model was developed using terrain data, satellite imagery, and field measurements. Results showed that Scenario2 significantly lowered water surface elevations and energy grade lines along critical sections. At River Station (RS) 836 during the 100 -year event, water surface elevation decreased from 18.82 m to 17.82 m, while velocity increased from $1.06 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ to $2.51 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, indicating enhanced flow conveyance. Additionally, Scenario 2 produced more stable, uniform flow profiles, reducing the risk of localized flooding. These outcomes confirm the structure’s effectiveness in mitigating flood risks at Caraga State University.

title: Semantic Link Adaptation and Rate Control with End-to-End System Design
authors: Eren Balevi, Chenxi Hao, Rui Hu, Taesang Yoo
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Joint source-channel coding (JSCC) implemented with deep learning models has been well-studied for real-time delivery of rich information. In this paper, we explore the impact of the data-driven JSCC to link adaptation and develop rate control algorithms for scalable deep JSCC models over bandwidth variations. It is hypothesized that data-driven JSCC can map more semantically important information to the channel input with larger magnitude such that it can go through even when the channel condition is bad. This brings additional robustness for link adaptation while eliminating the need for channel state information at transmitter (CSIT). Specially, we show that the deep learning aided JSCC design with analog transmission that does not use CSIT outperforms outer loop link adaptation (OLLA) via the realistic interference measurements acquired by a sophisticated system simulation tool. Then, low-complexity semantic rate control algorithms that can work with imperfect CSIT are proposed to avoid multiple dedicated neural encoder-decoder pairs per bandwidth, and their efficiency is compared with having a dedicated JSCC model that needs perfect CSIT.

title: Nonsingular Fixed Time Integral Sliding Mode Trajectory Tracking Control Design for Quadrotor
authors: Madhumita Pal, Sanjoy Mondal, Santosh Sonar, Debabrata Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper proposes a nonsingular Fixed-Time Integral Sliding Mode Control (FxT-ISMC) scheme based on a State-Dependent Variable Exponent (SDVE) framework for trajectory tracking control of quadrotor Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). The proposed approach addresses the challenges of achieving precise and robust trajectory tracking under external disturbances and system uncertainties while ensuring efficient use of control. Unlike conventional terminal sliding mode controllers (TSMC) that rely on constant exponent terms, the proposed method introduces variable exponents in the sliding surface and control design to achieve fixed-time stability (FxTS) of both the sliding variables and the system states, independent of initial conditions. The integral sliding mode formulation ensures non-singular implementation of control law. Moreover, SDVE framework offers simple structure in constructing sliding surface, thus reducing computational complexity, and improved implementation feasibility. Rigorous stability analysis using Lyapunov techniques confirms the FxTS and robustness of the control system. Simulation results on a quadrotor UAV demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed FxT-ISMC approach in achieving accurate, and robust trajectory tracking in fixed time, even in the presence of disturbances and uncertainties.

title: Progressive DNN-Based Joint Beamforming and Phase Shift Design for ISAC
authors: Joohyun Cho, Xiang Huang, Rong-Rong Chen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We present deep learning approaches for jointly optimizing base station beamforming and RIS phase shifts in integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems. A semi-supervised, separately trained, and iteratively executed (SST-IE-ISAC) framework improves communication sum-rate while meeting sensing SNR requirements. We also study a transformer-based architecture trained via supervised learning, achieving performance comparable to a modified block coordinate descent (BCD) benchmark while offering reduced computational burden. These results highlight the potential of learning-based ISAC optimization. Future work will integrate transformer models into the SST-IE-ISAC pipeline to strengthen adaptability, enhance modeling of spatiotemporal dependencies, and further improve joint sensing and communication performance.

title: Compressive Strength Prediction of Reactive Powder Concrete Through Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Muhammad Ajmal, Danish Ahmed, Md Shah Alam, Nuha Jamal Alzayani, Rashed Ismaeel, Sani I. Abba
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study develops an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) model to predict the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28{}^{\text{th}}$</tex> day compressive strength of reactive powder concrete (RPC). A feedforward ANN was built using Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm and the model was evaluated across four different architectures (30,40, 50, and 60 hidden neurons). The key mix design parameters considered in the study are water to cement ratio (W/C), cement content, silica fume, and superplasticizer content. Correlation analysis showed that the W/C ratio is the most significant parameter and has negative influence on strength (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{r} =-0.71$</tex>), while superplasticizer content showed the strongest positive correlation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.69$</tex>). The model with 60 neurons per hidden layer reached a near optimal stage where it achieved a perfect fit on training data (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1. 0}$</tex>). Also, the model with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0}$</tex> neurons generalized well on test data (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}=0.90$</tex>) and coupled with the lowest validation mean squared error (6.51). The model was also compared with other machine learning techniques. The optimizable ANN demonstrated superiority to Support Vector Machine, kernel, and ensemble counterparts in predictive accuracy. The results showed that ANNs has the powerful capacity to model RPC behavior with high accuracy for mix design optimization to minimize the excessive number of trials predominantly sought in empirical methods.

title: AI-Driven Analysis of Customer Sentiments
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, G Sunil, Ronak Agrawal, Anandakumar Haldorai, Devendra Kumar Sharma, Deep Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: During this period, the sentiment analysis by AI has developed into a broad-spectrum matter that not only influences the way politics work but the entire society's trust in media as well. Traditionally, distinction between Positive and negative reviews usually failed to follow with the speedy upgrading of strategies applied to hand over misleading information. The present paper describes the application of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), the most powerful yet expensive kind of deep learning network, for fake news detection and improving its accuracy and efficiency. The data that we used was a set with over 44,000 news articles from Counterfeit and Real News equally, for training and testing our machine. The data was processed to remove excessive data, standardize the text, and convert words into numerical features [or tokens] for use with neural networks. Our model, which can be characterized as the one based on Bidirectional LSTM layers, has been developed as an apt description of the context of the given text, while ensuring the capability of performing the flexible processing of the text sequence. It contains the combination of the modeling dropout layers for self-stopping feature and the early stopping mechanism to get the desired performance. And we hope that our experiments will be convincing. Using an RNN-based model, we got a score of 99.04 % accuracy on the test set and likewise exceeded this in precision and recall metrics as well, getting scores of 98.7 %. The fact that these deep learning techniques marked high accuracy rates suggest that RNNs are good choice models for the identification of fake news. We will be in addition tabulating a confusion matrix to show the pattern of predictive capabilities in more detail. With this solution, RNN technology can be viewed as effective for revealing sentiments thus serving as a way out of today's unique sentiment prediction challenge.

title: Signal Strength-Guided Return Navigation for UAVs in GPS-Denied Environments
authors: Oshada Rathnayake, Janith C Yapa, Lishitha Alahakoon, Aloka P Thilakarathna, Biswajith Dissanayake, Dharshana Athauda
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have become vital in a range of applications, including videography, inspection, agriculture, and delivery services. Despite their increasing popularity, there are still significant challenges in UAV technology, particularly concerning navigation reliability. One of the foremost issues is the risk of UAVs becoming lost or crashing due to sensor failures, with Global Positioning System (GPS) malfunctions being particularly critical. Given that UAVs significantly depend on GPS data as their primary localization method, a failure in this system can lead to catastrophic results. This paper proposes a novel approach to UAV recovery in the event of GPS signal loss, utilizing Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurement data as a supplementary navigation resource along with a basic adaptive optimization algorithm to plan the returning path. The viability of this method is assessed, with the aim of enhancing UAV safety and operational reliability in GPS-failure situations.

title: Hand Posture Generation for Object Manipulation Using Reinforcement Learning with Physics Simulation
authors: Ikuma Kuribayashi, Taishi Iriyama, Takashi Komuro
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: In this study, we propose a method for generating hand postures that match the shape of virtual objects in systems where users manipulate virtual objects using physical objects. In order to reflect the user's real-time manipulation of physical objects, the proposed method uses the hand postures as well as the positions and orientations of physical objects as input. Based on this input, the proposed method generates hand postures using physics simulation and reinforcement learning. This enables the generation of hand postures that match the shape of virtual objects while maintaining physical balance and reflecting the user's manipulation of physical objects. In experiments, we confirmed that the proposed method can stably generate physically plausible manipulation postures in real time.

title: Enhancing Cybersecurity of IT Infrastructure Through Robust Generative AI
authors: M.Lenin Kumar, Jillella Venkateswara Rao, Vijayalakshmi Chintamaneni, Linus Paulin, Bhavani. K, Vadali Srinivas
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The growing sophistication and rate of cyberattacks leads to the vulnerabilities of traditional rule-based and reactive cybersecurity systems that cannot easily identify zero-day exploits, polymorphic malware, and advanced persistent threats. In this paper, the researcher will provide a proactive cybersecurity architecture founded on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to train and approximate real-world adversarial pattern attacks. The suggested model produces artificial threat information and combines it with the detection of anomaly to increase flexibility and minimize false positive. GAN system gets trained on a variety of network traffic samples and tested both in simulated and real-life attacks. The results of the experiment show a better accuracy, recall, and robustness than using the traditional models of machine learning, with a maximum detection accuracy of 98 percent and reduced false-positive rates. These results confirm the possibility of the generative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> as the powerful, scalable, and resilient solution to enhance the security of modern IT infrastructure.

title: Efficient Resource Allocation for Integrated Sensing and Communication Towards 6G
authors: Jiaying Ren, Yahia Shabara, Shawn Tsai, Doru Calin

title: Reliable Identification of Apple Leaf Diseases Using a CNN-Based Hybrid Approach
authors: Anupam Bonkra, Deepak Banerjee, Manish Kumar Singla, Swati Singh, Ashish Kumar Singh, Vidhi Sharma, Amandeep Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The creative study presents a hybrid CNNlogistic regression framework, to prepare seven significant apple leaf infections that incorporate: Apple scab, Powdery mildew, Cedar apple rust, Alternaria leaf blotch, Frogeye leaf spot, Marssonina leaf blotch, and Fire blight. The training and testing are done using a total of 13,435 images, with the breakdown of images belonging to each of the classes as follows: Apple scab <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,905,14 \%)$</tex>, Powdery mildew <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2,005,15 \%)$</tex>, Cedar apple rust <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,755,13 \%)$</tex>, Alternaria leaf blotch <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2,085,16 \%)$</tex>, Frogeye leaf spot <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,995,15 \%)$</tex>, Marssonina leaf blotch (The model consistent classification findings were found to be very similar to each other with precision, recall and the F1-score measure of Apple scab being 97.64, Powdery mildew 97.76, Cedar apple rust 97.44, Alternaria leaf blotch 97.84 and Frogeye leaf spot 97.74 and fire blight 97.25. The model has demonstrated its strength, stability and balanced performance as the individual disease category attained an accuracy of 0.99. This is a clear indication of the effectiveness of the approach in the real world uses, say, in the concrete where and when in precision farming that the hybrid method can be effectively applied in the detection of the diseases of the apple leaf.

title: Efficient Sequence Analysis of ECG Signals for PVC Detection Using Symbolic Representation
authors: Yumin Shen, Chuangye Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: With the growing scale of biomedical data from wearable devices, automated electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis has become increasingly vital for cardiovascular disease monitoring and represents a key challenge in biomedical data mining. In particular, as one of the most common ventricular arrhythmias, the automated detection of premature ventricular contraction (PVC) poses a significant challenge in achieving high accuracy and robustness. This paper proposed a lightweight and interpretable PVC detection method based on waveform symbolic features. The proposed method applied sparse representation-based denoising combined with a hybrid fiducial point detection strategy to identify key ECG waveforms; it then integrated Piecewise Aggregate Approximation and symbolic encoding to extract compact and discriminative morphological features. The proposed method achieved accurate fiducial point detection on the QT database, high PVC classification performance on MIT-BIHAR database (0.9982 AUC), and consistent cross-database generalization on INCART database (0.9755 AUC), demonstrating robustness and efficiency.

title: Analysis of Distributed Government Data Using Artificial Intelligence Techniques
authors: Abdulrazak Almohamad Alamhmoud, Mohamad Bassam Kurdy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The analysis of government data contributes to monitoring government performance, participating in decisionmaking, and tracking financial and administrative activities to enhance transparency and accountability in government institutions. Despite the anticipated benefits of government data analysis, these data face several challenges due to their distributed and heterogeneous nature. Knowledge Graphs (KG) can provide an effective solution for representing information in an organized and logical manner. However, integrating knowledge graphs requires syntactic homogeneity among the vocabularies of RDFS graph schemas, which poses a significant obstacle to their integration. To address this issue, we conducted an alignment between semantic web techniques and artificial intelligence techniques to retrieve information and uncover implicit relationships based on context and shared semantics among texts. For this purpose, we utilized BERT embeddings. The cosine similarity algorithm achieved good results on long texts, but its performance on single words and short texts was extremely poor. We improved the cosine similarity calculation method on BERT embeddings, which enhanced the efficiency of the BERT model by 95%. Ultimately, we succeeded in developing an integrated pipeline for analyzing distributed government data, starting from data collection and organization, through processing and analysis, and concluding with visualization. The model's test results achieved an average accuracy of F1-Score =96 %.

title: Object Detection for Smart Retail Systems Using OpenCV and DeepLearning
authors: Sai Charan Yarrangari, Mhd Saeed Sharif, Halima Kure, Hamed Balogun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence continues to be pivotal in recent transformations of smart retail systems through inventory tracking, automated checkout processes, and shelf management, etc. We proposed an AI powered object detection pipeline for smart retail environments using two states of the art use of deep learning models for object detection, YOLOv5 and Faster R-CNN. Our system enables automatic identification and localization of retail products on supermarket shelves, thus reducing manual interaction, improving operational workflows, and enabling real-time decision making. We used a curated dataset of annotated shelf images and processed using OpenCV for image normalization, augmentation, and annotation. Both YOLOv5 and Faster RCNN show better performances compared to YOLOv2, YOLOv3, YOLOv7, and SSD trained on Pascal-VOC and MS COCO. The single-stage architecture used in YOLOv5 makes it excel in real-time inference scenarios because it converges fast, while Faster R-CNN has the best precision for densely packed or partially occluded objects through its two-stage process. We export our models to ONNX to show they can be deployed on edge devices and serve as a scalable efficient AIenabled solution for small-to-medium sized retailers.

title: Attention-Aided MMSE for OFDM Channel Estimation
authors: TaeJun Ha, Jeonghun Park
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper proposes an Attention-aided MMSE (A-MMSE) framework for OFDM channel estimation. In contrast to conventional deep learning methods that typically operate as direct Neural Estimators, the proposed approach adopts a Neural Filtering strategy. We employ a Two-Stage Attention Encoder to extract channel correlations and generate adaptive linear filter coefficients. By explicitly modeling the separable frequency and temporal dependencies, the network effectively captures non-stationary channel statistics that are challenging for conventional methods. This structure allows the inference phase to utilize efficient linear matrix-vector multiplication. Simulation results indicate that the A-MMSE provides improved NMSE and BER performance compared to existing baselines while reducing computational complexity.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging Using Wireless Power Transfer Technology
authors: Akash Vinod Nagmote, Anik Goswami, Anand Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The rise of electric vehicles (EVs) has highlighted the necessity of developing efficient and convenient charging infrastructure to meet the increasing demand for sustainable transportation. However, many EV users remain concerned about vehicle range. To tackle this challenge, this work introduces a wireless charging system. This proposed method enables electric vehicles to be charged while in motion by employing wireless power transfer technologies. The system is designed to adjust to the car's position and speed, thanks to its dynamic charging capabilities, ensuring smooth and continuous charging as the vehicle drives. By alleviating range anxiety, this approach promotes greater adoption of electric vehicles by making charging more convenient and accessible. Enhancing the accessibility and flexibility of EV charging not only reduces range anxiety but also encourages more individuals to consider switching to electric vehicles. This paper presents a comprehensive MATLAB/SIMULINK model to illustrate the proposed system, along with mathematical formulas to calculate the model's parameters.

title: Solving the Goal-Oriented Communication Problem through Policy-Level Constrained Programming
authors: Federico Chiariotti, Pietro Talli, Federico Mason, Andrea Zanella
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: A prototypal goal-oriented communication system consists of a transmitter agent that observes the state of a process and communicates it to a receiver agent that takes actions based on the information received to affect the behavior of the process itself. If the process is Markovian, this system can be modeled as a remote Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). Despite the simplicity of the scenario, the complexity of finding optimal transmission and control policies grows exponentially with the size of the state space, calling for heuristic solutions. This is due to the interplay of the agents’ strategies, which need to consider the implicit information acquired by the receiver when the transmitter stays silent. In this paper, we propose a new way to optimize remote POMDPs, posing them as single-agent problems by introducing policy-level constraints. The proposed formulation allows us to adopt standard optimization tools for quadratically-constrained linear programming, significantly reducing the computational complexity of the task. In particular, we show that a Disciplined Convex-Concave Programming (DCCP) approach can perform near optimally while maintaining a manageable computational burden in a scenario in which policy iteration-based heuristics have a wide optimality gap.

title: A secured decentralized IoT network with block chain: ensuring trust and data integrity
authors: Arfa Mahvish, V Surekha, K Archana, Vaseem Ahmed Qureshi, Ilanchezhian P, SK Piramu Preethika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The fast spurts of Internet of Things (IoT) devices have brought new challenges in security and privacy, such as data breaches, unauthorized access, and vulnerability of centralized systems. Traditional IoT security models are based on the centralized architectures and, therefore, have a lack of redundancy and a vulnerability to cyber-attacks. This paper introduces a secured decentralized IoT network based on blockchain technology to improve trust, integrity of data, and security in IoT ecosystem. The distributed ledger technology (DLT) of blockchain makes data transactions tamper-proof, access control transparent and based on decentralized authentication. By integrating smart contracts, the system automates secure device-to-device communication without intermediaries, reducing latency and improving efficiency. The proposed approach eliminates data silos, enhances network resilience, and mitigates security threats such as data manipulation, DDoS attacks, and unauthorized access. Performance analysis demonstrates that blockchain-based IoT security significantly improves data integrity, transaction transparency, and system reliability, making it an ideal solution for smart cities, healthcare, and industrial IoT applications.

title: Bridging the Age Gap: Do Privacy Literacy, Self-efficacy, and Concerns Explain the Effects of Age on Privacy Decisions?
authors: Reza Ghaiumy Anaraky, Kaileigh A. Byrne, Marten Risius, Bart Knijnenburg
abstract: None

title: Invited: From Evolutionary Algorithms to Analog Design, Electromigration, 3D Integration, and Beyond: On Jens Lienig's Contributions to Advance Physical Design
authors: Johann Knechtel, Susann Rothe, Robert Fischbach, Matthias Thiele, Tilo Meister, Andreas Krinke
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: Fault Branch Location Method for Substation Grounding Grid Based on ICSA Algorithm
authors: Bin Peng, Hanjin Xue, Chenbin Gong, Zhi Li, Tianci Li, Guangmin Gao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the difficulty of fault location caused by branch corrosion in substation grounding grids, this paper proposes a precise positioning method based on an improved cuckoo search algorithm. Considering that the resistivity of Q235 steel conductors is much lower than that of soil, the influence of soil resistance is ignored, and the grounding grid is equivalent to a pure resistive circuit. Based on KCL and KVL, a mapping relationship between node voltages and branch resistances is established, and a fault diagnosis model with minimum energy loss is constructed by taking the branch resistance increase multiple as the optimization variable. To solve the problems of fixed step size, slow convergence speed, and limited search accuracy of the standard cuckoo search algorithm, an adaptive step size mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust the scaling factor and discovery probability, optimizing the optimization performance of the algorithm. Through building a 60-branch and 60-branch and 36-node grounding grid simulation platform, verification is carried out under single-branch and multi-branch fault scenarios, and comparative experiments are conducted with the Co-evolution Genetic Algorithm (CGA), improved Artificial Bee Colony algorithm (YHABC). The results show that the diagnostic error of the proposed method is less than 10% with excellent stability, which can accurately identify fault branches. Moreover, it outperforms the comparative algorithms in terms of convergence speed and positioning accuracy, providing reliable technical support for the efficient operation, maintenance and inspection of grounding grids.

title: DrinkSync: A Sustainable, Universally Compatible Hydration Tracking System
authors: Karim Smires, Kareem Kholaif, Mohammad Daoud, Sasan Haghani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper presents DrinkSync, a low-cost and sustainable hydration tracking system designed to transform any reusable bottle into a smart device.
