In particular, the system designs a central decision-making drone, ESP32-CAMbased support drones, and a Flask-powered ground station interconnected through a private Ethereum blockchain. The system guarantees reliable UAV identification via dual mode detection with simulation radar and encrypted GPS signals. For threat assessment, the YOLOv8 model is used to achieve real-time payload classification. Extensive experimental results have verified an end-to-end response time around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 ~\mathrm{s}, 92 \%$</tex> radar detection accuracy, and an F1-score of 0.85 for AI-based classification. As such, it further confirms that SkyShield provides a scalable and efficient solution to secure UAV defense with potential future improvement for on-site radar deployment and swarm collaboration.

title: What Are They Asking? Analyzing Multilingual Student Questions to ChatGPT in Computer Science Education
authors: Anna Kruspe, Daniel Moritz Marutschke
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT are increasingly used by students to support their learning, yet little is known about what kinds of questions students ask and how they engage with these tools, especially in multilingual contexts. This study analyzes around 600 messages collected from students in an introductory computer science courses taught to international liberal arts students at a Japanese university, where participants were invited to interact with ChatGPT in either English or their native language. Using language detection, sentiment analysis, topic modeling, and intent classification, we explore the linguistic choices, thematic focus, and emotional tone of student inquiries. Contrary to expectations, students rarely used ChatGPT for basic or factual questions—instead, they predominantly posed in-depth, exploratory questions and sought creative ways to apply course content—topics that might not fit within typical course interactions or where students may fear judgment. Our findings reveal a preference for English, a wide range of course-related topics, mostly neutral sentiment, and a notable tendency toward creative and advanced engagement. These insights shed light on how LLMs can foster deeper learning and creativity in multilingual computer science education, informing the design of supportive AI-integrated learning environments.

title: DiffCNN: A Neural Network-based Diffusion Model for Identification and Quantification of Cracks in Concrete Bridges
authors: Vijay Prakash, Carl James Debono, Dylan Seychell, Muhammad Ali Musarat, Ruben Paul Borg, Wei Ding, Jiangpeng Shu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Concrete bridges suffer degradation and damage over time, eventually leading to failure and collapse. Therefore, monitoring these structures is necessary to identify damage early and plan timely maintenance to avoid structural failures. This paper presents a neural network-based diffusion model (DiffCNN) to identify and quantify cracks in concrete bridges' deck, wall, and pavement using both transfer learning (TL) and fully trained (FT) models. The noise control and segmentation outcome are improved with a Gaussian distribution, a CNNbased architecture, and a diffusion module. Experiments on the SDNET2018 dataset show that the proposed DiffCNN model achieves a crack detection accuracy of 96.85% on the bridge deck images, 94.61% on the bridge wall images, and 99.12% on the bridge pavement images in the FT mode. Furthermore, in the TL mode, the model achieved a crack detection accuracy of 99.71% on the bridge deck images, 99.62% on the bridge wall images, and 99.93% on the bridge pavement images, outperforming traditional Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) architectures and vision transformers.

title: Research on Robust Day-Ahead Dispatch for Dual-Type Demand Response in New Power Systems
authors: Shuaibin Yang, Hang Zhang, Genxu Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the expansion of wind power grid integration, the random fluctuations it introduces exert significant pressure on system peak regulation. Balancing operational safety with economic efficiency has become an urgent challenge in the field of dispatch. To address this, this paper proposes a robust day-ahead dispatch method for dual-type demand response tailored to new power systems. Regarding load-side response mechanisms, this paper employs a refined modelling approach for industrial flexible resources, categorising them into Continuously Regulable Loads (CRLs) and Discretely Regulable Loads (DRLs). CRLs utilise electricity price elasticity coefficients to describe their smooth power adjustment processes, while DRLs are characterised by binary variables depicting their discontinuous step-like behaviour. A source-load coordinated robust optimisation dispatch framework is established with the objective of minimising total system operating costs, solved using mixed-integer linear programming. Case study results demonstrate the model's effectiveness in mitigating risks arising from wind power forecasting errors. Compared to conventional models neglecting demand response, the coordinated participation of dual-type demand response reduces total system costs by 6.05%. The findings confirm that deeply tapping demand-side flexibility resources in conjunction with robust optimisation techniques represents an effective pathway to enhance wind power integration levels and economic operation capabilities within new power systems.

title: Real-Time Controller Implementation Using dSPACE RTI1202 and MATLAB/Simulink: Architecture, Integration, and Applications
authors: Ashok Kumar, Praveen Tripathy, Sreenath J.G, Ravindranath Adda
abstract: This paper presents a systematic framework for the implementation of real-time control algorithms using the dSPACE RTI-1202 rapid prototyping system integrated with MATLAB/Simulink and control-desk. The proposed workflow covers model-based design, code generation using real-time interface blocks, and automatic deployment onto the DS1202 hardware. Hardware configuration procedures for pulse width modulation (PWM) generation, analog-to-digital/digital-to-analog converter sampling, and general-purpose input output (GPIO) interfacing are explained in detail, along with the integration of controller logic within the Simulink environment. Real-time monitoring, parameter tuning, and data logging through control-desk are demonstrated for efficient experimental validation. To evaluate the system's performance, a notch filter-based PLL is implemented for grid synchronisation under distorted voltage conditions. The proposed setup precisely estimates the grid frequency and phase angle. The results confirm the high-speed processing, precise timing, and robust real-time capability of the dSPACE platform for advanced control and power electronics applications. This work aims to serve as a foundational guide for researchers and engineers seeking to develop and test real-time digital controllers using the dSPACE RTI1202 system.

title: An Intelligent Online Monitoring Method Based on the VQ-VAE Model
authors: Yuxuan Liu, Yan Fang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of intelligent manufacturing and information technologies, industrial systems are becoming increasingly dependent on real-time monitoring and data analysis. Traditional monitoring approaches typically rely on offline data processing, which limits their ability to respond promptly to system state variations and to extract latent features from large-scale time-series data. To address these challenges, this study proposes an intelligent monitoring method based on the Vector Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE). The proposed approach enables automatic feature learning and state prediction directly from raw sensor signals, eliminating the need for complex time-, frequency-, or time-frequency-domain feature engineering commonly required in conventional models. This greatly improves computational efficiency and model generalization capability. Experimental results based on an online monitoring platform demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior real-time performance, stability, and prediction accuracy, providing an efficient and scalable solution for intelligent monitoring and data-driven decision-making in complex systems.

title: Dendritic-Spine-Inspired Structural Optimization in a Lightweight Bowel Sound Analysis System for On-Device Colorectal Cancer Screening
authors: Haojie Zhang, Enze Li, Yang Tan, Lin Shen, Kun Qian, Jing Li, Bin Hu, Björn W. Schuller, Yoshiharu Yamamoto
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This study presents a non-invasive AI-assisted Colorectal Cancer (CRC) screening system based on Bowel Sound (BS) analysis. A portable BS acquisition device is integrated with a dendritic-spine-inspired lightweight neural model that mimics biological synaptic pruning to remove redundant parameters while preserving key diagnostic features. The pruned model achieves high accuracy with minimal computational and memory costs, supporting on-device inference on microcontroller. Based on this model, we develop a complete device and system capable of efficient BS screening, demonstrating strong practicality for accessible CRC risk assessment.

title: Electro-Thermal Coupling Modelling for Liquid Metal Battery Based on Equivalent Circuit Model
authors: Liming Tao, Lei Fan, Lingrong Pang, Mingwei Wang, Aji Hu, Aonan Li, Zhengwei Guo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper develops an efficient electro-thermal coupling model for a 200 Ah Li <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mid \text{Bi}$</tex> liquid metal battery, based on an equivalent circuit approach combined with a lumpedparameter thermal model. The electrical parameters, including state-of-charge dependent ohmic and polarization resistances, are characterized via hybrid pulse power characterization tests. Simulation results indicate that ohmic polarization dominates irreversible heat generation, while reversible entropy change remains the primary driver of temperature variation. During cycling, the battery temperature fluctuates between approximately 519 °C and 540 °C under 0.1 C conditions, and between about 520 °C and 557 °C under 0.2 C conditions. The proposed model balances computational efficiency with predictive capability, offering a practical tool for analyzing thermal behavior and supporting the design of thermal management systems for liquid metal battery modules.

title: AI-Driven Optimization of EV Battery Charging
authors: R Thamizharasan, Ramathilagam K, R Mythili, Sukitha D, Sivakarthikeyan G
abstract: This project presents an AI-based electric vehicle (EV) battery charging system utilizing a crossover renewable energy arrangement, comprising of a 630 W photovoltaic (PV) cluster and a three-phase wind energy system. The AC yield from the wind turbine is changed over to DC and combined with the PV control to supply a DC-DC converter. An Artificial Neural Network (ANN) (ANN)-based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) controller creates ideal exchanging signals for the converter, maximizing energy extraction from both sources beneath changing natural conditions. The directed DC yield is utilized to securely and effectively charge a 48 V, 31.25 V EV battery. MATLAB/Simulink recreations illustrate that the ANN-MPPT controller successfully makes strides energy collecting while guaranteeing a steady battery charging profile, highlighting the potential of AI-integrated renewable energy for economical EV charging.

title: Detection of Low Attachment and High Use of Safety Ropes Based on Mmpose+Qwen2-VL Model
authors: Xiaona Jiang, Gaoyang Zhang, Dongmei Ma, Tianguang Yang, Yu Zhang, Feng Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In the field of safety monitoring for high-altitude power operations, traditional methods for detecting violations such as low attachment and high use of safety ropes suffer from insufficient accuracy and low efficiency, leading to the risk of falls from heights for workers. To address this, we propose a collaborative detection technique based on mmpose and Qwen2VL multimodal large model. By employing a “mmpose detection + Qwen2-VL model inference” approach, the accuracy of detecting low attachment and high use of safety ropes is improved. This research mainly includes three aspects: Firstly, we constructed a high-precision labeled dataset containing 4000 frames of images, annotating key points of the human body and safety rope, including both compliant and non-compliant scenarios in highaltitude operations. Then, the accuracy of key point localization is improved by fine-tuning the HRNet model in mmpose. Finally, through the collaboration of mmpose and Qwen2-VL models, mmpose extracts the coordinates of the human waist and the endpoints of the safety rope, and Qwen2-VL is used for violation determination based on spatial relationship inference. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves precision, recall, and F 1 scores of 81.3 %, 80.8 %, and 81.05 %, respectively, in the detection task of low attachment and high use of safety ropes, significantly outperforming the single model. This method realizes automated and high-precision detection of violations, providing reliable technical support for the safety supervision of high-altitude power operations.

title: Chargeback Fraud Detection on Anonymised Merchant Data: An Industry Case Study
authors: Brayden Killeen, Minh Trung Tran, Fitrio Pakana
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Australasian Information Security Conference
abstract: None

title: Aspo: Constraint-Aware Bayesian Optimization for Fpga-Based Soft Processors
authors: Haoran Wu, Ce Guo, Wayne Luk, Robert Mullins
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Bayesian Optimization (BO) has shown promise in tuning processor design parameters. However, standard BO does not support constraints involving categorical parameters such as types of branch predictors and division circuits. In addition, optimization time of BO grows with processor complexity, which becomes increasingly significant especially for FPGA-based soft processors. This paper introduces ASPO, an approach that leverages disjunctive form to enable BO to handle constraints involving categorical parameters. Unlike existing methods that directly apply standard BO, the proposed ASPO method, for the first time, customizes the mathematical mechanism of BO to address challenges faced by soft-processor designs on FPGAs. Specifically, ASPO supports categorical parameters using a novel customized BO covariance kernel. It also accelerates the design evaluation procedure by penalizing the BO acquisition function with potential evaluation time and by reusing FPGA synthesis checkpoints from previously evaluated configurations. ASPO targets three soft processors: RocketChip, BOOM, and EL2 VeeR. The approach is evaluated based on seven RISC-V benchmarks. Results show that ASPO can reduce execution time for the “multiply” benchmark on the BOOM processor by up to 35 % compared to the default configuration. Furthermore, it reduces design time for the BOOM processor by up to 74 % compared to Boomerang, a state-of-the-art hardware-oriented BO approach.

title: Advanced MPPT Technique with High-Gain Modified SEPIC Converter for Solar Powered PMSM Motor Fed Electric Vehicle
authors: A. Jeyaraj, S. L. Sreedevi, M. Baskar, R. Vijayalakshmi, K. Sushita, Ananthan Nagarajan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: With the global emphasis on sustainable transportation and the growing demand for clean energy, Electric Vehicles (EVs) powered by renewable sources, particularly solar energy, have gained significant attention. Integrating Photovoltaic (PV) system with EV propulsion introduces the need for efficient power management techniques that adapt to environmental variations such as irradiance and temperature. This study proposes, an advanced Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) technique with a High-Gain Modified Single-Ended Primary Inductor Converter (SEPIC) (HGMS) Converter is proposed for enhancing the performance of a solar-powered Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM). White Shark Optimized (WSO) Artificial Neural Network (ANN) MPPT algorithms become crucial to confirm that PV array works and tracks maximum amount of output power at its optimal power point under all conditions. The use of a Three-Phase Voltage Source Inverter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \varnothing$</tex> VSI) results in improved power quality by producing output voltage and current that are more balanced and steadier also, the speed of PMSM motor is highly controlled with the aid of Proportional Integral (PI) controller. Simulation outcomes obtained from MATLAB validation demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms with a THD of 2.2% and tracking efficiency of 98%. Overall, it enhances tracking speed, improves the overall system efficiency, and ensures efficient power flow to the drivetrain of the EVs.

title: Spur-Line Loaded Microstrip Antenna for NonContact Ethanol Detection
authors: Muhammad Reval, Queen Hesti Ramadhamy, Mumuh Muharam, Rudy Fernandez, Hanalde Andre
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Microstrip antennas are compact and easy to fabricate and can function as sensors by detecting resonance frequency shifts caused by changes in the surrounding medium. This study presents a 2.4 GHz rectangular patch antenna with a spur-line structure for ethanol concentration sensing using a PMMA microfluidic chip on a FR-4 substrate. The antenna achieves a reflection coefficient below $\mathbf{- 1 0 ~ d B}$ and a VSWR under 2. Testing with ethanol concentrations from $\mathbf{1 0 \%}$ to $\mathbf{9 6 \%}$ shows measurable frequency shifts, with the antenna resonating at 2.43 GHz. Linear regression calibration yields a sensitivity of $\mathbf{5. 9 3 ~ M H z}$ per percent, an accuracy of 96.07%, and a mean absolute error of 7.6% over five trials. The highest precision occurs at 90% concentration with a coefficient of variation of 1.969% and an average variation of 23.352%. These results highlight the antenna’s potential as a compact, reliable sensor for liquid concentration monitoring.

title: A Comparative Evaluation of Routing Protocols in WBANs: Applications, Challenges, and Experimental Results
authors: Amanpreet Kaur, Prabhdeep Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A system called the Wireless Body Area Network (WBAN) employs wireless sensors to track and direct patients with serious medical illnesses, providing them with the information they need for additional treatments. The nearest hospital or medical facility or a neighboring destination node receives the data packets from the BS. The complete procedure requires wireless sensors to have an effective routing protocol without any exception or delay. Routing methods have suffered from numerous limitations and problems that are based on the routing and energy complexities. This comparative research article represents different temperature-aware, cluster-based, and posture-based routing methods are elaborated. The Adaptive Thermal Aware Routing Method (ATAR) is used to reduce the temp increase problem of fixed bio-medical Sensor Nodes (SNs). Despite WBAN's critical role, two primary challenges persist: (1) high energy consumption and limited network lifetime, (2) inadequate quality of service (QoS) in routing, where protocols often fail to ensure priority data transfer for different medical data types. This article's main objective is to study and analyze ATAR protocol and routing protocols for WBAN in the healthcare domain, focusing on addressing the identified routing and energy complexities.

title: Intelligent Monitoring System of Building Energy Consumption Data Based on Machine Learning
authors: Shui Liu, Jiao Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Aiming at the core pain points of single data dimension and lagging anomaly detection in building energy consumption monitoring, this paper proposes an intelligent monitoring system. The system realizes multimodal data collection through edge computing nodes and uses an improved variational mode decomposition algorithm to extract multi-scale features of non-stationary current signals. The core algorithm layer constructs a hybrid deep learning framework, in which the long short-term memory network (LSTM) captures the time-series dependency of energy consumption, the random forest algorithm processes discrete features such as the start and stop status of equipment, and realizes bimodal feature fusion through the attention mechanism. The system deployment layer adopts the edge-cloud collaborative computing architecture to build a digital twin-driven decision engine, decouple the energy consumption prediction results from the building equipment control logic, and generate a dynamic tuning strategy. Under this system, the building EER improvement rate reaches up to 29.5 %, and the cumulative carbon emission reduction is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17,968 \text{kg} \text{CO}_{2}$</tex>, providing an adaptive and evolvable intelligent solution for building energy consumption management.

title: Scaling Wideband Hybrid Beamforming for sub-THz Communication
authors: Amirmohammad Haddad, Oveys Delafrooz Noroozi, Canan Cebeci, Mark J. W. Rodwell, Upamanyu Madhow
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We investigate the capacity attainable for a multiuser MIMO uplink as we scale both array size and bandwidth for regimes in which all-digital arrays incur excessive hardware complexity and power consumption. We consider a tiled hybrid beamforming architecture in which each tile, or subarray, is a phased array performing analog (or RF) beamforming, followed by DSP on the tile outputs. For parameters compatible with sub-THz fixed access links, we discuss hardware and power consumption considerations for choosing tile size and number of tiles. Noting that the problem of optimal multiuser MIMO in our wideband regime is open even for the simplest possible channel models, we compare the spectral efficiencies attainable by a number of reasonable strategies for tile-level RF beamforming, assuming flexibility in the digital signal processing (DSP) of the tile outputs. We consider a number of beam broadening approaches for addressing the "beam squint" incurred by RF beamforming in our wideband regime, along with strategies for sharing tiles among users. Information-theoretic benchmarks are computed for an idealized MIMO-OFDM system, with linear per-subcarrier multiuser detection compared against an unconstrained complexity receiver.

title: Change Point Detection, Localization, and Imputation of Topological Signals
authors: Rohan Money, Baltasar Beferull-Lozano, Elvin Isufi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We propose a topology-aware online framework for detecting and localizing change points in partially observed temporal signals defined over cellular complexes. We model the process as a linear state space model. The latent dynamics follow a topology-consistent stochastic partial differential equation (SPDE), and the observations a topologically-filtered version of the state. Hidden states are inferred via a Kalman filter, while model parameters are adapted online through likelihood-based updates. Change points are detected by tracking deviations in the uncertainty parameters using a score-based criterion, and the responsible components are localized by mapping these deviations to specific cells in the complex. Under missing or noisy observations, backward Kalman smoothing—reinitialized at each detected change—provides consistent state reconstruction and reliable imputation. Experiments on synthetic and EPANET-simulated water networks demonstrate accurate detection, precise localization, and robust reconstruction under partial observability.

title: Intelligent solutions for E-Waste Management using IoT and Public Awareness
authors: R. Kalyani, M. Lakshmi, P. Sarah, P. Durga Jaya Ram, Lalitha Bai K, K. Sunithaa
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Management of electronic waste (e-waste) has emerged as a significant environmental issue, with improper disposal contributing to pollution and the depletion of valuable resources. A recent survey was conducted to assess public awareness, disposal habits, and perceptions regarding e-waste recycling. The results reveal that a notable percentage of participants (29.7%) opt to sell their old electronics, while 34.6% choose to store them; however, only 10.3% engage in active recycling. These findings highlight the need for better awareness campaigns and improved e-waste collection systems. Based on this problem, we are going to present a new design for the water flow level indicator using components from repaired e-waste. This system uses these disposed batteries to charge a solar panel and then use the energy to run a Node-MCU-based smart monitoring system to help better manage water. Not only does this method provide a sustainable solution towards conservation of resources, but it also demonstrates the exact applicability of e-waste. The approach involves the design of an arrangement with water level sensors, the incorporation of an energy-saving Internet of Things (IoT) system, and the renewal of energy storage using upcycled batteries. Tests carried out have proved the effectiveness of this technology, namely the ability of the system to detect water levels and to use the energy efficiently. The research underlines the sustainability of e-waste reuse in terms of introducing cost-effective and ecological solutions and also it advocates for public awareness and policy reforms

title: A Spatial Array for Spectrally Agile Wireless Processing
authors: Ali Rasteh, Andrew Hennessee, Ishaan Shivhare, Siddharth Garg, Sundeep Rangan, Brandon Reagen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Massive MIMO is a cornerstone of next-generation wireless communication, offering significant gains in capacity, reliability, and energy efficiency. However, to meet emerging demands such as high-frequency operation, wide bandwidths, co-existence, integrated sensing, and resilience to dynamic interference, future systems must exhibit both scalability and spectral agility. These requirements place increasing pressure on the underlying processing hardware to be both efficient and reconfigurable. This paper proposes a custom-designed spatial array architecture that serves as a reconfigurable, general-purpose core optimized for a class of wireless kernels that commonly arise in diverse communications and sensing tasks. The proposed spatial array is evaluated against specialized cores for each kernel using High-Level Synthesis (HLS). Both the reconfigurable and specialized designs are synthesized in a 32 nm process to assess latency, throughput, area, and power in realistic processes. The results identify conditions under which general-purpose systolic architectures can approach the efficiency of specialized cores, thereby paving the way toward more scalable and agile systems.

title: A Machine Learning Approach to Rainfall Estimation in Tropical Coastal Regions Using Doppler Radar and ERA5 Reanalysis Data
authors: Prince Alex, Krishnendu Lalaji, Deepak Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This work investigates the potential of supervised machine learning models for estimating hourly rainfall in a tropical coastal environment (lat 8.53, lon 76.86) by integrating C-band Doppler radar parameters with ERA5 atmospheric predictors and ground-based rain gauge observations. Six regression models were trained and evaluated using 744 hourly samples from October 2023, an extreme rainfall month. Random Forest achieved the best performance with an $\mathbf{R}$ score of 0.46 and RMSE of 3.77, closely followed by the ensemble Voting Regressor at $\mathbf{R}=\mathbf{0. 4 2}$. Feature-importance analysis revealed that differential phase and reflectivity were the leading strong predictors across models. Overall accuracy remained moderate due to the limited dataset and complex rainfall dynamics; however, the results set the perspective for developing hybrid radar polarimetric- reanalysis data model applications in quantitative precipitation estimation. This study sets up a baseline in data-driven rainfall prediction over the Thumba coastal region and emphasizes the urgent requirement for larger, multi-season datasets along with advanced hybrid models for operational forecasting.

title: Graph Contrastive Learning for Connectome Classification
authors: Martín Schmidt, Sara Silva, Federico Larroca, Gonzalo Mateos, Pablo Musé
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: With recent advancements in non-invasive techniques for measuring brain activity, such as magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), the study of structural and functional brain networks through graph signal processing (GSP) has gained notable prominence. GSP stands as a key tool in unraveling the interplay between the brain’s function and structure, enabling the analysis of graphs defined by the connections between regions of interest—referred to as connectomes in this context. Our work represents a further step in this direction by exploring supervised contrastive learning methods within the realm of graph representation learning. The main objective of this approach is to generate subject-level (i.e., graph-level) vector representations that bring together subjects sharing the same label while separating those with different labels. These connectome embeddings are derived from a graph neural network Encoder-Decoder architecture, which jointly considers structural and functional connectivity. By leveraging data augmentation techniques, the proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in a gender classification task using Human Connectome Project data. More broadly, our connectome-centric methodological advances support the promising prospect of using GSP to discover more about brain function, with potential impact to understanding heterogeneity in the neurodegeneration for precision medicine and diagnosis.

title: SmartCloud AI/ML-Infused Architectures: Sustainable Cloud Computing Architectures Environments Optimization
authors: Jagadeesh Varrey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The exponential growth of cloud computing has significantly increased its environmental impact, with data centers now accounting for approximately 1% of global electricity consumption. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) techniques into cloud computing architectures to enhance sustainability. Propose a novel carbon-aware computing algorithm that dynamically optimizes workload scheduling based on renewable energy availability and thermal management constraints. Our architecture leverages edge computing integration, federated learning, and AI-driven resource optimization to minimize energy consumption while maintaining performance benchmarks. The proposed solution demonstrates potential reductions in carbon emissions by up to 30% compared to conventional cloud architectures through intelligent workload distribution and predictive scaling. This research contributes to the emerging field of sustainable computing by providing a structured approach to balancing computational demands with environmental responsibility.

title: Microstrip Antenna with Interdigital Capacitor for pH Measurement
authors: Raisha Amilia, Hanalde Andre, Baharuddin, Darwison, Rudy Fernandez, Rizki Wahyu Pratama
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication, and experimental evaluation of a microstrip antenna integrated with an interdigital capacitor (IDC) for label-free pH sensing applications. The antenna operates at 2.45 GHz with a simulated Q-factor of 644.74, indicating good impedance matching and strong resonance characteristics. The fabricated prototype shows a small frequency shift of 12 MHz higher than the simulated result and a Q-factor reduction of approximately 4.5%, reaching 615.5. Experimental results demonstrate a nearly linear increase in resonant frequency with increasing pH, yielding an average sensitivity of 1. 1 4 ~ M H z / pH over the range of pH 3 to 10. These findings confirm that the proposed antenna provides stable and repeatable performance suitable for non-contact and label-free pH sensing applications.

title: Quad-Radiator MIMO with Asymmetric Feed Line for (n77/n78/n79/WLAN) 5G-Wireless Applications
authors: Manasa R, Trupti Shripad Tagare, B Lakshmi Narayana Reddy, S. Thulasi Prasad, Kolli Venkatrao, T. Saravanan, N. Karuppiah, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Deepak Kapila
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This article explores advanced antenna techniques specifically within the sub-6GHz frequency range, focusing on compact Quad port antennas tailored for 5G bands that offer extensive bandwidth capabilities. The primary aim of this research is to achieve a broader bandwidth and elevated data rates while preserving a nearly omnidirectional radiation pattern through a unique design methodology. To improve the necessary mutual coupling, the quad patch elements are arranged face-to-face with adequate spacing between the antenna elements in the MIMO (Multiple Input Multiple Output) system. The enhanced design features an inverted T-shaped partial ground and square patches equipped with stub elements on both sides to optimize impedance matching. Additionally, the role of the twin stub and the rectangular notch positioned at the top right corner of the patch is thoroughly examined. An asymmetrical feed line structure is incorporated for the operation of the Quad port antenna, which measures (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} \times \mathrm{L}$</tex>) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31 \times 40 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. This compact configuration achieves an impedance bandwidth (-10 dB) spanning from 2.27 to 5.34 GHz. The assessment of key antenna performance metrics, including correlation coefficient, diversity gain, and isolation—crucial in the current wireless environment—has been thoroughly addressed. Furthermore, the paper presents a highly effective design approach for n77/n78/n79 band antennas that enables wideband coverage, appropriate correlation, and high isolation for wireless 5G communications.

title: Causal Analysis of Graph Signals for Brain Effectome Inference
authors: Srikar Mutnuri, Aniruddha Adiga, Srinivasan Venkatramanan, Madhav V. Marathe
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Understanding the directed interactions between brain regions is critical for analyzing neuro-degenerative diseases like Alzheimer’s (AD). Traditional functional connectivity (FC) methods capture statistical associations but fail to infer causal relationships, limiting their ability to reveal structural disruptions in disease progression. In this exploratory work, we propose a causal graph inference and spectral analysis framework for EEG signals. Specifically, we leverage Granger causality and spectral graph methods to construct and analyze the effective connectome (effectome) of the brain. Our work reveals that AD networks exhibit lower algebraic connectivity (λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), reduced modularity (eigenvalue gaps), and increased structural sensitivity to perturbations, compared to healthy individuals. Additionally, a study of temporal dynamics over the inferred networks shows reduced and local node volatility patterns over time for AD brain networks.

title: Leveraging FPGAs for Homomorphic Matrix-Vector Multiplication in Oblivious Message Retrieval
authors: Grant Bosworth, Keewoo Lee, Sunwoong Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: While end-to-end encryption protects the content of messages, it does not secure metadata, which exposes sender and receiver information through traffic analysis. A plausible approach to protecting this metadata is to have senders post encrypted messages on a public bulletin board and receivers scan it for relevant messages. Oblivious message retrieval (OMR) leverages homomorphic encryption (HE) to improve user experience in this solution by delegating the scan to a resource-rich server while preserving privacy. A key process in OMR is the homomorphic detection of pertinent messages for the receiver from the bulletin board. It relies on a specialized matrix-vector multiplication algorithm, which involves extensive multiplications between ciphertext vectors and plaintext matrices, as well as homomorphic rotations. The computationally intensive nature of this process limits the practicality of OMR. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a hardware architecture to accelerate the matrix-vector multiplication algorithm. The building homomorphic operators in this algorithm are implemented using high-level synthesis, with design parameters for different parallelism levels. These operators are then deployed on a field-programmable gate array platform using an efficient design space exploration strategy to accelerate homomorphic matrix-vector multiplication. Compared to a software implementation, the proposed hardware accelerator achieves a ×13.86 speedup.

title: Blinding the Wiretapper: RIS-Enabled User Occultation in the ISAC Era
authors: Getuar Rexhepi, Hyeon Seok Rou, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas de Abreu, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: An undesirable consequence of the foreseeable proliferation of sophisticated integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) technologies is the enabling of spoofing, by malicious agents, of situational information (such as proximity, direction or location) of legitimate users of wireless systems. In order to mitigate this threat, we present a novel ISAC scheme that, aided by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS), enables the occultation of the positions of user equipment (UE) from wiretappers, while maintaining both sensing and desired communication performance between the UEs and a legitimate base station (BS). To that end, we first formulate an RIS phase-shift optimization problem that jointly maximizes the sum-rate performance of the UEs (communication objective), while minimizing the projection of the wiretapper’s effective channel onto the legitimate channel (hiding objective), thereby disrupting the attempts by a wiretapper of localizing the UEs. Then, in order to efficiently solve the resulting non-convex joint optimization problem, a novel manifold optimization algorithm is derived, whose effectiveness is validated by numerical results, which demonstrate that the proposed approach preserves legitimate ISAC performance while significantly degrading the wiretapper’s sensing capability.

title: Study Comparison of Dual Polarization Weather Radar Quality Control: Linear PhiDP and ZPHI Method for Mitigating Signal Attenuation
authors: Ali Wardhana, Rizaldi Boer, Bambang Dwi Dasanto, Danang Eko Nuryanto, I Putu Santikayasa
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Attenuation in C-band weather radar significantly impairs quantitative precipitation estimation (QPE). This study assesses two dual-polarization attenuation correction techniques, Linear PhiDP and ZPHI, for the BMKG C-band radar at Juanda, Surabaya. Utilizing data from heavy rainfall events, we validated corrected reflectivity against the radar’s factory product and ground-based AWS/ARG observations. Qualitatively, both Linear PhiDP and ZPHI successfully restored reflectivity amplitude and spatial coherence. However, quantitative rainfall validation revealed that QPE accuracy was not influenced by the correction method. A two-way ANOVA confirmed that the selection of attenuation algorithm (Linear PhiDP, ZPHI, or Factory) had no statistically significant impact on rainfall estimation error (p>0.32). Conversely, the Z-R relationship emerged as a statistically significant factor (p<0.09). A Tukey HSD post-hoc test identified the Rosenfeld Z–R equation as yielding the lowest mean absolute error. This finding was consistent across both validation sites, where Rosenfeld-based estimates provided the lowest RMSE. While all methods exhibited moderate temporal correlations ($r \approx 0.33-0.54$), they also exhibited negative $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ values, indicating systematic bias. Cross-correlation heatmaps confirmed that Linear PhiDP and ZPHI products were nearly identical ($\mathbf{r} \approx 0.99 - 1.00$). The findings substantiate that while phase-based correction restores reflectivity structure, QPE accuracy in this tropical environment is more contingent upon selecting an appropriate Z-R equation.

title: MolBioART: Molecular Biological ART for Drug Discovery
authors: Sourav Singh Sehgal, Rahul Shrivastava, Trasha Bansal, Rohit Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This work presents a hybrid framework combining Adaptive Resonance Theory (ART) clustering with deep neural network–based molecular restoration for efficient, target-specific drug optimization. Drug–protein pairs are first encoded into vector representations and clustered using ART to form interpretable prototype classes. For a new protein target, the nearest ART prototype serves as an initial candidate. A Protein-Guided Drug Restoration (PGDR) module then refines these candidates by learning to reconstruct optimal drug vectors from perturbed, protein-conditioned inputs. The restored molecules are further optimized in latent space under efficacy and pharmacological constraints. Experiments on benchmark DTI datasets show that the framework enhances interpretability and candidate quality, providing a scalable and biologically grounded approach to drug repurposing and de novo design.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Intelligent System Design for IoT Security using Stacking Ensemble Meta-Learning
authors: N. V. Dhandapani, Tannmay Gupta, Sakthi Prabha Ramachandran, Suma N G, Deepu B, Teena Vats
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Rising usage of IoT devices in different areas has caused security issues, including the chance for hacks like DoS, spoofing and malware. To solve these problems, this research suggests using the Stacking Ensemble Meta-learning (SEM) framework for detecting anomalies in IoT systems. The intended structure has five basic classifiers—DT, KNN, SGD, SVM and NB—organized in several layers and a meta-learner that enhances prediction accuracy by blending their outputs. The suggested approach outperformed others in experimental runs using a simulated DS2OS dataset which had <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> samples and eight different types of activity. SEM performed best with an accuracy of 96.5% as well as top precision and ROC-AUC scores out of all the models tested. The Friedman test added more proof that it is a reliable method. By using such an approach, IoT security is strengthened by detecting anomalies efficiently.

title: Memory-Efficient Keyword Spotting*
authors: Sanghyeon Ju, Sunggu Lee
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Efficient processing of speech input is an important basic signal processing task. The first step of speech processing is typically Keyword Spotting (KWS), which wakes up a smart edge device upon detection of a specific keyword. This work addresses an extremely memory-efficient implementation of KWS for edge devices with limited computational and memory resources. The proposed method uses a novel approach based on the inverse Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), which aims to significantly reduce memory consumption without compromising accuracy. It not only reduces memory usage but also has the potential to improve accuracy. Extensive experiments were conducted using standard KWS dataset, and the results show that the method leads to a significant reduction in memory usage while maintaining previous state-of-the-art accuracy levels. The accuracy variations remain minimal, indicating that the method provides an effective balance between reducing memory consumption and preserving model performance.

title: Adaptive Rate Limiting Against DDoS HTTP Flood Attacks Using Machine Learning
authors: Salem Omar Sati, Abobaker Elgasaier, Islam Alsigoutri, Mahmud Milud Mansour
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks, particularly HTTP Flood Attacks, pose significant threats to web service availability by exploiting legitimate HTTP protocols to overwhelm servers. Traditional detection methods often fail to adapt to evolving attack patterns, necessitating intelligent and explainable machine learning (ML)-based solutions. This paper evaluates three ML models Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and k-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN) for detecting HTTP Flood Attacks, the paper tests model performance through precision-recall curves and comprehensive metrics, including accuracy, recall, F1-score, and Average Precision (AP). Results demonstrate that all models achieve perfect AP, indicating optimal separability between attack and normal traffic. Further analysis reveals that RF and SVM exhibit superior robustness, with balanced precision and recall, while K-NN shows marginally lower recall at high decision thresholds. Our findings underscore that RF and SVM are ideal for real-time deployment, combining high detection accuracy (AP=1.00) with actionable insights for adaptive rate limiting. K-NN, despite its precision, may lack recall consistency in adversarial scenarios.

title: RGB and Thermal Image based Human Intrusion Detection in Off-Road Scenarios: A New Dataset and Benchmark
authors: Rohit Yadav, Amit Kumar Mitra, Rahul Kala
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: We introduce a novel RGB and thermal dataset specifically designed for human intrusion detection in challenging off-road environments, captured using multiple synchronised RGB cameras and a thermal camera mounted on autonomous ground vehicles (AGVs). The dataset encompasses diverse terrains, varying lighting conditions, and dynamic human activities such as walking, running, and crawling. To assess its effectiveness, we benchmark several state-of-the-art object detection algorithms on this dataset, evaluating their robustness and limitations in complex, unstructured off-road scenarios. Our results provide critical insights into the capabilities of image-only perception for reliable human detection, particularly under low-light and cluttered backgrounds, which are crucial for safe AGV operation. Experimental results show that DINOv2 gives a very good performance on both RGB and Thermal images, while the performance on RGB images is generally better. The performance of transformer-based models, including Grounding DINO, DETR and RT-DETR, along with YOLOv8, is better compared to more classic deep-learning approaches. The dataset is available at https://github.com/RoitYaadav/DT

title: Voltage Oriented Controller (VOC) Based Vienna Rectifier For Electric Vehicle Charging Stations
authors: Kandakatla. Vishnu, M.Malavika, Shaik.Khaisar, K. Ashwini, A. Siddharth Reddy, G. Srinivas
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) charging systems that are not only dependable and efficient but also compliant with current power grid regulations are becoming more and more necessary as EVs continue to gain popularity. The Vienna Rectifier, a three-phase, three-level converter renowned for its high efficiency, small size, and capacity to reduce power quality problems like harmonic distortion, is a viable option for transforming AC grid power into the DC power needed by EVs. Voltage Oriented Control (VOC) is frequently employed in such systems to guarantee accurate control and stable functioning. Active and reactive power components can be independently controlled by VOC, usually using proportional-integral (PI) controls. Despite being straightforward and popular, PI controllers frequently have trouble with abrupt load variations, non-linear behaviors, and system uncertainties.To address these challenges, this study looks at taking the place of the traditional PI controller with a Fuzzy Logic Controller (FLC) in a VOC-based Vienna Rectifier setup. With its more adaptable, rule-based methodology and lack of reliance on a precise mathematical model, FLCs are more appropriate for dynamic and complicated settings. Under the same conditions, both control strategies are examined using MATLAB/Simulink. Total harmonic distortion (THD), dynamic stability, transient response, and voltage regulation are used to compare their performance. With lower THD, faster time response, less overshoot, and better voltage management, the FLC dramatically enhances system performance, according to the results. According to these findings, FLCs may be crucial in the development of future EV charging stations that are more resilient and intelligent.

title: Semantic Communication for Speech Question Answering Tasks
authors: Zhikai Liu, Tharmalingam Ratnarajah
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents an efficient multi-user semantic communication system tailored for generative end-to-end speech question-answering (QA) tasks. Utilizing a time-slotted framework with a distributed memory module, the system leverages contextual information from previous interactions to enhance performance. A sophisticated semantic encoder converts speech inputs into discrete units, capturing essential semantics for streamlined processing. Robust transmission is ensured by a joint source-channel (JSC) codec, while a state-of-the-art language model at the central base station (BS) manages both extractive and abstractive QA tasks. A rigorous five-step training process, culminating in end-to-end system optimization, ensures seamless integration and peak efficiency of all components. Simulations demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms existing semantic communication-based speech QA models for both text and speech transmission. The text-based baseline consists of a pipeline integrating speech-to-text conversion, text-based QA, and text-to-speech synthesis, while the speech-based baseline involves centralized speech recovery, tokenization, and speech QA modules. In comparison, our system achieves up to 67.1% higher answer accuracy while reducing parameter complexity by 39.1%.

title: Person Re-Identification in Complex Environments Based on Multimodal Feature Extraction and Data Augmentation Methodology Research
authors: Yan Hao, Zhang Ming, Tongzhou Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Occlusion and viewpoint variation are core challenges in image-text cross-modal person re-identification (ReID). To enhance recognition robustness in occluded scenes, this study proposes a CLIP-driven data augmentation method, which improves system performance through a dynamic part decoder and a three-level fusion module based on SeNetV2. The dynamic part decoder employs learnable query vectors to adaptively locate discriminative regions, combined with a visibility predictor to suppress interference from occluded areas. The three-level fusion module constructs a hierarchical optimization architecture (Spatial Enhancement → Part Enhancement → Fusion Enhancement), achieving data augmentation by reinforcing spatial details, optimizing part discriminability, and enhancing feature compatibility. Experiments on the Market1501, DukeMTMC-reID, and Occluded-Duke datasets show that the method achieves improvements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+0.4 \%,+0.4 \%$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 2. 0 \%}$</tex> in mAP, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{0. 5 \%}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{1. 4 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+\mathbf{0. 5 \%}$</tex> in Rank-1 accuracy, respectively. The results verify the method's effectiveness in occluded scenarios, with particularly significant performance gains on the heavily occluded dataset (Occluded-Duke).

title: Wavelet-Based Conditional Denoising Diffusion Model for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Rujie Wang, Xuancheng Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The widespread presence of low-resolution images in the real world poses significant challenges. Most existing super-resolution methods operate solely within the original pixel space, which largely constrains the exploration of multi-scale image features and prevents deep models from fully leveraging the rich details required for high-resolution image reconstruction. Aiming to resolve the well-documented shortcomings of prior works, this paper proposes a novel super-resolution framework termed Wave-SRDiff. Built upon a conditional diffusion model, this framework effectively integrates the multi-scale feature extraction capabilities of wavelet transform, with a dedicated emphasis on enhancing the capture and utilization of critical frequency characteristics in images. Furthermore, by leveraging the multi-resolution analysis capacity of wavelet transform, we have redesigned the residual module to extract multi-granularity feature information. Extensive experiments were conducted on various controlled and in-the-wild datasets. The results establish Wave-SRDiff achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in image super-resolution tasks, delivering reconstructions with remarkably improved perceptual quality.

title: ARWNet: Auto-Regressive Wavelet Network for Learned Infrared Image Compression
authors: Qiao Cao, Yue Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Infrared imaging systems continuously produce massive data streams, imposing strict constraints on storage and transmission bandwidth. However, traditional codecs and existing learning-based compression models often struggle to preserve essential thermal structures under low bitrate conditions. To address this issue, we propose ARWNet, a learned infrared image compression framework that integrates wavelet frequency decomposition with channel-wise autoregressive entropy modeling. A Hierarchical Wavelet Fusion (HWF) module is designed to enhance structural-textural separation in the latent space, while an Adaptive Feature Refinement (AFR) module improves reconstruction fidelity through locally attention-guided feature aggregation. Experiments conducted on the FLIR-ADAS dataset demonstrate that ARWNet achieves superior rate-distortion performance compared with state-of-the-art learned compression baselines, achieving an average PSNR improvement of + 0.5 7 dB and up to 28% bitrate reduction under similar reconstruction quality. These results validate the effectiveness of combining wavelet decomposition and autoregressive modeling for highfidelity and efficient infrared image compression.

title: Medical Image Synthesis and Multi-View Reconstruction Using CGAN and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Nirmalrani V, D P School of Managementashekar, Arghya Das Dev, Divyanshi Rajvanshi, P. Mayilvel Kumar, Nittin Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In this work, a high-fidelity 3D view is generated by presenting a sophisticated Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN) model to convert 2D radiographic images into 3D upon which medical visualization can be improved. The model allows full multi-angle reconstruction from 0 to 360 with the possibility of focusing on specific bones like the knee, elbow, and lower limbs, similar to the result of a CT and MRI scan. The method involves a lot of pre-processing noise removal, resampling, rescaling, normalization, and augmentation to align heterogeneous DICOM datasets acquired through various sources in different hospitals. Utilizing procurement, bottleneck, and extending routes, Leaky ReLU, tanh, and sigmoid activations, our U-Netbased network successfully accomplishes 3D composition. Training is done with a hybrid loss function that uses L1, MSE, BCE and Wasserstein metrics, which is optimized with Adam optimizer. SSIM, PSNR, MSE and entropy-based evaluation show high visual quality and similarity to ground-truth data. The model provides a cheap, radiation-free option over conventional imaging, enhanced diagnostic performance, and accessibility in resource-limited medical care environment.

title: Enhancing Seismic DAS Data Through Multi-Stage Signal Denoising and SNR Analysis
authors: Noor Izni Zafirah Mohd Radzi, Norashikin Yahya, Ismailalwali Alobaid Magzoub Babikir, Jafreezal Jaafar
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) offers a costeffective solution for seismic monitoring by transforming standard fiber optic cables into dense arrays of virtual sensors. However, DAS data are often contaminated by diverse noise sources from system and environmental factors, complicating seismic interpretation. Effective preprocessing is crucial for enhancing signal clarity while preserving seismic event integrity. This paper proposes a multi-stage denoising framework that combines spike removal, amplitude clipping, notch filtering, frequency-wavenumber (FK) filtering, bandpass filtering, and automatic gain control (AGC) to address the distinct noise characteristics in DAS data. The approach is quantitatively evaluated using Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) analysis on field datasets from the Pyrenees dataset, France. Results show that the proposed framework provides robust noise suppression and improved signal quality compared to single-stage approaches, offering a practical and interpretable preprocessing strategy for DAS-based seismic applications.

title: AI-Driven Formal Verification for Efficient and Scalable Design Validation
authors: Aparna Mohan, Sivakumar Karuppiah, Uday Ashokbhai Korat
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As modern hardware and software designs grow increasingly complex, traditional formal verification techniques face critical challenges, including state space explosion and excessive computational cost, limiting their practical application in large-scale systems. This paper introduces a novel AI-driven formal verification framework that leverages machine learning models to intelligently guide and optimize the verification process. By predicting critical state exploration paths and automatically generating invariants, our approach significantly reduces the verification time and memory usage compared to conventional methods. We integrate a reinforcement learning agent with symbolic model checking, enabling adaptive decision-making that prioritizes relevant design regions while pruning irrelevant paths. Extensive experiments on benchmark hardware designs demonstrate up to 60% improvement in verification speed and substantial scalability gains without compromising correctness guarantees. The proposed method bridges the gap between rigorous formal guarantees and practical scalability, offering a transformative solution for efficient and automated design validation in the era of complex system development.

title: A Theoretical Model Explaining the Impact on Satisfaction, Engagement, and Repurchase Intention in Modern Retailing
authors: Do Bui Xuan Cuong, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study aims to test a theoretical model that explains how an omnichannel customer experience can improve satisfaction, engagement, and repurchase intention in modern retail. Using a quantitative research approach, a survey of 533 customers was conducted, and the data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings confirm that all proposed hypotheses were substantiated with a high degree of statistical significance, demonstrating a strong model fit. The results indicate that an omnichannel customer experience has a positive impact on satisfaction (SAT), engagement (CE), and repurchase intention (RI). Additionally, SAT and CE both positively influence RI and act as partial mediators between the omnichannel experience and repurchase behavior. These findings underscore the importance of delivering seamless experiences across all channels to foster customer loyalty and encourage repeat purchases. The study strengthens the theoretical foundation of the Stimulus-Organism-Response framework and offers practical insights for firms undergoing digital transformation.

title: User Side Fault Diagnosis and Intelligent Management Integrating Big Data and Intelligent Systems
authors: Xiaoxuan Guo, Yasai Wang, Leping Sun, Min Guo, Tonghe Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Agricultural parks consist of many functional parts for crops cultivation, including irrigation systems, environmental monitoring equipment, and other important infrastructure. In order to prevent and respond to various technical failures, accurate and real-time monitoring needs to be carried out. This article is based on big data and artificial intelligent technology, and conducted in-depth mining on it. It adopts an intelligent fault diagnosis and management system based on a combination of machine learning and big data analysis. By combining discrete data with intelligent algorithms, the fault diagnosis process is optimized to improve the real-time and accuracy of the system. By automatically collecting and analyzing various information during the production process, scientific decision-making basis is provided for enterprise management to reduce human errors and improve operational efficiency. This article also optimizes and improves existing user side fault diagnosis and intelligent management technologies. Our results indicate that the fault prediction accuracy achieved in this article is significantly improved. The research results can provide theoretical basis and practical guidance for the transformation, upgrading, and development model innovation of agricultural parks in China.

title: IoT-Based Smart Electric Tractor: Integrated Soil Quality Monitoring System and Solar-Powered Battery Management for Sustainable Precision Agriculture
authors: Darjat, Susatyo Handoko, Ajub Ajulian Zahra, Danendra Anggareksa Rasendria, Aulia Sefia Putri Melati, Rina Ayu Saraswati
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This research responds to the challenge of modern agricultural inefficiency by developing an integrated monitoring system on electric tractors to simultaneously monitor soil quality and battery power management. The system utilizes a 7-In-1 Soil Sensor to measure N, P, K, pH, temperature, and humidity parameters, as well as a PZEM-017 sensor to monitor the battery status, where all data is transmitted wirelessly via the ESP-NOW protocol to Blynk’s IoT platform and LCD display. The test results showed reliable system functionality, as evidenced by sensor calibration with an average error value of $\mathbf{p H}$ of $\mathbf{5. 6 2 \%}$, real-time ground data acquisition with an average of Nitrogen $222.6 \mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{kg}$, Phosphorus $24.7 \mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{kg}$, Potassium $175 \mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{kg}$ after ploughing, and efficient battery performance, where a solar-powered charging station can charge the battery in 222 minutes. For 90 minutes of operation time. By providing crucial dual data on a single platform, the system succeeds in being an effective solution to support data-driven decision-making, improve operational efficiency, and drive sustainable precision farming practices.

title: Enhanced Fault Diagnosis in Power Transmission Systems Using Machine Learning and Neural Network Models
authors: Thiyagesan M, J. Latha, L. Annie Isabella, Manoj Kumar N, Nirmal Kumar J, Rahul K
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A breakdown in the supply lines of electrical power may happen suddenly due to a great number of unpredictable factors, which may lead to severe destruction of the power grid in case they remain unnoticed and not followed over a long period of time. The given paper is oriented to fault classification that occurs in the electric transmission lines. In the study, the dataset utilized consists of 12,000 data points and six different features to test and train a number of machine learning models. The fault classification was performed with the help of such algorithms as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes, Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP) and XGBoost. The models were ranked against the metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and training time. The findings show that the tree-based algorithms were the best performing algorithms particularly the random forest and the decision tree with the highest level of accuracy of 100 percent in detecting and classifying faults. In comparison, Logistic Regression and SVM demonstrated comparatively poorer performance. The results emphasize the better efficacy of ensemble learning methods and neural networks in effectively identifying transmission line faults, hence providing a trustworthy solution for enhancing the reliability and efficiency of power transmission systems.

title: MIMO Communications with 1-bit RIS: Asymptotic Analysis and Over-the-Air Channel Diagonalization
authors: Panagiotis Gavriilidis, Kyriakos Stylianopoulos, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents an asymptotic analysis of Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) systems assisted by a 1-bit Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) under Ricean fading conditions. Using random matrix theory, we show that, in the asymptotic regime, the dominant singular values and vectors of the transmitter–RIS and RIS–receiver channels converge to their deterministic Line-of-Sight (LoS) components, almost irrespective of the Ricean factors. This enables RIS phase configuration using only LoS information through a closed-form Sign Alignment (SA) rule that maximizes the channel gain. Furthermore, when the RIS is asymptotically larger than the transceiver arrays, proper RIS configuration can render the end-to-end MIMO channel in the capacity formula asymptotically diagonal, thereby eliminating inter-stream interference and enabling Over-The-Air (OTA) spatial multiplexing without channel knowledge at the transmitter. Building on this result, a waterfilling-inspired SA algorithm that allocates RIS elements to spatial streams, based on the asymptotic singular values and statistical channel parameters, is proposed. Simulation results validate the theoretical analyses, demonstrating that the proposed schemes achieve performance comparable to conventional Riemannian manifold optimization, but with orders of magnitude lower runtime.

title: Enhancing Network Traffic Analysis with a Unique Imbalanced Data Algorithm in Deep Learning
authors: P Ashwini, Deepthi Potlakayala, Vivekanand Aelgani, Rajuladev Sridhar, Karthick K, Vishwa Priya V
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Cyber security depends on network traffic analysis to identify anomalies and stop intrusions. Vast network data contains seldom occurring harmful activities which traditional models struggle to detect traffic patterns in. The research created an innovative deep learning system to solve class imbalance problems while producing superior network traffic classification results. The proposed framework combines adaptive data augmentation with deep learning approaches together with cost-sensitive learning and feature optimization methods to boost detection capabilities. Results show that our proposed model surpasses conventional models through more precise metrics and improved performance measures including F1-score and AUC-ROC metrics when tested against benchmark datasets. Testing outcomes demonstrate that our system effectively detects minority class occurrences while maintaining high classification achievements. The research presents a scalable monitoring solution for real traffic inspection which enhances network security analytics technologies.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Malware Classification in Cybersecurity Applications
authors: Indumati R Nagesh, A. Daniel Das, Prateek Garg, Sanjay Bhatnagar, Dharani. V, Pushpalatha M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The proposed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture in our research paper is required to analyze malware in the field of cyber safety. The method combines opcode frequency mappings, control-flow graph embeddings and entropy-derived texture-related features into a common multi-channel Convolutional Neural Network, which allows to extract complementary spatial and structural features. The convolutional processing of each feature type is independent, which is then merged by residual fusion and convolutional attention to improve discrimination ability. Using a hybrid loss criterion that is a combination of cross-entropy and center loss augments intra-class condensation and inter-class separability. Experimental tests using various malware datasets were done to show the proposed method was better than current methods and was found to have 97.8 percent accuracy, 97.2 precision, 96.9 recall, and a 97.0 F1-score with less computational expenditure. Such findings confirm the scalability of the framework with respect to deployments on large scale, as well as the robustness against obfuscation and polymorphism. The solution suggested opens a gap between the static, and the dynamic analysis, and presents efficient and flexible solution to detecting emerging threats in dynamic cybersecurity environment.

title: Advancing Plant Health: Leveraging Machine Learning for Precision Leaf Detection in Agriculture
authors: Shiplu Das, Panchami Das, Nipashree Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In agricultural and botanical applications, accurate leaf detection underpins plant classification, disease identification, and yield estimation. Conventional image processing pipelines face performance degradation under intra-class shape variation, illumination change, occlusion, and complex backgrounds. We present a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)–driven framework that augments scarce and imbalanced plant-leaf datasets with diverse, high-fidelity synthetic imagery, thereby strengthening downstream machine learning performance. Our system integrates Progressive Growing GANs (PGGAN), transfer learning with state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN; ResNet, EfficientNet), Faster R-CNN for lesion localization, hybrid K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)+Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for low-resource inference contexts, and custom loss formulations (weighted cross-entropy, focal loss) to prioritize severe disease classes. Experimental evaluation demonstrates consistent performance gains across accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU) when GAN-augmented data are incorporated. Notably, Faster R-CNN with GAN augmentation achieved 97.1% accuracy and 92.3 mIoU, while CNN+GAN achieved 96.7% accuracy. These results highlight the promise of synthetic-data–driven, multi-model pipelines for robust, scalable, and early plant disease detection in precision agriculture.
