title: Cross-Layer Task Scheduling for NOMA-Assisted Satellite Edge Computing_supp1-3677654.pdf
authors: Guangming CUI
abstract: Ubiquitous, low-latency intelligence at the network edge is central to large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) deployments, yet effectively coordinating communication, computing, and backhaul operations across heterogeneous layers remains challenging. This paper presents a unified cross-layer framework for terrestrial–satellite edge computing IoT systems that integrates Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA)-based terrestrial access with local, satellite, and cloud execution. Unlike conventional terrestrial Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC)/edge– cloud scheduling, we jointly optimize partial offloading and path selection over a NOMA-coupled uplink and a multi-hop satellite edge/cloud execution chain under end-to-end latency coupling. The framework jointly determines path selection and partial offloading to minimize a latency–energy objective using accurate end-to-end models. Within this framework, two complementary scheduling algorithms are developed. The Centralized Optimal Cross-Layer Scheduler (COCS) formulates the joint scheduling problem as a mixed-integer nonlinear program (MINLP) with logarithmic and bilinear terms. It employs the spatial branch-and-bound (sBB) method within a commercial solver to obtain a global solution, serving as a performance benchmark. The Decentralized Game-Theoretic Scheduler (DGTS) models user decisions as an ordinal potential game (OPG) and achieves distributed convergence via best-response dynamics (BRD), enabling scalability and adaptability to large networks. Extensive simulations demonstrate that COCS achieves the global optimum while DGTS attains near-optimal performance with much lower complexity. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed cross-layer framework and highlight the importance of coordinated communication–computation–backhaul design for terrestrial–satellite integrated edge computing.

title: Stress-Strain Analysis and Fatigue Life Prediction of SnBiInZn High-Entropy Alloy QFN Solder Joints Under Random Vibration
authors: Gui Wang, Chunyue Huang, Jisheng Wei, Jingcheng Mo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: A finite element model of SnBiInZn high-entropy alloy(HEA) QFN solder joints was established and subjected to modal analysis. A random vibration stress-strain simulation was conducted, and the simulation results were validated experimentally. Based on Manson’s empirical formula for high-cycle fatigue, combined with Miner’s linear cumulative damage theory and the Steinberg model, the fatigue life of different solder joint materials under random vibration loading was comparatively analyzed. Building upon the confirmed significant influence of key structural parameters—namely solder joint height, pad length, and pad width—a regression analysis was performed using orthogonal experimental design combined with machine learning BP/MLP models. A predictive model for the random vibration fatigue life of QFN solder joints was thereby established, enabling effective prediction of fatigue life under arbitrary combinations of structural parameter levels. The results indicate that among the four solder joint materials (SAC305, SAC387, 62Sn36Pb2Ag, and SnBiInZn HEA), SAC387 exhibits the shortest vibration fatigue life, while the SnBiInZn high-entropy alloy demonstrates the optimal fatigue life, approximately 2.3 times that of SAC305. The developed BP/MLP model achieves high prediction accuracy, with the relative error between predicted and simulated values controlled within 5%.

title: A Novel Piezoelectric-Driven Small Bionic Underwater Robot_supp2-3672852.mp4
authors: Liang Wang
abstract: In deep-sea exploration, compact robotic systems encounter considerable difficulties in sustaining effective propulsion under tremendous hydrostatic pressure exceeding 100 MPa. Conventional electric drive systems suffer significant energy losses due to the requirement for large pressure-resistant enclosures, whereas current actuators utilizing functional materials frequently display constraints such as low thrust-to-weight ratios, elevated drive voltages, or insufficient pressure resistance. This work produced a unique seal-free small bionic underwater robot (SBUR) powered by piezoelectric actuation, inspired by anguilliform locomotion. A dual-SBUR system was employed to enhance the design of an underwater mobile platform (UMP), facilitating combined forward propulsion and steering capabilities. First, the structure and design of the SBUR and UMP are elucidated. Subsequently, modal features in air were examined by the transfer matrix method (TMM), while hydrodynamic responses in water were explored utilizing computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations. Performance evaluations conducted under standard pressure settings revealed a consistent SBUR forward speed of 152 mm/s. The UMP achieved a forward speed of 43.5 mm/s, generated a thrust of 20 mN, and performed rotations with a minimum radius of 130 mm. Experiments validated the practical feasibility of the SBUR at 110 MPa. The performance decline under elevated hydrostatic pressure did not surpass 3.3%, and it demonstrated negligible temperature sensitivity. Practical validation confirmed the SBUR’s ability to transport payloads of up to 10 kg, highlighting its suitability for deep-sea applications. The proposed robot offers advantages such as miniaturization, low operating voltage, and excellent adaptability to extreme hydrostatic pressure.

title: Blood Group Prediction Using Fingerprint Images with EfficientNet-B0
authors: R. Senthilkumar, Sudeep Yallappa Kalapur, Shivakumar V, Shivaprasad R K
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: In health care, emergency response, and forensic analysis, it is imperative to accurately determine blood group type in patients. The existing paradigm requires an invasive blood draw and laboratory, which is frequently not practical in emergency settings. In this report, we present a new approach to predicting blood group type from fingerprint images by using distinctive patterns, for example arches, whorls, and loops. The user-friendly interface allows the user to upload fingerprint images while ensuring confidentiality and user comfort. Using EfficientNet-B0 fine-tuned with a Bayesian approach, we achieved an accuracy of 94.8 % which outperformed established deep convolutional neural networks (CNN) architectures such as ResNet50 and DenseNet. Grad-CAM visualizations also supported that our blood group type predictions were founded on recognized, meaningful features within the fingerprint, allowing for a prodigy of explanations and trust. This innovative solution is truly open-sourced, sustainability designed towards humane and scalable applications in emergency settings, low-resource healthcare facilities, and forensic applications at a very low-cost. By focusing on humane considerations of user efficiency, user context, and confidentiality, our study proposes a new approach to predicting blood group type noninvasively which may proliferate innovations in healthcare and security.

title: Blockchain-Driven Decentralized Finance (DeFi): Transforming the Architecture of Traditional Financial Ecosystems
authors: Nilaish, Farhatnida Unnisa, Bhupendra Bahadur Tiwari
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Blockchain and Decentralized Finance (DeFi) are transforming the financial world by redefining how financial transactions and services operate. This paper explores the convergence of these technologies, emphasizing their potential to disintermediate traditional banking, enhance financial inclusivity, and increase transparency and efficiency. While these innovations hold significant promise, they are also met with challenges, including regulatory uncertainty, security vulnerabilities, and technological limitations. This study analyzes the underlying technologies, key platforms, and comparative frameworks that highlight how DeFi is challenging conventional financial systems, while suggesting avenues for sustainable adoption and global impact

title: A Deep Learning System for Detecting and Categorizing Anomalies in Distributed Systems Using Log Data
authors: Pankaj Kunekar, Atharva Tuljapurkar, Kunal Sonne, Shreeparth Torawane, Hariom Surwase, Raj Thakur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: With the fast growth distributed systems and cloud infrastructures, traditional monitoring solutions has been not able to meet this deluge of log data, and (real) detection of anomalies in those logs needs to use more advanced techniques. In this work, we present a novel hybrid deep learning based model which combines attention-augmented bidirectional LSTM networks with semantic log embeddings approach to further enhance the real-time anomaly detection tasks in distributed systems. Unlike existing works which are mainly based on syntactic patterns, we exploit the Drain algorithm for structured log parsing, SentenceTransformer-based embeddings for contextual correlation and a custom attention mechanism to consider both sequential/quantitative anomalies. Experimental results on the HDFS dataset show that our model obtains 84% and 82% training accuracy and validation accuracy respectively, with an average inference time of 12.3ms per sequence: A promising trade-off to be used for real-time deployment in mission-critical systems.

title: Feature Conditioned Diffusion for Audio Generation
authors: Shakthi Perera, Sandunika Ranasinghe, Senith Jayakody, Buwaneka Epakanda, Roshan Godaliyadda, Mervyn Parakrama Ekanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Audio generation has taken a leap forward with tools that recreate sounds using spectral data, like spectrograms. State of the art audio generation with diffusion models is typically conditioned only on Mel spectrograms. We explored a new approach using the DiffWave model with conditioning features such as Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), Constant Q Transform (CQT), Karhunen-Loève Transform (KLT), and their combinations, tested on LJSpeech, ESC-50, and IRMAS datasets for speech, environmental, and music synthesis. Among the tested methods, the combination of Mel spectrogram and CQT spectrogram achieved the lowest Fréchet Audio Distance (FAD) scores (5.45 on LJSpeech, 6.71 on ESC-50, 4.60 on IRMAS) while maintaining strong precision and coverage. Our results demonstrate that this technique significantly outperforms conventional conditioning methods, offering a promising new approach for audio generation.

title: A Bilingual Retrieval Augmented Generative AI Chatbot Architecture for Inclusive Retail Banking in Egypt
authors: Marwan El-Baily, Fares El-Sayed Mahmoud, Fathy Mahfouz, Yousef Ayman, Ahmed Yasser, Yara Ibrahim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents Cash Compass, a bilingual Modern Standard Arabic - English generative AI chatbot designed to advance ethical financial inclusion in Egyptian retail banking. The system provides real-time, context-aware guidance on deposits, credit, cards, savings, and investment products while scaffolding financial literacy for underserved users. Ethical design principles are embedded across privacy, fairness, transparency, accountability, accessibility, and user protection. The modular architecture integrates intent and entity parsing, a curated banking knowledge base, retrievalaugmented generation, compliance filtering, and adaptive dialogue management optimized for literacy and bandwidth constraints. Evaluation demonstrates improved accuracy, reduced latency, enhanced bilingual clarity, and increased user satisfaction, indicating that Cash Compass can enable trustworthy, inclusive, and scalable digital financial advisory in emerging markets.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Resource Allocation in Intelligent Communication Networks
authors: Dayal Chandra Sati, J. Logeshwaran, S. Dhanasekaran, Mukhtar Sama, Yash Kandi, Rishi Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: As intelligent communication implementations like 5G and IoT-enabled infrastructure meet technological advancement, provisioned network resource allocation dynamically and efficiently will be deemed crucial to accommodate diverse service demands and guarantee an optimized part of the network on demand. Resource management strategies based on traditional approaches often lack a sufficient response to the dynamic posture of network states and complex, heterogeneous environments. To address these challenges, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has emerged as a powerful methodology wherein deep neural networks are employed to enable intelligent and adaptive decision-making based on dynamic network conditions. In this paper, we study the potential of DRL for dynamic resource management in intelligent communication networks. We build a DRL-driven agent that enables optimal allocation policy learning by interacting with high-dimensional, stochastic network environments with variable traffic loads, user mobility, and heterogeneous quality-of-service (QoS) requirements. Realistic simulation scenarios show that the proposed DRL framework outperforms conventional allocation heuristics in terms of throughput, latency, and fairness among users. We elaborate on the ramifications of explorationexploitation tradeoffs, convergence stability, and compute efficiency in the context of scale deployments. This way, our results prove that DRL is a potential candidate for dynamic resource allocation in future intelligent communication networks due to its better adaptability and performance.

title: On Jointly CRB-optimal Arrays and Waveforms for Multi-Target Active Sensing
authors: Ids van der Werf, Geert Leus
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper investigates jointly CRB-optimal array-waveform pairs for active sensing with multiple targets and provides new insights into their structure. We first demonstrate that coherent beamforming, though suboptimal, achieves near-optimal performance. Building on this insight, we develop an iterative sensor relocation algorithm that converges to a locally optimal array configuration. The resulting geometries exhibit intuitive physical patterns and deliver significant performance gains compared to random and canonical designs.

title: Investigation of Longitudinal-Mode Guided Wave Propagation in Corrugated Aluminum Sheaths of High-Voltage Cables
authors: Pu Zhao, Minghua Zhao, Yuping An, Yuqiang Li, Xingxing Huang, Dong Feng, Dongxin He
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The propagation characteristics of longitudinal guided wave modes in wrinkled aluminum sheaths and the influence of corrugation geometry on guided wave behavior are investigated in this study. By comparing the dispersion curves and time-domain responses of smooth and corrugated structures, modal perturbation and cutoff frequency shifts induced by geometric deformation are revealed. To improve computational efficiency, a two-dimensional (2D) axisymmetric model is developed and validated against a three-dimensional (3D) model, confirming its applicability for longitudinal mode analysis. The differences in acoustic wave reception between corrugation crests and troughs, as well as the dispersion, energy distribution, and attenuation of longitudinal guided waves at different frequencies, are systematically investigated. The optimal excitation frequency and detection scheme are determined, and the accuracy of the numerical simulations is verified experimentally. A theoretical foundation for modal selection and frequency optimization in guided wave inspection of high-voltage cable aluminum sheaths is provided by the findings, offering valuable guidance for engineering applications.

title: Type-II Fuzzy Lion Optimization with Chaotic Map-Enhanced Relevance Vector Machine (T2-FLOCM-RVM) for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: D. Hariprasad, K. S. Gowri Laksshmi, S. Shylaja, B. Narasimhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) develops gradually and is usually neglected until it is severe, so it can have major implications and increases the mortality [1]. Early detection of the disease is therefore critical to timely treatment. Traditional machine learning techniques are sometimes restricted in dealing with clinical value ambiguity and noisy medical data, even though their potential in the prediction of the chronic kidney disease (CKD) has been demonstrated. In order to overcome these problems, in this study, Type - II Fuzzy Lion Optimization with Chaotic Map-Enhanced Relevance Vector Machine (T2-FLOCM-RVM) is proposed as a hybrid diagnostic framework. The structure of the architecture has three components: Type - II Fuzzy logic to model the uncertainty of medical data; fuzzy clustering and optimal parameters for better representation of features; and Relevance Vector Machine (RVM) for sparse and probabilistic classification. The accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision and F1-score of the framework were tested with a benchmark CKD data set. The results of the experiments demonstrate the applicability of the proposed method in comparison to the existing fuzzy and RVM-based models. This approach is part of creating intelligent decision support systems that can help healthcare providers identify chronic kidney disease (CKD) early and help improve treatment for the patient.

title: Research on Cable Anomaly Early Warning Technology Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Yiping Hu, Tianyi Guan, Youshi Cui, Kun Liu, Jingyuan Zheng, Xi Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The operating environment of power cables is complex. Relying on a single monitoring parameter is often not enough to accurately detect early insulation damage, leading to frequent false alarms. To solve these problems, this paper proposes an early warning method for cable anomalies based on multi-source data fusion. First, a multi-dimensional feature system is built, which includes online electrical data, environmental conditions, and historical records. Second, to handle the lack of fault samples, a combined weighting strategy using Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (FAHP) and the CRITIC method is proposed. This approach builds a model to quantify cable health, effectively solving the difficulty of training with unlabeled data. Based on this, the Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is used to automatically find the optimal parameters for XGBoost, establishing a model that links the multi-source features to the health scores. Experimental results show that this method effectively combines heterogeneous data. Its accuracy in identifying early anomalies is better than traditional BP neural networks and Support Vector Machine (SVM) methods, making it valuable for practical engineering applications.

title: Thermal Image-Based Bed Posture Recognition and Bed-Exit Detection System with Electronic Activity Log
authors: Yu-Hsien Lu, Jia-Han Yang, Po-Ting Lee, Yi-Shiang Chen, Cheng-Yi Huang, Ping-Hung Hsieh, Chih-Lung Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This work presents an in-bed posture and bed-exit monitoring system based on thermal imaging. The system integrates a ResNet-based Mixture of Experts (MoE) in Bootstrapped Language-Image Pre-training (BLIP) to recognize in-bed posture, bed-exit actions, and falls. The system generates textual descriptions of body condition to reduce the burden on caregivers. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system, combined with the proposed BLIP model, has high classification accuracy, reduced latency, and high specificity compared with YOLOv3-tiny. This result demonstrates that the system is practical for privacy-preserving, reliable, and real-time elderly monitoring.

title: Design and Performance Comparison of Three-Loop Pid Control Strategy for 7-Dof Manipulator Based on Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Yipeng Xie
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In the context of an aging population and the continuous upgrading of industries and services, service robots have become a key technological solution to alleviate labor shortages and enhance operational efficiency. Although 7-degree-of-freedom (7-DOF) robotic arms have excellent adaptability to complex environments due to their kinematic redundancy, their development still faces significant technical bottlenecks that require breakthroughs. In this study, the Denavit-Hartenberg (DH) Matrix and Newton-Euler equations were adopted to construct the kinematic and dynamic models of the 7-DOF robotic arm, enabling precise description of its motion characteristics and dynamic behaviors. To achieve high-precision PID control for robotic arm joints, this study proposes a three-loop cascade control strategy. The PID parameters were optimized using particle swarm optimization (PSO), with performance validation conducted through Simulink simulation platform. Simulation results demonstrate that all joints meet predefined performance metrics. Comparative analysis with sliding mode control (SMC) and adaptive control reveals this method exhibits faster response speed and superior steady-state accuracy. This simulation-based research establishes a solid technical foundation for practical engineering applications.

title: Generative AI Based Network Element Configuration Generator Using Large Language Models
authors: D Dharani, Padmashani R, A. S. Harini, Dharshini V, Kannishree K, Poorvajavani S. K, Prathika A. K
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The rapid evolution of network protocols and infrastructures requires that different Network Elements (NEs) possess efficient and error-free configuration procedures. The tool utilizes Mistral-7B, a Large Language Model (LLM), to simplify and automate the generation, utilization, and verification of NE configurations. Apart from minimizing manual intervention, the solution provides scalability and flexibility across numerous vendors, protocols, and release versions by translating natural language regulations into executable low-level configurations. Task definition, data representation, prompt engineering, model evaluation, tool integration, and validation are the six steps constituting the core of the proposed framework. The system provides fault detection and automated feedback loops with Element Management Systems (EMS) integration. The beneficial use of LLMs in real- world networking scenarios, with a proof-ofconcept model and addressing translation precision for complex inputs, and ethical concerns were showcased in demonstrating the practicality, effectiveness, and scalability of AI-powered solutions in addressing current networking challenges and revolutionizing network management.

title: Stabilizing Reinforcement Learning for Systems with Disturbances Based on Youla-Kucera Prameterization
authors: Long Zhang, ShaoBao Li, Juan Wang, XiaoYuan Luo
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: Ensuring closed-loop stability through reinforcement learning (RL) for systems with disturbances remains a critical challenge in control community. This paper proposes a model-free RL control framework based on Youla-Kucera (YK) parameterization, which embeds stability guarantees into the controller structure to decouple the learning process from closed-loop stability. By treating the stable parameter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex> as the learning agent, the method allows any deep RL algorithm to optimize control policies while retaining a fixed, interpretable controller structure (e.g., PI, PID), significantly enhancing design flexibility and industrial applicability. Leveraging data-driven techniques, the framework constructs an internal model without explicit system identification and employs Hankel matrices for dynamic prediction and noise robustness analysis. Stability analysis demonstrates that the generated control inputs are equivalent to those derived from classical YK parameterization, ensuring closed-loop stability. Simulations on a liquid-level control system validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm under disturbances, outperforming traditional RL methods in terms of average reward convergence and tracking performance.

title: AeroRescue-AI: A Reinforcement-Driven and Risk-Adaptive UAV Framework for Intelligent Search and Rescue Operations
authors: Swarnamouli Majumdar, Anjali Awasthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: As disaster frequency and complexity escalate globally, the need for intelligent, autonomous, and well-coordinated search-and-rescue (SAR) systems has become increasingly critical. This research introduces AeroRescue-AI, a conceptual framework that leverages unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) integrated with reinforcement learning (RL) and data-driven risk assessment to improve the safety, adaptability, and efficiency of SAR missions. Unlike conventional UAV deployments that rely on preprogrammed routes or static response strategies, AeroRescue-AI envisions a dynamic, self-learning system capable of perceiving environmental conditions, assessing situational risks, and autonomously adjusting flight and rescue strategies in real time. The proposed architecture integrates three core components: (i) context-aware reinforcement learning agents that support autonomous decision-making and path optimization, (ii) a risk-aware prioritization model that computes a Rescue Urgency Score (RUS) inspired by data-driven risk prediction techniques, and (iii) a multi-UAV coordination network based on Flying Ad Hoc Networks (FANETs) for distributed sensing and collaborative response. By bridging concepts from intelligent traffic recovery, UAV-enabled public safety operations, and predictive analytics, AeroRescue-AI seeks to provide a coherent blueprint for adaptive, trustworthy, and explainable AI-driven aerial rescue systems capable of operating in complex and uncertain disaster environments.

title: Design of Multi-Output Power Supply System for Distribution
authors: Zhou Geng, Haolin Chen, Yiming Luo, Mingjie Yuan, Ruijuan Zhang, Yachao Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To meet the power supply requirements of multiple specifications of loads in the distribution scenario, this paper designs a multi-output power supply system with three-phase <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AC} 380 ~\mathrm{V} / 50 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> as the main power source and DC24V as the emergency power source. The core of the system realizes two major functions: First, it converts the main power source into 9 AC220V and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \text{DC28V}$</tex> outputs, respectively meeting the voltage fluctuation requirements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AC} 220 ~\mathrm{V} \pm 15 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{DC} 28 ~\mathrm{V} \pm 2 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>. Second, it has the ability to switch between the main and backup power sources without interruption. When the main power source fails, the system automatically switches to the emergency power source to ensure that the load does not lose power, and simultaneously triggers audible and visual alarm signals. It also integrates overcurrent protection to enhance operational safety. Through hardware circuit design and functional verification, the results show that the system can stably adapt to multiple loads in the distribution scenario and outperforms the design indicators in terms of power source switching (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 15 ~\text{ms}$</tex>), protection response (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 8 \mathrm{ms}$</tex>), and voltage accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{AC 220 V} \pm {12\%}, \mathrm{DC 28 V} \pm \mathrm{1.5V}$</tex>).

title: Multi Objective Optimization Scheduling and Operation Control Method for Distributed Energy Storage Power Plants Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Qian Yu, Yong Xu, Hong Chen, Xiaoxiao Liu, Kai Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Distributed energy storage can not only solve the problem of unbalanced power generation and consumption in the electrical power system (EPS), but also provide a means for regulating EPS. However, due to the diversity of energy storage types and technologies, the rational application of distributed energy storage in EPS is an urgent problem to be solved. In response to the randomness of renewable energy source (RES) generation and the time series coupling characteristics of energy storage systems (ESS), this article proposes a multi-objective optimization scheduling and operation control method for distributed energy storage stations (D-ESS) that combines reinforcement learning (RL) and Group Search Optimization (GSO) algorithms. The research will decompose the optimization process into two stages: global and local. The global stage is modeled as a non convex nonlinear optimization problem and solved using GSO, while the local stage is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) solved using deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Combining DRL with GSO improves the training speed and convergence performance of RL, avoiding the difficulty of designing reward functions in complex environments. The results indicate that this method can effectively improve the flexibility of D-ESS scheduling, achieving dual optimization of scheduling economy and environmental friendliness.

title: Research on Nonlinear Control System of High Voltage Electrical Equipment Incorporating Fuzzy Adaptive Algorithm
authors: Hui Yan, Qingshan Wu, Jian Guan, Xin Wang, Wenchao Shang, Xuguo Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: High-voltage electrical equipment exhibits significant nonlinear characteristics and is highly susceptible to disturbances. Therefore, precise control is crucial. This paper proposes a nonlinear control system incorporating a fuzzy adaptive algorithm. Considering factors such as actuator saturation, dead zone, and electromechanical coupling, a nonlinear state-space model of the high-voltage equipment is created, and the input and state constraints are formally defined. Subsequently, a control architecture with outer-loop reference tracking and inner-loop fuzzy adaptive compensation is established. Gaussian membership functions and regular surface structures are designed, and the fuzzy inference output is reformulated into a parametric linear form. Gradient-type adaptive laws and projection operators are provided to achieve online adjustment of the regular weights. The Lyapunov method demonstrates that the closed-loop system is stable under bounded disturbances and parameter uncertainties. Using a high-voltage test platform, comparisons are made with PID and conventional fuzzy control in scenarios involving step following, load abrupt changes, voltage drops, and inertia deviations. Experimental results show that the proposed method can significantly reduce overshoot and steady-state error, with an average RMS error reduction of over $30 \%$, while maintaining control energy within an acceptable range. Further investigation into the sensitivity of the parameters reveals that if the adaptive gain and rule size are appropriate, a good balance can be achieved between performance and computational burden, providing an engineering-feasible technical means for the intelligent control of high-voltage electrical equipment.

title: MARTO: A Modular Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Botnet Detection, Reinforcement-Based Mitigation, Autonomous Rule Generation with Embedded Software Testing
authors: Manimekalai M, R. Lavanya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Botnets present continuously changing challenges to cybersecurity systems by disguising harmful actions within legitimate traffic through entropy-rich behavior and decentralized management.We introduce MARTO—Modular Adaptive Reinforcement-based Threat Orchestrator—an advanced IDS framework that independently identifies botnet activities, assesses threat levels, generates Suricata rules, and verifies behavior through integrated software testing methods.Innovative algorithms such as Adaptive Temporal Entropy Clustering (ATEC), Confidence-Weighted Multi-Agent Reinforcement (CWMAR), and Rule Life Expectancy Tracker (RLET) improve MARTO's flexibility and accuracy. Experimental findings indicate a detection accuracy of 94.6%, a response precision of 83%, and a rule efficacy of 95.2 %. A comparative analysis reveals enhanced automation, robustness, and testing integration.

title: Power Demand Forecasting and Decision Optimization Technology Based on Human-in-the-Loop
authors: Xiaorui Qian, Xiangpeng Zhan, Gaole Cai, Lingling Zhu, Kai Xiao, Yuying Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: A method integrating model prediction, manual correction and rolling prediction, and probabilistic decision optimization is proposed to address key issues in the traditional electricity demand forecasting process, such as the difficulty of considering expert experience and uncertainty in practical scenarios, and the difficulty of directly guiding market purchasing decisions based on prediction results. Firstly, based on historical load data and external influencing factors, a Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to achieve preliminary prediction of electricity demand, and the prediction results are displayed in a visual interface, allowing experts to manually correct unreasonable prediction results based on practical experience; subsequently, the revised data is used as new input, and a rolling prediction mechanism is adopted to dynamically update the electricity demand forecast for the next month. In view of the uncertainty of the prediction results, the probabilistic prediction method is further used to generate interval or probability distribution results, and the optimal market electricity purchase plan is formulated through the stochastic optimization model in combination with expert preferences. Based on historical electricity consumption data from January 1, 2019, to May 2024, the experiment forecasts electricity demand for June 2024. After manual correction, the mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of the rolling forecast is reduced to 0.19%. Combining probabilistic forecasting with optimized decision-making reduces electricity purchase costs by 4.6%. This method effectively improves forecast accuracy and decision rationality, providing intelligent support for power market procurement.

title: EEG and Forehead EOG-Based Driver Fatigue Classification Using Sparse-Deep Belief Networks
authors: Jie Zhang, Nanfeng Xiao
publish: 2021 IEEE 23rd Int Conf on High Performance Computing & Communications; 7th Int Conf on Data Science & Systems; 19th Int Conf on Smart City; 7th Int Conf on Dependability in Sensor, Cloud & Big Data Systems & Application (HPCC/DSS/SmartCity/DependSys)
abstract: In this study, we fuse electroencephalography (EEG) signals and forehead electrooculogram (EOG) signals to classify the driver's fatigue and alert states. Based on time-varying power spectral density (PSD) and Hilbert-Huang spectrum entropy extracted from EEG, 36 eye movement features from EOG, we develop novel sparse-deep belief networks (Sparse-DBN) for detecting driver drowsiness. PSD, four modalities, and support vector machine (SVM) classifier are used for comparison. The experimental results show that EEG and forehead EOG feature fusion can improve performance compared with two single modalities. Furthermore, the results show that the classification performance achieves an improved classification performance using two feature fusions and Sparse-DBN classifier with the sensitivity, specificity, accuracy,and area under the curve of 97.99% /96.72%, 94.73% /95.51%, 97.93% /97.91%, and 0.96273/ 0.96376, respectively. Additionally, EEG and forehead EOG features can be collected simultaneously using only four shared forehead sites and achieve comparative performance compared with traditional sites, which is the preferred approach for practical applications.

title: Research on Traffic Sign Recognition Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zhicheng He
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper systematically studies the traffic sign recognition algorithm based on deep learning. Aiming at the problems of difficult small-object detection, strong environmental interference, and high real-time requirements in complex road environments, an improved YOLOv8-CA model is proposed. This model enhances the focusing ability on key features by introducing the Coordinate Attention mechanism, and combines the multi - scale feature fusion structure with the targeted data augmentation strategy, effectively improving the recognition performance and robustness of the model under complex conditions such as illumination changes, occlusions, and scale variations. Experiments on the TT100K dataset show that YOLOv8-CA improves the mAP by 2.6% compared with the baseline model, and the detection accuracy of small objects is increased by 4.2%. It exhibits better generalization ability under different background complexities and interference conditions, and at the same time maintains a high inference efficiency, making it suitable for real-time detection tasks in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Delta-Attention-Based Deep Learning Model for Diabetes Prediction in Consumer Health Monitoring Systems
authors: Varsha Arya, Akshat Gaurav, Priyanka Chaurasia, Kwok Tai Chui, Brij B. Gupta, Ramakrishnan Raman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Diabetes is one of the most prevalent chronic diseases worldwide, requiring timely diagnosis and continuous monitoring to prevent severe health complications. With the growing integration of artificial intelligence in healthcare, smart and efficient models are essential for real-time analysis on consumer health devices. This study proposes a deep learning framework that integrates categorical embeddings with continuous medical features and introduces a novel Delta-Attention mechanism to dynamically capture variations in feature importance during training. Evaluated on the Kaggle diabetes dataset, the model achieved an accuracy of 91.98%, precision of 94.92%, recall of 91.98%, and F1-score of 92.91%. Through ablation and comparative analyses, the model demonstrated superior inter-pretability and computational efficiency, making it highly suitable for on-device diabetes prediction in consumer health monitoring systems.

title: A Note on Quantifying Mogul Skiers’ Turning Movements Based on Pose Estimation
authors: Aki Shigesawa, Masahiro Yagi, Sho Takahashi, Chinami Fukui
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel method for quantifying the movements of mogul skiers using video analysis. The proposed method extracts time series features that represent the turning movements based on the 2D pose estimation. The differences in movement between players are quantified by calculating the dynamic time warping-based distance between the time series features. Experiments using videos from an actual professional mogul skiing competition demonstrated the effectiveness of the method.

title: Big Data Analysis of Student Mental Health Based on Artificial Intelligence and Feature Extraction
authors: Lingling Ji, Tianran Li, Xiaomeng Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: As student mental health issues become increasingly prominent and traditional screening methods face bottlenecks such as time lag, limited coverage, and missed detection of concealed problems, there is an urgent need to build a dynamic and precise early identification and intervention system. This study proposes an improved AI-driven solution. First, multi-source heterogeneous data are integrated and correlated features are extracted. Second, a Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network (HGNN) model is constructed, dynamically modeling psychological risk propagation paths through a graph attention mechanism. Finally, the SHAP (Shapley Additive exPlanations) interpretability framework is introduced to generate personalized psychological profiles. Experimental results show that the multimodal model integrating text, behavioral, and physiological features achieves an identification accuracy of 89.2%. Furthermore, the graph neural network excels in identifying propagation paths. Interpretability analysis reveals that physiological modal features provide a reliable basis for precise interventions. This research provides technical support for building a precise campus psychological early warning system and advances the early identification and intervention of mental health risks.

title: Quantifying the Economic Value of Electric Vehicles User Experience via Conjoint Analysis
authors: Shahed Obeidat, Abdo Nassar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The mass adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) is primarily constrained by range anxiety, a psychological barrier rooted in human factors rather than purely technological limitations. While existing research focuses on the Willingness to Pay (WTP) for hardware attributes like battery range, this study quantifies the economic value of the vehicle's User Experience (UX) in mitigating this anxiety. Employing a Choice-Based Conjoint (CBC) Analysis and a Hierarchical Bayesian (HB) Logit model on a sample of N=500 potential EV buyers, we treat the anxiety-mitigating UX package as a measurable economic attribute. The results demonstrate that a Predictive & Integrated UX commands a WTP exceeding $50,000, a value significantly higher than the WTP for a 100-mile increase in battery range. This finding establishes superior UX as a core driver of consumer utility and market share. Furthermore, a strategic analysis reveals that this high WTP provides substantial pricing power: while a 10% price reduction can boost market share from 34% to over 67%, the revenue-maximizing price is approximately $69,200. This study offers a clear financial mandate for automotive manufacturers to shift investment toward design and behavioral-economic-informed software, positioning UX as the new, powerful competitive differentiator in the EV market.

title: A Methodology for Femm-Simulink Co-Simulation for Accurate Simulation of High Frequency Magnetics
authors: Kamran Asad, Nachiketa Deshmukh, Mayank Deo, Pramod Chaudhary
abstract: This paper presents a co-simulation methodology that couples the open-source 2-D finite element tool FEMM with MATLAB Simulink for integrated electromagnetic circuit analysis. The framework enables the realization of variable inductance through geometric modifications of the inductor, which cannot be directly modeled in MATLAB alone. By linking field-level solutions with system-level dynamics, the method allows accurate evaluation of inductance, flux density, and core losses under different loading and geometric conditions. Unlike conventional models, this approach dynamically reflects magnetic behavior in circuit performance. The effectiveness of the methodology is demonstrated using a three-phase interleaved buck converter as a case study. Flat and stepped air-gap inductors are modeled in FEMM and coupled with Simulink to evaluate converter efficiency and losses. The co-simulation improves system accuracy, and shows that the stepped air-gap design achieves a 1.56 % efficiency gain at light load. In addition, the essential MATLAB code for establishing the FEMM-Simulink co-simulation is provided in the Appendix section to support practical implementation. This approach provides a flexible platform for studying magnetic components in power electronic systems and can be applied to many other cases for different electromagnetic-circuits.

title: A Dynamic Compensated Gradient-Based Recurrent Neural Network Model for Dynamic Tensor Inversion in Time-Varying Systems
authors: Dongbo Yang, Xiao Ling
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Time-Varying Tensor Inversion (TVTI) serves as a fundamental problem in engineering and applied mathematics. Existing solutions are limited by their static compensation mechanisms, resulting in exponential growth in computational complexity and constrained convergence performance, which makes it difficult to meet the real-time requirements of time-varying systems. To address the aforementioned bottleneck, this study proposes a dynamic compensated gradient-based recurrent neural network model (DCGRNN). Innovatively designed with a dynamic compensation mechanism, DCGRNN achieves adaptive tracking of time-varying tensor characteristics through a speed adjustment factor. This factor incorporates the derivative information of the equation, stabilizing the residual error of the dynamic process at the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-5</sup>, enabling more effective capture of the evolution of dynamic systems while preserving the advantage of eliminating the need for matrix inversion. Furthermore, based on Barbalat's lemma, it is rigorously proven that under time-varying parameter conditions, DCGRNN exhibits global exponential convergence and has an upper bound on finite-time convergence. Finally, to verify the performance of the proposed model, five sets of comparative experiments are designed. The results demonstrate that DCGRNN exhibits the optimal convergence performance in various time-varying system scenarios, with a minimum convergence time of 1.28 seconds. Even under the influence of noise, it maintains a stable residual error at the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>, fully validating its excellent convergence performance and robustness.

title: Discrimination Method of Aging Degree of Oil Paper Insulation Based on Raman Spectroscopy and Machine Learning Interpretable Model
authors: Peihong Huang, Qingzhen Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In view of the destructiveness and limited information sources of traditional transformer oil paper insulation aging detection, this study proposes a nondestructive and interpretable oil paper insulation aging degree method based on Raman spectroscopy combined with advanced machine learning algorithm Firstly, the oil samples at different aging stages were obtained by accelerated thermal aging test and Raman spectrum detection; Then, combined with the improved competitive adaptive reweighted sampling (CARS) and Support vector machine (SVM) algorithm, the discrimination model of oil paper insulation aging degree is established; Finally, the key wavelength variables are resolved by Shapley additive interpretation (SHAP) The experimental results show that the improved CARS-SVM model is superior to the full spectrum-SVM in accuracy, accuracy, recall and F1 value, which can accurately identify the aging degree of oil samples, and the selected characteristic wavelength can effectively characterize the change of chemical bond vibration characteristics during the aging process of insulating oil.

title: Grid Forming Inverters (GFM) for Active Front End (AFE) Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs)
authors: Sudeep Guruprasad
abstract: Active Front-End (AFE) based Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs) enable bidirectional power flow, near-unity power factor, and reduced line current harmonics compared to diode rectifier-fed drives. However, conventional AFE systems employ Grid-Following (GFL) control reliant on PhaseLocked Loop (PLL) synchronization and current regulation, resulting in degraded performance in weak or distorted grids, including synchronization instability and poor voltage regulation. This paper proposes the integration of Grid-Forming (GFM) control into AFE VFD architectures, allowing the converter to operate as a controlled voltage source independent of PLL synchronization. By incorporating droop control and virtual impedance, the proposed GFM-AFE enhances transient stability, voltage regulation, and fault ride-through capability.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Using Quantum Convolution Neural Networks
authors: Karvi Pranay Kumar, Aryan Mahawar, Kunal Singh, Tarryn Reiner D Silva, Tapan Batla, Natarajan B
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Brain Tumor detection knows as tedious job poses huge intricacies. Though MRI(Magnetic Resonance Imaging) has made the life of clinicians easier by providing detailed cross-sections of the brain, thus making it easy to detect abnormalities, it is still very difficult to accurately categorize the different types of Brain Tumors. For addressing this problem, this research work proposes a novel approach to use a Hybrid Quantum Classical Convolutional Neural Network (HQC-CNN) model. The proposed model has been experimented on the BraTS2023 dataset, with a result of 98.7% accuracy, which is an improvement of 5.2% over traditional CNNs (ResNet, VGG). The HQC-CNN model simplifies parameter complexity by 40% and training time by 2.5 times via quantum parallelism. The complete flow is described in the architecture, hybrid training regimes, and issues in deploying QCNNs on near-term quantum hardware in this work. These findings strongly indicates the power of Deep Learning approaches and incorporation of Quantum ideas in medical imaging.

title: Research on Photovoltaic Power Prediction Based on Multi-Scale Feature Fusion and Cycle-Enhanced Transformer
authors: Chao Liu, Peiyan Mao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: To address the issues of insufficient multi-scale representation of features, inadequate physical periodicity, and meteorological noise interference in photovoltaic power prediction, the MT-Transformer model is proposed. Multi-scale convolution (MSC) is introduced to capture instantaneous fluctuations and evolutionary trends, periodic enhancement coding (CPE) is used to inject daily and seasonal physical priors, and dynamic gating of meteorological variables is achieved through a feature selection layer (FSL). Experiments show that the MT-Transformer performs excellently on a dataset of 12 solar stations, with an RMSE reduction of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 {\%}}$</tex> compared to the standard Transformer.

title: Implementation and Evaluation of a Zero-Trust-Based IoT security framework for Autonomous Mobile Robots
authors: Hibiki Oizumi, Nobuhiro Kobayshi, Ryozo Kiyohara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In the era of Society 5.0, where cyberspace and physical space are increasingly integrated, securing IoT actuators in mobile robots and autonomous vehicles has become critical. As malware becomes stealthier and AI-driven, conventional detection methods are often inadequate. This study proposes a Zero Trust IoT Security Framework (ZeTiots FW) for mobile robot platforms, focusing on TurtleBot3 as a representative case. The framework integrates zero trust principles with physical-layer safety using infrared sensors to proactively prevent real-world harm.

title: DCS-Oriented Optimal Control for Frequency Regulation of a Steam-Molten-Salt Integrated Unit
authors: JinYong Wang, Lei Wang, Shihai Liu, He Lin
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Fast frequency regulation is becoming increasingly challenging for steam-cycle units as system inertia decreases. At the same time, steam-header safety margins and actuator wear limit aggressive governor actions. This paper proposes a deployable, distributed control system (DCS)-oriented optimization strategy for an integrated steam and molten-salt unit. The strategy coordinates turbine generation with an electrically heated molten-salt thermal energy storage (TES) path to shape the net regulation power. A control-oriented dynamic model is used in a supervisory model predictive control (MPC) layer, incorporating hard constraints on steam pressure, steam temperature, and molten-salt thermal limits. The MPC also includes ramp-rate limits, actuation smoothing, and robustness-oriented constraint tightening, supported by an extended Kalman filter and bounded online parameter adaptation. Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) DCS tests under step, ramp, and AGC-like scenarios show that compared to droop control, PID control, and a steam-only MPC baseline, the proposed method improves frequency quality and reduces steam-side actuation stress. It achieves up to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 4 \%}$</tex> reduction in integrated frequency error while maintaining strict thermal safety.

title: Emerging Trends in Malware Detection: The Role of Generative AI in Cyber Defense
authors: B. Gayathri, K. Sundarakantham, M. Arul Tharani, M. Priyadharshan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Malware detection plays a significant role in the realm of cybersecurity, yet traditional methods frequently struggle to identify novel and polymorphic threats. It investigates the growing role of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), including Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), and Transformer-based models, in enhancing malware detection. We systematically reviewed and analyzed over 25 peer-reviewed studies and technical reports published between 2017 and 2024, including recent surveys and case studies on GenAI-based cybersecurity solutions. Four benchmark datasets-Malimg, UNSW-NB15, CIC-IoT23, and EMBER-were evaluated across the literature for their suitability in GenAI-based malware detection. The techniques are compared based on performance metrics such as classification accuracy, anomaly detection precision, F1-score, adversarial robustness, and computational efficiency. The paper also identifies key limitations, including mode collapse, low interpretability, and vulnerability to evasion and poisoning attacks. Finally, we identify various possibilities for future research, including real-time AI defenses, hybrid GenAI-discriminative approaches, and explainable AI integration. This study serves as a foundational reference for advancing GenAI-powered malware detection frameworks in cybersecurity. To provide a more reliable and precise malware detection pipeline, the suggested hybrid framework combines Transformer-based sequence modeling, VAE-driven latent feature encoding, and GAN-based data augmentation. Furthermore, the proposed hybrid model combining GAN-based augmentation, VAE-driven latent representation learning, and Transformerbased sequence modeling demonstrates improved robustness and detection accuracy, offering a unified approach to address malware diversity, imbalance, and evolving threat behaviors. The proposed hybrid model delivers the highest accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(96.4 \%)$</tex>.

title: A Novel Approach for Dc Balancing in Three-Level Neutral Point Clamped Inverter Based Statcom
authors: Ajindu N S, Manju A S, Aby Joseph
abstract: Achieving stable DC bus voltage across the splitcapacitor bank presents a significant technical hurdle in threelevel Neutral Point Clamped (NPC) inverters, particularly within grid-connected applications link Static Synchronous Compensators (STATCOMs), where rapid dynamic response and high power quality is considered essential. The unbalance in the split DC-link capacitors often tends to result in neutral point voltage drift, delivering distorted output voltages, increased stress on devices, instability in system operation, voltage shifting onto one capacitor and even affects STATCOM functionalities resulting in degraded reactive power control. This paper presents a novel approach for mitigating DC bus voltage unbalance in a threelevel Neutral point clamped inverter based STATCOM. Unlike traditional methods which solely rely on passive balancing, this method efficiently balances the DC link voltages by modifying the reference in response to the unbalance occurred. A simple but efficient algorithm has been realised to analyze the unbalance and suitably alter the reference so as to balance the voltage in the series connected DC link Capacitors. The method has been implemented and has been validated in a 50kVAR STATCOM system. Results demonstrate better regulation in voltage on implementating the algorithm

title: Versatile LLC Converter Based Multi-Input Multi-Output Bidirectional Onboard Charger for EV Battery and APM
authors: Tarun Panwar, Ashutosh Rai, Venkata Ratnam Vakacharla
abstract: A compact multi-input multi-output bidirectional onboard charger topology with G2V (grid-to-vehicle), V2G (vehicle-to-grid) and H2L (High voltage battery-to- low voltage battery) functionality is proposed in this paper. A dual input single LLC resonant tank interfaces the utility grid and a DC source using two half bridges connecting to the two terminals of resonant tank between the poles. A multi-winding transformer interfaces the high-voltage propulsion battery through a full bridge and the low-voltage auxiliary battery (APM) through a semi-active bridge. Variable frequency control (VFC) along with phase shift control (PSC) is used for achieving independent control of HV and LV ports. A secondary LLC resonant tank is formed at the output side which is utilized for HV to LV battery charging. V2G operation is possible in this topology, enabling power transfer from HV battery to grid. MATLAB simulations have been carried out to validate the theoretical analysis for the proposed system, showing effective voltage control between 200-350 V for the 2 kW HV battery port and 38-60 V for the 0.5 kW LV battery port.

title: Linear decomposed Image processing in FPGA
authors: Plasin Francis Dias, R. M. Banakar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents hardware implementation of synthetic aperture radar image processing algorithm for polarimetry Linear decomposition method. The proposed image processing technique in this paper uses the analysis of decomposition method to determine the scattering feature of the object detection. An FPGA implementation for the Linear decomposition technique used with image processing to make the system reliable to features the object parameters of detection. The hardware level program was developed for Verilog system modeling implemented using Vertex 5 FPGA from Xilinx employing ISE software. The hardware implementation of the Linear decomposition and software implementation of the techniques are analyzed for qualitative analysis. The proposed approach of hardware solution accuracy is corelated when tested with software implementation using system model. The FPGA model architecture evaluation depicts the greater precision of SAR target evaluation when compared with Python system model.

title: QuickCut: A Speech and Gesture Driven Framework for Automated Video Editing
authors: Christina Pappachan, Ashan Perera, Velibor Adzic, Hari Kalva
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The growing popularity of social media and short-form video platforms has increased the demand for simple, efficient editing tools. Traditional editors like Adobe Premiere Pro require technical expertise and remain tedious for casual creators. This paper presents QuickCut, a multimodal, speech- and gesture-driven automatic video editing framework that streamlines post-production through AI-based automation. Built on an open, modular Flask pipeline, QuickCut integrates Faster-Whisper, WhisperX, MediaPipe, YOLOv8-Face, OpenCV, and FFmpeg to interpret verbal and visual cues directly from recorded footage. The system performs operations such as cutting, zooming, captioning, face blurring, and visual enhancement without manual intervention. Experimental evaluation shows that QuickCut delivers comparable output quality while reducing editing time and cost by up to 97%, demonstrating its potential to make video editing faster, more accessible, and cost-efficient for creators at all skill levels.

title: A Method and System for Generating Safety Measure Tickets Based on Relay Protection Secondary Drawings
authors: Susu Pan, Qiongliang Chen, Mingfeng Chen, Junqiang Li, Gang Chen, Bingwei Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of manual dependence, low efficiency, and high error rate in safety measure ticket (SMT) preparation during relay protection maintenance in power systems, this paper proposes a method and system for generating SMTs based on relay protection secondary drawings. First, the method collects original secondary drawings in multi-source formats (paper scanning, on-site photography, electronic documents), and obtains high-quality target drawings through preprocessing steps including illumination correction, denoising, binarization, and normalization. Then, it extracts drawing feature tensors based on ResNet-50, and uses a modified YOLOv8 model, a hybrid OCR model (ResNet-34 + bidirectional LSTM + Transformer), and a graph neural network (GAT) to realize secondary component localization, circuit text recognition, and connection relationship modeling, generating structured analysis results of the drawings. Finally, it infers and generates compliant SMTs by combining a pre-constructed relay protection safety measure rule base, and realizes iterative optimization of analysis results through verification of actual execution outcomes. Experiments show that the proposed method increases the terminal block detection accuracy by 7.7% compared with the traditional YOLOv8, achieves a terminal number recognition accuracy of 98.5%, and a circuit connection inference accuracy of 96.7%, which significantly improves the standardization and intelligence level of relay protection maintenance operations.

title: Research on Real-Time Straw Density and Morphology Detection and Adaptive Baling Control Algorithm Based on Machine Vision
authors: Cao Lizhu, Li Pengfei
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the low precision and inefficiency of fixed parameter control in traditional straw balers, this study integrates machine vision and adaptive control technologies to construct an intelligent straw baling system. First, a 12-megapixel machine vision detection platform was built. An adaptive Gaussian filter and improved Otsu segmentation algorithm were proposed, combined with a BP neural network to establish a fusion straw density and morphology detection model with a detection error of <±2%. An adaptive baling control algorithm based on ARIMA prediction and fuzzy PID was designed. Matlab/Simulink simulations verified that the convergence time for parameter step changes was <0.3s and the overshoot was <5%. After completing the baler's structural design, optimization was performed using SolidWorks modeling and ANSYS simulation. Static simulations revealed that the maximum stresses of the crushing blade, extrusion plate, and lead screw were 180 MPa, 125 MPa, and 120 MPa, respectively, with safety factors greater than 1.8. Dynamic simulations verified that the first-order natural frequency of the entire machine was 15 Hz, avoiding the 5-10 Hz operating frequency range. Experiments after system integration demonstrated that machine vision detection of wheat/corn straw density had a relative error of 1.2%-1.8%, surpassing the 3%-5% operating frequency of traditional pressure sensors. The adaptive algorithm controlled compaction with an error of 2.1%-4.8%, achieving a baling efficiency of 14.5-15.2 bales/minute. This represents a 30%-40% improvement in accuracy and over 15% in efficiency compared to traditional fixed-parameter control, meeting the needs of large-scale straw processing.

title: ICCE 2026 List Reviewer Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)

title: State Estimation based Indoor Localization System for Complex Indoor Environments Using UWB
authors: Namina Wijetunga, Kavindu Shehan, Dinil Naotunna, Kasun Hemachandra, Subodha Charles
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Indoor localization is a critical technology that enables applications in complex and dynamic environments, such as supermarkets, factories, and smart infrastructures. Ultra-Wideband (UWB) systems, known for their high accuracy and robustness, face challenges in Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) conditions and dynamic obstacles inside complex indoor environments. This work presents a comprehensive solution for indoor localization in complex indoor environments by integrating an advanced positioning algorithm and a robust multi-device handling protocol that can localize a user inside a complex indoor environment with 40cm accuracy while seamlessly handling multiple UWB devices. Real-world tests conducted inside a complex indoor environment demonstrate that the proposed system achieves the aforementioned accuracy, and by integrating a path planning algorithm that incorporates static obstacles and multiple goals, this system further shows potential for tasks like smart retail navigation and infrastructure management. The proposed system excels in mitigating NLOS errors, adapting to dynamic environments, and enabling precise navigation. This integrated approach advances UWB-based indoor localization, combining multi-device coordination, robust positioning, and efficient routing to deliver a highly accurate and scalable solution for autonomous navigation, smart infrastructure, and optimized logistics in complex indoor environments.

title: QW-HHL: A Quantum Computer Amenable General Matrix Equation Solver
authors: Christopher D. Phillips, Helia Akbari, Vladimir I. Okhmatovski
publish: IEEE Journal on Multiscale and Multiphysics Computational Techniques
abstract: Quantum computation offers a promising alternative to classical computing methods in many areas of numerical science, with algorithms that make use of the unique ways in which quantum computers store and manipulate data often achieving dramatic improvements in performance over their classical counterparts. The potential efficiency of quantum computers is particularly important for numerical simulations, where the capabilities of classical computing systems are often insufficient for the analysis of real-world problems. In this work, we study problems involving the solution of matrix equations, for which there is limited research on efficient, general, and fully implemented quantum procedures. We develop a generalization of the Harrow/Hassidim/Lloyd algorithm by providing an alternative unitary for eigenphase estimation. This unitary, which we have adopted from research in the area of quantum walks, has the advantage of being well defined for any arbitrary matrix equation, thereby allowing the solution procedure to be directly implemented on quantum hardware for any well-conditioned system. The procedure is most useful for sparse matrix equations, as it allows for the inverse of a matrix to be applied with O(sN log(N)) complexity, where N is the number of unknowns, and s is an upper bound on the number of nonzero elements in any row of the system matrix. This efficiency is competitive with the most sophisticated available classical solvers, and is independent of the structure of the input matrix. Hence, this procedure represents an important step towards the achievement of quantum advantage over classical algorithms for practical problems in numerical science.

title: A Novel Ultrahigh-Resolution and Wide-Swath Sliding Spotlight SAR Imaging approach Based on Poisson Disk Sampling Strategy
authors: Xinchang Hu, Di Yin, Fei Teng, Lihua Zhong, Jitong Duan, Lijia Huang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Since multichannel sliding spotlight synthetic aperture radar (SAR) can simultaneously achieve high-resolution and wide-swath (HRWS) remote sensing observations, the large volume of data collected from multiple channels increases the burden on the data link, while the estimation and compensation of channel errors further complicate the signal processing. To reduce hardware costs and algorithm complexity while ensuring HRWS capabilities, this letter proposes a novel single-channel ultrahigh-resolution and wide-swath sliding spotlight SAR imaging approach based on a Poisson disk sampling strategy. First, the proposed method adopts a Poisson disk sampling strategy to collect nonuniform azimuth sampling echo data, which has well anti-aliasing effects for the sparsity-driven reconstruction. Second, a modified Chirp Scaling algorithm, which integrates a fourth-order range model (FORM4) to precisely account for the spaceborne SAR curved orbit, is presented to achieve full-aperture focusing of ultrahigh resolution sliding spotlight SAR data across the entire scene. Then, the complete imaging reconstruction process is given, which mainly involves the operation of Deramp preprocessing based on nonuniform sampling sliding spotlight SAR data, as well as threshold iterative processing. Simulation results powerfully demonstrate the capability of the proposed MCSA to achieve a 0.3m resolution over a 4km×4km swath in non-uniformly sampled sliding spotlight SAR, with a 20% reduction in data acquisitiion compared to traditional approaches.

title: A Polyaniline-SDS Based Flexible Sensor for Glyphosate Detection in Aqueous media
authors: Lazar Milić, Mitar Simić, Radmila Tomovska, Jadranka Blaževska-Gilev, Goran M. Stojanović
abstract: Glyphosate is one of the most widely used herbicides worldwide, and its extensive application has raised growing concerns regarding environmental persistence. Conventional analytical techniques for glyphosate detection, such as chromatographic and spectrometric methods, offer high accuracy but require complex instrumentation, extensive sample preparation, and skilled personnel, limiting their suitability for rapid and on-site monitoring. In this work, a flexible electrochemical sensor, based on a screen-printed electrode functionalized with a polyaniline/sodium dodecyl sulfate (PANI/SDS) composite is presented for glyphosate detection in aqueous media. Electrochemical characterization was performed using open-circuit potential, electrochemical impedance spectroscopy, and differential pulse voltammetry. The sensor exhibited a linear response to glyphosate concentrations in the range of 0.0013–0.02%wt, with a sensitivity of 134.0 μA/%wt. The detection mechanism was supported by mathematical modeling based on the Randles–Ševčík relation, confirming diffusion-controlled charge-transfer kinetics. Analytical figures of merit include a limit of detection of 6.7 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> %wt and a limit of quantification of 2.25⋅ 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> %wt. Selectivity was demonstrated against potassium. Owing to its simple fabrication, low-cost materials, and flexible format, the proposed PANI/SDS-based sensor represents a promising platform for scalable and field-deployable glyphosate monitoring in environmental applications.

title: Electromagnetic Scattering of Uniformly Moving PEC Objects-Part I: Integral Equation Formulations
authors: Guomin Liu, Gaobiao Xiao, Mengxia Hu, Junwei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel method is proposed for analyzing the electromagnetic scattering by an arbitrarily shaped perfectly electrically conducting (PEC) object in uniform motion in the vacuum. In Part I, the jump boundary conditions and the current continuity relation are simplified for PEC objects. A set of time-domain electromagnetic field integral equations on the surface of the moving PEC objects are formulated based on the jump boundary conditions. The relativity effect is properly addressed with the kinematic theorems. The hyper singularities associated with the Green’s function are analyzed and properly treated. The electromagnetic scattered fields generated by the induced current are determined according to the theory of special relativity (TSR) and the causality principle. All calculations are performed in the laboratory frame without explicitly applying Lorentz transformations. Consequently, it is also valid for analyzing systems including multiple objects uniformly moving with different velocities. The method is numerically verified using the marching-on in time algorithm that will be developed in Part II. It will be demonstrated that, for a PEC sphere with velocity near to the light velocity in the vacuum, the scattered fields calculated with the proposed method agree very well with the Lorentz-Mie analytical results obtained using the frame hopping technique.

title: DDoS Attack Detection using Machine Learning Trained Models
authors: Khondaker Refai Arafat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks can disrupt services by flooding networks with malicious traffic, making systems slow or unavailable. Traditional rule-based detection often fails to capture evolving attack patterns, so data-driven approaches are needed. This paper evaluates four supervised Machine Learning (ML) classifiers—Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), Logistic Regression (LoR) and Decision Tree (DT)—for binary DDoS detection using two realistic benchmark datasets (CICIDS-2017 and CICDDoS-2019). Using a unified pipeline (preprocessing, normalization and Extra Trees–based feature selection of the top 15 features), this work compares models with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The case study shows that RF achieves the best overall performance with 99.97% accuracy (F1=0.9997) on CICIDS-2017 and 99.91% accuracy (F1=0.9994) on CICDDoS-2019, while SVM, LoR, and DT also exceed 99% on most metrics. These results indicate that well-tuned classical ML models can deliver highly reliable DDoS detection on realistic traffic data and provide a strong baseline for practical deployments.

title: Mitigating Renewable Fluctuations via Price-Driven EV Coordination: A Microgrid Case Study
authors: Haodong Pi, Xinrui Du, Lingling Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This study proposes a price-responsive EV scheduling framework for microgrids to mitigate challenges from high renewable energy penetration. The model coordinates EV charging based on dynamic pricing and real-time PV generation to enhance local consumption and grid stability. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through comparative case studies, which demonstrate increased PV utilization and reduced load fluctuations compared to uncoordinated charging. Sensitivity analysis further confirms the robustness of the scheduling outcomes to variations in price elasticity coefficients, underscoring the model's practical applicability.

title: Road Sign Classification with Denoising Pipeline Approach
authors: Yu Letian, Yuqi Sun, Jiaqi Zhang, Huayu Liu, Zhuoyang Hu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: Reliable traffic sign recognition is critical for autonomous vehicles. However, real-world images are affected by noise, which degrades the' quality. This research proposes a denoiser-classifier pipeline approach, which uses a pre-trained color image denoiser, and compares it against an end-to-end approach (single classification models). This paper evaluates the pipeline approach and the singular model's image recognition performance on the German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark (GTSRB) dataset under Gaussian noise with standard deviation in pixel value units from 0 to 100. Tests on CNN and ResNet-18 show that the pipeline improves CNN models' performance under moderate noise but offers limited to no gains for ResNet-18, which generally performs better end-to-end. This study provides insights into the importance of model choice for noise-resilient recognition systems.

title: D-FINE with Sparse Attention and Grid Mask for Power Equipment Detection
authors: Shaohui Xu, Lifei Huang, Weilin Liu, Chen Xin, Weiyi Cheng, Yiqing He
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Real-time detection of power equipment is crucial for ensuring the reliability and safety of smart grids, yet it faces unique challenges due to the small size, dense distribution, and complex backgrounds of power components. Therefore, existing detectors often suffer from: a) inadequate discriminative capability for dense and small-size power equipment, which often leads to false positives; b) limited robustness to background interference in grid environment. To address these issues, we propose a novel D-FINE architecture with a Sparse Attention and Grid Mask data augmentation (SGD-FINE).
