The sparse attention of encoder reduces computational complexity by dynamically focusing on detail features of equipment, while the grid mask suppresses background noise and improves model robustness. Experiments demonstrate that SGD-FINE-L achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$74.7 \% \text{mAP}^{5095}$</tex>, outperforming D-FINE, RT-DETR and other mainstream detectors in accuracy. Source code, dataset and pretrained models are available at https://github.com/he13689/SGD-FINE.

title: Enhancing Hybrid Detection in Network Security: An Advanced IDS/IPS Framework Using Signature-Based Detection,Machine Learning and VirusTotal
authors: Vrutti H Tandel, Mukesh Patidar, Yarramalla Ramakrishna, Basikonda Jahnavi, Kota Dhanvanth Kumar, Kolli Venkata Jahnavi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: With the continuous growth of cyber threats, traditional security mechanisms no longer support. They fail to safeguard modern networks. Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems (IDPS) have developed into developed security solutions that are capable of detecting, analyzing, and mitigating sophisticated attacks in real time. Advanced Intrusion Detection System and Intrusion Prevention System leverage machine learning, anomaly detection, signature-based methods, and deep packet inspection to identify both known and unknown threats. Unlike traditional IDS, which only monitors and reports suspicious activity, advanced IPS actively blocks malicious traffic before it reaches critical assets. This project focuses on the design and implementation of an advanced IDS/IPS framework that combines signature-based and behaviour-based techniques for enhanced accuracy and reduced false positives. The proposed system improves network visibility, threat intelligence integration, and proactive defence mechanisms against emerging cyberattacks, making it suitable for enterprise and cloud-based environments.

title: Multi-Horizon Direction of Arrival Forecasting with Temporal Fusion Transformers: A Time-Series Learning Approach to Target Tracking
authors: Constantinos M. Mylonakis, Nikolaos V. Kantartzis, Sotirios K. Goudos, Pavlos I. Lazaridis, Panagiotis Sarigiannidis, Marco Di Renzo, Zaharias D. Zaharis
abstract: This paper explores the application of Temporal Fusion Transformers (TFTs) to target recognition and multi-horizon tracking, with a particular emphasis on enhancing Direction of Arrival (DoA) estimation in complex signal environments. By leveraging the advanced temporal modeling capabilities and dynamic feature selection mechanisms inherent to the TFT architecture, we adapt and optimize the model for improved performance in antenna signal processing, leading to a more accurate and robust estimation framework. Our analysis underscores the architectural strengths of TFTs, especially their capacity to model long-range temporal dependencies and to dynamically prioritize sensor-derived features—capabilities that are essential for precise target tracking and recognition. We train and evaluate the model under diverse conditions, including multi-horizon forecasting and varying prediction latencies across models. The proposed method is benchmarked against state-of-the-art algorithms, including recurrent neural networks and standard transformer-based models. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the TFT-based approach consistently outperforms existing techniques in forecasting accuracy, multi-target recognition, and computational complexity, highlighting its potential to advance the field of DoA estimation.

title: Enhanced Modulation Approach for Improving the Performance of Dual-Input Split-Source Inverters in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Mustafa Abu-Zaher, Ke Shen, Mokhtar Aly, Fang Zhuo, Alaaeldien Hassan, Mohamed Orabi, Jiachen Tian, Samir Kouro, José Rodriguez, Mahmoud A. Gaafar
abstract: Compact and efficient inverter architectures are rapidly becoming fundamental methods for integrating photovoltaic and hybrid DC energy sources into modern electrical grids. Among the emerging candidates, the dual-input split-source inverter (DSSI) stands out as a promising topology, capable of combining two independent input sources within a split-source inverter configuration. This design ensures a continuous input current, reduces the voltage stress across the semiconductor devices, and effectively regulates the DC-link voltage. To realize the full capability of the DSSI, this study introduces a dual-type modulation strategy: the conventional Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) technique and the Simple-Boost Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SB-SPWM) method. Instead of depending on additional hardware components, the proposed modulation strategy extends the achievable voltage gain and improves output voltage regulation purely through modulation. By carefully adjusting the modulation parameters, the system attains a higher level of flexibility, stability, and efficiency for various inputs. The Experimental results confirm the advantages of the proposed method, showing that the optimized SB-SPWM achieves a superior boosting capability gain, higher efficiency, and more consistent performance compared with the traditional SPWM under different source scenarios. These experiments support the applicability of the DSSI as a single-stage power conversion solution capable of meeting the demanding performance, reliability, and efficiency requirements of next-generation renewable and hybrid energy systems.

title: FCMD: Fine-Grained Text-Driven Cohesive Motion Generation With Diffusion Model_supp1-3677594.mp4
authors: wenfeng song
abstract: Generating continuous and expressive human motion from textual descriptions is a critical challenge in applications such as gaming and filmmaking. Existing methods often struggle to maintain global coherence, realistic frame continuity, and smooth transitions. To address these limitations, we propose FCMD, a novel diffusion-based model for generating cohesive motion sequences from fine-grained textual descriptions. FCMD introduces three key innovations: (1) Fine-grained Text Fusion, which integrates detailed textual cues with transitional narratives to enhance semantic consistency; (2) History Motion Guidance, ensuring motion accuracy and consistency across consecutive frames; and (3) Smooth Stitching Sampling, which leverages preceding and current motion information to achieve seamless transitions. Additionally, FCMD employs a large language model (LLM) to refine motion datasets by extracting fine-grained textual descriptions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FCMD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generating coherent, natural, and highly controllable motion sequences.

title: FWFR-SLAM: A Robust Monocular SLAM System for Flapping-Wing Flying Robots_supp1-3676200.mp4
authors: Qiang Fu
abstract: Simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) technology enables real-time and accurate positioning of flapping-wing flying robots (FWFRs) through sensor data such as vision, which is one of the key technologies for autonomous navigation of FWFRs. However, its robustness and accuracy in complex environments are still insufficient. In this paper, we propose a robust monocular visual SLAM system called FWFR-SLAM for FWFRs in complex environments. First, in the front-end feature matching, the two-stage feature matching method of global search and local matching in S-HFNET algorithm is used to improve the matching accuracy. Then, the uncertainty modeling of initialization map points is introduced in the monocular initialization process, and the reliable initialization map points are selected according to the evaluation results to reduce the front-end mismatches and redundant calculation. Furthermore, a keyframe selection strategy combining image information entropy and motion instability energy is proposed, which only retains representative frames. Finally, we conduct experimental verification through open source datasets and self-collected datasets. Quantitative results show that compared with ORB-SLAM3-S (ORB-SLAM3 after image stabilization), FWFR-SLAM improves positioning accuracy by more than 41% in challenging environments.

title: A Branched Fusion Network for the Recognition of Continuous Multi-Terrain Lower-Limb Motion
authors: Xiaohui Li, Xueyan Lyu, Hongfei Zou, Hao Zhou, Dezhi Yu, Wenzhuo Wang, Jiaqi Li, Guanglin Li, Lin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The real-time and accurate recognition of motion intention remains a significant challenge and serves as a fundamental prerequisite for achieving coordinated movement between lower-limb rehabilitation devices and the human body. Moreover, the scarcity of multi-terrain lower-limb motion datasets critically constrains the performance and generalization capability of recognition models. In this study, the Branched Fusion Network (BFNet) is proposed to achieve low-delay and high-accuracy recognition of continuous multi-terrain lower-limb motions. The BFNet, which fuses the sEMG Block and IMU Block modules, takes as input the complete accelerometer and gyroscope data from the previous gait cycle, alongside the onset-window surface electromyography (sEMG) data from the current gait phase. A continuous multi-terrain lower-limb motion dataset (CMLMD), comprising three scenarios (level ground, stairs, and ramps), five basic motion modes, and eight transitional modes, has been publicly released (DOI: 10.21227/cwgk-4a45). The dataset includes 35 participants and a total of 7000 gait cycles. An overall recognition accuracy of 95.41% was achieved on the publicly available CMLMD, with a corresponding time delay of only 114ms. The accuracy reached 88.61% in the cross-subject evaluation. The optimal sensor configuration was determined to consist of a single IMU sensor and six sEMG sensors. Under this configuration, the recognition accuracy remained at 94.38%. The superiority of BFNet in achieving both high recognition accuracy and low delay is validated through comprehensive comparisons with existing methods. The proposed BFNet provides a promising solution for human-machine collaborative movement of the lower limbs. The CMLMD is intended to serve as a foundational benchmark for developing and validating algorithms for continuous lower-limb motion recognition.

title: Machine Learning-Aided Latency Prediction in Packet-Switched Xhaul Networks
authors: Mirosław Klinkowski, Jordi Perelló, Davide Careglio
abstract: In this work, we address the challenge of accurately predicting latency in packet-switched Xhaul networks, enabling the convergent transport of fronthaul (FH) and midhaul (MH) traffic within radio access networks (RANs). Although deterministic worst-case (WC) models provide strict latency bounds, they tend to significantly overestimate actual flow latencies, leading to inefficient resource allocation. To address this limitation, we propose a machine learning-based (ML) latency prediction framework that leverages quantile regression (QR) to provide more accurate estimates of maximum one-way transmission latency for FH and MH flows—an essential requirement for reliable RAN operation. Our approach enhances WC estimations by incorporating additional latency-related features, and is validated using an extensive data set generated through the simulation of diverse ring and mesh topologies. We integrate the QR-based latency predictions into a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model for optimal flow routing and distributed unit (DU) placement. A comparative analysis reveals that QR-based latency prediction outperforms WC latency estimations, significantly improving network performance by reducing the number of active DU processing nodes by up to 20% without compromising latency constraints. The results highlight the potential of ML techniques to enhance the accuracy of latency modeling in dynamic, latency-sensitive Xhaul scenarios, thereby contributing to the realization of RAN Digital Twin systems envisioned for future 6G networks.

title: Clutter-Aware Integrated Sensing and Communication: Models, Methods, and Future Directions
authors: Rang Liu, Peishi Li, Ming Li, A. Lee Swindlehurst
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) can substantially improve spectral, hardware, and energy efficiency by unifying radar sensing and data communications. In wideband and scattering-rich environments, clutter often dominates weak target reflections and becomes a fundamental bottleneck for reliable sensing. Practical ISAC clutter includes “cold” clutter arising from environmental backscatter of the probing waveform and “hot” clutter induced by external interference and reflections from the environment whose statistics can vary rapidly over time. In this article, we develop a unified wideband multiple-input multiple-output orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (MIMO-OFDM) signal model that captures both clutter types across the space, time, and frequency domains. Building on this model, we review clutter characterization at multiple levels, including amplitude statistics, robust spherically invariant random vector (SIRV) modeling, and structured covariance representations suitable for limited-snapshot regimes. We then summarize receiver-side suppression methods in the temporal and spatial domains, together with extensions to space–time adaptive processing (STAP) and space–frequency–time adaptive processing (SFTAP), and we provide guidance on selecting techniques under different waveform and interference conditions. To move beyond reactive suppression, we discuss clutter-aware transceiver co-design that couples beamforming and waveform optimization with practical communication quality-of-service (QoS) constraints to enable proactive clutter avoidance. We conclude with open challenges and research directions toward environment-adaptive and clutter-resilient ISAC for the next-generation networks.

title: A Novel Magnetic Integrated Three-Phase EMI Filter for Differential-Mode and Common-Mode Interference Suppression in PMSM Drive Systems
authors: Haiyang Gao, Yongxiang Xu, Shaobin Li, Pan Wang, Jibin Zou
abstract: The application of three-phase electromagnetic interference (EMI) filters in permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive systems can reduce common-mode (CM) interference while filtering out differential-mode (DM) interference in phase currents. Conventional filters will increase the volume and weight of the system. This article proposes a fully magnetic integrated three-phase EMI filter, with a magnetic core consisting of a toroid and a Y-branch limb. The toroid is primarily employed to concentrate the CM flux while providing the path for the DM flux. The Y-branch limb is used to carry the DM flux. The distribution of the flux in the proposed filter under different winding distribution patterns is analyzed. The DM and CM inductances are derived. The optimal winding distribution pattern is determined through quantitative and qualitative comparison. The magnetic saturation analysis is provided by deriving the maximum magnetic flux density of the proposed filter. The magnetic flux density of the proposed filter is verified through ANSYS simulation. The experiment is conducted to evaluate the performance of the proposed filter. The results show that the proposed filter can significantly reduce the volume and weight of the system compared to the conventional filter under the same DM and CM suppression performance. In addition, a DC side EMI filter is manufactured to assess its impact on the electrical performance of the motor side. The results indicate that the DC side EMI filter cannot replace the proposed filter. The proposed filter can enhance the performance and reliability of the PMSM drive system.

title: A Multi-modal Emotion Recognition Algorithm Based on Multi-scale Temporal Attention
authors: Zichen Liu, Jia Wei, Xinyue Ma, Zhuojiang Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a multimodal emotion recognition algorithm based on multi-scale temporal attention, aiming to construct a collaborative fusion recognition framework by deeply integrating electroencephalogram (EEG), electrooculogram (EOG), and electrocardiogram (ECG) signals. Addressing the shortcomings in cross-modal semantic alignment, spatio-temporal feature extraction, and dynamic interaction modeling, this study designs an EEG emotion recognition module based on spatial-temporal attention mechanism, an EOG signal processing module based on channel attention and Transformer, and an ECG signal modeling module based on periodic feature perception. Ultimately, a dual-level fusion mechanism at the decision and feature levels is adopted to achieve effective complementarity and collaborative representation of multi-source heterogeneous information. The model first proposes a spatial-temporal feature extraction architecture that integrates graph attention network (GAT) and convolution operations, utilizing electrode topological relationships and local-global feature interactions to extract discriminative sparse spatio-temporal representations from EEG signals. Secondly, it introduces channel attention mechanisms and multi-head self-attention Transformer to enhance key channels and model long-range dependencies in EOG signals, effectively capturing subtle emotional patterns in eye movements. Additionally, the Transformer structure is employed to explicitly model periodic patterns in ECG signals, capturing rhythm changes related to emotional states. In terms of fusion strategies, this paper constructs a multi-modal synergy network (SynergyNet-MER), which uniformly represents and fuses high-level features of each modality through concatenation, fully connected layers, and a Softmax classifier. Experiments are conducted on the public SEED dataset. Results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models and typical deep models (such as CNN, DGCNN) in multiple evaluation metrics. This study provides a new architectural reference for cross-modal perception and collaborative modeling, demonstrating strong theoretical significance and practical application value.

title: Algorithm Research on an Agricultural Environmental Intelligent Decision Support System
authors: Heyuan Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the resource waste, delayed response times, low fusion accuracy, and poor dynamic adaptability of existing algorithms in traditional agricultural decision-making, this paper designs an adaptive fusion and dynamic decision-making algorithm for multi-source heterogeneous agricultural environmental data (AEF-DDA), building a two-layer "fusion-decision-making" architecture. Experiments used data from a 100-mu winter wheat base in North China (containing 508,640 real-time data points and 1,440 historical data points) to compare the performance with traditional LSTM, RF, and SVM algorithms. Results show that AEF-DDA achieves the highest fusion accuracy (RMSE = 0.82, MAE = 0.65, representing 47.4% and 49.2% reductions compared to the traditional LSTM algorithm). The decision-making accuracy reaches 92.3%, an 8.5% improvement over RF. The response time for processing 1,000 data points is 0.32 seconds, a 57.3% reduction compared to RF. When the data missing rate was 20%, the accuracy dropped by only 3.6 percentage points (RF was 11.6). The accuracy rates at the jointing stage and the grain filling stage reached 93.7% and 95.2%, respectively. This demonstrates the algorithm's advantages in accuracy, real-time performance, and stability, and can provide support for smart agricultural environmental decision-making.

title: Design and Experimental Study of a Dance Imitation Scoring System Based on Computer Vision
authors: Luyun Deng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This research introduces a new dance imitation scoring system leveraging computer vision techniques to assess dance performance quality. The proposed system utilizes sophisticated pose estimation methods, such as OpenPose and MediaPipe, to identify human body key points and track movements within dance videos. The system evaluates essential aspects of dance imitation, including pose accuracy, timing synchronization, and movement coordination. By combining dynamic time warping with deep learning models, the system compares imitated dance routines to reference performances, generating a quantitative score. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves high accuracy, with results aligning closely with human judges across various dance styles, including ballet, hip-hop, and contemporary dance. The system offers significant advantages in terms of automation, precision, and consistency when compared to traditional manual scoring methods. However, challenges remain in handling rapid movements, occlusions, and ensuring real-time performance. Proposed improvements include the integration of 3D pose estimation and multi-angle tracking. This system has wide applications across entertainment, virtual reality, and personalized dance training, presenting new possibilities for automated dance assessment.

title: Gradient Descent Provably Solves Nonlinear Tomographic Reconstruction
authors: Sara Fridovich-Keil, Fabrizio Valdivia, Gordon Wetzstein, Benjamin Recht, Mahdi Soltanolkotabi
abstract: In computed tomography (CT), the forward model consists of a linear Radon transform followed by an exponential nonlinearity based on the attenuation of light according to the Beer–Lambert Law. Conventional reconstruction often involves inverting this nonlinearity and then solving a linear inverse problem. However, this nonlinear measurement preprocessing is poorly conditioned in the vicinity of high-density materials, such as metal. This preprocessing makes CT reconstruction methods numerically sensitive and susceptible to artifacts near high-density regions. In this paper, we study a technique where the signal is directly reconstructed from raw measurements through the nonlinear forward model. Though this optimization is nonconvex, we show that gradient descent provably converges to the global optimum at a geometric rate, perfectly reconstructing the underlying signal with a near minimal number of random measurements. We also prove similar results in the under-determined setting where the number of measurements is significantly smaller than the dimension of the signal. This is achieved by enforcing prior structural information about the signal through constraints on the optimization variables. We illustrate the benefits of direct nonlinear CT reconstruction with cone-beam CT experiments on synthetic and real 3D volumes, in which metal artifacts are reduced compared to standard linear reconstruction methods. Our experiments also demonstrate that logarithmic preprocessing alone is sufficient to produce metal artifacts, even in the absence of other causes such as beam hardening.

title: Resilient Current Sampling Method With Fault- Tolerant Capability for Dual Three-Phase PMSM
authors: YunJiang Wu, Wenxiang Zhao, Tao Tao, Yifan Chen, Sen Ma, Xiang Li
abstract: In the dual three-phase permanent magnet synchronous motor (DTP-PMSM) drive, the loss of current information will lead to degradation of control performance. This paper proposes a resilient current sampling method to ensure fault-tolerant operation even after the failure of four current sensors. First, the prerequisites for complete phase current acquisition are derived in a DTP-PMSM drive system. Then, the resilient current sampling method with multiple sampling paths per phase current is proposed. Furthermore, the feasibility of the proposed method to maintain continuous operation is verified by these prerequisites under various current sensor faults. With the proposed resilient current sampling method, the lost current information caused by the fault is recovered, ensuring the high performance of the motor. Finally, experimental results validate the proposed resilient sampling method.

title: Inflation forecasting in Thailand: Bayesian Shrinkage and Factor Models in a small open economy
authors: Paponpat Taveeapiradeecharoen, Nattapol Aunsri
abstract: Forecasting inflation in Thailand is challenging because limited time series and strong external exposures create an imbalance between few observations and many potential predictors. We evaluate modern Bayesian shrinkage and factor models, including Horseshoe regressions, factor-augmented autoregressions, factor-augmented VARs, dynamic factor models, and Bayesian additive regression trees, using more than 450 domestic and international indicators. The results show that factor models dominate at short horizons, when global shocks and exchange rate movements drive inflation, while shrinkage-based regressions perform best at longer horizons. These models not only improve point and density forecasts, but also enhance tail-risk performance at the one-year horizon. Shrinkage diagnostics, on the other hand, additionally reveal that Google Trends variables, especially those related to food essential goods and housing costs, progressively rotate into predictive importance as the horizon lengthens. This suggests that these variables may contain forward-looking information about household inflation concerns in Thailand.

title: A Novel Model Predictive Control-Based Energy Management for Stable and Efficient Operation of Renewable Integrated DC Microgrids
authors: Narendra Babu Kampa, Sandeep Vuddanti, S. V. K. Naresh
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical model predictive control (HMPC)–based energy management system (EMS) for a DC microgrid integrating photovoltaic (PV) generation, wind energy conversion, battery energy storage, and dynamic DC loads. The proposed framework coordinates multi-source power flows while ensuring robust DC bus voltage regulation, efficient renewable energy utilization, and battery-friendly operation. A detailed MATLAB/Simulink model of the DC microgrid is developed and evaluated under realistic operating conditions, including variable solar irradiance, fluctuating wind speeds, bidirectional battery operation, and dynamic electric vehicle and industrial load demands. The performance of the proposed HMPC is quantitatively compared with an existing adaptive model predictive control (AMPC) strategy. Numerical results demonstrate that HMPC significantly improves DC bus voltage stability, reducing voltage deviation from ±1.67% under AMPC to below ±0.83% during renewable power variations and to ±0.083% under battery power fluctuations. Voltage standard deviation is reduced by up to 70–90%, indicating enhanced damping and faster convergence. Furthermore, HMPC achieves smoother battery power transitions and superior state-of-charge regulation, limiting SoC ripples to below ±0.005 and reducing electrochemical stress by approximately 60–70%. These improvements confirm stronger stability margins, improved disturbance rejection, and higher overall EMS efficiency. The results validate HMPC as a robust and scalable control strategy for reliable and stable operation of modern DC microgrids with high renewable penetration.

title: A comprehensive study on the quality of commercially available medium voltage surge arresters
authors: Kuba Wójcik, Marek Olesz, Leszek S. Litzbarski, Stanisław Wojtas, Michał J. Winiarski, Daniel Jaworski, Marcin Łapiński, Paweł Ławniczak, Bartłomiej Andrzejewski
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the quality and reliability of commercially available medium voltage surge arresters, with particular emphasis on their electrical performance and structural degradation under aging conditions. Samples from four manufacturers were subjected to 8/20 μs aging impulses and accelerated durability tests. Electrical analysis revealed that some products exhibited excessively high residual voltages (Ures) exceeding declared values by an average of 7.5% and high partial discharge (PD) levels. The resistive component of the leakage current (ILCR) indicated that medium voltage (MV) surge arresters possess higher thermal resistance to aging as compared to the low voltage (LV) ones. Structural analysis performed using powder X-ray diffraction (pXRD) confirmed the presence of ZnO as the primary phase, along with intergranular δ-Bi2O3 and Sb2O3-based spinel and pyrochlore phases. Study of the powder X-ray diffraction patterns using theWilliamson–Hall method revealed the microstrains exhibiting significant variations in residual lattice strains. This suggests that during varistor ceramic processing distinct sintering protocols have been utilized. Elevated microstrain values (εWH) correlated with increased porosity indicated that volatilization ZnO was promoted by high sintering temperatures. Aging processes led to dopants diffusion into grain boundaries and growth of the spinel phase (Zn7Sb2O12). Dielectric spectroscopy measurement revealed changes in the real part of varistors’ permittivity (ε) up to 50% under thermal aging. This provides insight into the thermal stability and long-term reliability of medium voltage surge arresters under test.

title: Large Area Micro-Transfer-Printed TFLN-on-SiN Racetrack Resonators for Nonlinear Photonics
authors: Md S. I. Sumon, Samir Ghosh, Artem S. Vorobev, Nicola Maraviglia, Fatih B. Atar, Chiranjeevi Maddi, Rezwan Zakaria, Grayson Ullman, Ganga C. R. Devarapu, Imad I. Faruque, Sarvagya Dwivedi, Robert Bowman, Liam O’Faolain, Brian Corbett, Shamsul Arafin
abstract: We demonstrate large-area micro-transfer-printed thin-film lithium niobate (TFLN) on silicon nitride (SiN) racetrack resonators for integrated nonlinear photonics. A 300nm-thick TFLN coupon with a 670 × 680 μm² area was deterministically placed on top of patterned SiN. In the near-infrared wavelength region, the fabricated racetrack resonators achieve a loaded quality factor Q of ~70,000 with no contribution from scattering losses of TFLN sidewalls. Second-harmonic generation (SHG) is realized via modal phase matching (MPM) between the TE00 pump and TE01 second harmonic modes. A cavity-free single-pass waveguide exhibits a sinc2 -shaped second harmonic conversion efficiency with ~1 nm bandwidth, whereas integrating the same MPM section into a racetrack resonator generates bright green emission when the resonances overlap the SHG bandwidth. This μTP TFLN-on-SiN approach is wafer-scalable, offering a versatile pathway to on-chip visible generation and χ² functionality co-located with ultra-low-loss SiN routing.

title: Si CMOS Electronic Sensors for Terahertz Biomedical Characterization
authors: Dmytro B But, Adam Kowalczyk, Valeri Mikhnev, Cezary Kołaciński, Wojciech Knap
publish: 2026 International Conference on Medical Microwave Imaging Applications (ICMWIA)
abstract: Electrodynamic interactions between biological objects and electromagnetic radiation in the microwave and terahertz frequency ranges hold great promise for various medical applications. In this work, we discuss electronic sensors designed in silicon complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor technology and their role in advancing terahertz dielectric spectroscopy, based on our experience. Furthermore, we demonstrate the representative applications of these devices in the terahertz regime.

title: Hybrid Convolutional Autoencoder with Spiking Neural Network-Based Compression Approach with Binary Spike Latent for Telemedicine and Archival
authors: Keerthana Krishnan T, Krishna K M, Neethu K Shine, Dhuja Shaheem, Nitin K A, Arumuga Arun R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Deep learning has pushed the bounds of image compression to depiction and latent feature representation that are visually high-quality and efficient. This research work addresses the redundancy inherent to latent spaces and the need for compressed and more energy efficient solutions for image compression deep learning in a Medical Imaging context. We introduce a new hybrid compression solution by combining a Convolutional Autoencoder (CAE) with Spiking Neural networks (SNN) essentially creating a new energy aware compressive framework that introduces latent features and reduces redundancy and associated storage. The proposed CAE identifies dense latent features while the SNN uses asynchronous temporal timeencoded binary spikes - these spikes further reduce redundancy and storage costs. We performed experiments on grayscale medical images and rely on traditional Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) measurements to evaluate the performance. Results from experiments using two medical datasets show that our hybrid CAE + SNNspike model compressed images and was efficient and reconstructed images that had similar or improved quality to the standard CAE (Hybrid: PSNR = 34.31 dB, SSIM = 0.9520; Plain CAE: PSNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=33.14 ~\text{dB}$</tex>, SSIM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.9195$</tex>) while reducing the size of the latent space. In proposing a hybrid CAE with SNN, we have created a new energy aware compressive framework that enables both storage efficiency and energy awareness, which is promising for medical imaging analysis designs, particularly when integrating the efficient image transmission and storage along with energy efficiency in a low power context.

title: Obstacle-Aware Control for 3D Cranes via Dynamic Window Planning and Optimal Tracking
authors: Nezar M. Alyazidi, Ali Mustafa, Khaled S. Bin Gaufan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: Efficient and safe operation of 3D overhead cranes requires precise sway suppression and reliable motion planning in obstacle-rich environments. This paper presents a hybrid planner–tracker framework that integrates the Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) for local path planning with the Integral Linear Quadratic Regulator (ILQR) for optimal tracking and sway reduction. The DWA generates dynamically feasible velocity commands that balance progress, clearance, and smoothness, while the ILQR ensures accurate trajectory tracking and disturbance rejection. An observer is incorporated to reconstruct unmeasured states, further improving robustness under noisy conditions. Simulation studies in MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the proposed approach can accurately track trajectories, effectively suppress sway, and safely avoid obstacles in complex layouts. The results highlight the framework as a practical solution for obstacle-aware automation of industrial overhead cranes.

title: Research on a Dynamic Priority Allocation Algorithm for Interactive and Collaborative Control of Flexible Resources in Cross-Regional Power Grids
authors: Zhifu Wu, Li Yao, Zhiqiang Xu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Intelligent algorithms demonstrate strong potential in resource optimization and dynamic scheduling of power systems, providing innovative approaches and effective means for addressing the increasingly complex challenges of grid operation. This paper, leveraging intelligent algorithms, investigates the dynamic allocation of priorities for interactive, coordinated control of flexible resources across regional power grids. The research foundation for dynamic allocation of priorities for interactive, coordinated control of flexible resources across regional power grids is clarified. When allocating flexible resources across regional power grids, a dynamic priority concept is introduced to establish a priority indicator framework. The analytic hierarchy process is used to assess flexible resource allocation priorities. Once priorities are determined, coordinated control is implemented to minimize allocation costs and energy consumption, while also defining constraints. A genetic algorithm is employed to solve this objective function. Individuals are identified based on their fitness values and other factors, achieving optimal resource allocation. Application results show that in the interactive and coordinated control of flexible resources across regional power grids, resource utilization rates are higher than those of comparable methods under all loads, enabling dynamic priority adjustment to improve utilization efficiency. Energy consumption increases steadily over the $\mathbf{4 0}$-hour control period, reaching only 60.2 kWh. The average latency reduction reaches 40.7% when the number of services ranges from 100 to 800, effectively suppressing latency growth even under extreme loads, significantly improving the grid’s real-time control capabilities.

title: Evaluating the Influence of Machine Learning on Accounting Practices and Tax Compliance
authors: Susheelamma K H, Vinutha S V, T. Selvaprabhu, Poorani M, S Vishnupriya, Ramachandran C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) integration into accounting and tax services signifies radical changes inside the operational landscape of these professions. This study explores adoption of ML technologies, elucidating their implications for efficiency, accuracy, and decision making processes. Through a comprehensive review of current literature and real world case studies, this research highlights important areas where ML enhances traditional accounting tasks, such as data analysis, predictive modeling, and audit processes. Furthermore, it examines the ethical considerations and challenges inherent in deploying these advanced systems, including data privacy, regulatory compliance, and algorithmic bias. The findings underscore that while ML provides much opportunity to enhance service delivery and client satisfaction, it simultaneously necessitates a reevaluation of skill sets and professional standards within the accounting and tax sectors. This research provides insightful information for practitioners and scholars alike, contributing to a deeper understanding of ML's role in shaping the future of accounting and taxation.

title: Optimisation Study of Oil Well Production Prediction Method Based on Sparrow Search and Gradient Boosting
authors: Yonghao Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: In the intelligent management of petroleum extraction, oil well production forecasting holds a significant position within the petroleum industry’s intelligent management framework. Traditional forecasting methods often exhibit limited accuracy due to data complexity and nonlinear characteristics. This study aims to enhance prediction accuracy by optimising intelligent algorithms, proposing a hybrid model based on an improved Sparrow Search algorithm and Gradient Boosting. The methodology first employs hybrid chaotic mapping and the firefly algorithm to enhance the initialisation of populations and global search capabilities within the Sparrow Search algorithm, thereby mitigating premature convergence. Subsequently, the improved algorithm optimises the hyperparameters of the Gradient Boosting Tree algorithm to construct an intelligent prediction model. Experiments demonstrate that after 400 iterations, the model reduces the root mean square error to 0.310 m3 on real oil well datasets, representing an average 27.23% reduction compared to other models. The coefficient of determination reaches 0.981 m3, achieving an average 5.38% improvement over other models. Under 30% noise interference, its mean absolute error was 0.576 m3, representing an increase of merely 125.88%. These results demonstrate that integrating the improved optimisation strategy with gradient boosting mechanisms effectively captures the nonlinear patterns of oil well production, providing a high-precision solution for production forecasting.

title: Feature Mining Method for Distributed PV Performance Fluctuation Based on Multi-Scale Decomposition
authors: Xianjun Wang, Yong Li, Gaomeng Jia, Rui Zhao, Yanan Li, Xu Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems that the output of distributed Photovoltaic (PV) systems exhibits strong volatility and intermittency due to the influence of environmental and equipment factors, and that existing feature mining methods suffer from insufficient data reliability, low separation accuracy of multi-scale features, and weak feature practicability, this paper proposes a multi-scale decomposition-based feature mining method for distributed PV output fluctuations. First, the method eliminates outliers through the $\mathbf{3} \sigma$ criterion combined with trend consistency judgment, and fills in missing values using an improved neighborhood-weighted imputation algorithm to complete data preprocessing. Second, it identifies the key environmental driving factors of PV output via the Pearson correlation coefficient. On this basis, an initial temporal fluctuation feature set is constructed by means of a sliding time window; multi-scale fluctuation separation is realized using Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), and core feature extraction is completed by integrating Principal Component Analysis (PCA). Furthermore, four types of indicators, namely fluctuation intensity, frequency, persistence, and stability, are quantified. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed multi-scale decomposition method can effectively improve decomposition performance and accurately characterize the fluctuation features of distributed PV output.

title: Selection and Simulation Verification of Transformer Dc Blocking Devices Based on Transient Fault Conditions
authors: Wenxuan Zhao, Jingxuan Xu, Chunming Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: DC bias in transformers poses a serious threat to the safe and stable operation of power grids. To suppress DC bias, DC blocking devices are typically installed at the transformer neutral point. This paper proposes a selection and design method for capacitive DC blocking devices. First, typical operating conditions such as transient fault currents and DC disturbances that the device must address are clearly defined. The main topology, working principle, bypass system, and monitoring unit design are elaborated in detail, with quantitative calculations and verification of key parameters for major equipment. Subsequently, simulations are conducted in the PSCAD/EMTDC software environment. By comparing simulated waveforms with theoretical dynamic response processes, the effectiveness and feasibility of the device topology and selection are validated.

title: Conference Organization

title: Optimizing the Thermal Performance of Oilimmersed Transformers Through Transverse Duct Width Design
authors: Yang Shao, Xiang Dong, Xiaolei Fang, Xiaokun Han, Dao Zhou, Chenyu Huang, Jiayu Duan, Wensheng Gao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The thermal performance of oil-immersed transformers is critical for operational reliability. The width of transverse ducts between winding discs significantly influences internal oil flow and cooling efficiency. This study investigates an SSZ11-25000/110 transformer using a sequential electromagnetic-thermal-fluid coupling method. A refined 2 D axisymmetric model was developed based on 3D finite element loss calculations and model order reduction. The model analyzed steady-state and transient temperature fields, along with laminar flow distribution under rated conditions. Focusing on the critical low-voltage winding, four transverse duct width schemes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3.82-16.32 ~\text{mm})$</tex> were designed to quantify their impact. Results show that increasing duct width reduces the maximum winding and insulating oil temperatures by up to 39.50 % and 22.92 %, respectively, while core temperature is largely unaffected (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<2.6 \%$</tex>). Duct widening also improves inter-disc flow uniformity and reduces stagnation, but its effect on adjacent narrow longitudinal ducts is negligible, revealing a nonlinear relationship between duct geometry and cooling performance. This work provides a theoretical basis for optimizing transformer cooling structures.

title: IoT-Assisted Hybrid Multiport Converter for Battery Swapping Charging Station with V2G Capability
authors: Akshay Chabukswar, P Sirisha Devi, Sree Hari J B, Chekka Supriya, Korivi Yogananda Reddy, Priyanka Chabukswar, Praveen Vankadari, Rupesh Wandhare
abstract: Battery swapping charging stations for two-wheeler electric vehicles (2 W EVs) are gaining adoption, but existing solutions often lack high-gain, isolation, multi-port for integration of renewable sources, vehicle-to-grid (V2G) capability, and intelligent monitoring in a single unit. This paper presents a renewableintegrated battery swapping station using a multi-port high-gain Dual Active Half-Bridge (DAHB) converter. DAHB at the charging port enables fast EV battery charging, while a single-phase totem-pole Active Front-End (AFE) supports efficient grid-tovehicle (G2V) and V2G power exchange. During V2G operation, the EV battery directly regulates the photovoltaic (PV) voltage at its Maximum Power Point (MPP) using Phase Shift Modulation (PSM). When no EV is connected or in G2V mode, the AFE maintains PV operation at MPP through a dual-loop Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) control. An IoT-enabled framework provides real-time monitoring of battery parameters, such as state of charge (SoC) and charging duration, and allows operators to perform V2G energy exchange even in the absence of swapping loads. Simulation studies for a commercial EV - Revolt RV400 2W EV removable battery demonstrate improved efficiency, enhanced operational flexibility, and effective integration of renewable energy compared to conventional charging stations.

title: Spatiotemporal Wind Power Forecasting across Multiple Wind Farms Using GAT-BiLSTM with Online Adaptation
authors: Xihan Zhao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To improve the accuracy and stability of shortterm wind power prediction under multi-site spatial distribution conditions, this paper proposes a deep learning prediction framework that integrates Graph Attention (GAT) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks. An online adaptive update strategy based on sample loss feedback is designed to address the dynamic changes in data and concept drift issues in wind farm operation. The method first constructs a spatial graph structure based on the similarity of historical data among multiple source sites, and uses GAT to mine spatial dependencies. Then, a BiLSTM module is introduced to model the temporal evolution characteristics of wind power. Finally, a dynamic fusion strategy is used to achieve weighted integrated prediction of the multi-source model outputs. Experiments on multiple real wind farm datasets show that the proposed model outperforms mainstream comparative models in terms of MAE, RMSE, and MAPE, especially exhibiting stronger robustness and generalization ability under conditions of gradual data integration and abnormal weather such as low wind speeds. This verifies the effectiveness and engineering practical value of the proposed method in complex wind power prediction tasks.

title: Deep Learning-Based Gas Well Gas Recovery Prediction Study
authors: Qinchuan Xu, Xinwang Li, Jingwen Luo, Xinyu Pan, Lingtong Liu, Yihong Sun
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the continuous growth in energy demand, optimizing production and resource scheduling requires accurate forecasting of daily gas output at natural gas well stations. This study proposes a deep learning model integrating Time Convolutional Networks (TCN) with attention mechanisms for precise forecasting of daily gas production at natural gas well stations. Leveraging multidimensional feature variables from historical production data, the research employs a TCN architecture with multiscale feature extraction capabilities to effectively capture complex nonlinear relationships within time-series data. To enhance the model’s sensitivity to critical time steps and feature dimensions, an attention mechanism is introduced to assign weights to significant features. Comparative experiments demonstrate that this hybrid model significantly outperforms traditional time series analysis methods and conventional machine learning models in prediction accuracy, particularly in modeling temporal dependencies and nonlinear features. The research outcomes provide a reliable intelligent forecasting tool for optimizing natural gas production dynamics, holding significant engineering application value.

title: AI-Powered Review Analyzer for Mobile Applications
authors: Kshitij Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The exponential growth of mobile applications across platforms such as the Google Play Store has resulted in an overwhelming volume of user-generated reviews. These reviews contain valuable insights that, if effectively analyzed, can inform developers about user satisfaction, common issues, feature requests, and overall sentiment. Manual analysis of such reviews is often time-consuming, biased, and infeasible at scale. This paper introduces an AI-powered review analyzer designed to extract meaningful feedback and provide developers with actionable insights through natural language interactions. The system allows users to ask questions such as “What features do users dislike?” or “What improvements can be made?”, and dynamically generates context-relevant responses based on real review data. Unlike many existing solutions that rely on cloud-based services or paid APIs, this approach is privacyconscious, lightweight, and operates entirely offline. Evaluations conducted on real-world applications, including Instagram and PhonePe, demonstrate its practical utility for developers seeking to enhance user experience and product quality. The proposed solution supports scalable review understanding and offers a foundation for intelligent, real-time decision-making in mobile application development.

title: Securing CAN Bus Transmissions with Lightweight AEAD Ciphers
authors: Joshua Copeland, Leonie Simpson, Geoff Walker
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Australasian Information Security Conference

title: Ethical Prompt Engineering for AI-driven SE: Evidence-informed Interaction-time Governance Roadmap to 2030
authors: Patrizio Migliarini, Marco Autili, Paola Inverardi, Patrizio Pelliccione
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Research on Information Security Protection in Power Scheduling Networks Based on Distributed Intrusion Tolerance Technology
authors: Yanyan Wang, Li Mou, Yifan Hu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the increasing digitization of power systems, ensuring the security and reliability of power dispatch networks has become critically important. This study proposes an innovative information security protection algorithm based on distributed intrusion tolerance technology. The algorithm integrates dynamic data collection and machine learning methods to predict potential faults and optimize resource allocation, thereby enhancing the system’s fault tolerance. Experimental results demonstrate that the virtual machine layer exhibits the shortest and most stable fault detection time, followed by the hardware and host layers. Detection accuracy is highest and most stable at the virtual machine level, while the host layer shows lower and more variable accuracy. Load stress tests indicate that the algorithm maintains stable performance under CPU and memory stress, with some fluctuations under mixed I/O stress due to disk I/O failures. These findings provide valuable insights for designing robust fault tolerance mechanisms in power scheduling networks.

title: Short-Term Load Forecasting During Pandemic: The Cambodia Case Study
authors: Sovannarith Sao, Wusheng Shi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Massive outbreaks of infectious diseases, known as pandemics, can rapidly and discreetly spread across borders, leading to a substantial increase in fatalities and morbidity beyond vast areas of the globe. Throughout history, pandemics have influenced the development of societies. Impacts on energy infrastructure and consumption patterns may result from these unprecedented pandemics, potentially leading to a shift in social and economic norms. In the face of unexpected changes in electric power generation and demand, as well as the necessity to maintain a supply to consumers during pandemics, understanding variations in power demand and their effects on load forecasting is essential for ensuring the reliable operation of the power grid. This paper examines a background of historical major pandemics for the last two decades with the power grid and contributes to the development of a tool that will assist system operators in performing short-term load forecasting (STLF) for 24 hours to serve as a preparatory task during unforeseen pandemics, using the COVID-19 situation in Cambodia as a case study. This work set out to use a proposed CNN-LSTM model to look at how exogenous inputs all play a role in determining an ultimate result. To evaluate the performance of this proposed model, multiple measurement indicators are used, including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). The results demonstrate that adding more exogenous factors enhances the performance of the proposed model.

title: Valuation of Carbon Emission Allowances Based on an Improved PCV Model: Low-Carbon Technology Parameterization and Engineering Validation
authors: Ziyu Ma, Guona Yang, Xiaochun Zhang, Ming Zeng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: This study focuses on low-carbon retrofit pathways in the power sector and proposes an evaluation method for Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technologies by integrating key operational parameters, forming a carbon allowance valuation framework based on the Putty-Clay Vintage (PCV) model. The model incorporates indicators such as unit availability, coal consumption, and carbon oxidation rate to establish a mapping between technical parameters and carbon emission intensity. A grey clustering method is applied to adjust for external macro factors, enhancing the model’s adaptability and generalizability. Validation using real-world data from a typical coal-fired power plant demonstrates strong technical sensitivity and engineering applicability. The proposed approach provides methodological support for CCUS and other low-carbon technologies, contributing to the technical advancement of carbon emission in the power industry.

title: AI-Driven Precision Medicine: From Genomic Variant Detection to Explainable and Ethical Clinical Decision Support
authors: N Shahidabegum, Vibha M B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) plays significant role in precision medicine as it can quickly study and understand huge amounts of medical data unlike traditional methods, which cannot easily handle voluminous information. AI is being used to find genetic changes, discover new drugs, help doctors make better decisions, and make sure the systems are fair and easy to explain. AI tools can more accurately detect genetic differences and help doctors choose the right drug doses for each patient. AI can also combine information from health records, images, and wearable devices to support real-time care. However, there are many issues. These include poor quality of data, lack of testing in all populations, difficulty in adding AI into daily hospital work, and delays in getting government approvals. Future work should focus on creating better datasets, building AI models that are easier to use and explain, testing them in real-hospitals, and ensuring strong ethical rules. Solving these issues will make AI a trusted and reliable part of everyday healthcare.

title: Generation-Side Primary Frequency Control Strategy of Compressed Air Energy Storage
authors: Xue Chen, Zhengtang Sun, Zhimei Cao, Hanchen Liu, Kai Wang, Laijun Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: As renewable energy constitutes a growing share of next-generation power grids, the low-inertia characteristics of power systems have become more pronounced, leading to growing challenges in frequency stability. The Advanced Adiabatic Compressed Air Energy Storage (AA-CAES) system is known for its high efficiency, fast response, and excellent frequency regulation capability. However, traditional PID control strategies suffer from slow response and cannot meet the demand for efficient frequency regulation. To address this issue, this study presents a Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR)-based control strategy for primary frequency regulation in AA-CAES systems. A primary frequency regulation model of AA-CAES is established considering the dynamic characteristics of each component. The LQR is introduced along with the system's State of Charge (SOC) to design an output power control strategy for AA-CAES, optimizing its actual output under different SOC conditions. Simulation results based on the MATLAB/Simulink platform show that the proposed strategy exhibits optimal frequency regulation performance when the system is fully charged. Compared with PI control, the proposed strategy minimizes the maximum frequency deviation under step disturbances and significantly suppresses frequency peak-to-peak variations under continuous load disturbances, thereby effectively mitigating system frequency deviations and improving the primary frequency regulation response of the power system. This provides strong support for the active participation of AA-CAES in grid primary frequency regulation services.

title: A Two-Stage Robust Optimization Framework for Dynamic Aggregation of Virtual Power Plant Feasible Power Regions
authors: Shijin Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources, power system operation is subject to growing uncertainty. This paper develops a two-stage robust optimization model to characterize and expand the dynamic aggregated feasible power region of a virtual power plant (VPP). Heterogeneous resources, including controllable generators, energy storage, renewables, and flexible loads, are integrated into a unified framework. The upper stage maximizes regulation capability, while the lower stage minimizes deviations under worst-case dispatch. An improved C&CG-CCP algorithm is developed, and case studies verify its effectiveness.

title: Thermal Performance Study of a Lithium-Ion Battery Based on a Bionic Fishbone-Shaped Flow Channel Liquid Cooling Plate
authors: Jin Huang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: A bionic fishbone-shaped channel liquid cooling plate is proposed to enhance power battery cooling performance. The effects of different parameters on heat transfer coefficient $\left(h_{c}\right)$ and pressure drop $(\Delta \mathrm{p})$ were simulated with Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD). The single-factor analysis and orthogonal experiment showed that the main channel width w<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>, collecting channel width w<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, the number of branch channels n, and mass flow q are the main factors that affect the performance. The optimal value found in the experiments corresponds to the following combination of factors: the main channel width (w<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>) is 16 mm, the collecting channel width (w<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) is 4 mm, the number of branches (n) is 30, and the mass flow (q) is $50 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{s}$. Under these conditions, the heat transfer coefficient $\left(h_{c}\right)$ increases by $7.6 \%$, the pressure drop ($\Delta \mathrm{p}$) decreases by $34.7 \%$, and the heat transfer performance factor (HTPF) increases by $82.7 \%$ as compared to the initial design.

title: Adaptive Graph-Based Risk Scoring for Real-Time Instant Payment Systems
authors: Vimal Teja Manne
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Instant payment rails and mobile money platforms such as UPI, Pix, and FedNow require fraud decisions in a few hundred milliseconds, but most deployed and published fraud models are batch oriented and sequence only. They are trained and evaluated offline, treat each account in isolation, and provide limited visibility into mule networks and collusive cash out structures. This paper presents a graph based streaming risk scoring architecture that brings graph neural network (GNN) style inference into the authorization path while respecting a sub 300 ms end to end service level objective. The system maintains a dynamic transaction graph over accounts, devices, merchants, and institutions, updates this graph as payments arrive, and uses an online GNN to propagate risk so that emerging mule rings can be flagged even when individual transfers appear benign. Raw scores are calibrated into three operational actions, allow, step up, and block, under explicit fraud loss and customer friction budgets. Using a labeled synthetic mobile money dataset representative of instant payment rails, we build a real time risk engine around this architecture and quantify its latency, queueing behaviour, and decision quality. A strong tabular baseline representative of existing non graph deployments is implemented inside the same engine and evaluated side by side, providing a concrete comparative analysis between current practice and the proposed architecture.

title: AI-Enhanced Predictive Maintenance of BLDC Motors Using Machine Learning for Fault Diagnosis and Prevention
authors: Visham Kumar V, Rohith Rajesh, Krishna Priyanka R, Vetrivel SK, M Venkatesh Kumar, Ilango. K
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Brushless DC motor (BLDC) is used in industrial sectors, especially in electric vehicles, due to its better efficiency, reliability, and high durability. Even though there are scenarios where BLDC motors fail due to electrical faults, overheating, or misalignment of shafts in rotating machines, which can lead to failures and unexpected downtime, predictive maintenance is used to prevent this. However, it is challenging to obtain sufficient historical data on real faults to facilitate accurate prediction. Hence, in this study, along with real-time data, AI-generated data sets are also used to improve the accuracy of the predictive model. BLDC motors can be monitored efficiently using AI-enhanced data and real data, which is more efficient than relying solely on real data. Various machine learning models, such as KNN, Decision-Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine(SVM), XGBOOST, and ANN, have been developed. The most suitable model is chosen by comparing the accuracy. The confession matrix is used to evaluate the models. This way, industries can reduce downtime, improve motor reliability, reduce maintenance costs, and make fault detection much easier.
