title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Cyber Threat Intelligence and Decision Making
authors: Roopa H M, Sahana J Upadhyaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The number and complexity of cyber-attacks rapidly increases, which propels the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to automatically detect and respond to the threat. Nevertheless, a majority of AI models are opaque and thus performing poorly in high- security settings where the explainability and accountability of the AI model is required. In this paper, the author suggests the use of a hybrid framework by combining both Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques and machine learning models to improve cyber threat intelligence systems. The paper compares four detection models, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) on publicly available datasets, which are CICIDS2017 and UNSW-NB15. SHAP, LIME and counterfactual methodologies based on post hoc explanation were adopted to aid optimistic comprehension of explanations by humans. BiLSTM had the best detection accuracy value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(96.3 \%)$</tex>, and Random Forest the highest performance to explainability ratio. To visualize the results of the models, it has been developed as a real- time analyst dashboard. The findings show that, using XAI, the system is more transparent, the analyst is more confident in it, and easier to use, providing a repeatable,scalable, and explainable cybersecurity solution.

title: OpenMMLab-Based Road Surface Crack Detection with Faster R-CNN, YOLOv5s and YOLOv7
authors: Hao Bai, Shulong Zhuo, Lifeng Jiang, Xiongwei Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Road-surface cracks are early indicators of pavement deterioration and require timely detection for efficient maintenance planning. Traditional visual inspection and handcrafted image-processing methods are labor-intensive and often degrade under complex backgrounds (e.g., shadows, stains, and strong texture). Deep learning-based object detection offers a scalable alternative, but detector choice for road crack images remains sensitive to implementation details and accuracy-speed trade-offs. This paper benchmarks three representative detectors—Faster R-CNN, YOLOv5s, and YOLOv7—under a unified OpenMMLab pipeline, using MMDetection and MMYOLO on a COCO-format road crack dataset. Experimental results show that YOLOv7 achieves the best overall performance, reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{map} {a} 0.5=0.955$</tex> and 193 FPS, indicating strong potential for real-time and large-scale road inspection.

title: Cross-Site Evaluation of Private 5G LANs Interconnected via Public WAN for Synchronised Immersive Applications: Connecting Fan Zones
authors: Mohammad Abdus Sobur, Ruth Falconer, Laith Al-Jobouri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Synchronised immersive media across venues requires high throughput and predictable timing. This paper reports a live, cross-site trial connecting two UK fan zones, each with an on-site private 5G network, separated by approximately 280 miles, over a public wide-area network during the 2025 Six Nations broadcast. Throughput, latency, jitter, and packet loss were measured under (i) on-site operation and (ii) dual-site WAN operation while multi-stream media drove an immersive and interactive live sports experience. The private 5G path sustained 66.5 Mb/s TCP, ~5 ms RTT, and 0.055 ms jitter, exhibiting deterministic behaviour suitable for frame-accurate workflows. The WAN-bridged path averaged 3.84 Mb/s, with ~0.465 ms jitter and RTT excursions up to 197 ms, challenging tight synchronisation. Contributions: (1) reproducible cross-site evidence that private 5G affords low-jitter transport for time- critical media while public-WAN variability disrupts synchrony; (2) a portable, site-parity methodology for multi-venue measurement; and (3) actionable guidance for hybrid architectures keeping interactive media on the on-site private-5G with edge offload and reserving WAN links for non-critical exchanges informing the path to 5G Advanced/6G.

title: Research on State Identification and Control Signal Fusion Methods for Elevating Platforms of Substation Equipment Aimed at Intelligent Operation and Maintenance
authors: Weiwei Li, Jianbo Li, Dinghao Yuan, Xingyu Tu, Jianping Mao, Hong Li, Song Zheng, Wei Zhang, Qunhai Zhou
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To enhance the state recognition accuracy and control response capability of lifting platforms for substation equipment under complex operation and maintenance conditions, an intelligent control method integrating multi-source perception signals and model predictive control is established. A nonlinear state estimation model based on the unscented Kalman filter is developed, and a multi-channel control signal fusion strategy under a gated weighting mechanism is designed. A constrained model predictive control algorithm is introduced at the control logic layer to achieve closed-loop regulation of critical state variables including platform pose, tilt angle, and rope tension. Experimental platform validation demonstrates that compared to traditional Proportional-Integral-Derivative control (PID) strategies, the designed system exhibits superior robustness and control accuracy under steady-state positioning, variable-speed lifting, and unbalanced load disturbance conditions. Displacement root mean square error is reduced by $34.2 \%$, with maximum tension fluctuations controlled within $\pm 3.1 \mathrm{~N}$, showcasing excellent real-time performance and engineering adaptability.

title: Evaluation of a 3D-Printed Capacitor Applied to the Circuit Line of a 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PCB
authors: Augusto Ruy Machado, Cesar Biasi de Moura, Laisa C. C. De Biase, Roseli D. Lopes, Marcelo K. Zuffo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Additive Manufactured Electronics (AME) enables:

title: Performance Evolution of Enhanced Quadratic-Gain Converter with Voltage Multiplier and Reduced Devices Voltage Stress for Solar PV Integration
authors: Seshagiri Rao Vemparala, M. S. Bhaskar, Mahmoud F. Elmorshedy, Dhafer Almakhles, Kumaravel S, Sivaprasad Athikkal
abstract: Quadratic boost converter (QBC) is reported in literature by using two boost converter network to enhance the voltage gain over a conventional boost converter. However, the voltage stress on the switch and the output diode remains equivalent to the converter's output voltage. To enhance voltage gain and minimize voltage stress on the switch and output diode, a switched capacitor inductor network (SCLN) has been studied by adding a diode and a capacitor to the QBC. The voltage stress on the switch and output diode is reduced below the output voltage; however, it resembles QBC. To reduce the voltage stress on the switch of the SCLN converter, by substituting one of the diodes in the SCLN with a switch, a converter to reduced voltage stress of the switched capacitor inductor (RVSCL) converter is studied. However, the voltage gain remains the same as the SCLN converter. To obtain high voltage gain with reduce the voltage stress on the switch and continuous source current, two classical boost converter network is combined with a voltage multiplier stage and an intermediate capacitor-diode pair. This reduced voltage stress switched capacitor inductor configuration, referred to as the VM-RVSCL power circuit, is studied for performance assessment. The steady-state gain of the VM-RVSCL power circuit is discussed and comparison between the VM-RVSCL and traditional converters is given. The components of the VM-RVSCL power circuit have been designed to obtain an output voltage and power of 650 V and 500 W, respectively, and its performance is verified by simulation results.

title: C-HIL Validation of Reduced Device Stress Multilevel PFC Rectifier for EV Charging Application Using OPAL-RT
authors: Manish Kumar Barwar, Akhilesh Kumar Tiwari, Sai Teja Cherla, Lalit Kumar Sahu, Sameer Singh, Prasanta Kumar Jena
abstract: In multi-stage battery chargers for the electricvehicle (EV) application, the AC to DC stage plays a crucial role in defining overall power quality. To enhance this, multilevel rectifiers (MLRs) utilise lower voltage-rated switches and offer an improved grid profile. In this paper, a single-phase five-level rectifier equipped with self-balancing capabilities, maintaining grid profile and closed-loop control, is specifically designed for EV charging applications. The implemented converter reduces voltage stress across power switches and offers the added benefit of eliminating the need for a DC-side filter, as the load is connected in parallel with one of the capacitors. Its continuous conduction mode and five-level operation eliminate the need for both AC-side capacitive and DC-side inductive filters. The paper comprehensively details the rectifier's profile in different aspects. The proposed control method provides better output voltage regulation under varying load conditions while maintaining a unity power factor at the input. Further, the Controller Hardware-in-the-loop (C-HIL) validation of the reduced device stress MLR topology has been done using the latest hardware from OPAL-RT (OP4610). This study presents a comprehensive analysis of the C-HIL employing OPAL-RT for any converter topology.

title: A Method for Separating Temperature in Electric Drive Systems Considering Torque-Temperature Time Delay and Adaptive Robust Regression
authors: Fang Wang, Xin Zhang, Wujun Zou, Huanhuan Li, Min Wang, Meiqiu Song, Junling Shi, Cheng Cheng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In the field of electric drive condition monitoring, to accurately separate torque-related normal fluctuations from temperature signals and support early fault diagnosis of electric drive systems, an adaptive robust regression model is constructed considering the thermal inertia of the electric drive system and the continuously changing characteristics of temperature-torque nonlinearity. Experimental verification shows that the torquetemperature effect extracted by the electric drive temperature analysis technology combining torque-temperature time delay and adaptive robust regression exhibits a significant correlation with the torque signal, ensuring the effective separation of torquerelated normal fluctuation signals from electric drive temperature signals.

title: Energy-Accuracy Tradeoff in Energy-Aware YOLOv11 Optimization for Edge AI
authors: Hariharan P, Anju S Pillai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Nowadays, running a deep learning models in environments with limited resources, like edge devices and servers, has become difficult due to its power and hardware restrictions. It has become important as edge computation and embedded AI technology increase at a rapid pace. Edge devices running for AI application generally operates on tight power budgets in returns needed to have a careful balance between energy use and model accuracy while also maintaining strict limits on power, computation, and inference time. In the work below, the energy-accuracy tradeoffs YOLOv11 models for edge deployment are thoroughly examined. When optimisation strategies like quantization, pruning and hybrid techniques. When applied apart from minimal degradation, findings showed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 x$</tex> of energy savings compared to baseline model with retaining 95% and 99% of precision and recall. Apart from this results, hybrid techniques shown 10x speed up compared to baseline model. This new result provides as with a sustainable method for edge deployment and also shows how structure aware pruning is needed while pruning related optimisation is applied.

title: Face Liveness Detection Against Spoofing Attacks
authors: Vibudesh R B, Sivadeep N, Varshine T, E. Gothai
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: With face recognition based authentication being applied extensively in banks, e-learning and safe access control systems, demonstrating the existence of an actual human has become a critical problem. The attacks by photos and videos and by 3D masks and virtual camera injection invalidate the effectiveness of dominant systems. Most of the existing methods rely solely on passive liveness detection and failed in identifying advanced spoofing attempts, while high sized models are preventing deployment in real-time application. This paper presents a hybrid AI-based face liveness detection system by combining passive, active and environment-based methods to ensure real time, light-weight and safe authentication. The system employs MiniFASNet to passively identify distortions in a texture-based manner and to detect by active microinteraction such as blinking and turning of the head by using MultiFTNet. YOLOv5 is used to apply to face detection and DeepFace to verify identity once liveness is determined. The proposed remedy ensures strong robust- ness to photos and video attacks by spoofing and attacks by conventional 3D masks and virtual cameras while maintaining scalability, light weight character and reasonableness in real world application. By combining both deep learning and hybrid detection techniques this paper is an important movement toward perfecting usability and safety in authen- tication by using biometrics.

title: A New Control Scheme for Adjustable Speed Drives Using 3-Phase 2-Level Cascaded Boost Inverter
authors: S S Phaniram Musti, Ravikumar Bhimasingu
abstract: Cascaded boost converter and inverter topology is predominantly used in various applications like electric drives, microgrids etc. Conventional control methods for these cascade topologies involves multiple control loops and involves tedious control gain tuning for both boost converter and inverter controllers. It is proposed here to device a control scheme with common control loop for both boost converter and inverter. Proposed control scheme with switching frequency modulation is devised to execute the control action for the cascaded network consisting of boost: inductor, capacitor and filter: inductor, capacitor along with motor. In the proposed scheme, boost converter is switched on during zero states of Inverter space vector modulation, thus within the same sub-interval of space vector modulation both boost and inverter switches share the duty cycles. i.e., during idle states of inverter, boost converter charges the inductor and releases the stored energy during active states of inverter. While comparing with conventional control methods for cascaded topologies from literature, this method implements a common controller with conventional space vector control and also boost action happens integral to the control loop to meet the required speed demand. Also proposed control scheme has potential to get away with the bulky DC link capacitors.

title: Adaptive and Stress-Responsive Wearable Self-Defense System with Concealed Multi-Modal Actuation for Women Safety
authors: S. Ramesh, A. Kannammal, R. Kishore, J. Jenshya, P. Ramya, T. Parimalam
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Passive alarm systems are insufficient to guarantee women's safety in emergency situations and therefore require intelligent wearables to provide instant, multilayered self-protection. This paper introduces a wearable system that integrates physiological stress monitoring, adaptive defense cascading, and hidden actuation. Biosensors (heart rate, skin conductance, accelerometer) track stress and abnormal movement. Defense can be initiated manually or automatically, progressing through alarm at high volume, pepper spray, and low-voltage electroshock. Modules are inserted unobtrusively into clothing, with simultaneous GPS notifications transmitted through the KAAVALAN app. Prototype testing under attack-like conditions indicated greater than 90 % accuracy in detecting stress, actuation response time of one second or less, and high user acceptability. The system presented here illustrates a realizable route to smart, multi-layered wearables that integrate countermeasures with contemporaneous notifications, providing more protective coverage than alarm-only solutions while satisfying considerations of usability, safety, and compliance.

title: Deep Learning Architectures for Skin Lesion Classification: A Comparative Study of CNN, Transformer and Hybrid Models
authors: Darshan S P, Karthika R, Rajith S, Sriharish V J, Vishal Srivatsan S, Gurukarthi K C, Gurukumar K C
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Skin cancer is a highly prevalent and potentially lethal cancer, demanding precise and timely diagnosis. This study presents a comparative evaluation of four deep learning architectures applied for the classification of seven classes of dermoscopic skin lesions in the HAM10000 dataset: an InceptionV4-DenseNet201 ensemble, a PatchNet-ResNet50 ensemble, a Vision Transformer (ViT), and a MaxViT-Hybrid model. Model performance was compared according to class precision, recall, F1-score and overall accuracy. The MaxViTHybrid achieved the highest macro-averaged F1-score (0.85) and an overall accuracy of 0.90, demonstrating a good performance over all types of lesions including challenging classes like DF, VASC and AKIEC. The InceptionV4DenseNet201 ensemble achieved the highest overall accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.91)$</tex> and weighted-average metrics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.91)$</tex>. These results indicate that hybrid transformer-based models with spatial feature fusion hold promise for reliable and generalizable automated diagnosis of skin cancer.
