title: “Smart Railway Crossing Gate Automation System with AI Human Detection”
authors: Jothibasu M, Sanjay K A, Vinoditha M, Risha Shafrin S, Nithya G, Saratha R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Railway level crossings are critical junctions that often pose safety hazards and contribute to unnecessary delays for pedestrians and vehicles. This paper presents an ONE GATE ONE TRAIN IoT-based Smart Railway Gate Control System that leverages GPS and LoRa communication technologies for automated and reliable gate operation. The proposed system employs two ESP32 microcontrollers-one mounted on the train to transmit real-time GPS coordinates via LoRa, and another at the gate to receive data, control a servo-driven barrier, and provide visual updates through an LCD display. To enhance safety, an AI-powered webcam is integrated to detect human presence near the tracks, preventing the gate from closing when obstacles are detected. The system also connects to the Blynk IoT platform, enabling remote monitoring and live status updates. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the system ensures accurate train detection, timely gate operation, and enhanced public safety, offering a scalable and cost-effective solution for modern railway automation.

title: Design and Implementation of a Lightweight Intrusion Detection System for Controller Area Network Communication in Automotive Cybersecurity
authors: K Prabu, D. Karthika Renuka, L. Ashok Kumar
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This work proposed a new method to improve intrusion detection reliability and system efficiency. It mainly supports within the vehicular communication networks through structured profiling and deviation scoring. Existing methods are offering detection accuracy difference between 99.2 % and 92.8% with latency variation from 0.85 ms to 1.60 ms. Additionally, the resource usage reaching up to 23 % under varied traffic conditions. However, all those fixed-standard detection approaches mishandle difficulties. Especially with anomaly credit, scalability outside heavy load, and behind performance below dynamic situations. To address all these gaps, this proposed method pertains a covered profiling framework. It is supported by simulation with representative vehicular data for aiming to enhance detection reliability and limit computational cost within the controlled system complexity.

title: Low Noise and High Resolution Micro-thrust Measurement Under Heavy Loads for Space-borne Gravitational Wave Detection
authors: Jiabin Wang, Jianfei Long, Mingshan Wu, Jiawen Xu, Ning Guo, Detian Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The Taiji program is a space-borne gravitational wave detection project conducted in heliocentric orbits, utilizing micro-thrusters for drag-free control to maintain satellites in an ultra-quiet and ultra-stable state. High-precision and wide-band ground evaluation of the thrusters is crucial. As the mass of micro-propulsion systems increases, this imposes requirements for low thrust-to-weight ratios and low noise on micro-thrust measurement devices. This paper develops a flexible inverted pendulum-based micro-thrust measurement device capable of handling heavy loads. With an experimental mass of 8 kg, by adjusting the center of mass to coincide with the center of the flexible element, the system’s elastic restoring force is provided solely by the flexible element, ultimately achieving a minimum resolution of 0.05 μN for the sub-micro-Newton thrust measurement system. The background noise is better than 0.1 μN/Hz−1/2 in the 0.1 mHz to 1 Hz range, Partial frequency point dynamic thrust inversion tests were also conducted, providing a strong ground testing foundation for the engineering implementation of the Taiji program.

title: A Multi-Component Sparse Near-Field Scanning with Peak-and-Trough Searching
authors: Di Wang, Xing-Chang Wei, Si-Yao Tang, Yu Tian, Ming-Jie Pang, Richard Xian-Ke Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In order to improve the efficiency of traditional near-field scanning measurement, a multi-component sparse near-field sampling method is introduced in this article. The proposed method features three strategies: equal-height searching, random distribution, and peak-and-trough searching. This method is capable of effectively detecting both peaks and troughs of near-field distribution patterns, ensuring sufficient accuracy to characterize multiple components of electromagnetic field. Two simulation examples are employed in efficiency validation of the proposed method. Subsequently, the proposed method is coded into a practical near-field scanning measurement system for evaluation using three real-world cases. Compared to the traditional full scan method, the proposed method reduces the scanning time to 26.97% for a wireless router, 25.37% for a WIFI module, and 44.67% for a camera module. Furthermore, the relative errors for all electromagnetic field components of the three devices under test remain below 7%, certifying the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: 750kV Tank Circuit Breaker Closing Resistor Component Structural Design Optimization Simulation Analysis
authors: Dingyun Wen, Yanyan Bao, Kang Liu, Feng Wang, Wei Chen, Yongliang Yao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The closing resistor is a key component in the 750kV tank circuit breaker, primarily responsible for suppressing short-scircuit currents and ensuring the proper operation of the breaker. However, during actual operation, the closing resistor is subjected to significant transient mechanical shocks. After frequent operations and long-term use, mechanical damage such as edge wear and contact surface erosion may occur, affecting the reliability and safety of the breaker. Therefore, this paper conducts a finite element simulation study on the closing resistor component and proposes an optimization solution by installing pure aluminum transition pieces between the resistor plates to improve stress distribution and enhance the mechanical stability of the breaker. This paper, combining simulation analysis and experimental verification, demonstrates the effectiveness and practical value of this optimization design.

title: Contextual Text Analysis for Assessing Reading Comprehension in Digital Education Tools
authors: Hulkar Mirzakhmedova, Shuhrat Mirziyotov, Sakhibakhon Sirojiddinova, Akbar Kodirov, Dilnoza Mashrabova, Gulchehra Niyetova
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The reading comprehension of digital education tools is the area of assessment that can be used to determine the cognitive aspect of learning text. The contextual text analysis will give information on how students receive and process information in the digital learning context. Conventional forms of assessment, including multiple-choice test or matching a key word, do not usually reflect in-depth learning, contextual associations, and the complexity of language interpretation and thus restricts individual commentaries. In a bid to overcome these shortcomings, a Bidirectional Encoder-Decoder Architecture (BEDA) is proposed in this paper. The BEDA model is a two-way process of student responses and captures the pre-contextual and post-contextual information and produces semantic representations to assess the understanding. With the addition of this structure to digital educational programs, teachers have the opportunity to check the level of understanding automatically, give feedback in real time, and change the level of content complexity depending on the specifics of a specific learner. The outcomes of experiments prove that the proposed method increases the effectiveness of assessing comprehension, detects learning gaps, and allows individual learning strategies.

title: Estimating Snapping Shrimps Biovolume: An Improved Simulation-Based Approach
authors: Melike Girgin, Tayfun Akgül, Saif alZahir
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Snapping shrimps (SS) can generate snapping sound pressure level (SPL) exceeding 200 decibels, referenced to 1 micro-pascal (200 dB re 1 µPa) which makes them one of the loudest biological sound producers in the water. Due to its high intensity, the snapping sound interferes with underwater communications, naval sonar calibration and bioacoustics monitoring. This makes SS biovolume estimation indispensable. Recently, researchers’ interest in this topic have been elevated to unprecedented level and many methods have appeared in the literature. Although these methods’ results were satisfactory, but they failed to report on some crucial parameters including the absolute density of SS which exemplifies the real reading of shrimps’ numbers. This paper presents an improved simulation-based method that uses a single underwater HydroMoth recorder that can efficiently detect ultrasonic signals in aquatic environments. A sensitivity analysis was conducted to determine the optimal height at which the HydroMoth is to be placed over the predetermined shrimps’ bed to minimizes undesired shellfish sounds. In addition, we calculated the absolute density of the SS in each location and measured the temperature, salinity, and turbidity at different times. This research is novel in that: (i) it is the first time to estimate SS biovolumes in three different seas surrounding Türkiye in one research; and (ii) this research reports on the absolute density of SS in 17 locations; and (iii) it tests for agreement between the environmental variables and acoustics parameters. Our results established that spatial environmental variability such as salinity, temperature, and turbidity exert a strong influence on the acoustic behavior and detectability of snapping shrimps and it confirmed that warmer, saline, and clearer waters such as those of the Aegean and Mediterranean Seas enhance sound speed efficiently allowing shrimp snaps to propagate over longer ranges with minimal attenuation. Finally, we compared our results with results from 4 published papers and found that the proposed method results are highly accurate, consistent, and more qualified than those in the other published papers.

title: Neural Text Generation Model for Personalized Storytelling in Primary Education
authors: Gulnorakhon Kosimova, Zilola Sattorova, Maqsudali Madraximov, Maxzuna Mamasodiqova, Kdirbayev Qonisbay, Firuza Khayitova
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Individual narration in primary schools is crucial in developing literacy, creativity, and interest among the young learners. The process of adapting the stories to interests and reading levels of a specific reader promotes understanding and motivation during early learning stages. Current digital storytelling techniques are mostly based on generic content or text generation rules that are not personalized and fail to meet the needs of different students and, thus, yield less engagement and less optimal learning. In order to overcome these shortcomings, the given paper suggests a Neural Text Generation Model based on the conditional variational autoencoders (CVAE) to create personalized stories. The CVAE framework can be used to produce contextually adequate and age-appropriate narratives that are conditioned on the profile of the students, such as their reading skills, interests, and learning goals. The suggested model can give both the teachers and the learners dynamically generated story content thus creating interactive and customized learning experiences. Evidence of experiments shows that the model has a great impact on increasing the engagement, comprehension, and motivation of students, when compared to the traditional storytelling methods.

title: Improving Fraud Detection in Banking with Machine Learning Innovations to Secure Financial Transactions
authors: Raji Krishna, D.Jayanthi, Naveen Kumar Maurya, P.Ilanchezhian, A.Umamaheswari, K.Kavitha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The online financial world has seen rapid growth, leading to a rise in fraudulent activities within online banking. With the increasing sophistication of fraudulent methods, traditional rule-based systems fail to provide robust security. Leveraging machine learning (ML) algorithms offers a dynamic and adaptive approach to identifying anomalous patterns in vast, complex banking datasets. As the digital landscape of finance evolves, this study aims to contribute to ongoing efforts to build robust systems capable of identifying and preventing fraudulent activities. We have used class weight-tuning hyperparameters to differentiate between legitimate and fraudulent transactions to improve fraud detection. Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM), Categorical Boosting (Cat Boost), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithms in ML are applied to training data with the use of 5-fold cross-validation to ensure model robustness. Deep learning (DL) techniques are integrated to fine-tune hyperparameters and make them more effective in identifying evolving fraud tactics. Evaluation metrics assess the model’s effectiveness by minimizing false positives. This paper uses a Stacking Classifier, an ensemble ML algorithm, which combines the predictions of base classifiers like Random Forest and LGBM to make the final prediction. This model gave Precision is 0.85, Recall is 0.75, F1 Score is 0.80, and an accuracy of 99.93%. This model outperformed existing methods when evaluating real-world data compared to other methods.

title: Research on Low Frequency Load Reduction of Distributed Photovoltaic Proportion Integrated Grid
authors: Guanyu Zhang, Mingyang Liu, Chenghao Li, Qionglin Li, Xueqing Pan, Di Zhang, Ze Gao, Wei Yao, Shanyang Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Under-frequency load shedding (UFLS) as the third line of defense in power systems is an effective means of preventing system frequency collapse. The grid-connected large-scale distributed photovoltaic power generation will change the frequency response characteristics of the power system, while the traditional UFLS strategy has not taken into account the impact of distributed photovoltaic power generation, which may result in an unreasonable load shedding amount after a large power deficit. This paper mainly proposes one solution which is to redefine the expression form of the under-frequency load shedding index, adopt the new index, and use the existing UFLS round-by-round setting values to ensure that the UFLS strategy effect under the condition of large-scale distributed photovoltaic grid-connection is the same as that without distributed photovoltaic grid-connection and the UFLS strategy effect. The simulation verifies the feasibility and adaptability of the proposed solutions. The results show that the solution is meaningful in practical engineering to the threat of frequency instability caused by distributed photovoltaic power generation.

title: Research on AI Indoor Smoke Warning Technology Based on Image Recognition
authors: Wenjing Wu, Chao Long, Donglin Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Fires usually cause huge and irreversible losses to people's production, life and even their lives. To achieve early warning of indoor fires and detect fire hazards in advance, this paper proposes an AI indoor smoke early warning technology based on image recognition. Video images are obtained in real time through front-end cameras. After preprocessing, suspected smoke areas are identified and extracted. Then, multiple smoke features are extracted and the feature vectors are input into the support vector machine for the discrimination between smoke and non-smoke. Once a smoke area is detected, an alarm will be triggered immediately to achieve automatic fire warning. The superiority and feasibility of the algorithm have been proved through simulation experiments. Compared with traditional fire detection methods, such as those that utilize the particle and temperature characteristics of smoke, detectors need to be placed close to the fire source and a flame must be generated before they can be detected. This paper adopts advanced image recognition technology. Through real-time image analysis, it reduces the cost of detectors and can alarm at the early stage of smoke, which wins precious time for fire control and can minimize the risk of fire to the greatest extent. It has a good application prospect.

title: Cracks Under the Lens of AI: A Deep Learning Review for Smarter Concrete Inspection
authors: Aditi Shah, Shveta Mahajan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Crack detection is a critical component of structural health monitoring for concrete infrastructure. Traditional inspection methods are often labor-intensive, subjective, and inefficient. The emergence of deep learning, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks, has introduced a revolutionary shift, enabling accurate, automated, and robust crack detection. This paper presents a comprehensive review of deep learning-based methods for concrete crack detection, synthesizing findings from recent literature. We examine three primary approaches: image classification for identifying crack presence, object detection for real-time localization using models like Faster R-CNN, YOLO and semantic segmentation for precise, pixel-level crack mapping with Fully Convolutional Networks. The pivotal role of transfer learning in overcoming data limitations is highlighted. Furthermore, we explore advanced applications such as the quantification of crack parameters like width and depth, and innovative methods for detecting internal defects. Key challenges including dataset scarcity, data imbalance, and model generalization under diverse environmental conditions are discussed. Finally, we outline promising future directions, including real-time integrated systems, unsupervised learning techniques, and the development of novel fused architectures for more efficient and comprehensive structural assessments.

title: One Trigger, Multiple Victims: Clean-Label Neighborhood Backdoor Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Huaxin Deng, Yong Fang, Qiang Zhang, Chengwei Liu, Yang Liu, Yijia Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable success in modeling structured data. Recent studies, however, reveal that they are highly vulnerable to backdoor attacks, which can implant triggers into training data to mislead predictions on nodes injected with triggers while maintaining accuracy on clean inputs. Despite recent advances, existing graph backdoor attacks often rely on explicit training interventions and substantial trigger injection while focusing solely on single-node misclassification, which limits their practicality in real-world deployments. To address these limitations, we propose a clean-label graph backdoor attack that induces one-hop neighborhood misclassification under a minimal trigger injection budget. Without altering target nodes’ features or labels, our method attaches a single trigger node to a target node, thereby misclassifying both the target and its immediate neighbors as the target class. To maximize effectiveness while preserving stealthiness, we propose a poisoned node selection strategy guided by semantic consistency and structural activeness, and design a conditional diffusion-based trigger generator optimized with multiple auxiliary objectives. Extensive experiments on multiple real-world benchmarks and mainstream GNN architectures show that our approach achieves over 95% attack success rate on both target nodes and their neighbors in most settings, including under state-of-the-art defenses. These findings underscore the urgent need for more robust graph learning systems and reveal novel attack surfaces in graph security.

title: Research on the Working Condition Recognition Technology of Rod Pumping System Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zhenhua Liu, Junxian Wang, Yuxuan Feng, Shuangye Yang, Guo Biao, Bintao Yan, Li Qian, Zhao Kai, Xiaoyi Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Petroleum resources are a vital strategic resource related to national development. During the oil extraction process in China, pumping units operate in harsh environments for long periods, with complex working conditions and a high risk of equipment damage. Timely detection and understanding of the working conditions of the rod pumping system are of great significance for improving the production efficiency and economic benefits of oil fields. This paper adopts a method based on deep learning for the working condition recognition of the rod pumping system, taking the indicator diagram data of the rod pumping system as the research object and preprocessing the data. Three convolutional neural network models, namely LeNet5, AlexNet, and ResNet, are built for training and recognition. Through analysis, research, and experimental comparison, it is found that the ResNet model has the better condition in the working condition recognition of the rod pumping system, which can better meet the actual requirements of working condition recognition of the rod pumping system. The research shows that the deep learning method has strong recognition ability and good feasibility in the working condition recognition of the rod pumping system.

title: A Hybrid Heterogeneous CNN-LSTM Ensemble Framework for Sentiment Classification via Meta-Learning
authors: Chuqiao Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Accurately classifying sentiment in complex and contextually relevant text remains challenging for traditional machine learning methods, due to their reliance on handcrafted features and limited ability to capture semantic relationships. This study proposes a hybrid ensemble framework that combines a 1D-CNN and a LSTM as base learners and integrates them through meta-learning to improve classification performance. The CNN component focuses on extracting local n-gram features, while the LSTM is used to capture long-term dependencies and contextual information. The probabilistic outputs of the two models are concatenated and input to a meta-learner, including LR, RF, DT, and KNN. Experiments on a Kaggle dataset of 3,613 labeled text samples from four sentiment categories demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms the individual base models. Logistic regression achieves the highest performance with an accuracy of 0.7095 and an F1 score of 0.7100. The results validate the effectiveness of combining deep learning architectures with meta-learning strategies to leverage their complementary strengths for robust multi-class sentiment classification.

title: ELEXCOM 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)

title: Swin-Transformer-Based Atmospheric Turbulence Compensator for Vortex Beams
authors: Aditya Jaiswal, Avishek Kumar, Laxminarayan Laxminarayan, Praveen Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: In the area of optical fields, atmospheric turbulence is a serious challenge in all free-space propagation applications of Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) beams. It severely distorts the beam profile, leading to reduced mode purity, inter-modal crosstalk, and degraded performance in high-speed optical communication, imaging, and quantum systems. To address this, we introduce OAMRestoreNet, a physics-informed deep learning model that restores OAM beams distorted by atmospheric turbulence. It combines fundamental knowledge of beam propagation and turbulence with modern network components like Swin Transformers and attention-based skip connections. Compared to traditional correction methods, OAMRestoreNet offers faster, real-time restoration without the need for bulky hardware. It generalizes well across different turbulence strengths and beam types, making it a practical solution for turbulence compensation in real-world optical systems.

title: Origami-Based Inner Channel Securing Mechanism for Soft Growing Robots
authors: Sanghun Lee, Nam Gyun Kim, Shinwoo Park, Dae-Young Lee, Jee-Hwan Ryu
abstract: Soft growing robots possess unique advantages, such as the ability to navigate confined and complex environments. Although securing a stable inner channel is essential for enabling a wide range of applications, such as sensor integration, tool passage, and material transfer, the pressure required for eversion based growth imposes compressive loading on the channel, causing it to collapse by constriction. Existing approaches for channel stabilization rely on auxiliary actuation or complex control, which introduce leakage, deformation, or limited scalability. To over come these limitations, this paper presents an origami-inspired mechanism embedded into the robot membrane that inherently forms a structurally rigid inner channel without additional actuation, while preserving the intrinsic benefits of soft growing robots. The design further enables user-defined customization of channel geometry to meet application-specific requirements. A comprehensive modeling framework is developed to characterize the geometric and mechanical behavior of the mechanism, and its validity is confirmed through simulations and comparative experiments against conventional soft growing robots. Demonstrations, including inner channel visualization, steering, growth through confined paths, and parameter-dependent scalability, validate the practicality and versatility of the proposed approach.

title: Design of 2D Nanodrum with Suspended Mass for Synchronous Detection of Acceleration Shock and Atmospheric Pressure
authors: Haoran Wang, Lijia Zhang, Zhujie Zhao, Yubo Huang, Yuanlin Xia, Dong F. Wang, Cao Xia, Xiaoyu Liu, Zhuqing Wang
abstract: In the fields of aerospace dynamic navigation and high overload shock monitoring, the detection of acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure plays a crucial role. This work proposes a synchronous sensing mechanism of acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure with high sensitivity utilizing a graphene nanodrum with a suspended proof mass for the first time. The proposed sensing mechanism can convert inertial forces and atmospheric pressure loads into nanodrum deflection, which is then transduced into electrical signals via the piezoresistive effect. Signal separation of acceleration shock (rapidly varying signal) and atmospheric pressure (slowly varying signal) signals is further achieved using a discrete wavelet transform with Sym8 mother wavelet. The finite element simulation results indicate that the designed 2D nanodrum with suspended proof mass has an acceleration sensitivity of up to 1.01192×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup>%/g with linearity of 0.9993, and an atmospheric pressure sensitivity of up to 4.20422×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>%/mbar with linearity of 0.9755. More importantly, after signal separation processing, the measuring accuracy of the acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure signal is up to 93.07% and 99.5% respectively. Macroscopic equivalent experiments were further carried out to verify the feasibility of a sensing mechanism. Experimental results indicate that both acceleration shock and atmospheric pressure can be detected with high linearities of 0.9993 and 0.9755, respectively. The mechanism proposed in this work is expected to provide a solution for synchronous high-sensitivity measurement of acceleration and air pressure in high dynamic environments.

title: Design and Characterization of a Novel MOS Photodetector for Chemiluminescence
authors: Alberto Alejo Moreno-Guerrero, Peter Lomax, Jonathan G. Terry, Ian Underwood
abstract: compact MOS photodetector architecture integrating a vertical optical window and a catalytic micro-chamber has been developed as a proof-of-concept platform for chemiluminescence detection in aqueous media. The vertical trench sidewalls are designed to act as lateral optical windows, promoting photon propagation parallel to the depletion region and facilitating efficient absorption within a miniaturized footprint.This design enables chemiluminescence sensing without external illumination, supporting low-power operation and potential portability. Electrical measurements indicate a rectifying junction with a dark current in the lower nA range, while optical characterization shows bias-dependent responsivity with optimal performance under moderate reverse bias, achieving 0.21 A/W at 470 nm (EQE ∼57%) and a sub-picowatt NEP. Chemiluminescence experiments using luminol confirm stable photon detection, with normalized drift rates on the order of 0.3-1.7 %/min, decreasing to ∼0.02 %/min after operation in aqueous conditions. These results support the feasibility of the proposed architecture as a compact, integrable platform for aqueous-phase chemiluminescent sensing.

title: Thickness Measurement of Silicon Carbide Epitaxial Layer Based on Savitzky-Golay Filtering
authors: Yimeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In this paper, we focus on the thickness measurement of silicon carbide (SiC) epitaxial layers. Firstly, for the ideal scenario where high - order reflections are ignored, a two-beam interference mathematical model is constructed. By analyzing the optical path of the "air - epitaxial layer - substrate" system and combining Snell's law of refraction with the principle of optical path difference calculation, a correlation formula between the epitaxial layer thickness and the wavenumber spacing of extreme points in the interference spectrum is derived. To avoid systematic deviations caused by the single - refractive - index assumption, the Sellmeier equation is introduced to parameterize the dispersion characteristics of SiC in the mid - infrared band. Secondly, a complete computational algorithm is developed to efficiently transform the theoretical model into the ability to precisely analyze measured spectral data. The algorithm integrates key modules: the Savitzky - Golay filter is introduced for data preprocessing to suppress noise, and the peak/valley of interference fringes are automatically identified based on peak significance and minimum spacing criteria. This algorithm is applied to measured data at different incident angles of 10° and 15°. Through independent calculations and cross - validation, the thickness results are obtained: 10.48 μm at an incident angle of 10° and 9.52 μm at an incident angle of 15°, with a relative difference δ = 9.6%.

title: M-AuRA: Mutual Authentication and Remote Attestation over EDHOC
authors: Yuxuan Song, Elsa Lopez Perez, Geovane Fedrecheski, Thomas Watteyne, Mališa Vučinić
abstract: The proliferation of Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices in critical infrastructure requires robust security mechanisms to verify device integrity and trustworthiness. Remote Attestation (RA) is a security mechanism for validating the software and hardware state of remote devices. Existing RA solutions for resource-constrained IoT devices lack comprehensive frameworks for secure attestation channels and focus primarily on local evidence generation without addressing end-to-end security. This paper introduces M-AuRA, a lightweight RA solution that fills these gaps by leveraging the newly standardized Ephemeral Diffie-Hellman over COSE (EDHOC) protocol. M-AuRA seamlessly integrates attestation with authentication, enabling both unilateral and mutual attestation modes while maintaining minimal resource overhead. Our framework specifies how to transport existing attestation mechanisms in parallel with secure communication establishment, providing a complete end-to-end security solution for IoT deployments. We demonstrate M-AuRA’s practicality through implementation on the nRF5340 microcontroller running at 64 MHz, evaluating performance across both software and hardware cryptographic back-ends. In mutual attestation mode, our implementation uses only 4,692 B RAM and 19,350 B flash memory, occupying 0.9% and 1.85% of available nRF5340 resources, respectively. The four-message EDHOC exchange (45 B, 65 B, 177 B and 120 B) enables mutual trustworthiness verification in 10.46 s using a software-based cryptographic back-end, or only 0.43 s with hardware acceleration, consuming 171.43 mC and 7.97 mC of charge, respectively.

title: Design of 60 Steel Rail Surface Damage Recognition and Detection System Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yu Wang, Hetian Mo, Mengting Ying, Jing Liu, Haoxing Chen, Zihao Chen, Jiongfeng Liang, Jinyuan Hu, Bingren Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This study focuses on the demand for surface damage detection of 60 steel rails under the background of expanding railway transportation scale. To address the problems of low efficiency and poor accuracy of traditional manual detection, a deep learning based intelligent recognition and detection system is designed and implemented. Firstly, steel rail surface images are collected under multiple working conditions using an industrial line array camera. The data quality is improved through preprocessing such as grayscale conversion, filtering and denoising, histogram equalization, and normalization, Furthermore, a deep learning model with an improved ResNet-34 as the backbone network is constructed, incorporating deformable convolution to enhance local feature capture capability. The FPN multi-scale feature fusion structure and dual branch output architecture (synchronously implementing damage type and location prediction) are combined to optimize the loss function through weighted cross entropy and Smooth L1 joint optimization. Actual railway field tests have shown that the system has high detection accuracy and low false alarm rate, significantly better than traditional manual features or shallow machine learning methods. It provides reliable technical support for railway track maintenance and has important application value in ensuring operational safety and reducing maintenance costs.

title: Research on the quality and efficiency evaluation model of power full business scheduling based on multi-dimensional feature fusion
authors: Zhang Wen, Chen Yan, Guo Hongbo, Yin Jiangbo, Wang Xuejiao, Chen Liang, Zhou Chun, Zhu Yajing, Zhao Jing
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The existing work order-driven power business dispatching mode has become difficult to meet the requirements of service quality and efficiency assessment. The research has established a dynamic comprehensive evaluation model for the quality and efficiency of all-service power dispatching. It includes three dimensions: the quality and efficiency of overall business scheduling, the quality and efficiency of back-end support, and the quality and efficiency of service implementation. It has established a multi-level comprehensive evaluation index system. Considering the correlation and uncertainty among the indicators. A subjective and objective combined weighting method combining the Analytic Hierarchy Process and the entropy weight method is proposed. Quantify the correlation between each dimension and the overall level of scheduling. Intelligent evaluation overcomes the limitations of independent index assumptions in traditional evaluation methods. It can effectively quantify and evaluate the overall quality and efficiency level of the entire scheduling process. It provides reliable decision support for power enterprises to optimize resource allocation, improve service quality and operational efficiency.

title: Joint Beamforming and Reflection Design for Hardware-Impaired MIMO-ISABC Systems
authors: Bin Lyu, Yongchun Xiang, Zongyuan Deng, Yuxuan Yang, Feng Tian, Abbas Jamalipour
abstract: In this letter, we investigate a multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) integrated sensing and backscatter communication (ISABC) system that accounts for hardware impairments (HIs) in the joint design of transceiver beamforming. In the proposed system, the passive tags serve as both sensing targets and backscatter transmitters and reuse the same backscattered signals for target sensing and secondary transmission to improve spectrum and energy efficiency. Meanwhile, both the base station and the reader are equipped with multiple antennas to exploit spatial degrees of freedom and enhance sensing and communication performance. Based on this setup, we formulate a minimum sensing signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) maximization problem by jointly optimizing transceiver beamforming, sensing signal covariance matrix, and tag reflection coefficients in the presence of HIs. To tackle this non-convex problem with highly coupled variables, we develop an alternating optimization (AO) algorithm that integrates weighted minimum mean-square error, quadratic transform, and successive convex approximation techniques. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly improves the minimum sensing SINR and exhibits strong robustness against HIs.

title: Graph Relation Aggregated Spectral Perception Network for Hyperspectral Classification
authors: Jiaojiao Li, Xinyi Yan, Haitao Xu, Chengyuan Wang, Rui Song, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Cross-domain few-shot hyperspectral image classification (CDFSL HSIC) presents significant challenges, primarily due to spectral variability across domains and the scarcity of labeled target samples. To tackle these issues, we propose a Graph Relation Aggregated Spectral Perception Network (GRASP-Net), which progressively models spectral and structural information from the sample level to the category level. First, an Enhanced Spectral Perception(ESP) module is developed to refine sample-level features by enhancing discriminative spectral cues while suppressing redundancy. On this basis, a Prototype-guided Graph Construction (PGC) module establishes a sample–prototype bipartite graph, integrating geometric neighborhood relations with non-geometric priors to generate compact and robust category-aware embeddings. Furthermore, a Gradient-Aware Metric Function interprets gradients as directional vectors to adaptively refine sample–prototype relations, thereby overcoming the limitations of conventional scalar distance metrics. Extensive experiments on multiple cross-domain benchmarks demonstrate that GRASP-Net achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared with state-of-the-art methods, particularly in scenarios with limited labeled samples and large domain shifts.

title: Humanoid Locomotion and Manipulation: Current Progress and Challenges in Control, Planning, and Learning
authors: Zhaoyuan Gu, Junheng Li, Wenlan Shen, Wenhao Yu, Zhaoming Xie, Stephen McCrory, Xianyi Cheng, Abdulaziz Shamsah, Robert Griffin, C. Karen Liu, Abderrahmane Kheddar, Xue Bin Peng, Yuke Zhu, Guanya Shi, Quan Nguyen, Gordon Cheng, Huijun Gao, Ye Zhao
abstract: Humanoid robots hold great potential to perform various human-level skills, involving unified locomotion and manipulation in real-world settings. Driven by advances in machine learning and the strength of existing model-based approaches, these capabilities have progressed rapidly, but often separately. This survey offers a comprehensive overview of the state-of-the-art in humanoid locomotion and manipulation, with a focus on control, planning, and learning methods. We first review the model-based methods that have been the backbone of humanoid robotics for the past three decades. We discuss contact planning, motion planning, and whole-body control, highlighting the tradeoffs between model fidelity and computational efficiency. Then, the focus is shifted to examine emerging learning-based methods, with an emphasis on reinforcement and imitation learning that enhance the robustness and versatility of loco-manipulation skills. Furthermore, we assess the potential of integrating foundation models with humanoid embodiments to enable the development of generalist humanoid agents. This survey also highlights the emerging role of tactile sensing, particularly whole-body tactile feedback, as a crucial modality for handling contact-rich interactions. Finally, we compare the strengths and limitations of model-based and learning-based paradigms from multiple perspectives, such as robustness, computational efficiency, versatility, and generalizability, and suggest potential solutions to existing challenges.

title: A Closed-Loop Adaptive Gate Delay Control for Fast Dynamic Voltage Balancing of Series-Connected SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yuanxin Li, Zhangyong Chen, Yunfeng Wu, Xiang Wang, Yuliang Dong, Fan Feng, Xiaoyu Zhou
abstract: The series-connection of silicon carbide metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors (SiC MOSFETs) is essential for extending the blocking voltage capability in high-power applications, yet it inevitably suffers from dynamic voltage imbalance during switching transients. Dynamic voltage imbalance originates mainly from discrepancies in device intrinsic parasitic parameters as well as in circuit parasitic parameters, including stray inductances in the power loop and parasitic capacitances to ground. Passive balancing techniques incur additional energy dissipation, while most active strategies suffer from limited response speed or introduce excessive gate capacitance that degrades switching performance. To address these issues, this article proposes a novel adaptive gate delay control (AGDC) method. The proposed technique features a simple circuit that converts the slew rate of the drain-source voltage (vds) into a square-wave signal, from which a time difference is extracted by a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) for adaptive iterative adjustment. Experimental comparisons with conventional gate drive (CGD) and with an existing active scheme confirm the method’s effectiveness in achieving active voltage balancing without compromising the inherent switching speed of the devices.

title: An Improved Grid-Voltage Full-Feedforward Strategy for the Grid-voltage-harmonics Rejection of Grid-connected LCL-type Inverter Based on Padé Approximant
authors: Jinhong Liu, Xiaobing Zhang, Baohua Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: With an increasing deployment of renewable energy generation system, the interaction between inverter-based renewable energy generation system and grid harmonics is inevitable. The output power quality of widely adopted LCL-type inverter, which is the key to achieve power transformation of inverter-based renewable energy generation system, will be deteriorated because of the grid harmonic distortion. Aiming at this problem, an impedance analysis method is adopted to analyze and compare the inherent stability and grid-voltage harmonic rejection capability of inverter with two typical control structure, when digital time delay and grid equivalent impedance are considered. Compared with the inverter with active damping structure, it is revealed that inverter with single-loop structure not only has at least treble in stability range, but also has the advantage in grid harmonics suppression. Therefore, a control strategy, which combines Padé-based optimized grid-voltage full-feedforward and single-loop structure, is proposed for enhancing the stability and grid voltage harmonic rejection capability with a relatively simple control structure. The experimental results are consistent with the theoretical analysis, and desired PWM harmonic reduction and grid-voltage harmonics rejection performance are achieved, even in the face of the variation of digital time delay and grid equivalent impedance.

title: A Novel Solution of Fuse Coordination in Heavy-Load Multi-Wheel Vehicles DC Power Systems
authors: Bin Liu, Shuguang Wei, Jiaqi Li, Dong Yuan, Guangsheng Li, Kewei Chen, Feifan Xu, Hengliang Zhang
abstract: Heavy-load multi-wheel electric vehicles are deemed to have significant potential by their superior fuel utilization efficiency and low-carbon environmental benefits. The DC power systems with relatively high bus voltages are adopted for HLMWEVs and inevitable to faults under harsh operating conditions, which threaten the safety and stability of the system, even lead to equipment damage and personnel injuries. Therefore, it is essential to implement comprehensive fault protection for HLMWEV DC power systems, encompassing both local-level and system-level protection. As means of local-level fault protection, fuses play a crucial role in ensuring the reliability and safety of power system operations. In order to minimize impacts of the fault or to avoid other undesirable situations in the system, fuses should totally clear the fault in the designed time while ensuring uninterrupted operation of healthy feeders. However, due to the flexible topology, diverse operating conditions, and complex fault current characteristics, it is a significant challenge to achieve the selectivity and sensitivity of fuses. This paper proposes a novel approach by means of additional branch inductors and bus capacitors to coordinate fuse protection actions and improve fault response performance. The principle of the proposed approach is introduced and the selection criteria for these components are discussed in detail. By modeling and simulating the DC power system of HLMWEVs, the fault clearing time of feeder protection is analyzed to validate the proposed method. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively enhances the selectivity and sensitivity of feeder protection.

title: Design and Analysis of a Gate Driver Power Supply Architecture for Common-Mode Current Reduction
authors: Seungmin Ha, Jaewon Rhee, Sanguk Lee, Jiseong Kim, Seungyoung Ahn
abstract: High-speed switching devices, including wide-bandgap semiconductors such as SiC and GaN, generate common-mode (CM) current through the parasitic capacitances of gate driver power supplies (GDPSs), which affects switching device operation and stability and contributes to electromagnetic interference (EMI). Existing CM mitigation methods rely on additional passive components, increasing system cost. This paper proposes a GDPS architecture that reduces CM current through a change in the power supply connection method, without adding extra components. The CM current reduction mechanism is analyzed using a high-frequency equivalent circuit, focusing on the effect of wire inductance on CM impedance resonance. The proposed architecture is verified through measurement-based simulations. Compared to conventional architecture, the proposed approach achieves a 25.3% reduction in peak-to-peak CM current and up to a 4.1 dB reduction of the CM current under varying dv/dt conditions, demonstrating a CM current reduction method for high-speed gate driver applications.

title: Index Modulation for Streaming QR Codes
authors: Vijen Sewpersadh, Narushan Pillay, Hongjun Xu
abstract: In this paper, we present a new streaming quick response (QR) code (SQRC) system which leverages index modulation (IM) for higher throughput and spectral efficiency gains called SQRC-IM. We show that the mask bits, angle of orientation and color inversion of a QR code can be used to carry part of the input data. We hypothesize through numerical analysis that version 1 numeric mode QR codes could achieve bit rate and spectral efficiency improvements of up to 4.88% with SQRC-IM. We then prove through SQRC-IM experiments using a simulated and then real screen-camera communication (SCC) channel that version 1 numeric mode QR codes achieve throughput improvements of 1.47% and 9%, respectively. We discuss performance issues related to the processing times for angle of orientation, color inversion, channel setup and video capturing functions and conclude that IM is a useful technique for throughput and spectral efficiency improvements in SQRC systems; however, performance degradation due to inefficient image processing libraries and hardware limitations need to be addressed in future studies.

title: Research on the Design Method of Coil for the Cos-theta High-Field Low-Temperature Superconducting Magnet With Small Round Superconducting Cable
authors: Zhengnan Han, Mingzhi Guan, Wei Wu, Yu Liang, Canjie Xin, Tongjun Yang, Tianfa Liao
abstract: This paper introduces a cos-theta type large-aperture high-field dipole magnet, which is wound with small round superconducting cables (Nb3Sn and NbTi cables). The magnet coils are fabricated by processing grooves on a stainless steel former and winding the cables into these grooves. Compared with the commonly used Rutherford cable, the small round cables can be arranged more flexibly to achieve the required magnetic field. The formers and grooves provide support for the coils and help distribute the stress. Excessive grooves can reduce the magnet's magnetic efficiency, increase the magnet's volume, and raise the amount of superconducting wire used. Conversely, too few grooves can lead to insufficient support for the coils, making them prone to deformation, increasing the difficulty of winding, and raising the risk of stress concentration. This paper describes the coil structure and presents a coil design method based on linear programming algorithms. Various groove schemes, such as ‘all-wide grooves’, ‘all-narrow grooves’, and ‘end-narrow grooves with straight-side wide grooves’ are proposed, with detailed design methods and multiple sets of design results provided. Two-dimensional and three-dimensional finite element models of the coils are established. Based on the calculation results, the advantages and disadvantages of different schemes, as well as their applicability, are discussed from the perspectives of magnetic field design, structure design, and manufacturing process for both Nb3Sn and NbTi magnets.

title: Full-Scale Design of a Superconducting Wireless Power Transfer System for Maglev-Cobra
authors: João Marques, Vitor Caetano, José Alberto, João Murta-Pina, Roberto Oliveira, Hugo P. Ferreira, Richard M. Stephan, José-Ángel Velaz, Belén Rivera
abstract: Inductive wireless power transfer (WPT) offers a promising solution for contactless energy delivery in transportation systems, particularly for magnetic levitation (maglev) vehicles. This paper presents the design and analysis of a mid-range WPT system for the Brazilian Maglev-Cobra train, integrating a high-temperature superconducting (HTS) receiver coil to enhance efficiency. The system employs a Litz-wire copper transmitter coil at the station and an HTS receiver coil onboard the vehicle, taking advantage of the existing cryogenic infrastructure. A finite element method (FEM) model was developed and experimentally validated using a laboratory-scale prototype, demonstrating accurate prediction of coupling effects, efficiency variation with distance and frequency, and quality factor. Based on this validated framework, a full-scale system was designed considering the train's energy requirements and the integration of supercapacitors for onboard storage. The optimized design achieves efficient and reliable contactless charging, demonstrating the feasibility of superconducting WPT technology for future maglev transportation systems.

title: A Portable Multimodal Sensing System for Localized Air Quality and Respiratory Health Assessment
authors: Devansh Shah, Sarfraz Hussain
abstract: Air pollution exposure varies significantly across micro-environments and directly impacts respiratory health, yet conventional monitoring approaches rely on sparse fixed stations that provide city-level measurements without capturing individualized exposure conditions or physiological responses. This lack of personalized monitoring limits the ability to assess how localized pollution affects individual respiratory health in real time. To address this gap, this paper presents LungHero, a portable multimodal sensing system that integrates localized air quality measurement with physiological respiratory indicators for personalized exposure assessment. The system combines low-cost particulate matter and gas sensors with temperature-humidity sensing, pulse oximetry monitoring, and acoustic cough analysis. A weighted Air Quality Index (AQI) is computed from normalized environmental sensor readings, while cough signals captured through a mobile device are analyzed using Mel-spectrogram features and a convolutional neural network to assess respiratory severity. Experimental evaluation demonstrates stable air quality sensing, effective cough classification with 89.44% accuracy, and observable correlations between degraded air quality, reduced <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$SpO_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> levels, and adverse cough patterns. The results suggest that multimodal sensing at the personal scale can provide meaningful insights into respiratory health under polluted conditions, offering a practical alternative to traditional station-based monitoring systems.

title: 75dBm EIRP W-band Electronic 2-D Beam-steering Antenna Integrated with a GaAs Array Transmitter via Quasi-Optical Spatial Power Combining
authors: Viktor S. Chernikov, Artem R. Vilenskiy, Göran Granström, Lars Manholm, Sam Agneessens, Marcus Gavell, Marianna V. Ivashina
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: We present a W-band (92–96 GHz) electronically steerable reflector antenna system for 6G backhaul that cointegrates a GaAs multi-channel transmitter (Tx) with a compact waveguide focal-plane array (FPA) via a quasi-optical multi-input feeding network (QOFN). Unlike conventional architectures that separate power amplifier (PA) power combining and beamforming into distinct modules, or switch a single PA among QOFN inputs, the proposed QOFN simultaneously combines the power of all PAs while synthesizing the required FPA excitation for electronic beam steering. This eliminates a separate lossy switch/combiner stage and equalizes per-channel power, critical for maintaining high Tx efficiency. Methodologically, we develop an active-system modeling framework that extends conventional passive antenna array formulations by incorporating PA output mismatch effects, channel-to-channel variations, and discrete phase-shifter states into the beam synthesis. The formulation yields closed-form realized-gain and EIRP (Effective Isotropic Radiated Power) maximization solutions under realistic PA constraints. To compensate for mast swaying, a rim-extended offset parabolic reflector is co-designed with the FPA, providing ∼ 50 dBi gain with ETSI Class 3 sidelobe mask compliance over a 2-half-power-beamwidth 2-D steering range. A prototype with six Tx PAs shows that across 92–96 GHz and multiple beams, the minimum measured EIRP is ≥ 75 dBm, the on-axis beam reaches ∼ 80 dBm, and the QOFN insertion loss is 2.3–3.7 dB. The results experimentally validate a unified architectural and modeling approach to high-EIRP W-band backhaul transmitters, demonstrating a compact and scalable alternative to large silicon-based phased arrays that typically peak near 60 dBm EIRP.

title: Compact Indoor Setup for Radar Dual-Antenna Characterization Using a Symmetric Lens-Based Spherical–Plane Wave Transformer
authors: Hsi-Tseng Chou, Nai-Yuan Hsu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A compact antenna test range (CATR) is proposed for high-fidelity radar antenna characterization, employing a symmetric lens-based wavefront transformer. Unlike conventional metal-reflector CATRs that suffer from direct coupling and near-field interference, the proposed design separates the antennas and target on opposite sides of a gradient-index (GRIN) lens. This spatial symmetry minimizes electromagnetic scattering, suppresses TX/RX coupling, and improves the accuracy of radiation and sensitivity analysis. The system replicates far-field conditions by preserving spherical wavefronts from the TX antenna and scattered fields from the target, while producing planar illumination toward both the target and RX antenna. This dual transformation ensures high frequency-response fidelity. The concept is validated using full-wave FEKO simulations and benchmarked against reflector-based CATRs, demonstrating enhanced TX/RX characterization accuracy and reduced interference artifacts.

title: A Feature-Driven Analysis of Global Cybersecurity Regulations and their Impact on Safeguarding Data Value
authors: Angelica Marotta, Stuart Madnick
publish: Journal of Data and Information Quality
abstract: None

title: An Integrative First-Year Induction Program for Computing Students: Design and Evaluation
authors: Toni Moilanen, Ville Isomöttönen, Paavo Nieminen, Tiina Koskelainen
