Smart contracts facilitate toll collection, transaction confirmation, and record keeping for V2I vehicle communication via an unbiased blockchain ledger that is immutable. Therefore, the proposed framework creates transparency, tamper-resistance, and data security for the exchange of V2I communication. This work achieves an improvement in communication infrastructurerelated latency and scalability, allowing for applications in many different real-time V2I cases by substituting the communicationintensive nature of POS for the consensus-based approach of existing blockchain systems.

title: Pose-Enriched Text-Image Person Re-Identification Via Cross-Modal Learning
authors: P. Merlin, Paul T Sheeba
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Text-Image Person Reidentification is an emerging area within the field of Person Reidentification and a crucial research domain in Computer Vision. It has extensive applications in public security surveillance. In Text-Image Person Reidentification, each input RGB image is associated with a textual description of the image. Textual Description often comprise of color, appearance, gender and attributes. Existing works utilize Pose information to align the human body parts, which is vital for handling viewpoint and occlusion challenges. This Paper proposes a novel approach by incorporating the pose cues to the Textual description to form a Pose-Enriched Text. The Confidence score of the keypoints and Scoring Algorithm are used to obtain a person orientation and it is termed as Pose Label. Then, the pose label is merged with the textual caption to create a Pose-Enriched Textual Caption. A visual language model, Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP), is used to align Pose-Enriched Text and Image features in a shared embedding space. This aids in matching query text with a gallery of images leading to a significant improvement in TextImage ReID model performance. Our model's performance is evaluated on the RSTPreID dataset, achieving a Rank 1 accuracy of 33.9%, Rank 5 accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 6. 2 0 \%}$</tex>, and Rank 10 accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 6. 5 \%}$</tex>.

title: PR-EWC: Period Replay and EWC for Class-Incremental Maritime Vessel Detection
authors: Hongyan Guo, Junjian Feng, Bing Zheng, Chao Dong
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Traditional object detection approaches tend to perform poorly in dynamically changing environments, as they are typically trained on static datasets that fail to capture real-world variability. However, these methods are particularly prone to catastrophic forgetting—the phenomenon in which learning new object categories significantly degrades performance on previously acquired knowledge. To address this issue, we propose PR-EWC, an incremental learning-based approach for maritime vessel detection. At the parameter level, we incorporate an Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) mechanism, which quantifies the importance of each parameter using the Fisher Information Matrix and applies parameter-specific regularization to mitigate catastrophic forgetting during incremental learning. At the data level, we adopt a data replay strategy, leveraging a periodic replay mechanism to reinforce both newly learned and previously acquired knowledge. Experimental results on the SeaShips dataset demonstrate that the proposed method effectively mitigates catastrophic forgetting. Compared with direct incremental learning using YOLO11n, which achieves a mAP50 of only 23.4%, the proposed approach significantly improves performance, reaching a mAP50 of 79.90% and showing a remarkable enhancement in performance.

title: Real Time YOLOv8 Training Monitor: A Lightweight Diagnostic Tool with Overfitting Detection and Remote Alerting
authors: M. Indirapriyadharshini, Muhammed Roshan S, Kiruthiga A, Ragul S J
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: YOLOv8 is a prominent object detection framework recognized for combining real-time processing speed with strong detection accuracy. However, when trained on limited or imbalanced datasets, it may still encounter overfitting, leading to weak generalization and inefficient use of computational resources. To address this, we introduce a lightweight real-time monitoring tool that dynamically visualizes both training and validation loss metrics. The system detects overfitting patterns, such as a consistent increase in validation loss over multiple epochs, and can issue automatic alerts via the Telegram Bot API. When overfitting persists for five or more epochs, it may halt training to prevent further resource waste. This approach reduces unnecessary GPU consumption, improves model reliability, and integrates seamlessly into existing YOLOv8 workflows with minimal setup cost.

title: The Use of Chatbots in Higher Education: A Bibliometric and SWOT Analyses
authors: Wilber B. Sabado
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The emerging landscape of integrating technology in higher education has provided both benefits and challenges. Chatbot, a popular technology, is used in transforming the teaching and learning pedagogies in higher education. This computer program can communicate with students and teachers using text or voice. Chatbots in higher education can provide personalized learning experiences, do certain tasks, or just provide any assistance 24/7. Similarly, disadvantages are also present. Despite the expanding research landscape, research in this area remains limited in developing countries and the long term effect on the use of chatbots in higher education must also be checked especially in the part of data collection. This research endeavor aimed at identifying the research publication trends in the use of chatbots in higher education using a type of methodology called Bibliometric Analysis. It utilized the Scopus database in investigating the 4329 research papers and performed a SWOT analysis on the top 10 highly cited papers. This study identified an increasing publication trajectory within the last 2 decades (from 2006 to 2025). It also presented which papers have the most citations, the countries where the research papers were produced, the common keywords, and the relationships of the cited references, authors, and keywords. The tools used in this research were Microsoft Excel, RStudio, and Biblioshiny.

title: RT-Pencil: an RT-DETR-Based Algorithm for Pencil Defect Detection
authors: Yiheng Sheng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Industrial pencil defect detection requires identifying slender and low-contrast scratches. To this end, we construct a pencil defect dataset and propose an RT-Pencil framework by introducing a MAD backbone and an MSFA neck into RTDETR. The former captures directional cracks using multishape dynamic convolutions, while the latter aligns multi-scale features and employs large-kernel depthwise convolutions with distribution-driven weight modulation. With these improvements, RT-Pencil achieves a 3.5 percentage point gain in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A P}_{50: 95}$</tex> with nearly unchanged FLOPs, and shows higher medium- and largescale recall as well as lower classification and background false positives in scale analysis and error decomposition. Grad-CAM visualizations further demonstrate that the proposed architecture can continuously cover elongated scratches, confirming its effectiveness for pencil defect detection.

title: Estimation Method of Voltage Collapse Critical Point for Distribution Network Loop Closing Operation Based on Continuous Power Flow Method
authors: Chichao Jin, Jianjun Li, Xinyu Chen, Yingfei Hen, Chao Wang, Yiting Wu, Huibao Mao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The integration of computational algorithms and artificial intelligence has demonstrated substantial benefits in predicting voltage collapse thresholds during the loop-closing process in distribution networks. Guided by this, this paper investigates how to apply the continuous power flow method to distribution network loop-closing processes, aiming to enhance both the precision and efficiency of voltage collapse critical point calculation. The operation of the distribution network loop will change the system topology and operation mode. The stability theory of the power system provides a framework for studying the critical point of voltage collapse, while the continuous power flow method consists of estimation, correction, parameterization, and step control, which can be used to clarify the critical point. When planning and estimating the scheme, ensure that the relevant variables are within the upper and lower limits to determine the maximum load of the system. The Euler predictor is employed to approximate the subsequent solution point, which serves as the initial guess for the Newton-Raphson iterative process. This iterative correction continues until convergence is achieved, yielding the estimated voltage collapse threshold. Experimental findings indicate that after 6 iterations, the convergence index of the proposed method drops sharply-from the magnitude of $10^{-2}$ to approximately $10^{-5}$. Across various loop-closing scenarios, the mean prediction error for critical load power remains as low as 0.97%, the average error of voltage estimation during transient process is 0.08%, with a maximum of 0.11%, and the average error of current estimation is 0.12%, with a maximum of 0.17%. The method has high estimation accuracy and full coverage ability and high reliability of all operating conditions.

title: Research on Active Power Control Strategy for Wind Farm Clusters Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Yanni Kou, Xiaoqiang Peng, Shengqing Hu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel hierarchical regulation strategy for active power allocation in wind farm clusters under wind curtailment conditions. Based on ultra-short-term wind power prediction, a two-layer regulation model is established, including a sub-farm layer regulation model and a unit layer regulation model. An improved genetic algorithm is used to solve the model, optimizing the allocation of active power output tasks for individual farms and units. This achieves coordinated operation of active power output among individual farms and units, increases the scale of wind power integration into the grid, and reduces wind curtailment loss. Taking an actual wind farm cluster as an example, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed strategy are verified.

title: GAN-Based Quantization Table Optimization for Enhanced JPEG Image Compression
authors: Vinoth Kumar B, Madesh N
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The increasing dependence on image-centric applications has created a strong need for compression techniques that reduce storage and bandwidth usage while maintaining visual quality. Although JPEG is widely used, it relies on fixed quantization tables (QTs) that do not adapt to image content, resulting in visible artifacts and loss of fine details at low bitrates. Earlier works attempted to improve JPEG using optimization techniques such as Genetic Algorithms (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Differential Evolution (DE), but these methods involve high computational cost and are unsuitable for real-time applications. Deep learning-based methods like QNet provide adaptive quantization but require large datasets, complex training, and heavy processing resources, which limits their practicality in resource-constrained environments. To overcome these issues, this work proposes a GAN-inspired JPEG approach that does not involve any GAN training or discriminator–generator learning process. Instead, the method adapts quantization behaviour using local image characteristics such as variance and texture strength, enabling content-aware adjustment in a lightweight manner. The approach is evaluated on standard images using PSNR, MSE, bitrate comparison, and visual analysis, and the results show consistent improvement over standard JPEG, especially at 10% and 25% compression levels, providing better visual quality with similar compression efficiency. Thus, the proposed method offers a practical and efficient solution for high-quality JPEG compression without the need for complex deep learning training.

title: Acknowledgements ICTCT 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)

title: Using Vision-Language Models to Detect Anomalies in CCTV Surveillance Videos
authors: Jiho Choi, Sung-Woo Byun, Jong Seung Park, Seung Pyo Hong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Real-time detection of anomalous behaviors in CCTV footage remains challenging due to the rarity of incidents, varied environments, and class imbalance. While large Vision-Language Models like CLIP have demonstrated robust image understanding, they lack temporal modeling needed for video anomaly recognition. In this work, we propose a temporal-aware CLIP-based framework to detect abnormal events in surveillance video. The model integrates a lightweight temporal transformer and a multimodal fusion mechanism that combines visual features with class-specific text embeddings, enabling context-aware video understanding. To address the scarcity of rare events, we augment the training data with synthetic video clips generated using OpenAI’s Sora text-to-video model. Experiments on a public CCTV anomaly dataset show that our enhanced CLIP model outperforms a baseline image-level CLIP classifier, with substantial improvements in precision, recall, and F1-score. In particular, synthetic augmentation boosted the detection of infrequent behaviors like "fight" and "fall down." These results demonstrate the practical benefit of combining pre-trained vision-language representations with generative data augmentation for anomaly detection. We also discuss the contributions of each component and remaining challenges.

title: Starting Transient Analysis and Pre-Charge Resistor Design for Diode-Based or Active Front-End Converters
authors: Anfas Rahman N, V. S. S. P. Kumar Hari, G. Narayanan
abstract: Pre-charge resistors are essential in diode-based and active front-end converters to limit inrush current during DC-link capacitor charging. This paper presents a systematic design methodology, deriving analytical expressions for capacitor voltage, inrush current, resistor power, energy, and transient thermal response using a first-order RC thermal model. The design is validated analytically and through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, demonstrating accurate predictions of current, power, and temperature transients under startup conditions. Additionally, a system-level hardware test is performed on a 50 kVA diode bridge rectifier to verify practical operation. The results provide guidelines for selecting pre-charge resistors that ensure reliable, thermally safe startup.

title: Plenary Keynotes & Tutorials
authors: N/A

title: Research on Intelligent Agent and Automated Decision Support System for Distribution Network Planning Based on Large Language Models
authors: Qingyu Zhao, Mao Miao, Fei Zheng, Xinjie Huang, Xianglong Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This study aims to address the dual challenges of inefficiency in traditional distribution network planning methods and the limited feasibility of purely large language model-based approaches. It proposes an intelligent agent collaborative planning system that integrates the cognitive abilities of large language models with the computational accuracy of specialized simulation tools. By constructing a multi-agent architecture consisting of “task analysis - simulation calculation - decision generation,” the system utilizes retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) techniques to inject domain knowledge. An automated workflow engine is developed on the Apache Airflow platform, enabling end-to-end automation from natural language input to planning output. Experimental results show that the system reduces planning time from an average of 255 minutes with traditional methods to 12.7 minutes, achieving a 95% improvement in efficiency. The technical feasibility of the plans in three test scenarios reached 100%, and the investment cost was reduced by an average of 14.6% compared to traditional methods. Cost optimization in specific scenarios was achieved as follows: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3. 7 \%}$</tex> for distributed photovoltaic integration, 14.8% for load growth, and 16.0% for fault recovery. This study validates the effectiveness of the hybrid architecture of “large language model cognitive guidance + precise simulation tool calculation” and provides a new technical approach for smart grid planning.

title: Smart Community Microgrid Energy Planning Strategy Based on Improved Grey Wolf Algorithm
authors: Xianghu Cui, Xiaohuan Wu, Yumin Hu, Fengru Jia, Haojie Meng, Yunlong Chen, Jinggui Shen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: In order to give full play to the demand response capability of the user side, reduce the user 's electricity cost, and maintain the stable operation of the home microgrid, this paper proposes an improved grey wolf algorithm (DSGWO), which improves the original algorithm through dynamic inertia weight and sine cosine optimization catfish effect. Firstly, the main energy-using equipment of the user is modeled. Secondly, a community and individual price incentive demand response strategy is proposed to regulate the user 's energy consumption. Finally, four experimental scenarios are established, and the DSGWO algorithm is used to solve the problem with the goal of minimizing the total cost. The feasibility of the coordinated scheduling strategy is verified.

title: Multi-Agent Machine Unlearning in Healthcare AI: Neural, Symbolic, and Coordination
authors: Farzeen Basith, Deepti A R, Gousiyabanu R B, H. Meenakshi, Nisarga M R, Kothapalli Deekshitha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: The growth of AI systems in healthcare has led to complex ecosystems where the same patient information affects several interlinked models and knowledge bases. Current system unlearning techniques tackle some of those system components but do not guarantee complete data removal across entire healthcare structures. This paper presents a comprehensive integrated forgetting framework featuring neural unlearning, symbolic knowledge transferring and multi-agent consensus protocols. Our approach enables formal guarantees of privacy using differential privacy mechanisms and cryptographic verification. The framework is validated by two extensive simulation studies: synthetic datasets (over 100, 000 patients and 50 AI systems) and real dataset healthcare arranged scenarios (involving over 12,000 patient records across 8 clinical AI systems). The simulation experiment results imply that 84.7 percent of forgetting accuracy with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex>-differential privacy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon= 0.01$</tex>), 82.3 percent of utility retention and 3.2 seconds of average time to complete the deletion has been obtained. The simulated model is reachable to 35 % in the membership inference attack and has a reasonable robustness against reconstruction attack (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M S E=0.73$</tex>) while maintaining HIPAA compliance. Clinical validation by simulation-based testing using 45 health professionals has demonstrated an acceptance rate of 94% signifying the deployment feasibility.

title: Development and Engineering Realization of a Multi-Mode Control System for an Air–Sea Cross-Medium UAV
authors: Jiayuan Shao, Yunfa Wu, Yonghui Li, Lihua Wu, Jianhua Zhang, Yunlan Xue, Zhiming Li, Aiwei Yi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: As the global energy sector rapidly shifts toward sustainable and low-carbon solutions, offshore wind energy has emerged as a prominent focus due to its high efficiency and environmental advantages. Nevertheless, existing inspection approaches that depend on human intervention or single-domain unmanned platforms are unable to comprehensively examine both wind turbine blades in the air and structural foundations beneath the sea. This inadequacy results in reduced operational efficiency, extensive inspection blind zones, and limited adaptability to complex marine conditions. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes the design and implementation of a multi-mode control system for an air-sea amphibious unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV). By addressing challenges associated with unstable air-water transitions and restricted underwater communication, a flexible multi-mode control strategy is developed to enable seamless switching across heterogeneous environments, improving inspection performance and data transmission reliability. The proposed UAV integrates several key technologies, including a cross-media dynamic control framework, a tilt-rotor coaxial propulsion mechanism suitable for multi-environment operation, an external chain-type tethered optical communication relay for underwater tasks, and a cross-domain intelligent inspection system. Experimental results indicate that the UAV maintains stable operation under sea conditions up to level 3, significantly enhancing offshore inspection efficiency.

title: Deep Skin Net++: An Enhanced Ensemble Framework for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Dadavali S P, Suchitra R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Skin cancer is one of the most prevalent and potentially fatal diseases worldwide. Early and accurate classification of skin lesions is critical for timely intervention. This paper presents DeepSkinNet++, a comprehensive deep learning framework that integrates advanced preprocessing, GAN-based data balancing, ensemble learning, and explainable AI for robust skin lesion classification. The pipeline leverages pretrained CNNs (MobileNetV2, ResNet50, Xception, NASNetMobile), combines predictions through ensemble learning, and provides visual explanations via Grad-CAM. The system is deployed using a Flask-based web interface for real-time inference. Experiments on HAM10000 and ISIC datasets demonstrate superior performance compared to baseline models with 96.8% of result.

title: Guest Editorial—Selected Papers From the 2025 IEEE International Symposium on Circuits and Systems
authors: Hanjun Jiang, Ulkuhan Guler, S. Abdollah Mirbozorgi, Ka-Meng Lei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Circuits and Systems

title: Cross-Modal 3D Gaussian Reconstruction: Fusing Image, Geometry, and Open-Vocabulary Semantics for Large-Scale Real-World Scenes
authors: Yuchen Li, Jing Li, Kai Qin, Haitao Wu, Yin Gao, Banghui Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3D GS) has revolutionized scene representation, efficient photorealistic rendering, and novel view synthesis. However, when applied to complex large-scale real-world scenes, 3D GS faces two major challenges which are geometric errors and rendering artifacts. To address these challenges, we propose FuseGS, a novel cross-modal Gaussian reconstruction framework that seamlessly fuses multi-view images and dense LiDAR point clouds, along with open-vocabulary semantics from large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to achieve more accurate global geometry and realistic renderings than the existing methods. FuseGS introduces three key innovations. First, to overcome low geometric accuracy in image-only methods, we propose a novel LiDAR-guided Gaussian initialization method to ensure strong geometric supervision. Second, to deal with the difficulty of integrating 2D VLMs semantics into 3D space, we propose a novel category-specific dual-cue refinement method to improve semantics and geometry alignment. Third, to remove rendering artifacts caused by dynamic objects, we introduce a novel semantic-based adaptive density control method to effectively maintain static background reconstruction. To demonstrate the robustness of our framework, we evaluate FuseGS using both self-collected data from handheld and Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) platforms, as well as public datasets. Compared to the baseline 3D GS method, FuseGS unifies joint optimization of photometric, geometric and static-aware semantics, resulting in 50% improvement in geometric accuracy and 4.8 dB improvement in rendering quality. The method achieves 67.43% mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) without manual annotation. Ultimately, FuseGS has demonstrated excellent potential for Earth observation applications such as smart cities and autonomous driving.

title: IIoT Attack Detection Using Genetic Algorithm Optimized Neural Networks
authors: Hamad Alblooshi, Kevser Ovaz Akpinar, Mustafa Akpinar
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) is accelerating digital transformation in industrial sectors through connected sensors, edge devices, and data-driven automation; however, this connectivity also increases exposure to cyber threats that can disrupt safety-critical and mission-critical operations. This study addresses the need for effective IIoT intrusion detection by proposing a hybrid model that combines a genetic algorithm (GA) with a neural network (NN), in which the GA optimizes NN parameters to improve classification performance in complex traffic environments. The proposed approach is evaluated on the Edge-IIoTset dataset, a labeled IIoT intrusion detection benchmark that includes normal traffic and multiple attack families (e.g., DDoS, malware, and injection-related attacks), represented by heterogeneous feature types and characterized by pronounced class imbalance. Dimensionality reduction is applied using principal component analysis to reduce feature redundancy and improve learning efficiency. Experimental results show that the GA-optimized NN achieves up to 95% overall accuracy across the evaluated attack categories, with particularly strong discrimination for dominant attack groups. The findings indicate that evolutionary optimization can enhance the performance of NN-based IDSs and provide a flexible optimization framework for IIoT settings, thereby supporting the development of more resilient intrusion detection solutions for industrial networks.

title: LandScope: Embodied Interaction and Situational Experience of Chinese Landscape Painting in VR-Based System
authors: Yihui Cai, Yuewen Zhang, Shuzi Yin, Nan Luo, Yinghe Xiao, Xinyi Fu, Haipeng Mi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: As a unique art form, Chinese landscape painting, with its high aesthetic value and philosophical connotations, has become a precious cultural heritage of the Chinese nation and holds a pivotal position in traditional Chinese culture. However, due to its high aesthetic and philosophical values, Chinese landscape painting poses certain difficulties in terms of comprehension. Moreover, the existing teaching and publicity methods have some problems, which are not conducive to the dissemination, promotion, and inheritance of this cultural heritage. This study developed a virtual reality (VR) system called LandScope, based on the renowned Chinese landscape painting Spring Excursion. Additionally, the four major dimensions and cultural content of Chinese landscape painting were systematically summarized, and an embodied interaction learning framework for landscape painting in a virtual environment was proposed. Based on this knowledge, a series of interactive experience tasks were designed to enable users to understand the cultural knowledge of Chinese landscape painting through embodied interaction in the virtual scene. A user study involving 40 participants was conducted. The results of the user study indicate that the system can provide users with a favorable experience, contribute to enhancing users’ understanding of Chinese landscape painting knowledge, and thus facilitate the dissemination, promotion, and inheritance of this cultural heritage.

title: Advances in Hybrid Modular Climbing Robots: Design Principles and Refinement Strategies_supp1-3678003.mp4
authors: Ryan Poon
abstract: This paper explores the design strategies for hybrid pole- or trunk-climbing robots, focusing on methods to inform design decisions and assess metrics such as adaptability and performance. A wheeled-grasping hybrid robot with modular, tendon-driven grasping arms and a wheeled drive system mounted on a turret was developed to climb columns of varying diameters. Here, the key innovation is the underactuated arms that can be adjusted to different column sizes by adding or removing modular linkages, though the robot also features capabilities like self-locking (the ability of the robot to stay on the column by friction without power), autonomous grasping, and rotation around the column axis. Mathematical models describe conditions for self-locking and vertical climbing. Experimental results demonstrate the robot's efficacy in climbing and self-locking, validating the proposed models and highlighting the potential for fully automated solutions in industrial applications. This work provides a comprehensive framework for evaluating and designing hybrid climbing robots, contributing to advancements in autonomous robotics for environments where climbing tall structures is critical.

title: Effects of fast polarization perturbation on self-starting pathways of a mode-locked fiber laser
authors: Xuecheng Wu, Dongyuan Li, Jiahui Shao, Kaihui Liu, Fengqiu Wang
abstract: The self-starting process of mode-locked lasers is complicated and of stochastic nature. While the final, steady states of mode-locking are well-studied, probing and active control of the transient dynamics leading to mode-locking only began in the last few years. Here, we demonstrate for the first time that a fast polarization perturbation introduced by an intra-cavity photoelastic modulator (PEM) can influence, in a deterministic fashion, the self-starting dynamics, i.e. the sequence of temporal processes, of a polarization-insensitive passively mode-locked fiber laser. Without perturbation, the laser starts via an intrinsic damped relaxation oscillation with a frequency of ~29 kHz. When the PEM is turned on with a moderate peak retardation of 0.2π, the relaxation oscillation process is mitigated and pulled close to the PEM modulation frequency, and at a further enhanced peak retardation of 0.4π, the self-starting transient is seen to bypass relaxation oscillations and directly starts with a Q-switching phase at 42 kHz, in synchronization with the PEM modulation frequency. Our results pinpoint fast polarization perturbation, a largely neglected physical factor, as an effective knob in governing the transient ‘self-starting’ dynamics of mode-locked lasers, and may help contribute to a full and generalized model that describes the dynamic interactions between various ultrafast processes in complex nonlinear systems.

title: Prompt-Driven Lightweight Foundation Model for Instance Segmentation-Based Fault Detection in Freight Trains
authors: Guodong Sun, Qihang Liang, Xingyu Pan, Moyun Liu, Yang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate visual fault detection in freight trains remains a critical challenge for intelligent transportation system maintenance, due to complex operational environments, structurally repetitive components, and frequent occlusions or contaminations in safety-critical regions. Conventional instance segmentation methods based on convolutional neural networks and Transformers often suffer from poor generalization and limited boundary accuracy under such conditions. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight self-prompted instance segmentation framework tailored for freight train fault detection. Our method leverages the Segment Anything Model by introducing a self-prompt generation module that automatically produces task-specific prompts, enabling effective knowledge transfer from foundation models to domain-specific inspection tasks. In addition, we adopt a Tiny Vision Transformer backbone to reduce computational cost, making the framework suitable for real-time deployment on edge devices in railway monitoring systems. We construct a domain-specific dataset collected from real-world freight inspection stations and conduct extensive evaluations. Experimental results show that our method achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$74.6~AP^{\text {box}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$74.2~AP^{\text {mask}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> on the dataset, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and robustness while maintaining low computational overhead. This work offers a deployable and efficient vision solution for automated freight train inspection, demonstrating the potential of foundation model adaptation in industrial-scale fault diagnosis scenarios. Project page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/MVME-HBUT/SAM_FTI-FDet</uri>

title: Contact-Aware Morphology Optimization Via Physically Consistent Differentiable Simulation
authors: Filippo Luca Ferretti, Diego Ferigo, Alessandro Croci, Carlotta Sartore, Omar G. Younis, Silvio Traversaro, Daniele Pucci
abstract: Optimizing robot morphology and behavior remains an open challenge, primarily because hardware parameters affect system and contact dynamics, and naive parametrization can yield physically inconsistent designs and unreliable gradients. We propose a simulation-based co-design approach for gradient-based morphology optimization through intermittent contact by expressing inertial quantities, collision geometry, and contact forces as smooth functions of bounded hardware parameters. This parametrization preserves physical consistency across admissible designs and enables trajectory-level differentiation through contact transitions. The methodology is simulator-agnostic and is instantiated in a JAX-based differentiable simulator to leverage accelerator execution and automatic differentiation. We validate the approach by optimizing a single-leg morphology to reach a target jump height under torque limits. Controlled studies further assess correctness: synthetic system identification recovers ground-truth parameters to numerical precision, cross-engine calibration against MuJoCo reaches MSE below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-5}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and second-order derivatives match finite differences. Compared to genetic algorithms, gradient-based optimization is substantially faster but more sensitive to initialization, while remaining computationally practical for co-design.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Comments and Corrections Corrections to “A Systematic Review on the Development of Transhumeral Prostheses: Structural Design, Control Systems, and Functional Integration”
authors: Paul A. Chasi-Pesántez, Cisne Ortega-Cabrera, Martín León, Jorge O. Ordoñez-Ordoñez, Luis J. Serpa-Andrade, Freddy L. Bueno-Palomeque, Luis F. Guerrero-Vásquez
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, (A Systematic Review on the Development of Transhumeral Prostheses: Structural Design, Control Systems, and Functional Integration).

title: Multi-Granularity Token Learning for Domain Generalized Semantic Segmentation
authors: Muxin Liao, Yingqiong Peng, Yuting Sun, Wenju Huang, Yinglong Wang, Kun Zhan, Yuhang Zhang
abstract: Recent Domain Generalized Semantic Segmentation (DGSS) methods have harnessed vision foundation models (VFMs) to achieve remarkable performance in unseen domains. Two key issues require immediate attention from these methods. First, these methods ignore the learning of contextual information, since VFMs are always pretrained on the image classification task, which means that the frozen backbone of these VFMs struggles to extract contextual information used for dense prediction tasks. Second, these methods focus on enhancing domain-invariant information within the learnable tokens or the features extracted by the frozen backbone (e.g., normalizing their style variances), inevitably losing the effective information. To address these two key issues, this paper proposes a Multi-Granularity Token Learning (MGTL) approach to fine-tune VFMs for DGSS, which uses the visual and text embeddings from the CLIP model for optimizing a set of learnable tokens to learn the domain-invariant information of two granularities, including the domain-invariant contextual and conceptual information. Specifically, first, a contextual relation learning strategy is proposed to assist tokens in capturing and embedding contextual information into VFMs, which is crucial to the semantic segmentation task. Second, a conceptual learning strategy is proposed to acquire instance-level conceptual information for the set of learnable tokens and embed it into VFMs to enhance instance-level domain-invariant information. The proposed MGTL approach is evaluated on multiple DGSS benchmarks, achieving superior performance to recent DGSS methods while maintaining low computational cost in trainable parameters during the fine-tuning stage.

title: The Use of Value Propositions in DevOps Software Product Management
authors: Henri Bomström, Markus Kelanti, Elina Annanperä, Kari Liukkunen, Terhi Kilamo, Kari Systä, Veli-Pekka Eloranta
abstract: Software product management involves overseeing a product throughout its lifecycle to maximise business value. However, with the multitude of choices available, identifying valuable future development options and determining how to pursue them can be challenging. This process requires input from multiple stakeholders, adding to the complexity of the task. Effective communication is essential, especially within a DevOps framework, as it relies on collaboration and information sharing among practitioners in various roles to create value. This article aims to provide a deeper understanding of the collaborative elements involved in value estimation within DevOps environments. Thematic analysis was employed to investigate data from two previous studies as part of a research inquiry. The analysis identified 12 value propositions across five dimensions, which were employed in three stages. The results emphasise the importance of continuous requirements management, especially concerning product request intake and reducing overhead associated with value-driven activities in customer-focused DevOps environments. Moreover, promoting a shared understanding of value and its flow among stakeholders can lead to better decision-making in product management.

title: Towards an FEM Approach to Radial Resistance Prediction in Solder-Impregnated HTS Coils
authors: Raymond Hu, Emily-Kei Brewerton, Sriharsha Venuturumilli, Konstantinos Bouloukakis, Kris Chappell, Nancy Zhou, Xiyong Huang, Rodney A. Badcock, Ratu C. Mataira
abstract: Solder-impregnated high-temperature superconducting (HTS) coils inherit challenges in predicting the coil characteristics due to their complex radial resistive pathways. Unlike insulated magnets, which require active quench protection and management schemes, non-insulated (NI) coils benefit from inherent quench tolerance through radial current bypass, but require advanced thermal-electrical models to predict charging time-constants and quench-tolerant performance under designed operating regimes. This research advances the electrical resistivity characterization of solder alloys at cryogenic temperatures, improving modelling predictions of coil properties. This paper will showcase the methodology utilized for characterizing low-temperature solder alloys, such as InBi, InSn, BiSnAg, and InAg, which enable solder-impregnation at lower reflow temperatures, therefore minimizing tape degradation. Experimental characterization data are validated against existing studies of SnPb solder. Solder characterization data are incorporated into finite element method (FEM) models to accurately describe temperature-dependent electrical resistivity characteristics between tape turns; thus, by ignoring the HTS layer to be insulating, together with temperature-dependent parameters, the radial resistance of coil 1A is evaluated from the model and compared against the experimental results. This work enhanced the understanding of solder-impregnated HTS coils, determining the design process of our future magnet systems.

title: Active Style-Content Dual-Branch Domain Adaptation for Semi-Supervised SAR Object Detection
authors: Xi Yang, Quantao Xie, Yirong Yang, Nannan Wang
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images offer unique advantages in all-weather, all-day remote sensing, but the high acquisition costs and time-consuming annotation processes limit their widespread implementation. Semi-supervised domain adaptation leverages abundant annotated optical images and a small number of labeled SAR images to achieve great performance on SAR images. However, existing semi-supervised domain adaptation object detection methods typically select SAR domain labeled samples randomly, making it difficult to fully exploit the valuable information and distinctive features inherent in the target domain data. Moreover, there is a significant style and content gap between optical and SAR images, and previous methods have not adapted to them in a task-specific manner. To this end, this paper proposes an active style-content dual-branch domain adaptation method specifically designed for semi-supervised object detection in SAR images. The proposed approach employs Task-aware Active Sampling (TAS) module to select the most valuable SAR samples, addressing inefficiencies in random sampling. Also, we employ a dual-branch framework to address the style and content gaps between optical and SAR images. Multi-layer Feature Alignment (MFA) module ensures style alignment by maintaining consistent feature representations across different visual styles, while Gaussian-SAM Image Fusion (G-SIF) module is employed to integrate content from the source domain into the target domain, effectively bridging the gap between optical and SAR images. Extensive experiments on multiple ship and aircraft datasets demonstrate the exceptional generalization capabilities of our proposed model.

title: Optimization of Sulfonated Poly(Arylene Ether Ketone) Electrode Ionomer Toward Improving the Performance and Durability of Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cells
authors: Haidan Lin, Chunbo Liu, Xuefeng Li
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs) can achieve excellent output power and long-term durability through rational design of electrode ionomers; however, the structure–property relationships of hydrocarbon-based ionomers remain insufficiently understood. In this work, sulfonated poly(arylene ether ketone)s (SPAEKs) with systematically varied degrees of sulfonation (DS, 0.6-1.4) and molecular weights (10-140 kDa) were synthesized and evaluated as cathode ionomers in fully hydrocarbon membrane electrode assemblies (MEAs). Owing to their excellent solubility of SPAEKs in water/alcohol mixed solvent, homogeneous catalyst inks were readily prepared and uniformly sprayed onto membranes. The electrochemical performance and stability of the resulting MEAs were systematically investigated. Among them, the SPAEK ionomer with a DS of 1.2 exhibited the highest peak power density in both direct methanol fuel cell and H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/ air PEMFCs, endowing 92 mW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and 514 mW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, respectively. The molecular weight of 90 Kg/mol for ionomers was beneficial for improving electrode microstructure and electrochemical performance in PEMFCs. This study highlights the practical potential of tailored SPAEK ionomers for applications in PEMFCs with high output power and long-term stability.

title: The Evolution of Digital Cultural Heritage Towards Human-Centered Storyliving Virtual Worlds
authors: Eleftherios Anastasovitis, Georgia Georgiou, Spiros Nikolopoulos, Ioannis Kompatsiaris
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: In the last fifteen years the evolution of immersive and emerging technologies had a high impact in creative and cultural heritage sectors. Initially, 3D graphics applications evolved into experiences of varying degrees of immersion, depending on the type of extended reality used. The evolution of graphics and computer technology shapes the digital transformation of cultural heritage experiences. This study offers, through four indicative use cases of virtual reality applications, the evolution of digital culture over the last decade. Starting from the 3D application of the virtual anthropology museum, it moves on to the highly immersive experiences of the Scladina cave and the immersive experience of the Virtual Palace of Aigai, supported by enhanced gamification. The research culminates in the innovative storyliving virtual museum EPANASTASIS-1821 and its initial evaluation. At the same time, it highlights future trends in the transformation of digital culture in the light of virtual worlds.

title: Graph-Based Lexical Network for Enhancing Vocabulary Acquisition in Language Curricula
authors: Luiza Fatkulina, Gulchehra Yusupova, Asliddin Isomiddinov, Feruza Erkulova, Gulnoz Samandarova, Nargiza Babaniyazova, Saodat Shamshaddinova
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The vocabulary learning is an important part of the language learning process, and the proper organization of the lexical knowledge contributes to the understanding and expressing. The graph based lexical networks provide a structural method of displaying the relationships between words in the curriculum. Nevertheless, the old techniques usually depend on memorial practice or word lists, which restrict a contextual interpretation and the durability of remembering. To solve this issue, the presented framework utilizes Knowledge Graph Embedding (KGE) to find semantic, syntactic, and pragmatic relationships between words in a multidimensional space. The capability of inserting the lexical items into a graphic format also allows the learners to navigate dynamically between related terms by exploring the interconnected concepts. The approach facilitates the accommodation of language education programs in individual learning plans, adaptive activities, and semantic grouping. The results of experimental testing show that the strategy enhances retention, speeds up language growth, and enhances using language in context, as opposed to traditional instructional activities.

title: Portable Near-Infrared Non-Invasive Imaging Tool for Early Detection of Oral Potentially Malignant Disorders
authors: R. A. I. A. Rajapaksha, Krishnasamy T., K. A. K. D. J. P. Jayasuriya, Uditha L. Wijewardhana, Umaya Balagalla, Sajith Edirisinghe
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Oral potentially malignant disorders (OPMDs) are established precursors to oral cancer, and early identification greatly improves treatment outcomes. This study introduces the design, development, and preliminary validation of a portable, low-cost, and non-invasive near-infrared (NIR) imaging device tailored for point-of-care OPMD screening in resource-limited settings. The system combines a 940 nm NIR light source, a compact USB endoscope camera, an ESP32-C3 microcontroller, and a VL53L0X time-of-flight (ToF) sensor to maintain a consistent 2–3 cm imaging distance. Image acquisition is supported by a Python-based Flask/OpenCV pipeline that performs real-time illumination correction (Gaussian blur subtraction), local contrast enhancement using contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), adaptive statistical thresholding, and morphological filtering, ultimately generating intuitive heatmaps of potential lesion sites. Bench tests confirmed stable hardware performance, robust distance control, and more than two hours of uninterrupted battery-powered operation. Initial qualitative evaluation by clinicians indicated enhanced lesion visibility in processed images compared to raw captures. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed device as an affordable and effective screening tool, with future work directed toward large-scale clinical trials and integration of machine learning for automated diagnosis.

title: Differential Capacitance-Based Flow Transducer Using Dual Semicylindrical Sensors at Pipe Constriction
authors: Anamika Lata, Praveen Maurya, Rajan Sarkar, Nirupama Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and experimental validation of a cutting-edge non-contact capacitive flow sensor that features a semi-cylindrical electrode design for accurate and reliable flow measurement. The sensor operates on the principle of capacitance change caused by flow-induced displacement, enabling a direct correlation between capacitance variation and flow rate. To enhance sensitivity and minimize the effects of environmental factors like temperature and pressure fluctuations, a differential capacitive setup was employed. The model incorporates copper electrodes mounted externally on a dielectric pipe and was tested with water as the working medium across a flow range of 1–60 LPM. The experimental findings confirmed a nonlinear yet highly repeatable response, achieving a maximum repeatability error of just 1.28% and an overall accuracy of ±2.07%. Additionally, the sensor demonstrated resilience against transient flow disturbances, showcasing its potential for multifunctional applications.

title: Efficient Solar Water Pumping System for Wastewater Management
authors: Sarathkumar Duraisamy, Prasanth Chenniyappan, Suhash Chandrasekaran, Tamilkumaran Nagaraj, T P Saravanan, Arun Shunmugam D, P. Manikandan, Brindha G, Sivakumar Annamalai, J.Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a solar-driven wastewater pumping system aimed at improving energy efficiency and stability in water management applications. The system operates traditional grid-powered pumps by PV panels with a renewable, autonomous solution. A 12V solar panel, managed by MPPT charge controller and boost converter, charges a 12V battery that serves as a primary energy source. An arduino mega 2560 microcontroller makes the original of the control unit, the real -time data from the sensor, a voltage sensor, the automatic water level for pump operation based on the real -time data from the sunlight and dust sensor. The system uses a two-level water sensor configuration to determine when the sewage tank requires pumping. When detecting a full tank, Arduino activates the main pump and continues until the tank is evacuated at a safe level. A single-axis solar tracking mechanism adjusts panel orientation using a servo motor, which increases energy harvesting throughout the day. To maintain optimal panel efficiency, the dust detection sensor triggers a cleaning pump when soiling is detected. Additionally, the voltage sensor batteries monitors health and enables automated source to switch to grid power when the battery level comes below a limit, which ensures uninterrupted operation. There is also a dust sensor for environmental monitoring in the system and 20x4 LCD for real -time performance of the operational status. Protective components such as diode, fuses and voltage regulators have been included to increase safety and circuit stability. Integration of relay enables intelligent load control and smooth switching between power sources and pump functions. This intelligent configuration reduces the manual power.

title: A Novel MEMS Piezoelectric Microphone for Multiband Acoustic Sensing
authors: Zhuoyue Zheng, Chen Wang, Michael Kraft, Rui P. Martins, Pui-in Mak, Yuan Wang
abstract: This article presents a novel multiband piezoelectric microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) microphone. Equipped with a fully clamped membrane and a closed-loop frequency-tracking system, the proposed microphone achieves acoustic signal detection in both infrasonic and audible frequency bands (with an experimentally verified bandwidth of 0.1 Hz–3.8 kHz). Key innovations include: 1) concurrent infrasound and audio-band operation via a single structural design; 2) piezoelectric actuation-enabled mechanical modulation of acoustic signals to support frequency and amplitude modulation (FM/AM) readout in the low-frequency regime; 3) an optimized electrode pattern tailored for low frequencies to enhance FM/AM sensitivities; and 4) cost-effective performance realized using standard piezoelectric MEMS processes. Experimentally, for audible detection, the microphone attains a baseline sensitivity of −52 dB (re 1 V/Pa) and a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 61.1 dB. For low-frequency detection, it exhibits an FM sensitivity of 109.3 mHz/Pa and an AM sensitivity of 51.1 <inline-formula content-type="math/tex" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX" version="MathJax">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>V/Pa, achieving 2.7<inline-formula content-type="math/tex" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX" version="MathJax">$\boldsymbol{\times}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and 2.2<inline-formula content-type="math/tex" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX" version="MathJax">$\boldsymbol{\times}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> SNR improvements over the unmodulated signal at 20 Hz. With customized configurations, the FM and AM sensitivities are further enhanced to 1198.9 mHz/Pa and 457.6 <inline-formula content-type="math/tex" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX" version="MathJax">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>V/Pa, respectively. The FM enhancement stems from reallocating the original sensing electrode to drive the membrane on the same device, while the AM improvement relies on the revised electrode pattern. These findings demonstrate the device’s great potential as a solution for wearable devices targeting both voice capture and biometric monitoring via subsonic and low-frequency signatures, including heart rate, respiration, and facial movements.

title: From Image Synthesis to Digital Twins: A Paradigm Shift in AI-Driven Medical Image Computing
authors: Xuhang Chen, Changhong Jing, Zhuo Li, Yanyan Shen, Guoheng Huang, Yan Liu, Baiying Lei, Chi-Man Pun, Shuqiang Wang
abstract: The integration of generative artificial intelligence (AI) in medical imaging represents a transformative paradigm shift from traditional discriminative models to sophisticated data synthesis frameworks, ultimately advancing toward the vision of patient-specific Digital Twins. This comprehensive review examines the technical evolution, clinical applications, and future perspectives of generative AI in medical image generation. The review categorizes clinical applications into three domains: (1) diagnostic enhancement through image quality improvement and cross-modal synthesis, (2) pathology simulation and prognostic prediction via longitudinal disease modeling, and (3) medical research and education through privacy-preserving data augmentation. We identify critical challenges impeding clinical translation, including limited evaluation metrics that fail to capture clinical validity, fairness and robustness issues arising from biased training data, lack of clinical interpretability in black-box models, and persistent privacy and security concerns. Looking forward, we envision a future generative medicine ecosystem built on three pillars: multi-omic holographic Digital Twins integrating imaging with genomic and clinical data, enhanced human-machine collaboration paradigms, and comprehensive regulatory frameworks. This review provides essential insights for researchers and clinicians working to harness generative AI’s potential while addressing the fundamental challenges required for its responsible clinical deployment.

title: Machine Vision Technology Empowers AI-Powered Ship Target Recognition Algorithms for Maritime Navigation
authors: Xiandong Zhao, Yonggang Zong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of fog, rain, sudden changes in nighttime illumination, and low recognition accuracy for small ship targets in maritime scenarios, a machine vision ship target recognition algorithm based on the Sea State Adaptive Dynamic Attention Network (HADAN) is proposed. This algorithm utilizes a three-layer architecture: multimodal preprocessing, sea state-adaptive feature extraction, and multi-scale fusion recognition. First, the algorithm optimizes visible and infrared image quality through improved CLAHE and wavelet transforms. A dynamic attention module is then constructed, combining real-time visibility and wave height parameters. Feature weights are dynamically adjusted to suppress background interference. Finally, multi-scale feature fusion is used to enhance the representation of small ship targets. Experiments conducted on a dataset of 12,000 multimodal images collected in the Bohai and Yellow Seas (covering four scene types: clear, foggy, rainy, and night), comparing ResNet50, ViT, and FusionNet algorithms, revealed that HADAN achieved a test set accuracy of 96.8%, a 7.5 percentage point improvement over ResNet-50. In foggy conditions (visibility ≤ 500m), the accuracy reached 93.5%, with a small target miss rate of only 4.8%, outperforming FusionNet by 4.2 and 4.7 percentage points, respectively. The inference speed reached 28.6 FPS, meeting the real-time requirements of maritime navigation. The experiments demonstrated that HADAN effectively addresses the challenges of ship recognition in complex sea conditions and provides reliable support for target perception in autonomous maritime AI systems.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Detection Algorithm for Athlete Movement Recognition
authors: Kai Zhang, Jia Zhang, Liang Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a convolutional recurrent neural network (ST-ACGRU) based on a spatiotemporal fusion attention mechanism to improve the accuracy and efficiency of athlete movement recognition. First, the characteristics of athlete movement data are analyzed. Multi-dimensional metrics such as sit-and-reach and grip strength from uploaded data are combined to provide a basis for algorithm design. The algorithm utilizes a convolutional neural network to extract spatial features of the movements, capturing their spatial structure. A gated recurrent unit (GRU) is used to extract temporal features of the movements, processing their sequential variations. Furthermore, an attention mechanism is introduced to adaptively focus on key movement features. In the experimental simulation, a dataset is constructed using athlete test data. After normalization and other preprocessing, it is divided into training, validation, and test sets. The experiments are conducted in a specific hardware and software environment, with appropriate training parameters. The ST-ACGRU algorithm is compared with classic CNN and RNN algorithms, using precision, recall, and F1 score as evaluation metrics. The experiment constructed a dataset based on 48 samples from four scenes featuring 12 athletes (reduced to 102 training samples after Min-Max normalization and data augmentation). The performance of the four-class classification task was compared with that of simple CNNs and RNNs on an Intel i7-12700K and RTX 3090. The results showed that the ST-ACGRU achieved 100% accuracy on the test set, a 25 percentage point improvement over the CNN (75%) and a 12.5 percentage point improvement over the RNN (87.5%). Both the macro recall and macro F1 values were 100%, and the validation set loss remained stable during training (less than 0.1 difference from the training set loss). The model's generalization ability was superior to that of the competing models, providing technical support for accurate athlete movement recognition.

title: Study on Inspection Path Planning of Warehouse Robots Based on Deep Learning
authors: Pan Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Aiming at the defects of the traditional Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithm in warehouse robot inspection path planning, such as slow convergence speed, easy falling into local optimization, and difficulty in adapting to dynamic warehouse environments, this paper proposes an improved ACO algorithm integrated with deep learning, which makes it suitable for the inspection path planning of warehouse robots in complex warehouse scenarios with multiple shelves and dynamic obstacles. The Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) framework is introduced to optimize the core mechanism of the algorithm: the initial path generated by the Probabilistic Roadmap Method (PRM) combined with the prediction results of the deep neural network is used to construct the initial pheromone distribution model of the ACO algorithm, increasing the convergence speed by more than 30%; a hybrid heuristic function integrating path length, inspection coverage rate and direction continuity is designed, and the weight parameters are dynamically adjusted through the Q-learning algorithm to effectively avoid the trap of local optimization; an obstacle density perception module based on Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is embedded to replace the traditional heuristic function, realizing the accurate identification and avoidance of warehouse obstacles such as shelf gaps and temporary stacks; the cubic B-spline curve is used to further smooth and optimize the planned path, and the corner cost function is combined to reduce the number of steering times of the robot and lower mechanical losses. Simulation experiments show that in warehouse grid maps of different scales from 10×10 to 50×50, the improved algorithm has an average reduction of 42% in the number of iterations and 28% in the number of corners, and still maintains 91% path stability in the multi-robot collaborative inspection scenario. This algorithm provides technical support for the efficient inspection of intelligent warehouse robots and has important engineering application value.

title: Research on Precise Electricity-Carbon Measurement and Uncertainty Quantification Based on Spatiotemporal Differences
authors: Fei Lin, Siyuan Liang, Wenqi Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: With the construction of the new type of power system, the large-scale integration of renewable energy has led to increasingly complex spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of power flow and carbon flow in the system. Traditional carbon accounting methods often use fixed regional average emission factors, ignoring the spatiotemporal variability of power flow and the uncertainty of measurement data, which results in deviations in the allocation of carbon responsibility on the user side. This paper proposes a method for assessing the uncertainty of electricity carbon accounting based on an improved carbon emission flow theory combined with the Monte Carlo method (MCM). First, a calculation model based on the network-wide carbon flow balance equation is constructed to analyze the spatiotemporal transmission mechanism of carbon flow in the grid. Next, the Monte Carlo simulation method is introduced to quantify the impact of random errors in input parameters on node carbon potential and carbon accounting results. Finally, the effectiveness of this method is verified using the IEEE 14-bus standard case. The research results show that this method can effectively reveal the spatiotemporal evolution pattern of carbon emissions and provide a range of accounting results with confidence intervals, offering theoretical support for precise carbon accounting in the electricity market.

title: A Comprehensive Study on Thermal Effects, Management and Optimization in Electric Vehicle Motors and Testing Dynamometers
authors: Anandu S Nair, Bobin Saji George, Kanthi Mathew K, Boby Philip
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicle (EV) technologies requires improved performance, efficiency, and safety, all of which are greatly impacted by thermal effects in powertrain components and the systems used to evaluate them. The objective of this study is to thoroughly evaluate the most recent approaches for controlling and improving thermal conditions in EV motors and testing dynamometers. This multifaceted approach includes thermal modeling, experimental research, and complex cooling structures such as liquid cooling, improved air-cooled rotors, and hybrid waste heat recovery systems. The study found that sophisticated cooling techniques, such as integrated CO2 heat pump and phase change material (PCM) systems, housing and shaft water jackets, and lumped parameter thermal networks (LPTNs), significantly improve thermal stability and energy efficiency. Furthermore, dynamometer systems that use optimal airflow methods and model-based liquid cooling control improve temperature regulation, test accuracy, and allow for longer operation under high thermal loads. This report provides a road map for future research and industrial applications by compiling the most recent advances and industry best practices.

title: FPGA Implementation of a Pipelined RV32IM Processor with Dynamic Branch Prediction
authors: Maharishi Sonwani, Rahul Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: As fields like AI and robotics continue to expand, the demand for processors that are both powerful and energy-efficient is growing. This imposes the need for the development of such processors at a large scale and at a faster rate, which is possible through open-source development. While the current computing industry is dominated by proprietary technologies and licensed architectures, open-source hardware is rapidly gaining popularity across all industry segments, from high-performance computing to edge-IoT applications.This paper presents the design and FPGA implementation of a 32-bit five-stage pipelined processor core based on the RISC-V open-source Instruction Set Architecture (ISA). The proposed design presents a simple implementation of the RISC-V core, incorporating a 2-bit branch predictor. This makes it easier for beginners to understand the fundamental architecture of a pipelined RISC processor and branch predictors. Additionally, the design has considerable potential for future upgrades. The core is developed using SystemVerilog and has been tested through simulations in Verilator and Xsim, as well as by deploying it on a Basys3 FPGA board.

title: Research on the Application of Reinforcement Learning in Intelligent Translation and Spoken Language Training Systems
authors: Yue Hai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient translation accuracy, delayed spoken language assessment, and poor algorithm adaptability in traditional intelligent translation and spoken language training systems, this paper proposes a dynamic reward-aware reinforcement learning algorithm (DRP-RL) and applies it to intelligent translation and spoken language training systems. The DRP-RL algorithm innovatively constructs a dynamic reward function that dynamically adjusts reward weights based on multi-dimensional metrics such as translation accuracy, fluency, and spoken pronunciation standardization, combined with real-time task feedback. It also integrates a deep learning model to enhance language feature extraction capabilities and optimizes agent decision-making using a policy update approach combining temporal difference and Monte Carlo. To validate the algorithm's performance, an experimental environment was established and simulation experiments were conducted on the WMT2023 translation dataset and the L2-Arctic spoken language dataset. The algorithm was compared with DQN, A2C, mainstream Transformer models, and the Google spoken language assessment model. Results show that in intelligent translation tasks, the DRP-RL algorithm achieved a translation accuracy of 92.3% for the English-Chinese language pair, a 6.7 percentage point improvement over DQN, and a BLEU score of 41.2, surpassing A2C's 35.8. In spoken language training tasks, its pronunciation assessment error rate was only 3.2%, and its error localization accuracy was 89.5%, representing a 1.9 percentage point reduction and a 5.8 percentage point improvement, respectively, over the Google spoken language assessment model. Furthermore, the algorithm converged 33.3% faster than DQN, and its computational efficiency approached that of the Transformer model. This research confirms that the DRP-RL algorithm can effectively improve the performance of intelligent translation and spoken language training systems, providing new insights into the application of reinforcement learning in language processing.

title: Agent-Based Dynamic Vehicle Routing in Urban Logistics under Real-time Traffic Conditions
authors: Zhehao Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Urban logistics systems increasingly operate in environments characterized by dynamic traffic conditions and high levels of uncertainty, which pose significant challenges to traditional vehicle routing methods. Conventional centralized routing models often rely on static assumptions and lack the flexibility required to respond effectively to real-time traffic changes. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an agent-based dynamic vehicle routing model that incorporates real-time traffic information into decentralized decision-making processes. The proposed model allows individual vehicles to function as autonomous agents that adapt their routing strategies based on continuously updated traffic data. Through distributed decision-making and adaptive coordination, the model enhances system responsiveness and reduces inefficiencies caused by congestion and unexpected traffic fluctuations. Compared with centralized optimization approaches, the agent-based framework offers greater flexibility and scalability, making it more suitable for complex urban logistics environments. Simulation-based experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of the proposed model under dynamic traffic scenarios. The results indicate that the model improves travel time estimation accuracy and overall system efficiency, particularly in situations involving frequent traffic changes. These findings suggest that integrating agent-based modeling with real-time traffic information provides a practical and effective approach for improving urban logistics performance in uncertain and dynamic environments.

title: Robust Spectral Signal Processing-Based Framework for Infrared Interferometry-Driven SiC Epitaxial Thickness Estimation
authors: Yibo Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper addresses simulation-based parameter estimation for epitaxial-layer thickness from infrared reflectance spectra and proposes a hybrid computational framework that integrates a double-beam interference model, a robust signal-processing pipeline, and a Fabry–Perot regime detector. We design a data workflow with three-sigma outlier removal, moving-window smoothing, and an integer-order constraint derived from adjacent extrema to avoid direct order solving; when multi-beam interference is detected (FWHM < 3 micrometers and sharpness > 30), we switch to a multi-beam fitting module to debias thickness estimates. On two silicon-carbide datasets measured at different incidence angles, the framework yields mean thicknesses of 18.5987 and 17.1213 micrometers with standard deviations of 0.4615 and 0.6202, achieving relative standard deviations below 5% and a cross-angle discrepancy of about 8.27%; for silicon samples the estimated means are 11.6348 and 11.5518 micrometers. After multi-beam correction, the silicon-carbide thicknesses adjust to 21.6914 and 21.1064 micrometers with cross-angle discrepancy reduced to 2.73%, demonstrating robustness and practical accuracy. The result is a deployable, generalizable computational solution for optical metrology in manufacturing settings.

title: Anti-Collision Algorithm for RFID Tags Based on Deep Learning
authors: Ye Yang, Haowei Zhang, Ziyuan Lu, Dongbi Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: RFID technology finds extensive application across diverse domains including manufacturing, logistics, and beyond. Yet, as the demand for information management grows, upon simultaneous data transmission from multiple tags to a reader, tag collision events emerge, degrading the system's identification efficiency. Addressing this challenge, the core task involves precisely predicting the number of tags to be identified and dynamically optimizing frame length through adaptive mechanisms. This approach aims to mitigate collision impacts by tailoring communication frames to real - time tag quantities, thereby enhancing the system's throughput and reducing unnecessary slot consumption. By leveraging advanced neural network architectures (as explored in the proposed framework), the solution integrates multi - modal feature extraction (via CNN, RNN, and Transformer components) to model tag - slot interactions, enabling accurate quantity estimation and frame adjustment. This not only resolves collision issues but also improves the overall robustness of RFID - based systems in handling large - scale tag environments, aligning with the evolving needs of modern industrial and logistical information management. In this paper, this paper proposes an improved anti-collision algorithm based on deep learning for Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) systems. This algorithm combines convolutional, recurrent, and transformer-based encoder modules to construct a neural network, thereby enabling precise estimation of the number of tags. By introducing a tag grouping mechanism, the system can dynamically adjust the frame length based on the predicted number of tags.

title: SSNeT: A Lightweight Residual Network for Single Image Super Resolution
authors: Prathibha P G, P.S Tamizharasan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: In recent years, Convolutional Neural Networks enabled notable advancements in the domain of single-image super-resolution. However, the high computational demands and resource requirements of most of the previous research have hindered their feasibility for deployment in real-time applications. In this article we introduce a single image super-resolution model that explores lightweight residual block and attention mechanism. We designed a lightweight residual block based on Sandblock architecture, which incorporates an inverted residual block, resulting in an hourglass-shaped structure. An attention module is integrated inorder to enhance the features extracted by the residual block. The modules provide extensive and refined feature extraction with very less number of parameters The proposed approach yields an improvement of 0.48db from the second-best performing model on Set14 dataset and enhanced performance on other datasets

title: Neural Machine Translation Technique for Interpreting Ancient Texts in Philology Education
authors: Hulkar Mirzakhmedova, Shuhrat Mirziyotov, Gavharoy Kholisova, Islomjon Umrzaqov, Yana Kuchkarova, Nargiza Kadirova
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) can be used as a transformative potential in the education of philology since it allows the automatic interpretation of ancient texts. The method contributes to the maintenance of linguistics and greater availability of classical knowledge. The methods that exist are, however, usually limited by certain problems, including limited parallel corpora, semantic ambiguity, and transfer of knowledge between poorly resourced ancient languages. To address such difficulties, the suggested framework uses Zero-Shot Translation Model (ZSTM), which utilizes multilingual embeddings and transfer learning to understand texts without having to deal with direct parallel data. This model helps to conduct crosslinguistic analysis, helps students to understand the archaic linguistic forms, and allows the real-time educational activities in the field of philology.
