Besides the paper offers suggestions for smooth implementation of strategies to address scalability issues and computational performance problems alongside integration challenges. The hybrid method delivers improved transaction security through enhanced protection and simultaneously produces reduced false positive incidents and speeds up cyber threat response times. This research unites blockchain's trustless system with ML's intellectual ability to develop a modern security solution for financial deals and digital asset protection and e-commerce transactions.

title: Smart Reconstruction: AI-Driven Image Super-Resolution via CNN
authors: Nihar Shetty S, Ankitaa Pupneja, M J Akshaya, Anisha, Siddharth Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The demand for high-resolution (HR) digital imagery is ever-increasing across various domains, yet images are often limited to a low-resolution (LR) format due to hardware or bandwidth constraints. While state-of-the-art deep learning models achieve remarkable reconstruction quality, their immense computational cost often limits their practical deployment. This paper introduces EffiSR-Net, a computationally efficient Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed to address this performance-efficiency trade-off. Our approach involves modifying the established ESRGAN architecture by reducing its network depth from 23 to 10 residual blocks, thereby creating a lightweight variant. We conduct a systematic performance analysis, comparing EffiSRNet against its parent model, ESRGAN, the Transformerbased SwinIR, and a traditional bicubic baseline. Quantitative evaluation demonstrates that EffiSR-Net achieves a significant reduction in model parameters and inference latency—nearly halving the size and doubling the speed of ESRGAN—while maintaining high-fidelity image reconstruction with only a marginal decrease in PSNR and SSIM scores. The results validate EffiSR-Net as a compelling alternative for applications where computational resources are limited, offering a practical balance between speed and quality for real-world superresolution tasks.

title: Comparison of CNN and SVM Models for Automated Seizure Detection via EEG Signals
authors: Dhruv Aggarwal, Sachin Choudhary, Aarchi, Mahadev Ajagalla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: A popular neurophysiological technique for tracking electrical activity in the brain, electroencephalography (EEG) is especially useful for assessing epileptic seizures. The need for automated methods is highlighted by the fact that traditional methods that depend on manual feature extraction frequently miss subtle but significant signal characteristics. In this work, seizure events are classified using a pyramidal-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Accurate detection of epileptic seizures is difficult because they can happen at any age, are very unpredictable, and may be influenced by genetic factors. Pre-ictal, ictal, and post-ictal are the three stages into which epilepsy is typically classified clinically. The suggested CNNbased framework provides a dependable framework for realtime seizure monitoring and diagnostic assistance by not only predicting seizure occurrences but also differentiating between various categories of brain activity. The proposed integrated CNN architecture achieves an average accuracy of approximately 95% during training and 75% during testing. Furthermore, comparative analysis between the two-label CNN model and the two-label Support Vector Machine (SVM) model indicates that the CNN model exhibits superior classification performance. This improvement can be attributed to the inherent capability of CNNs to model complex non-linear relationships, whereas SVMs require carefully designed kernels to capture such non-linearities.

title: Data-Driven Methods in Simultaneous EEG-fPET-fMRI: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Penghui Du, Jingyuan E. Chen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Recent advances in human dynamic functional imaging now allow simultaneous tracking of electrophysiological activity (EEG), hemodynamic responses (fMRI), and metabolic fluctuations using a novel functional [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]-fluorodeoxyglucose PET technique (fPET-FDG). This tri-modal approach yields an exceptionally rich dataset that captures complementary physiological processes across distinct spatial and temporal scales. This conference paper addresses the analytical challenges and opportunities posed by such datasets, with a particular focus on time-resolved fusion methods capable of probing the intricate interplay among neuronal, hemodynamic, and metabolic dynamics. We first outline the specific signal characteristics and experimental design considerations for tri-modal EEG-fPET-fMRI acquisition. We then discuss existing and emerging approaches for time-resolved data integration and highlight methodological considerations for leveraging versatile, data-driven techniques to advance holistic interpretations of brain function.

title: Heterogeneous IoT Traffic in Data Center Networks: Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Ze Zhang, Chongxiang Zhang, Shaohan Wu, Liye Zhu, Qian Dong, Limin Yu, Gordon Owusu Boateng, Junjie Zhang, Long Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: With the increasing demand for reliable computing power and large-scale storage, the Internet of Things (IoT) has been rapidly expanding, interconnecting a vast number of heterogeneous devices and enabling data-intensive applications. Data Center Networks (DCNs) play a critical role in processing massive volumes of sensor-generated data while ensuring high throughput and low latency. However, IoT traffic often combines continuous high-volume flows from devices such as cameras with short and bursty transmissions from low-power sensors. This heterogeneity often results in congestion, packet loss, and elevated latency within DCNs, thereby undermining overall network efficiency. Currently, most DCNs rely on the conventional TCP transport protocol, which is ill-suited to manage such diverse traffic dynamics and frequently exacerbates performance issues. This paper reviews existing research on IoT traffic characteristics, summarizes key performance requirements, and examines the limitations of traditional transport protocols. Finally, it discusses emerging approaches and outlines future directions for optimizing IoT traffic management in DCNs.

title: A Multilayered Approach to Constructing a Patent Field of Technology Ontology
authors: Xuchun Qiu, Yatong Zhou
publish: ACM Transactions on Knowledge Discovery from Data
abstract: None

title: Research and Development of an Automatic Detection System for Weld Radiographic Film Defects Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jiahang Dong, Jian Zhang, Jiawei Jiang, Chang Gao, Zhiguo Fu, Yixuan Du, Yifei Zhang, Xiaoyu Zhu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Radiographic testing (RT) is a critical method for weld defect evaluation. However, the industry still primarily relies on manual film interpretation for radiographic film defect assessment, which suffers from low efficiency, high subjectivity, fatigue-induced missed detections, and high professional requirements for inspectors. To address these issues, this paper develops an automatic weld radiographic film defect detection system via deep learning-based defect recognition technology. Firstly, ResNet18 network structure is adopted to construct a deep learning model. Test results indicate that the independently trained model achieves an overall recognition accuracy of 99.67% with no missed detections of non-defective welds, enabling efficient and accurate identification of common defects in weld radiographic films. Secondly, a visual interactive interface is designed and developed using Tkinter, featuring intuitiveness and ease of operation. Finally, PyInstaller is used to encapsulate the system into an executable file, supporting local deployment and lowering the usage threshold for on-site personnel. This system realizes full-process industrial application from image input to result output, contributing to the digital and intelligent upgrading of the weld defect inspection industry and empowering the high-quality development of high-end equipment manufacturing.

title: A Variable-Speed Fault Diagnosis Method for RV Reducer Using Fused Multi-Attention Transformer Network
authors: Yan Chen, Guangqi Qiu, Xiaofeng Zhan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: RV reducers are subjected to long-term operation under variable speed and heavy load conditions, which makes them prone to failure. The current feature extraction methods are unable to effectively address these issues, resulting in a low accuracy rate of the fault diagnosis methods. In this study, a variable-speed fault diagnosis method with an MSCFEM (MultiScale Convolution Feature Extraction Module) feature extraction module and an SDT model is proposed to address the problems related to time-frequency feature distortion and low diagnostic accuracy have been addressed. The Swin_DA Transformer (SDT) model has been constructed by integrating sliding window attention and deformable attention for hierarchical and multiscale extraction of semantic features with global dependency relationships. The proposed fault diagnosis method has been demonstrated to achieve excellent classification accuracy and stability through comparative experiments. In the RV reducer experiment, the proposed method has been evaluated under variable speed condition with the highest accuracy of 94.5% being obtained, and an accuracy improvement of 8.5% was achieved relative to the well-performing SqueezeNet.

title: EMG-Based Prosthetic Arm Control Using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Models
authors: Akshan K, Dharshanaa K B, Ganesan M
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: This paper demonstrates a complete framework for intent recognition in prosthetic arm control using Surface electromyography (sEMG) signals. Both Deep learning and Machine learning models were developed and comparative analysis has been made to decide the best model for this application. sEMG signals were preprocessed, segmented and then used to extract handcrafted features. Classical Machine learning models, such as Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), XGBoost (XGB) and Logistic Regression (LogR) were implemented and optimized using grid search with crossvalidation. Deep learning models such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), TabNET and Transformer based models, were trained on the extracted feature sets and also on the raw sEMG signals. Experimental evaluation reveals that among traditional Machine learning models, XGBoost and Random Forest attain the highest accuracy of 91%, while within Deep learning approaches, the TabNET model achieves the highest accuracy of 90%. Feature importance and Channel importance analysis were performed and it highlights the most discriminative sEMG channels and features, providing insights for model interpretability and practical prosthetic control. The study provides a comprehensive analysis of existing Machine learning and Deep learning models to present the best approach for realtime intent recognition, offering a benchmark for future prosthetic arm control systems.

title: Intelligent Data Acquisition System and Monitoring System for Real-Time Fault Detection in Electric Motors
authors: Sujitha S, Bhavana C, Charan Kora, Charan D S, Vinayaka H S
abstract: Industrial settings require real-time monitoring and control of various parameters such as temperature, voltage, and current to maintain operational efficiency, conduct preventive maintenance, and ensure safety compliance. This paper outlines the process undertaken to develop a micro-controllerbased Data Acquisition (DAQ) system to acquire, process, and display important industrial parameters. The system consists of various analog sensors, signal conditioning circuits, a microcontroller to convert the analog signal to a digital signal for visual representation with LabVIEW software for visualization and logging. Through careful hardware and software design, the project was able to achieve cost, reliability, and scale for monitoring applications in an industrial setting. This data acquisition solution demonstrated the ability to collect and transmit the data accurately which represents a viable option for industries looking to modernize their legacy monitoring systems and utilize more of a digital environment.

title: Improved Third-Order Generalized Integral Flux Observer with Extended State Observer for PMSM Sensorless Control
authors: Zhiteng Huang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The flux observer based on a generalized integrator offers high observation accuracy due to its band-pass characteristic, which effectively suppresses DC bias and highfrequency harmonics in the back electromotive force (EMF) integration. However, as an open-loop observer, it suffers from poor robustness, and the balance between DC bias suppression and dynamic performance is influenced by parameter selection. In this work, a novel robust hybrid active flux observer method is proposed. Based on the generalized integral flux observer, this method uses the current model as a reference model and employs an extended state observer to compensate the active flux observed by the voltage model. The proposed method features a simple structure, a wide speed control range, and good dynamic performance. Simulation results verify that the proposed method exhibits good control performance across both high and low speed ranges and is robust against load disturbances and parameter mismatches.

title: Design and Validation of an Intelligent Operation Error Prevention Mechanism for Substation Systems
authors: Zongbao Ding, Yongting Zhang, Binxiao Deng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To enhance the safety and intelligent management of substation operations, this study proposes a closed-loop control mechanism aimed at preventing operational errors. The system centers on state recognition, authorization verification, and operation feedback, forming a logical closed-loop model to ensure orderly, controllable, and traceable workflows. By integrating intelligent sensing terminals and dynamic permission logic, it offers real-time monitoring and responsive control of critical operations. A tailored experimental setup validates the mechanism’s effectiveness in detecting unauthorized commands, blocking workflow deviations, and responding to terminal anomalies. Test results demonstrate high accuracy in error identification and consistent control, significantly improving operational safety in complex substation conditions. This work provides theoretical foundations and practical reference for developing advanced intelligent error prevention systems.

title: Efficient Step-Up Voltage Conversion Using Switched Inductor High-Gain (SIHG) DC-DC Converter Topology
authors: Vailla Rohith Chandra, Manikanta Kuraganti, Ramulu Chinthamalla
abstract: A Switched Inductor High Gain (SIHG) DC-DC Converter is a high-level DC voltage boosting topology that aims to provide high voltage gain with low duty ratios. It takes advantage of the switching action of inductors and capacitors to suppress the output voltage and current ripples, thus improving performance and stability. In contrast to traditional high-gain converters, which use numerous components, the SIHG converter provides a higher step-up voltage with a more compact size, lowering circuit complexity, power loss, and cost. It also offers continuous conduction mode (CCM), which improves dynamic response and reduces voltage stress on circuit elements. Because of its high efficiency and reliability, this converter is well suited for high-voltage applications, including electric vehicles (EV) charging, and industrial power supplies.

title: Testbed for Custom Open-Source Power Management Integrated Circuit in Harsh Space Environments
authors: Ítalo Muñoz, Jorge Marin, Felipe Illanes, Marcos Díaz Quezada, Christian A. Rojas
abstract: This work presents the development and application of a testing protocol for evaluating a three-level integrated DC-DC converter with a floating capacitor topology, intended for use in CubeSat low Earth orbit (LEO) missions. The device, fabricated in SkyWater 130 nm technology, has previously demonstrated high efficiency under nominal laboratory conditions. In this work, performance is assessed in a thermovacuum environment emulating LEO conditions, including high vacuum and temperatures spanning above and below nominal operating levels. The protocol defines the key variables, figures of merit, and step-by-step procedures for reproducible testing in both singlecore and interleaved configurations. Experimental results indicate that the converter maintains stable output voltage and achieves efficiency peaks near 93% across all tested conditions, with temperature-dependent variations becoming more pronounced at higher output currents. Interleaved operation extends highefficiency ranges, while reduced ambient temperatures enhance performance. The findings confirm the suitability of the proposed converter design for space applications and highlight the influence of thermal conditions on efficiency.

title: AI-Assisted Research Workshop: A Strategy to Enhance Engagement and Motivation in Engineering Education
authors: Angélica Lizeth Sánchez-López, Maricruz Sepúlveda-Villegas, Edgar Rene López-Mena, Miriam Irene Jiménez-Pérez, Yocanxochitl Perfecto-Ávalos, Juan Esparza-Sánchez
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in higher education is opening new opportunities to enhance research skills and motivation. This study presents the implementation and evaluation of an AI-assisted research workshop designed to improve engagement, basic research competencies, and vocational interest in scientific writing among undergraduate engineering students. The workshop integrated generative AI tools throughout various stages of the research process, including literature review, data analysis, and scientific writing, while emphasizing ethical considerations and responsible AI use. A mixed-methods approach, combining pre- and post-tests, perception surveys, and the MUSIC motivation inventory-was employed to assess the intervention's impact. Results demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in students' understanding of basic research and scientific writing concepts <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.001)$</tex>. The MUSIC model indicated high motivation across all dimensionsempowerment, usefulness, success, interest, and caring-with over 93% agreement levels. Qualitative feedback highlighted strong perceptions of AI's usefulness and relevance to academic work, alongside some concerns about trust and ethical issues. The workshop also positively influenced students' vocational orientation toward research, Overall, these findings underscore the potential of AI tools, when integrated within 2 well-structured and ethically guided frameworks, to catalyze student engagement, skill development, and sustained interest in learning and research.

title: A Hybrid EWT-BiTCN-Transformer Model for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Jiameng Zhang, Yuchen Li
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: To address the non-stationarity, multi-scale fluctuations, and interference from various optical and meteorological factors in photovoltaic power series, this paper proposes a short-term power prediction model that integrates the empirical wavelet transform (EWT), a bidirectional temporal convolutional network (BiTCN), and a Transformer encoder. This model first performs EWT decomposition on multimodal time series data including photovoltaic power, solar irradiance, temperature, humidity, and wind speed to extract stable modal signals in different frequency bands. It then encodes local multi-scale features using a BiTCN network, and finally uses a Transformer architecture to model their global dependencies. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms competing models such as LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) and Informer (Efficient Transformer Architecture) in both accuracy and stability, demonstrating greater robustness under fluctuating environmental conditions. This approach has practical implications for improving power prediction for photovoltaic systems under complex meteorological conditions.

title: Quantitative Characterization of Performance-Memory Trade-Offs in Stack Optimization for Hardware-Accelerated ML-DSA
authors: Eunsung Lee, Keunyoung Park, Jaeyong Lee, Dong Jin Park
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic study of stack optimization strategies for the NIST PQC standard ML-DSA signature verification algorithm in resource-constrained devices such as IoT devices, sensors, and security modules with limited RAM. Building on the optimization techniques proposed for Dilithium by Bos et al. (AFRICACRYPT 2022), we reorganize them into nine distinct strategies and systematically evaluate their cumulative impact on Cortex-M processors with hardware acceleration, using the Samsung Exynos 2500 security subsystem as a representative platform. Our analysis shows that stack optimization must balance memory savings while maintaining hardware acceleration and account for memory placement constraints, such as whether keys and signatures are stored internally or externally. This balance becomes especially critical in devices where verification time accumulates across multiple signatures. Overall, the findings provide practical guidance for diverse deployment scenarios, from extremely memory-limited devices to accelerator-enabled devices and balanced devices such as security modules.

title: Advancements in Image De-Noising for Improved MRI Brain Image Analysis in Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis
authors: S. Chithra, R. Vijayabhanu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Image processing uses mathematical models and algorithms to process and evaluate digital images. The aim is to improve the image quality to discover useful information from the images. The preliminary step in image processing is to eliminate the noise from the images. Noise occurs in the processes of image acquisition, storage and transmission. This study considers the Magnetic Resonance Image (MRI) of a human brain to identify AD is a neurological disorder that impacts the remembrance function. In this paper, a novel de-noising technique is proposed to overcome the limitations of the existing de-noising techniques such as adaptive filter, Weiner filter, weighted median filter, wavelet transform, Gaussian filter, bilateral filter, spatial filter etc. Denoising the image would lead to worsen the image details like the edge of the image, the structure of the image and reduced image resolution. The image degenerationwould lead to a blurred image. The obtained results overcomes the limitations of the prevailing image de-noising techniques. The results are analysedusing the PSNR, SSIM and RMSEmetrics. The enhanced approach eliminates the noise efficiently compared to existing denoising methods.

title: Optimization Algorithm for Inertia Support Capacity of Intermittent New Energy Grid Connection Considering Carbon Emission Constraints
authors: Guangzeng Sun, Bo Yuan, Yichao Zou, Peng Xia, Bin Chen, Teng Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: The inertia support capacity of intermittent new energy grid connection is usually configured in a fixed proportion. However, due to the random fluctuation of new energy power, the pre-configured inertia support capacity may fail to meet the actual needs of the system. A multi-objective optimization is constructed. Constraint conditions are flexibly set. In the process of model solving, the sparrow search algorithm is used to explore the optimal capacity configuration and optimize the inertia support capacity of intermittent new energy grid connection. The experimental results show that this method has the best comprehensive performance in the network structure, with a total cost of 1.308 million yuan, a carbon emission of 2405 tons, an inertia support capacity of 175 MW · s, and a maximum frequency deviation of 0.38 Hz. This method provides a lower carbon and more stable solution for intermittent new energy grid connection, and effectively promotes the green and efficient development of the power industry.

title: Chunking Strategies in RAG Pipelines: Evaluating the Impact on LLM Response Quality Using Consumer Electronics User Manuals
authors: Edma Urtiga de Mattos, Elton Nascimento, Diego Amoedo, Victória Guimarães, Pedro Matias, Emanuel Oliveira, Luca Naja, Matheus Castro, Pedro Oliveira, Matheus Uchoa, Cristian Maia, Lucas Botinelly, Rosiane Brito, Bruno Cardoso, Laura Leite, Lucas Pessoa, Ana Gabriela Pires, Alexandre Miranda, Agemilson Pimentel, Ruan Belém, Rômulo Fabricio, Celso B. Carvalho, Waldir S. S. Júnior
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems are a promising solution for consumer electronics technical support. They can generate consistent answers even when consulting extensive user manuals in different formats. This work investigates the impact of fragmentation strategies on the quality of LLM answers using a dataset constructed from user manuals from several TV manufacturers. Throughout the study, we kept the LLM embedding, vector index, prompt, and generator fixed, varying only the fragmentation. We evaluated 14 strategies for answer quality in a real user manual Q&A scenario. Textual and similarity metrics were used to measure performance. The results show that, considering manuals from different manufacturers, strategies that preserve local continuity were superior: the enriched context strategy performed best overall. However, performance may vary by manufacturer: in Philips (and, to some extent, in Samsung), sliding window and fixed size performed very well; In LG and AOC, enriched context remains the best option (with hierarchical standing out in LG and recursive/fixed-length as good complements in AOC). The analysis suggests that in consumer electronics user manuals, instructions, and prerequisites often appear in neighboring sentences, and capturing this cohesive block increases the quality of retrieval and, consequently, the accuracy of answers. The repository with the datasets and evidence is available at https://github.com/edma-urtiga/chunking_strategies_user_manuals.

title: AI-Driven Exam Hall Monitoring System Using Flask and Face Recognition
authors: S Diwakaran, M. Anbuselvam, Mamidala Suryateja, B.Sakthi Ganesh
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence offers a solution to the long-standing challenge of maintaining examination integrity within educational institutions. Traditional supervision methods involving human invigilators are prone to oversight, manipulation, and human error. This paper presents an AI-driven Exam Hall Monitoring System (EMS) designed to automate attendance recording, seating management, and real-time behavioral monitoring. Built with Flask, SQLAlchemy, and OpenCV, the system employs facial recognition and head pose detection to continuously track student activities. A role-specific dashboard provides administrators and examiners with real-time analytics, alerts, and comprehensive PDF-based reports. By integrating these capabilities, the proposed approach enhances transparency, impartiality, and operational efficiency in exam management while significantly reducing faculty workload.

title: Edge-Aware Vision Assistance: A Real-Time Transformer-Based Object Detection and Reading System for Visually Impaired Users
authors: Kalpavi C Y, Ravikumar H C, Mahadev S Khanderao, Sindhu K, Deepthi V, Shalini M G
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Worldwide statistics reveal that approximately 285 million individuals suffer from varying degrees of sight loss, with nearly 39 million classified as completely sightless. Conventional navigation tools such as tactile walking sticks and trained assistance animals provide restricted environmental awareness, delayed hazard recognition, and cannot interpret textual content. Contemporary developments in direct end-to-end real-time detection frameworks like RT-DETR have begun to challenge YOLO's established supremacy in computer vision by removing the computational burden of non-maximum suppression preprocessing while maintaining comparable processing velocities. This paper presents Edge-Aware Vision Assistance (EVA), a wearable, fully offline system that fuses an optimized RT-DETRv3-R18 detector, lightweight optical-characterrecognition, and spatialized audio on a Raspberry Pi 4 to augment environmental awareness for visually impaired persons. EVA reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48.5 \% \text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> on MS-COCO at 4.5 FPS edge-inference latency, achieves 89.3 % printed-text accuracy in noisy street scenes, and sustains 220 ms end-to-end response on battery power. A two-week longitudinal study with 45 blind participants recorded a 71 % reduction in safety incidents, 18.9 % navigation-accuracy gain, and average usability score of 8.7/10 over a YOLOv5-Lite baseline. The open-hardware design, code, and the 12 k -frame EVA-Egocentric datasets are released to facilitate reproducibility.

title: Modeling of Fixed Configuration for Virtual Circuits in Secondary Systems and Autonomous Configuration Algorithm for Intelligent Transformation
authors: Xiaoyong Sun, Zhen Cai, Xin Wang, Fuliang Wang, Hongbin Wang, Yayun Zeng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the deepening of intelligent transformation in electric power system(EPS) and other fields, the intelligent demand for virtual loop configuration of secondary system becomes more and more urgent. This article focuses on the fixed configuration modeling and autonomous configuration algorithm of virtual loop in secondary system for intelligent transformation. By analyzing the theoretical basis of secondary system and virtual loop, this article constructs a fixed configuration model composed of device layer, link layer and configuration management layer, and designs a set of autonomous configuration algorithm to simulate manual configuration logic. The algorithm includes requirements analysis, equipment screening, link search, configuration generation, check optimization and result output. Experiments show that in 50 typical sample experiments, the configuration accuracy of this algorithm is $98 \%$, the average configuration time is 2.5 seconds, the average CPU utilization rate is $31 \%$, and the average memory occupation is 12GB. Compared with traditional manual configuration algorithm and rule-based heuristic configuration algorithm, it has obvious advantages in configuration accuracy, efficiency and resource utilization. The research results provide technical support for the intelligent transformation of the secondary system.

title: Simulation Study and Performance Analysis of Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell System for Aviation Applications
authors: Jiabing Mao, Le Kang, Shixuan Lin, Junkui Mao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: As the aviation sector faces mounting decarbonization pressure, the Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC) system has emerged as a promising candidate for future onboard power systems owing to its high efficiency and zero-emission characteristics. Nevertheless, its performance degradation under complex flight environments remains insufficiently understood. In particular, degradation effects induced by inclination, vibration, and aging are difficult to capture accurately and efficiently using conventional modeling methods. This study develops a rapid performance prediction framework that integrates neural networks with mathematical modeling to establish a multifactor degradation model for PEMFC system, incorporating the effects of inclination angle, vibration intensity, and aging state. This hybrid approach enables efficient prediction of system performance under coupled degradation influences. Results show that at a representative flight altitude of 10,000m, the combined impact of the three degradation factors and extreme environmental conditions can reduce system performance by up to 49%. Among them, the degradation of the fuel cell stack contributes most significantly underscoring the necessity for protective design strategies and anti-degradation mechanisms. The proposed method provides an effective framework for performance modeling of PEMFC system under coupled degradation and environmental conditions, facilitating its reliable integration into future aircraft applications.

title: Bypass Cable Working Current Monitoring Technology and Its Application in Power Systems
authors: Yuanyuan Zhang, Caiyun Li, Baoqi Tian, Shuai Wang, Meiyi Liu, Ning Hao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: The rapid development of artificial intelligence technology has brought about a complete transformation in current monitoring technology. In view of the problems such as poor monitoring effect existing in the current flexible cable current monitoring technology, this study combines the Hilbert-Huang transform algorithm and long short-term memory neural network to design a current monitoring module, adopts the predator optimization algorithm to develop a load control module, and combines the controller, temperature transformer and other components to design a current monitoring device for real-time monitoring of the cable working current. The simulation experiment results show that the start and end times of the harmonic disturbances detected by the current monitoring device designed in the research are 103ms-662ms respectively, which are basically in line with the theoretical start and end times of the harmonic disturbances and can analyze the harmonic disturbance phenomenon more accurately. The experimental results show that the current monitoring device proposed in the research can conduct current monitoring more accurately and stably.

title: Collaborative Capacity Determination Method for Wind Solar Energy Storage System in the Terminal Power Grid
authors: Haiyang Ma, Tian Dong, Xing Tian, Xinwei Liu, Xing Li, Da Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of green electricity consumption year by year, the reasonable capacity allocation of wind solar energy storage complementary systems in the terminal power grid has become a research hotspot. This article aims to improve the economic efficiency and stability of the system, and adopts an improved NSGA-II multi-objective optimization algorithm to synergistically optimize energy storage power and capacity. Based on the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method, the Pareto solution set is evaluated for capacity determination. The results show that this method can significantly reduce system costs and energy waste rates while ensuring power supply reliability. The optimal configuration is an energy storage capacity of approximately 43.24 kWh and a power of 9.21 kW, with a total system cost of 75.92 thousand yuan. The load shortage rate is close to zero, and the clean energy waste rate is only 0.18%. This study provides an efficient and reliable decision-making method for optimizing the capacity of wind solar energy storage systems.

title: Task-Oriented JSCC with Adaptive Deep Compressed Sensing
authors: Mohammad Amin Jarrahi, Eirina Bourtsoulatze, Vahid Abolghasemi
abstract: In this letter, we propose a Task-oriented Joint Source-Channel Coding framework based on Compressed Sensing (TCS-JSCC), designed to enable end-to-end wireless image transmission for downstream tasks without requiring full image reconstruction. The framework integrates a rate-adaptive CS module with the unique ability to adaptively encode semantically meaningful features informed by both image contents and channel conditions. Specifically, semantic feature representations and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) are jointly exploited to enable dynamic rate allocation and enhance robustness against channel impairments. A lightweight decoder then processes the received features to perform task-specific inference, such as face detection and image classification, directly on the compressed data. Experimental results demonstrate that TCS-JSCC achieves competitive task accuracy under varying channel conditions, offering a scalable and effective solution for edge-intelligent, bandwidth-constrained applications.

title: Scenario-Adaptive Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding for CSI Feedback
authors: Xiyu Wang, Yiran Guo, Wei Chen, Bo Ai
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) technology is expected to be a critical technology for next-generation wireless communication systems. A major challenge in frequency division duplexing (FDD) MIMO systems is the realization of low-overhead, high-spectral-efficiency channel state information (CSI) feedback. The advent of deep learning has introduced a promising paradigm for artificial intelligence (AI)-based CSI feedback enhancement and has drawn attention in the standardization of AI-native wireless communication systems. However, existing studies still face the critical challenge of limited generalizability across diverse channel scenarios. CSI feedback networks trained for specific environments often suffer from degraded performance under channel scenario changes, or require model switching, which introduces latency and results in increased model storage overhead during practical deployment. In this paper, we propose a scenario-adaptive deep joint source-channel coding (DJSCC) architecture. Channel scenario information is incorporated as auxiliary information to enable the network to adapt both feature extraction and feedback bandwidth according to the communication scenario. The proposed network architecture, named JSBANet, achieves simultaneous scenario-adaptation and bandwidth-adaptation, thereby improving CSI reconstruction accuracy while reducing feedback overhead. Extensive experiments on multi-scenario CSI datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed method and demonstrate its performance advantages over traditional and baseline AI-based CSI feedback approaches.

title: Low-Complexity Multi-Step Signal Prediction for Underwater Acoustic Communications: A Joint Reservoir Computing and Transfer Learning Approach
authors: Senthan Prasanth, Adnan A. Cheema, Gökhan Seçinti, Dac-Binh Ha, Berk Canberk, Octavia A. Dobre, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Underwater acoustic communication faces significant challenges due to multipath propagation, signal attenuation, and environmental variability. Accurate signal prediction supports adaptive transmission by forecasting upcoming samples and enabling efficient resource allocation. This work presents a complexity-reduced echo state network (ESN) framework for underwater acoustic signal prediction using real-world shallow-water at-sea experimental data. By reducing the reservoir to 200 neurons and optimizing hyperparameters, the model achieves strong performance with low computational complexity. Single-step prediction on the first hydrophone (H1) yields a test R2 of 0.9978. Furthermore, multi-step forecasting on H1 is performed using a recursive prediction strategy and evaluated up to 6 steps ahead, achieving R2 = 0.8232 at step 6. Using transfer learning, we extend the H1-trained model across 14 additional hydrophones in the vertical array by retraining only the readout layer, which reduces retraining time and computational complexity. Cross-dataset validation between different shallow-water environments further demonstrates robustness to changes in propagation conditions. Across the vertical array, single-step transfer learning achieves R2 ≥ 0.997 for most hydrophones, while cross-dataset transfer yields R2 values typically in the range 0.93-0.97.

title: When Beneficial Intelligence Turns Hostile: A Survey of Adversarial Threats in AI-Native 6G
authors: Mücahit Altintaş, Sümeye Nur Karhan, YasÿIn Emre Tok, Amÿine Gonca Toprak, Öykü Berfün Mercan
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) wireless communication technology is not merely an incremental evolution of mobile systems, but a foundational infrastructure envisioned to support transformative applications ranging from tactile internet to brain-computer interfaces. This vision necessitates the widespread use of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML)-based approaches across different components of the network architecture; however, it also introduces emerging and still poorly understood security vulnerabilities. In particular, adversarial attacks targeting AI components pose serious risks to network performance, reliability, and privacy across multiple layers of the 6G architecture. The fact that beneficial intelligence, expected to enhance network efficiency and adaptability under normal conditions, can exhibit hostile behavior under malicious inputs and interactions, potentially propagating across the system, emerges as a fundamental security concern. This paper is a survey that systematically examines adversarial threats reported in AI-native 6G architectures, covering a broad spectrum from the physical layer to the application layer. To organize the scattered literature, the survey adopts a taxonomy that brings together adversarial attacks from a holistic perspective. Attacks discussed in the literature are analyzed by considering where they occur, what they target, and how they propagate. Based on these dimensions, adversarial attacks are grouped into four main categories: physical-world attacks, input manipulation attacks, model-level attacks, and cross-domain attacks. This classification aims to support a more structured understanding of attack surfaces in the AI-intensive, cross-layer interactive, and distributed nature of 6G systems. The survey further reviews defense approaches proposed in the literature, outlining the threat types they address and their known limitations. Commonly used evaluation metrics and benchmark studies are discussed in terms of their strengths and shortcomings. In addition, research topics that have received limited attention so far, such as adversarial drift and cross-domain propagation, are highlighted. The aim of this study is to draw attention to vulnerability factors that should be considered in the design and security of future 6G networks and to raise awareness in this domain.

title: Applicability of DIN ISO 14505-2 in short-term Neutral Thermal Comfort Assessment for Highly Automated Vehicles
authors: Manuel Kipp, Chira Beji, Klaus Bengler
abstract: Highly automated vehicles enable non-driving-related activities (NDRAs) such as working, reading, or listening to music which modify posture and local boundary conditions relevant for thermal comfort. DIN EN ISO 14505-2 provides an equivalent-temperature framework for vehicle comfort assessment but its application has primarily focused on conventional driver postures. This study evaluates the applicability of DIN EN ISO 14505-2 and the Nilsson comfort-zone concept to NDRA-relevant postures under stationary near-neutral conditions. A two-step workflow is applied in which a segmented equivalent-temperature dummy first identifies near-neutral combinations of heated-panel surface temperature and outlet air temperature under controlled radiant and convective conditions in a climatic-chamber vehicle mock-up. The resulting setpoints are then transferred to a subject study with 33 participants performing working, reading, and listening to music for 12 min each. Two counterbalanced sequence groups (1–2–3 vs. 3–2–1) are used to assess order effects under identical boundary conditions. The protocol targets short-term near-neutral exposures and is not designed to establish thermophysiological steady state. Global and local sensation and comfort are collected with standardized scales and a core-temperature proxy is recorded. Across activities, sensation remains between slightly cool and neutral and no significant activity-related comfort differences are observed within the short exposures, while localized effects occur in selected regions. Core-temperature proxy tendencies are small and interpreted within measurement uncertainty. The results support DIN EN ISO 14505-2 as a practical near-neutral target framework for NDRA-relevant postures and motivate longer and more thermally challenging scenarios for future work.

title: FEM-Based Quantification of Eddy-Current Losses for MV Cables in Trefoil Formation with Non-Magnetic Screens
authors: Marko Šućurović, Dardan Klimenta, Nikolay Hinov, Dragan Tasić, Mladen Banjanin, Darius Andriukaitis
abstract: Circulating- and eddy-current losses occur in non-magnetic screens and armors of three medium-voltage (MV) single-core cables installed in trefoil formation, where they are bonded and earthed at both ends. According to IEC 60287-1-1, eddy-current losses in non-magnetic screens and armors should be neglected, whereas the technical brochure CIGRÉ TB 880 recommends that these losses should always be taken into account. For the considered screen bonding design, a reliable quantitative separation of circulating- and eddy-current losses is therefore essential. Such a separation cannot be performed accurately while simultaneously using experimental methods alone, but it can be achieved through a combination of analytical modeling and finite element method (FEM)-based electro–magneto–thermal simulations. This paper proposes and applies a new hybrid FEM-based procedure for the quantitative determination of circulating- and eddy-current losses in MV cables. The method is applied to Cu/XLPE/CTS/PVC/AWA/PVC 1/C 19/33 kV (BS 6622) cables with twelve different conductor cross-sections. The accuracy of the proposed procedure is verified by comparisons with IEC-based analytical calculations that account for eddy-current losses in accordance with the technical brochures CIGRÉ TB 272 and CIGRÉ TB 880. The FEM-based electro–magneto–thermal models are calibrated and validated using experimental data already published for PL 1×300 RM 6/10 kV cables. The developed calibration strategy represents another novelty. The results demonstrated that, for the BS 6622 cables with conductor cross-sections ranging from 70 to 1000 mm², the screen and armor eddy-current loss factors lie within the ranges 0.00094–0.03625 and 0.00719–0.1951, respectively. The deviations between the FEM- and IEC-based eddy-current loss factors range from −6.398% to 5.302%, demonstrating a very good agreement.

title: UAV Trajectory Planning for Computational Offloading and Resource Allocation Optimizing in UAV-assisted MEC with Unavailable Base Station
authors: Xiao Zeng, Guofeng Yan, Hengliang Tan, Jiao Du, Xia Deng, Heng Wu
abstract: The task offloading and resource allocation strategies aim to provide efficient edge computing services for ground mobile devices in mobile edge computing (MEC) environments. In this paper, we propose a UAV-assisted dynamic deployment and decision-making framework (dDDM) to address the issue of insufficient base station coverage in remote areas or emergency scenarios, which leverages the flexibility and computational capabilities of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) to supplement terrestrial cellular networks. We focus on optimizing the UAV trajectory to support and enhance the effectiveness of task offloading and resource allocation strategies under conditions of user mobility and stochastic task arrivals. Therefore, a joint optimization problem is formulated with the objective of minimizing the weighted sum of the total latency of ground mobile devices and the overall energy consumption of UAV. To solve this problem, the original optimization problem is decomposed into two subproblems: UAV trajectory planning, and decision-making for task offloading & computing resource allocation. For the UAV trajectory planning subproblem, we propose an improved particle swarm optimization(PSO) algorithm integrated with K-means clustering, i.e., KMPSO algorithm, enabling dynamic UAV deployment in response to user mobility. For the task offloading and resource allocation decision-making, an improved black-winged kite algorithm (IBKA) is developed to determine optimal offloading and allocation strategies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms existing benchmark algorithms in terms of both latency and energy consumption.

title: Physical-Semantic Aware Multimodal Facial Expression Recognition for Human-Centric IoT
authors: Ke Wang, Dongxu Li, Xin Xie, Xiaoyi Tao, Xiulong Liu, Zheng Gong, Sheng Chen, Keqiu Li
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) serves as a foundational sensory interface for Human-Centric IoT, supporting applications such as smart healthcare monitoring and affective intelligent environments. However, real-world performance is often hindered by the Semantic Gap, where models confuse visually similar expressions that arise from fundamentally different physiological muscle movements. To bridge this gap, we propose the Physical-Semantic-Aware Multimodal Framework (PSM-FER), which introduces 3D Blendshape (BS) coefficients as explicit physical priors to encode high-level muscle motion semantics. Our framework utilizes two synergistic pathways: Direct Physical Gating (DPG) for robust feature modulation and Semantic-Guided Spatial Attention (SGSA) for anatomical spatial recalibration. Additionally, an auxiliary physical regression task enforces anatomical consistency by regularizing the latent features to follow underlying biomechanical laws. Extensive experiments on the RAF-DB dataset demonstrate that PSM-FER achieves an accuracy of 92.37%, establishing a robust and interpretable foundation for affective sensing in complex IoT ecosystems.

title: Enhanced Resolution in Single-Pixel Imaging via DMD and Mask-Based Dual-Modulation
authors: Xiaoxi Tong, Haonan Ren, Na Jia, Shuhang Bie, Tun Cao
abstract: Single-pixel imaging (SPI) is a computing technology that can reconstruct 2D images through spatially encoded measurements, thus eliminating the need for traditional pixel arrays. Masks are gradually being applied in optical path modulation due to their low-cost and high-speed coding characteristics. However, because of the high difficulty in manufacturing high-resolution masks, their application in high-resolution imaging is limited. This paper proposes a dual-modulation scheme that combines the simplicity and low cost of high-speed rotating masks with high-resolution digital micromirror devices (DMD) to achieve two-dimensional resolution enhanced imaging. The experimental results show that, under the condition of not changing the mask modulation rate but only increasing the sampling rate, this method, in combination with the modulation of DMD, enables arbitrary integer-fold (1× to 5×) resolution enhancement.

title: Fast and Smooth Trajectory Planning and Control for Vehicle Platoons During Cut-In Maneuvers
authors: Zixuan Li, Wei Zhang, Zeyuan Xu, Antonella Ferrara
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Fast and smooth cut-in maneuvers in vehicle platoons are challenging due to abrupt structural changes that induce reference discontinuities and downstream disturbance propagation. From a theoretical perspective, existing string stability formulations do not explicitly account for reference discontinuities caused by cut-in–induced platoon reordering. To address this gap, a novel definition of local string stability is introduced and formally proven, ensuring local spacing regulation and attenuation of reference-induced disturbances within downstream sub-platoons. Building on this theoretical foundation, this paper proposes a three-stage framework that integrates offline pre-planning, traffic-aware online re-planning, and distributed MPC to accommodate time-varying traffic conditions while preserving local string stability during cut-in maneuvers. A hybrid reference tracking strategy with adaptive weighting is designed to enable fast and smooth trajectory transitions during cut-in maneuvers. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of maneuver execution quality and convergence performance under representative cut-in scenarios.

title: Safe-DRL-Based Resource Allocation for Traffic Matching Between Transmission and Computation in QoS-Guaranteed MEC-IoT Networks
authors: Guang Chen, Yueyun Chen, Zhen Wang, Liping Du
abstract: In internet of things (IoT) networks, resource-constrained devices can offload data to mobile edge computing (MEC) servers for processing. Nevertheless, offloading incurs additional energy consumption, making energy-efficient offloading a critical issue. Furthermore, the mismatch of transmission and computation traffic rates further exacerbates energy inefficiency. Additionally, ensuring quality of service (QoS) requires not only meeting the per-slot processed data volume thresholds of devices but also satisfying their long-term energy consumption limits imposed by limited battery capacity. The coexistence of these heterogeneous constraints renders algorithm design more challenging. In this paper, we propose a safe deep reinforcement learning (Safe-DRL)-based resource allocation strategy for traffic matching of transmission and computation in QoS-guaranteed MEC-IoT networks, aiming to maximize energy efficiency. Specifically, we formulate an energy efficiency maximization problem that captures the interdependence among transmission, buffering, and computation traffic, subject to both data processing and energy consumption constraints. To reduce complexity, closed-form solutions for a subset of variables are derived via mathematical analysis. Subsequently, we develop a Safe-DRL algorithm, termed action projection augmented Lagrangian soft actor-critic (APAL-SAC), which integrates a penalty-based action projection mechanism to enforce per-slot constraints and a Lagrangian dual method to ensure long-term constraint satisfaction. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of APAL-SAC.

title: Movable Antenna-Enabled MIMO Integrated Sensing and Communication: A Unified Mutual Information Framework
authors: Guangyi Chen, Ruoyu Zhang, Xinrong Guan, Qingqing Wu, Boyu Ning, Yu Zhang, Wen Wu, Rui Zhang
abstract: Movable antenna (MA)-enabled multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems offer a promising enhancement for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) applications. Unlike conventional MIMO systems with fixed-position antenna (FPA) arrays, MAs can flexibly adjust their positions within a given region, enabling reconfiguration of both communication and sensing channels with additional spatial degrees of freedom. In this paper, we propose a unified mutual information (MI) framework for MA-enabled MIMO ISAC systems, where MI characterizes communication performance as reliably conveyable information and sensing performance as extractable target information in cluttered environments. We formulate an optimization problem to maximize the weighted sum of communication and sensing MI by jointly optimizing the transmit beamforming matrix under a transmit power constraint and the MA positions under practical constraints, with a weighting coefficient characterizing their trade-off. To tackle the non-convexity arising from the log-det objective, position constraints, and the nonlinear coupling between optimization variables, we develop an alternating optimization-based algorithm that iteratively updates the transmit beamforming matrix and the MA positions. Specifically, with the fixed MA positions, we optimize the beamforming by approximating the objective function using weighted mean square error and majorization-minimization methods, yielding a closed-form solution. Moreover, with fixed beamforming, the MA positions are sequentially refined by decomposing the position optimization into simpler subproblems, resulting in an efficient suboptimal solution. Numerical results show that the unified MI framework with MAs significantly outperforms conventional FPA systems in both communication and sensing. Channel amplitude heatmap visualizations further illustrate how MA positioning strategies exploit spatial flexibility in array geometry to enhance overall system performance.

title: Waveformer: Dual-Branch Adaptive Network with Wavelet-Guided Cross-Context Decoding for Colorectal Polyp Segmentation
authors: Liming Liang, Ting Kang, Yulin Li, Jiaming Liang, Xiaoqi Sheng
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: With the advancement of deep learning, polyp segmentation in endoscopic images has achieved remarkable progress. However, clinical polyps often exhibit variable morphology, blurred boundaries, and low contrast with the intestinal mucosa, hindering accurate lesion localization and edge delineation. Moreover, complex conditions of low light, luminal distortion, and mucosal folds further exacerbate the problem with identification, resulting in frequent misdetections and omissions in computer-aided diagnosis. Accordingly, we propose Waveformer, a local-global co-modeling segmentation network, to improve segmentation accuracy. Concretely, the encoder employs parallel CNN-Transformer branches to synergistically extract detailed and global features, thereby enhancing the completeness and discriminative power of the representation. The decoder integrates a wavelet-based frequency decomposition unit (WFDU), a camouflage identification module (CIM), and an information fusion layer (IFL). These modules collaboratively enhance edge responses and semantic aggregation across scales, significantly boosting the framework's capability in boundary modeling and lesion discernment. Extensive experiments on CVC-ClinicDB and Kvasir-SEG datasets achieve Dice Similarity Coefficients (DSC) of 95.60 % and 94.11 %, outperforming fourteen state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Cross-dataset evaluations further verify its strong generalization ability, with DSC scores of 81.0 % and 79.2 %, respectively.

title: Hela: A System Call Restriction Framework for Protecting the Entire Containers Lifecycle
authors: Shaohu Li, Jin Zhou, Xinxin Li, Weizhi Meng, Bei Gong, Jun Hu
abstract: Limiting the number of system calls used by container processes can effectively reduce the kernel attack surface. Existing container system call restriction schemes only focus on the minimum system call set of applications in containers, and lack restrictions on the container runtime runc and other container components that create containers. To solve these problems, this paper proposes Hela, a system call restriction framework that can limit container runtimes and container applications. Hela introduces the Attack Surface Exposure Score (ASES), defined as the dot product of a container's system call usage vector and a risk-weight vector, to quantify exposure. Hela calculates and compares the ASES indicators of various partitioning schemes and selects the best partitioning boundary in the common hook nodes of runc. Hela divides the container creation phase into two phases and generates a minimum set of system calls for each phase. The advantage of Hela is that it combines seccomp with eBPF to achieve accurate parameter checking and efficient system call whitelist switching. Experimental results show that Hela can reduce the kernel attack surface of runc in container runtime compared to traditional schemes. Security experiments prove that our method can mitigate vulnerabilities involving runc and system call parameters.

title: Internet of Medical Things Through Explainable AI at the Edge: A Practical Case Study
authors: Arcangela Rago, Alessio Fascista, Giuseppe Piro, Gennaro Boggia, Luigi Alfredo Grieco
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is emerging as an enabler of trustworthy and human-aligned decision making in next-generation IoT ecosystems. As 6G envisions a highly distributed Global Internet of Things paradigm, embedding explainability of AI models at the network edge will be essential for supporting critical emerging domains such as the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT). In this paper, we explore a practical integration of XAI methods at the edge of 6G IoT architectures and propose novel explainable feature selection and adaptive sample selection strategies that allow low-cost IoT devices to improve data acquisition, on-device processing, and communication efficiency in large-scale deployments. Through a practical case study using an experimental IoMT dataset and comparing different learning models, we show how SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) can be used to interpret patient-related data, improve clinical insight, and significantly boost key performance and value indicators such as learning accuracy, energy consumption, and bandwidth utilization, outperforming traditional dimensionality-reduction techniques.

title: On the Effectiveness of Radio Environmental Map Prediction Through Image-Based Deep Learning
authors: Mohammad Hossein Zadeh, Marina Barbiroli, Enrico Maria Vitucci, Vittorio Degli-Esposti, Franco Fuschini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper investigates the use of deep learning techniques for predicting 2D radio environment maps in urban microcellular scenarios. Starting from the common framework established by prior works, a comprehensive and systematic analysis is conducted to evaluate the impact of multiple factors—including neural network architecture, auxiliary input data, data augmentation strategies, and training dataset fidelity—on the prediction performance. A modified U-Net architecture, referred to as DeepUNet, is proposed, incorporating hybrid loss functions and deep supervision to enhance learning effectiveness. Extensive experiments are carried out by training the model via synthetically generated data using standard ray tracing, as well as multiple types of auxiliary data, including empirical path loss predictions, line-of-sight predictions, and sparsified or interpolated radio environment maps. Results demonstrate that, by properly combining the aforementioned techniques, the proposed deep learning model achieves high prediction accuracy, even in challenging configurations. Furthermore, generalization capabilities are evaluated by training and testing on different ray tracing datasets. A detailed performance analysis is provided to offer practical insights and design guidelines for future radio environment map prediction tasks.

title: Design and Implementation of a High-Speed Shuttle UUV Control System
authors: Jiaguang Wei, Xiaomin Zhang, Xiaowei Feng, Weiwei Xie, Wenxuan He, Xingke Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Deep-sea space stations overcome surface weather and wave constraints, enabling sustained deep-sea operations. This paper focuses on emergency communication between such stations and ground control, using shuttle high-speed UUVs for high-speed vertical navigation and rapid information transmission. It centers on longitudinal velocity and pitch attitude control in the vertical plane. For the control system, an integrated design is proposed, covering UUV control system hardware and software development. A special kinematic model of the UUV is derived via coordinate system rotation mapping; combined with Newton's second law and Euler's rigid body dynamics, a six-degree-of-freedom dynamic model is established. Based on backstepping sliding mode control, a hyperbolic tangent function is used to mitigate chattering, designing a velocity controller with exponential convergence laws. Simulations confirm its superior high-speed tracking performance over conventional PID controllers. To counter ocean current interference and model parameter fluctuations, a Linear Expanded State Observer (LESO) is integrated into vertical pitch control, which enhances the controller's anti-disturbance ability and stability as verified by comparative simulations.

title: MSHCNet: A Multi-Scale Hierarchical Complementary Network for EEG-Based Motor Imagery Classification
authors: Jun Yang, Jiahao Zhang, Tao Shen, Fanan Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: By deciphering electroencephalography (EEG) signals, the brain-computer interface (BCI) allows for direct interaction with external devices. However, the low spatial resolution, strong noise interference, and non-stationarity of EEG signals severely limit decoding performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a model named MSHCNet, based on hierarchical feature extraction and a multi-scale complementary architecture, for multi-scale modeling of non-stationary EEG signals. The model employs spatio-temporal convolution to extract local features, while integrating sliding windows, multi-head self-attention, and temporal convolutional networks to capture long-range dependencies, thereby extracting global temporal features and generating sliding-window features through multi-window integration. Subsequently, a fusion module is designed to integrate local and global features, and an adaptive complementary architecture is introduced to fuse the global and sliding-window features, enhancing the model’s robustness to individual differences and noise. Experiments on the BCI Competition IV 2a dataset show that MSHCNet achieves an accuracy of 80.75% for motor imagery classification, and under few-shot fine-tuning conditions, it attains a cross-subject recognition accuracy of 65.02%, demonstrating its effectiveness and potential in complex EEG pattern recognition.

title: SkyCast: Intelligent Web-Based Weather Forecasting Using Real-Time Data Analytics
authors: P. Varalakshmi, Ranjith C, Ranjith S, Remlin raj RL, RKarthik Raja M
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Accurate and timely weather forecasting is essential for agriculture, transportation, disaster management, and daily planning. This research introduces SkyCast, an intelligent webbased weather forecasting system that leverages real-time data analytics and machine learning to deliver precise, locationspecific predictions. The aim of this study is to design a scalable system capable of processing continuous meteorological data streams and generating actionable forecasts. The objectives include integrating data from IoT weather stations and public APIs, employing advanced models to capture temporal and nonlinear patterns, and providing an interactive web interface for visualization and alerts. The problem addressed is the inadequacy of traditional forecasting approaches, which often rely on computationally intensive physics-based models and provide generalized predictions that fail to adapt to microclimatic variations. The proposed method combines LSTM networks for sequential data modeling with Random Forest regression to capture complex relationships among weather variables, alongside robust preprocessing for data cleaning and normalization. Results demonstrate high predictive accuracy, with temperature forecasts achieving an RMSE of 1.8° C and precipitation prediction precision of 87%, outperforming conventional statistical methods. In conclusion, SkyCast proves that integrating real-time data with machine learning in a web-based platform provides an efficient, scalable, and user-friendly solution for accurate and dynamic weather forecasting.

title: Spec2LLM: A Spectral-to-Language Reprogramming Framework for Power Transformer Temperature Forecasting
authors: Franck Junior Aboya Messou, Jinhua Chen, Tong Liu, Shilong Zhang, Weiyu Wang, Tao Yu, Keping Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Spec2LLM is a novel framework for long-horizon time series electricity forecasting that leverages spectral reprogramming to adapt frozen large language models (LLMs) to multivariate time-series inputs. The architecture integrates three key components: (i) a localized Enhanced Patch Embedding module that extracts top-k frequency magnitudes, phases, and local slope features via Fast Fourier Transform (FFT); (ii) a Spectral Prompt Builder that encodes global frequency priors into a natural language prompt; and (iii) a lightweight LLM Reprogramming Layer that projects spectral patch embeddings into the LLM’s hidden space using a learnable mini-vocabulary attention mechanism. The combined prompt and token sequence is processed through a frozen LLM, followed by a compact regression head. Experiments on four benchmark datasets show that Spec2LLM improves MSE by 5% and MAE by 3.5% over Time-LLM, underscoring its ability to unlock stronger forecasting accuracy from frozen LLMs.

title: ICCE 2026 Opinion
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)

title: Design and Simulation of an Induction Heating Coil to Melt Aluminium Scrap
authors: Bhaskaran Barman
abstract: In this paper, a complete design and simulation of an induction heating coil to melt Aluminium scrap is given. An induction heating furnace basically consist of a coil and some high-frequency power electronics converter. The proposed coil design method is applicable for Aluminium melting. Based on this coil design, FEM simulation is done. Simulation results are tabulated and shown. Closed-loop control of the current source inverter with wide band gap devices is also discussed.

title: Design of a Quick-Change System for Gripping Mechanisms and Force-Controlled Gripping Planning for Warehouse Automation
authors: Aiguo Su, Yayun Lin, Lixin Wu, Huabao Lin, Shangling Guo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the challenges of complex material types, limited end-effector compatibility, and fragile precision instruments in automated warehouse handling, this paper proposes a rapid reloading and force control planning method for grasping mechanisms, aiming to achieve highly flexible and reliable automated handling. First, a modular design method is used to construct a rapidly replaceable end-effector interface for the robotic arm, compatible with three types of work units: a vacuum chuck, a parallel gripper, and an electromagnetic adsorption module. The interface positioning accuracy is controlled to ±0.1 mm. Second, an equivalent contact model of the grasped object is established based on dynamic modeling. Impedance control is introduced in the control algorithm to adjust the force-displacement relationship in real time, achieving coupling between force control and posture control. Finally, a pre-contact buffering phase, a contact force feedback adjustment phase, and a steady-state handling tracking phase are implemented in the control process to ensure safe grasping of objects of different materials. Finally, performance verification is conducted on an experimental platform. Results show that the designed system can complete end-effector module replacement within 3 seconds, with an optimal module reinstallation accuracy of 0.04 mm. The average grasping success rate is increased to 96.5%, and the average contact force for glass sheets is kept within 2.1 N, achieving non-destructive handling.

title: Rethinking Retrieval: Adaptive vs Traditional RAG in Real-World E-Commerce Chatbots
authors: Anand Prakash, Gopal Krishna Shyam, Rashmi Basavaraj Paruti, Shwetha P. R, Harish S
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Traditional RAG systems have historically struggled to keep up with the demand for an accurate and timely response due to frequent changes to product data. This paper focuses on comparing standard RAG against Adaptive RAG, which adapts retrieval depending on the query type and the freshness of the product data. The adaptive RAG utilizes OpenAI embeddings with Pinecone indexing to achieve improved accuracy and quicker response times. Therefore, the adaptive RAG model is ideal for retailers operating in an online business environment and subject to change frequently.

title: Performance Analysis of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Using Combined FOC-MTPA and Conventional FOC: A Comparative Study
authors: Amitesh Kumar, Kumaravel S.
abstract: A combined FOC-MTPA approach is proposed to enhance efficiency and torque performance of PMSM in this paper. Using the proposed combined FOC-MTPA controller, the output torque is increased at starting, and the required current for achieving the same output torque is decreased. As a result, losses due to the current are reduced and the efficiency of the motor is improved. A comparative analysis is employed in the study, evaluating key performance metrics such as speed, torque, and current. The effectiveness of the combined FOCMTPA strategy over the conventional FOC is assessed using MATLAB/Simulink simulation. The observed simulation results prove that FOC combined with MTPA significantly improves the efficiency and torque of the motor compared to conventional FOC. This finding further attracts the potential application of PMSMs in Electric Vehicle (EV), where varying operational demands are common.

title: MobileNetV2 Model for Multiclass Tomato Disease Prediction Using Blended Dataset for Smart Farming
authors: Iita Anatolia, Srinu Sesham, Mateus Abisai, Kenneth Gideon
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Agriculture remains a cornerstone of Namibia's economy, yet small-scale crop farmers continue to face significant productivity losses due to late or inaccurate diagnosis of plant diseases. Tomato, a major crop in the country's semi-arid regions, is highly susceptible to fungal and bacterial infections that spread rapidly under local climatic conditions. Manual inspection is labor-intensive, subjective, and ineffective for largescale monitoring. In the literature, many studies have used highquality datasets to train deep learning models. However, these datasets are not real-time and rarely reflect Namibia's specific atmospheric and climatic conditions. To address this challenge, this study uses a blended dataset combining the PlantVillage Tomato Leaf Dataset from Kaggle with real-time images collected from small farms in oshana region of Namibia. The study further investigates a resource-efficient and reliable deep learning model, namely MobileNetV2, for multiclass Tomato plant disease classification. The performance of the proposed MobileNetV2 model is compared with VGG16 and ResNet50 architectures, all trained and fine-tuned using the blended dataset. Their performance is evaluated based on overall prediction accuracy and computational efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed MobileNetV2-based multiclass classification model achieved the highest accuracy of over 90 % with significantly lower computational resources. The proposed method showed rapid inference that can enable mobile deployment for on-field crop monitoring.

title: A Spurious-Free Antenna for Rectifying Antenna Applications
authors: Udayabhaskar Pattapu, Bikkili Alekya Himabindu, Hima Bindu S, J Nireesa, T. Santosh Kumar, Vijaychandra Kavuri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: A simple rectangular microstrip-patch antenna has been designed in this paper for rectifying antenna applications. In this proposed structure compact in size and also spurious free antenna up to the fourth harmonics has developed by using rectangular slot etched in ground plane and U shaped slot etched at feed line. Antenna is designed at operating frequency of 5.8 GHz, simulated gain and radiation efficiency are 4.58 and 70% respectively at operating frequency. The difference of Cross and Co-Polarization difference both orthogonal planes more than 35 dB. These fruitful properties are well suited for rectifying antenna applications.

title: A Hybrid AI Framework for Dynamic Talent Trajectory Modeling and Proactive Outcome Optimization
authors: Jian Jia, Ping Wang, Jun Zuo, Yunxia Shang, Jihai Guan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Effectively aligning individual competency development with dynamic market demands is a complex challenge in talent management and human capital systems. Conventional systems for career pathing often rely on static, aggregated data, leading to suboptimal individual outcomes and inefficient resource allocation. To address this, we propose a hybrid artificial intelligence framework for dynamic talent trajectory modeling and proactive outcome optimization. The framework is composed of four interconnected modules: (1) A dynamic Market Knowledge Graph (MKG) constructed using BERT-based information extraction to structure competency requirements from large-scale, unstructured text data. (2) A Personalized Competency Pathing (PCP) module, which employs a novel Graph-based Knowledge Tracing (G-KT) model to generate optimal skill acquisition sequences for individuals. (3) An Outcome Misalignment Prediction Model (OMPM), leveraging XGBoost to forecast the risk of suboptimal career outcomes by analyzing multi-modal data streams, including performance metrics and behavioral logs. (4) An Optimized Resource Allocation (ORA) engine that utilizes a Genetic Algorithm (GA) to strategically assign high-impact developmental opportunities.
