Experiments conducted on a simulated cohort of 5,000 individuals show that our framework improves the Competency-Market Alignment Score (CMAS) by up to 28% and achieves an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.92 in early-stage outcome prediction. This research presents a novel, data-driven paradigm for personalized talent development and human capital optimization.

title: Boost-Integrated Single-Stage AC-AC Configuration for Induction Cooking Applications
authors: Thoutreddy Deepthi Reddy, S Porpandiselvi
abstract: Induction heating (IH) is widely used in domestic appliances, due to its efficiency and reliability. In induction cooktops, resonant inverter circuits are utilized due to their higher efficiency and soft switching, which improves the performance and reduces losses. With the increase in demand for multi-output cooking systems, dual-output IH configurations have gained more attention. This paper proposes a single-stage AC-AC resonant inverter topology with integrated boosting capability, which is designed for multiple loads induction cooking (IC) applications. This study proposes a novel inverter which is designed for such applications, focusing on minimizing component count to reduce both circuit size and cost. In the proposed configuration, two IH loads are powered by using Asymmetrical Voltage Cancellation (AVC) control technique and achieves independent power control. The inverter operates under zero-voltage switching (ZVS) conditions, which reduces the switching losses and achieves high efficiency. The performance of the proposed inverter is assessed by using MATLAB/Simulink software.

title: High Gain Converter Based on Dual-Switch Single-Inductor and Switched Capacitor Network with Reduced MoSFET Stress
authors: V. Srimaheswaran, M.M. Rajan Singaravel
abstract: This paper presents the Dual-Switch Single-Inductor and Switched Capacitor Network-based High-Gain Converter (DSSLSCC). The DSSLSCC provides a high voltage gain, operates at a lower duty ratio, experiences reduced stress on switch, and requires lesser components. The design aims a maximum operating duty ratio of less than 0.5, which helps reduce losses and improve overall performance. The converter is evaluated in terms of voltage gain, stress calculation, power loss distribution, and efficiency. The theoretical analysis is validated through simulations carried out in the PSIM environment, confirming the expected performance. Furthermore, a hardware prototype is implemented with a power rating of 200 W, an output voltage of 400 V, an input voltage of 48 V, and an operating switching frequency of 50 kHz. The hardware efficiency of the DSSLSCC converter is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 3 \%}$</tex>. Comparative studies indicate that the DSSLSCC offers significant advantages over conventional converters, making it well-suited for grid integration.

title: A Unified OTFS Framework for Communication and Real-Time Sensing Using USRP B200 SDR
authors: Visalakshi P, Netra K, Shrinidhi V, Nandini Priya R, Sharan R
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation has emerged as a promising physical-layer candidate for next-generation Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) systems. Unlike OFDM, which suffers from Doppler sensitivity in high-mobility environments, OTFS represents information symbols in the delay-Doppler domain, ensuring superior robustness against time-frequency selective fading. This work presents a unified OTFS framework that performs simultaneous wireless communication and real-time sensing using a single Software Defined Radio (SDR) platform-USRP B200. The system includes OTFS modulation and demodulation implemented in MATLAB and real-time transmission and reception through the SDR. The received signal is processed to obtain both Bit Error Rate (BER) performance and delay-Doppler based Range-Velocity (R-V) estimation. Experimental evaluation shows a BER of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{0.5}$</tex> under current conditions and a clear target peak in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}-\mathbf{V}$</tex> map, demonstrating the feasibility of achieving joint radar and communication using one waveform and one hardware chain. The results validate the potential of OTFS-based ISAC for future smart transportation and vehicular environments.

title: Geometrical constructions of purity testing protocols and their applications to quantum communication
authors: Róbert Trényi, Simeon Ball, David G. Glynn, Marcos Curty
abstract: Purity testing protocols (PTPs), also known as Bell state testing protocols, i.e., protocols that decide with high probability whether or not a distributed bipartite quantum state is a tensor product of Bell states, have been proven to be a useful tool in many quantum communication applications. In this paper, we provide geometrical constructions for such protocols that originate directly from classical linear error correcting codes (LECCs), in a way that the properties of the resulting PTP are completely determined from those of the LECC used in the construction. We investigate the implications of our results in various quantum communication tasks, including error detection, entanglement purification for general quantum error models and quantum message authentication.

title: Detection of Inter-Turn Short Circuits Using Search and Rogowski Coils in a Permanent Magnet Machine Driven by Multiple Inverters
authors: Johannes Mühlthaler, Bastian Lehner, Andreas Reeh
abstract: Inter-turn short circuit detection in electric machines can be crucial for the safe operation of drive systems. This paper presents a comprehensive study of invasive fault detection methods for inter-turn short-circuit faults in permanent magnet synchronous machines during passive and inverter-driven operation. A focused comparison of stator field monitoring using search coils and the analysis of the sum current in the end-windings using Rogowski coils is provided. Both methods are investigated in a permanent magnet synchronous machine employing tooth coils and a double layer winding configuration. Unprecedented experimental parameter studies provide insight into fault detectability. The number of three-phase systems, rotational speeds, load conditions, resistances in the short-circuit path and inverter settings are varied to assess influences. A novel fault introduction method using a solid-state power switch enables emulation of rapidly occurring low resistance short-circuit faults. Experimental measurements are shown to match supporting electromagnetic simulations well. The two fault monitoring methods are evaluated with respect to their sensitivity and reliability for detecting different types and severity levels of inter-turn short-circuit fault cases. Whilst Rogowski coils enable detection using raw signals or low-order harmonics, the search coil signals are shown to provide promising indicators due to the inverter interaction.

title: Multi-Task Adversarial Attacks for Wireless Communication Signals
authors: Zihao Zhang, Shilian Zheng, Jiakai Liang, Chao Wang, Shenping Wu, Keqiang Yue, Wenjun Li
abstract: With the rapid development of deep learning in wireless communication for signal detection, target recognition, and parameter estimation, the vulnerability of models to adversarial examples poses a critical challenge to system robustness and security. Existing adversarial attacks mainly focus on single-task models, limiting their applicability in multi-task scenarios. To address this issue, we propose a Multi-Teacher Distillation-guided Multi-Task Attack (MTDMA) framework. It integrates three high-performance teacher models—direct sequence spread spectrum (DSSS) detection, modulation recognition, and direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation—to train a unified student model through hybrid knowledge distillation. By fusing soft targets from multiple teachers, the student jointly learns discriminative features of multiple tasks within a shared representation space. Furthermore, we design a Dual-Representation Momentum Attack (DRMA) that generates perturbations in both amplitude and IQ feature spaces with a momentum mechanism, improving attack transferability and stability. Experimental results demonstrate that MTDMA achieves high attack success rates on three task models and transfers effectively to heterogeneous architectures, outperforming FGSM, BIM, PGD, and MIM. This work extends adversarial attacks from single-task to multi-task settings, providing new insights into robustness evaluation and multi-task adversarial sample generation for wireless communication models.

title: Terahertz Beamforming and Group Sparse Channel Estimation Relying on Low-Resolution ADCs in MU Hybrid MIMO systems
authors: Abhisha Garg, Suraj Srivastava, Akash Kumar, Nimish Yadav, Aditya K. Jagannatham, Lajos Hanzo
abstract: A unified beamforming and channel estimation framework relying on Bayesian learning is conceived. Recognizing the limitations imposed by low-resolution analog-to-digital converter (ADCs) and frequency-dependent propagation effects occurring in the Terahertz (THz) band, we formulate a dual-wideband channel model incorporating root raised cosine (RRC) pulse shaping. To address the non-linear distortions introduced by low-resolution ADCs, Bussgang decomposition is employed, leading to a tractable linearized inference process. By leveraging the shared sparsity inherent in a multi-user (MU) scenario of THz systems, we propose a Hierarchical Bayesian Group-sparse Regression (HBG-SR) based channel learning technique that exploits the group-sparse structure of THz band channels. The estimated dominant angle-of-arrival/ angle-of-departure (AoA/AoD) indices are then exploited for appropriately configuring the true-time-delay (TTD) elements in the hybrid transceiver, enabling precise beam alignment across subcarriers and the effective compensation of the beam-squint effect occurring in wideband THz systems. Extensive simulation results validate the efficiency of the proposed channel estimator and the TTD-aided beamforming architecture, highlighting their robustness and performance gains under practical wideband THz system constraints.

title: Non-Destructive Testing of Temperature Uniformity of SiC MOSFETs Based on Distribution Parameters
authors: Chunsheng Guo, Tengjin Zhang, Jiapeng Li, Yamin Zhang, Hui Zhu, Shiwei Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC)-based metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors (MOSFETs) are characterized by a more uneven temperature distribution because of their decreasing chip size and increasing power density. High temperature distribution gradients may lead to transient burnout failure of the devices and have a significant impact on their long-term reliability. In this paper, we proposed an electrical measurement method for temperature inhomogeneity of SiC MOSFETs based on temperature distribution parameters, and established a multi-dimensional calibration temperature profile library using the parasitic diode junction voltage (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SD</sub>) as a temperature-sensitive electrical parameter, to non-destructively obtain the peak temperature, the average temperature, and the temperature distribution uniformity of the chip. The accuracy of the measurement method was verified by comparison with the infrared thermography method. The obtained results showed that the deviations of the peak and average temperatures of the SiC MOSFETs measured using the method in this paper were less than 1°C compared with the infrared method.

title: Multi-Terabit Coherent Free-Space Optical Communication Over a 4.6 km Urban Channel
authors: Vincent van Vliet, Menno van den Hout, Kadir Gümü¸s, Eduward Tangdiongga, Chigo Okonkwo
abstract: We established Reid Photonloop, a permanent 4.6 km testbed for free-space optical (FSO) communications, in Eindhoven, The Netherlands, to characterize the urban FSO channel in detail and validate the implementations of the FSO communication system. In this paper, we present an experimental field demonstration of a coherent wavelength-division multiplexed (WDM) system capable of transmitting data with a multi terabit-per-second net rate over the 4.6 km terrestrial urban FSO link, measured continuously for 12 days. By employing 22 WDM channels with 50-GHz channel spacing and using modulation formats 4-, 8-, and 16-QAM, we measure low density parity-check-decoded data rates of up to 7.73Tb/s. Additionally, we investigate the concurrent channel conditions by analyzing the fiber-coupled optical received power, as well as the impact of rainfall and visibility information collected by a weather station on the FSO data transmission. The 12-day measurements enable a detailed analysis of channel attenuation, scintillation, and outage events under varying weather conditions, revealing the impact on data transmission performance. We further quantify the outage probability of the link and its corresponding availability for different atmospheric conditions and modulation formats, highlighting the trade-off between availability and decoded data rate. The results advance understanding of the FSO communication channel dynamics in urban environments, demonstrate its multi-terabit-per-second potential, and can inform future designs of high-capacity FSO networks in urban areas.

title: CondFusionSAR: A Bidirectional Multi-Conditional Fusion Network for Generating High-Resolution SAR Images with Complex Backgrounds
authors: Moran Ju, Mulin Li, Tengkai Mao, Sinian Jin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Currently, data-driven deep learning models advance slowly in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship image processing, largely because acquiring SAR images is quite challenging. Though SAR generation models offer a solution, their practical application value is often limited due to inadequate modeling of scene elements, poor controllability of ship positions, and ship offset or loss issues in complex scenes, To tackle the above challenges, we design a bidirectional multi conditional fusion network named CondFusionSAR with the location and class of targets as multi-conditional inputs to controllably generate SAR ship images with complex backgrounds. We decouple SAR synthesis into bidirectional branches, namely a ship synthesis branch and a background synthesis branch. The former adopts anencoder-decoder architecture with the proposed Semantic Injection Module (SIM), which injects mask priors to address the issue of ships target loss in complex scene. The latter employs a mask-based diffusion model enhanced by Mask-Aware Cross-Attention (MaCA) to guide feature generation with the mask, and Multi-Scale Aggregation (MSA) to enhance background modeling capabilities. Moreover, a ship-background fuser with the Spectral Attention Module (SAM) is designed to integrate outputs by applying learnable attention in both spatial and frequency domains, generating complex SAR image. Extensive experiments conducted on improved dataset show that CondFusionSAR can generate high-fidelity SAR ship images. Particularly, with the provided target annotation, the synthesized images can be directly used in other SAR ship tasks, which saves the time required for manual annotation.

title: Research on an Application Model of Action Recognition Technology in Human-Computer Interaction
authors: Hongbing Ba, Xiaoling Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the feedback lag and reliance on subjective judgment in traditional human-computer interaction during sports training, this study designed a simplified three-layer "perception-processing-interaction" action recognition human-computer interaction model, adapted for middle- and long-distance running posture correction and basketball shooting training scenarios. The perception layer uses an RGB camera (1280×720 resolution) and a 6-axis MEMS inertial sensor (±16g acceleration range) for multimodal data acquisition. The processing layer, based on an optimized MobileNetV3-Small model (without the SE module), simplifies the "preprocessing-keyframe extraction-matching" process. The interaction layer establishes a "recognition result-correction instruction" mapping mechanism, integrating tactile and visual feedback. Experimental verification showed that after 30-35 training cycles in two scenarios, the model achieved stable accuracy (88.5% ± 0.3% for middle-distance running and 86.3% ± 0.5% for basketball), interaction latency ≤ 98ms (highest for shooting), and athlete and coach satisfaction ≥ 4.1 points (feedback clarity rating reached 4.5 points). Simulation plots show that accuracy increases with training cycles, latency increases slightly with movement complexity, and report generation time increases linearly with training duration (generating a report for 50 minutes of training data takes 12.5 seconds). This research confirms that the model can meet the low-cost, high-real-time interaction requirements of sports scenarios, providing technical support for intelligent training.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Dynamic Context-Aware Translation System in AR Scenarios
authors: Yanyan Ren, Zhengsheng Dang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In augmented reality (AR) applications, the ever-changing context is a challenge for performance in translation systems. The paper presents the design of a dynamic context-aware translation system which is based on the combination of a real-time computer vision module and a context-aware natural language processing model. In addition, it adapts a lightweight inference engine and adaptive rendering pipeline for minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, the system further improves the contextual flexibility by temporal context modeling and dynamic vocabulary adaptation mechanism. Experimental results show that the system has good results in BLEU and METEOR metrics with the scores of 0.782-0.845 and 0.715-0.792, respectively, and end-to-end latency of fewer than 128.9 milliseconds. This proves its success in terms of enhancing translation accuracy and cultural suitability in such a situation as real-time conversation and document scanning.

title: Cross-border Short Video Platform System for Dynamic Adaptation to Legal Conflicts, Regional Regulatory Analysis, and Intelligent Content Filtering Based on Natural Language Processing and Intelligent Algorithms
authors: Wenzhou Shu, Ao Liu, Minzhe Cui, Haoyuan Yang, Yushuo Zhang, Yongjun Li, Bowei Xiang, Chengying Sun, Yaling Deng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Cross-border short videos often face legal conflicts due to regional regulatory differences, where traditional manual review struggles to balance real-time responsiveness and accuracy. This study constructs a compliance system for China-France scenarios using natural language processing and intelligent algorithms. It employs multimodal evidence collection, modularized regulation modeling, and cross-language Transformer matching, proposing a conflict priority function for dynamic clause adjudication. The system integrates RAG and NLI mechanisms to ensure consistency and traceability in regulation parsing and content filtering. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance over baseline models in F1 score, MCC, and calibration error metrics. Conflict resolution success rates and explanation adoption rates significantly improve, with latency controlled within publishing-grade thresholds. Conclusions indicate the system effectively maps legal rules to technical actions in cross-border environments, providing an actionable technical pathway for compliant and secure short video distribution.

title: Doubly Minimized Petz & Sandwiched Rényi Mutual Information: Properties
authors: Laura Burri
abstract: The doubly minimized Petz Rényi mutual information of order α is defined as the minimization of the Petz divergence of order α of a fixed bipartite quantum state relative to any product state. The doubly minimized sandwiched Rényi mutual information is defined analogously using the sandwiched divergence in place of the Petz divergence. In this work, we establish several properties of these two types of Rényi mutual information. In particular, for the Petz case, we prove additivity for α ∈ [1/2, 2]. For the sandwiched case, we establish a novel duality relation for α ∈ [2/3,∞] via Sion’s minimax theorem, and we subsequently use this duality relation to prove additivity for the same range of α. Previously, additivity for the sandwiched case was known only for α ∈ [1,∞], but it had been conjectured to hold for α ∈ [1/2,∞].

title: A Phase-Noise-Based Method for Calculating Random Jitter Amplification in Clock Channels
authors: Tao Wei, Xiuqin Chu, Yuhao Huang, Haiyue Yuan, Yajun Lv, Jun Wang, Yushan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As data transmission rates escalate across successive generations, the impact of timing jitter on the performance of high-speed systems becomes increasingly significant. Moreover, jitter is exacerbated by frequency-dependent channel characteristics as signals propagate through the interconnects. In this paper, an analytical methodology based on phase noise theory is proposed to accurately quantify the random jitter amplification factor in clock signals. To ensure the high accuracy, the proposed method incorporates the higher-order spectral components of the clock waveform rather than relying on sinusoidal approximations. Extensive simulations and empirical measurements conducted on transmission lines and 5th-generation double data rate (DDR5) channels validate the effectiveness and precision of the proposed approach. This methodology provides essential insights into jitter mitigation strategies pertinent to high-speed channel design.

title: Diffusion Bridge Generation of Visible Light to Infrared Images Based on Multi-Scale Enhancement from Aerial Photography Perspective
authors: Xin Wang, Wei Cai, Yao Ding, Xingyu Di, Zhongjie Yin, Haoran Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: To address the challenges of imbalanced multi-scale feature rhythm, network representation defects, and insufficient loss constraints in the generation of infrared images converted from aerial visible light images, a diffusion bridge generation model focusing on multi-scale enhancement is proposed. Starting from the full-link design of multi-scale generation, the model, in terms of diffusion process scheduling, designs a multi-scale scheduling optimized differential equation, adjusts multi-scale features by combining with a gated polynomial enhancement term, and realizes time-varying variance scheduling with a thermodynamic factor, so as to adapt to the logic of first constructing large-scale structures and then refining small-scale thermal details. In terms of network structure enhancement, it builds a time-modulated multi-scale enhancement network, which regulates the intensity of infrared thermal features at each scale according to time steps—ensuring the thermal distribution of large-scale structures in the early stage and improving the authenticity of small-scale details in the later stage; it also proposes a frequency decomposition and fusion module, which decouples high-frequency and low-frequency features through discrete wavelet transform, repairs high-frequency details by virtue of discrete Fourier transform, and realizes dynamic fusion by combining time parameters. In terms of the loss function, it uses high-, medium-, and low-frequency losses to constrain pixel accuracy, and matches LPPIS perceptual loss to optimize semantic consistency, thus constructing an information closed loop. In the comprehensive scene test on the DroneVehicle dataset, the model outperforms 14 mainstream Generative Adversarial Network(GAN) and diffusion models, with core indicators as follows: PSNR(Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) of 15.06, SSIM(Structural Similarity Index Measure) of 49.78, LPIPS(Learning-Perceived Image Block Similarity) of 30.71, and FID(Frechet Inception Distance) of 43.88; it also shows outstanding performance in sub-scenarios—for single-target scenarios, PSNR is 16.78 and SSIM is 59.48; for dense-target scenarios, FID is 34.67; for mixed-target scenarios, compared with the second-best method, PSNR increases by 0.58% and FID decreases by 15.36%. The model successfully distinguishes cross-category multi-scale features and realizes the generation of multi-scale infrared images in complex aerial scenarios.

title: Cheaper Clicks from Boxes: Cyclic Querying for Interactive Small Object Detection
authors: Xiang Yuan, Ruixiang Yao, Junwei Han, Gong Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Interactive object detection paves a promising way to alleviate the annotation burden, particularly for size-limited instances in remote sensing images. While early studies emphasize fortifying object representations through click-based interactions, the alternative strategy of harnessing image as a prior to derive informative clicks remains largely under-explored—despite its substantial potential in advancing interactive understanding. To address this limitation, we devise a CycLIc Querying (CLIQ) paradigm tailored for interactive small object detection in remote sensing imagery. The core lies in an Interactive Semantic Querying framework, where user clicks and visual representations interact in a mutually reinforcing fashion: the click prior first shepherds feature modulation and correlates with a set of class-wise queries to elicit rich semantic responses; the resulting heatmap, in turn, dynamically functions as a spatial activator to highlight small-instance regions. Furthermore, we devise a simple yet effective Informative Click Querying strategy to pinpoint potential click candidates, thereby completing the closed image-to-click interaction loop and ameliorating the performance under few-click scenarios. Thanks to its lightweight cyclic querying design, the proposed CLIQ framework achieves competitive results on the AITOD-R and Tiny-DOTA benchmarks with negligible computational overhead, significantly outperforming pioneering attempts in small object detection. More importantly, the superior performance of our interactive paradigm under low-data regimes underscores its strong potential as a cost-effective and adaptive alternative for data-efficient detection workflows. Code is available at https://github.com/shaunyuan22/CLIQ.

title: Modeling and Simulation of User Survival Space in UGC Platforms Integrating Sentiment Analysis and Behavior Prediction
authors: Shengjie Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: In response to the problem that the 30-day retention rate of ordinary users on UGC platforms (such as TikTok, Weibo) is less than 40%, and the existing user modeling lacks the integration of emotional dimension and behavior prediction, this paper constructs a user survival space model that integrates "emotion - behavior - social - content" four dimensions to achieve quantitative assessment of user status and retention prediction. The study uses 1000 ordinary Weibo users (data from October to December 2023) and 1200 ordinary TikTok users (data from September to November 2023) as samples. Through the BERT model, sentiment analysis is completed (the accuracy rate of Weibo data is 88.6%, and that of TikTok data is 86.3%), combined with the LSTM time series model to predict the future 30-day user behavior intensity (the MAE of Weibo data is 0.72, and that of TikTok data is 0.68), and using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) to determine the weights of each dimension (the behavior dimension is 0.30, the emotional/social dimension is each 0.25, and the content dimension is 0.20). Finally, the user survival space index (U ∈ [0, 100]) is formed. Simulation experiments show that the survival space index of this model has a Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.75 (TikTok) - 0.78 (Weibo) with the actual retention days of users, and the retention prediction F1-score is 0.80 (TikTok) - 0.82 (Weibo), which is 7-8 percentage points higher than the baseline model without an emotional dimension or behavior prediction. This model can provide a quantitative tool for UGC platform user churn warning and personalized operation, and enriches the theoretical application of user behaviorology and computational sociology in platform ecosystem research.

title: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
authors: N/A

title: System Robustness and Collaborative Optimization Based on Graph Model
authors: Qing Xu, Weiwu Li, Zhengying Liu, Zhen Zhang, Xinying Wang, Tianshou Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To enhance the robustness and collaborative optimization ability of systems based on graph models, this paper innovatively proposes a robust optimization framework (MAGL-ROF) that integrates multi-agent collaborative reinforcement learning and graph structure learning. Firstly, a dynamic graph structure optimization algorithm based on meta-gradient guidance is introduced. This algorithm assesses the importance weights of edges in the graph online through a multi-agent collaborative learning strategy and dynamically adjusts the graph connections by using meta-gradient backpropagation to enhance the system's adaptability to external attacks. Secondly, a Graph Information Bottleneck (GIB) driven distributed collaborative training mechanism was designed. By compressing and filtering the node feature information during the message passing process and retaining the feature representations most relevant to the global task, the robustness of the model in a noisy environment was significantly improved. And the distributed alternating direction multiplier method (ADMM) is adopted for cross-agent parameter collaborative update to ensure global consensus. Experiments show that in the two adversarial scenarios of edge attack and node feature noise injection, the MAGL-ROF framework increases the system performance retention rate to 93.5% and 90.2% respectively, which is significantly better than the comparison baselines GCN-Jaccard (75.8%, 72.4%) and the STABLE framework (86.1%, 84.9%). In addition, the convergence speed of the framework on large-scale networks has increased by approximately 35%, effectively addressing the challenge of balancing stability, collaboration, and efficiency in the optimization of large-scale graph models.

title: Research on Helicopter Flight Trajectory Tracking Control Based on Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Chenliang Liu, Zhang Lei, Zhiyong Yan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Addressing the issues of inaccurate system modeling and uncertain environmental disturbances faced by small unmanned helicopters (SUH) during autonomous flight, this paper proposes a helicopter flight trajectory tracking control method based on deep learning. By combining the structures of Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) and Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control (LADRC), a cascade control loop is constructed to perform hierarchical control of position, velocity, and attitude, thereby improving the system's robustness and tracking accuracy. To further optimize the controller parameters, the Bacterial Foraging Optimization-Flower Pollination Algorithm (BFO-FPA) is introduced. By integrating the global search and local optimization capabilities of the two algorithms, automatic tuning of the controller parameters is achieved, effectively addressing the problems of traditional methods that rely on manual experience and have poor adaptability. Simulation experiments show that the proposed ADRC-LADRC controller can maintain high trajectory tracking accuracy under model parameter variations and external disturbances. The tracking error is significantly lower than that of traditional ADRC and PID controllers, verifying the effectiveness and superiority of the deep learning algorithm in improving the autonomous flight performance of unmanned helicopters.

title: Design of Electronically Controlled First-Order Filter Using LT1228 IC
authors: Aamir Suhail Taray, Ajay Kumar Kushwaha, Ashok Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: The LT1228 discrete integrated circuit is utilized in this paper to design and analyze a first-order multifunction filter. The proposed circuit simultaneously produces low-pass (LP), high-pass (HP), and all-pass (AP) responses. An external bias current can be used to electronically tune the filter's cut-off frequency without changing the values of the passive components. The performance of the filter is confirmed by theoretical analysis, SPICE simulations, and experimental measurements. Filters with electronic tuning are key components in analog signal processing applications, particularly in shaping frequency responses, conditioning signals, and reducing noise Analog Building Block (ABB), Operational Transconductance Amplifier (OTA), Current Feedback Amplifier (CFA)

title: Pioneering Robust UART Systems Leveraging Riccati Based Sampling for Superior Bit Error Rate Performance
authors: Hilay Patel, Madhav Tandon, Mahesh A. Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to enhance the reliability of Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter (UART) communication systems under noisy conditions by implementing a Riccati-based sampling method. The UART model has been developed in Verilog to simulate realistic hardware behavior, capturing the effects of bit-flip noise on serial data transmission. Simulations have been conducted to compare the Bit Error Rate (BER) performance of the proposed Riccati based sampling against the traditional fixed sampling with majority voting, across noise probabilities ranging from 0 - 20%. The results demonstrate that the Riccati-based method significantly reduces BER, achieving approximately a 50% improvement at high noise levels, with a BER of 0.0013 compared to 0.0026 for fixed sampling at 20% noise. This enhancement is attributed to the Kalman filter’s ability to estimate bit states from multiple noisy samples, offering a robust solution for error-prone environments. This research validates the approach through detailed simulations, with three professional plots (linear, logarithmic, and scatter with error bars) illustrating the performance gain.

title: Hybrid K-means Clustering and DID Model for High-Dimensional Data Intervention Evaluation within a Multiple Linear Regression Framework
authors: Lunwu Jin, Lei Kuang, Lanyue Cui, Xiaofen Zhong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid analytical framework integrating K-means clustering and Difference-in-Differences (DID) modeling to address high-dimensional data distribution analysis and intervention effect evaluation. A multiple linear regression model is first established to capture the linear mapping between feature and target variables, optimized via gradient descent with a mean squared error cost function and MinMax normalization to mitigate feature scale heterogeneity, with statistical significance verified through an F-test. Subsequently, the K-means algorithm performs unsupervised partitioning of high-dimensional data, where the optimal cluster number is determined by the elbow method, and data points are iteratively assigned to centroids based on minimized Euclidean distance. The DID component employs a bidirectional fixed-effects model to quantify causal effects by comparing temporal changes between treatment and control groups, effectively controlling for confounders. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework ensures robust clustering performance and accurate causal inference, offering a scalable and generalizable solution for complex data-driven intervention analysis.

title: Application and Evaluation of an AI-Assisted Design Process in Textile Pattern Development
authors: Na Lin, Xiaoming Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the long development cycles, reliance on experience, and poor fabric compatibility of traditional textile patterns, this paper proposes a multimodal textile pattern generation and optimization algorithm (MPGO-Textile) and builds a complete AI-assisted design process. The MPGO-Textile algorithm integrates multimodal features from textual style descriptions, designer sketches, and fabric textures. It generates initial patterns using an improved Diffusion Model, introduces a periodic loss function to ensure seamless pattern splicing, and optimizes patterns using a fabric compatibility assessment model. The algorithm also supports real-time interactive adjustments by designers. To validate the algorithm's performance, a dataset of 3,000 textile patterns and 20 fabric textures was constructed. MPGO-Textile was compared with StyleGAN2 and Stable Diffusion on an NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU. Results show that MPGO-Textile achieves a seamless stitching error (SSE) of 0.82, a 65.1% reduction compared to StyleGAN2 (2.35). Its peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) on silk fabrics reaches 32.6dB, a 10.5% improvement over Stable Diffusion (29.5dB). The time required to generate a single 1024×1024 pixel pattern is 8.3s, a 33.6% reduction compared to StyleGAN2 (12.5s). In a subjective evaluation, 20 professional designers gave it an average Meaning of Score (MOSS) of 4.23, an improvement of over 13.7% over the comparison algorithm. An AI-assisted design process based on this algorithm reduced the pattern development cycle for a summer silk dress from 15 days to 3 days. A survey of 15 textile companies revealed that 86.7% of designers recognized the efficiency gains. Research demonstrates that the MPGO-Textile algorithm can effectively improve the quality and efficiency of textile pattern design, providing technical support for digital design in the textile industry.

title: Research on an Intelligent File Query System Model Integrating Natural Language Understanding and Information Retrieval
authors: Yang Nan, Cheng Wang, Peng Li, Yingchao Zhu, Guorui Wu, Ming Yang, Haitao Gao, Yuwen Hu, Zixuan He
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To address the problems of low semantic matching accuracy and poor adaptability to multi-format files in multimodal file queries, this paper designs an intelligent file query system that integrates natural language understanding and information retrieval. The system adopts a four-layer progressive architecture (input layer, preprocessing layer, fusion core layer, and output layer). Its core is the Dynamic Semantic Mapping Retrieval algorithm (DSMR). This algorithm achieves accurate matching through a five-step process: query semantic encoding, hierarchical semantic index construction, and dynamic semantic mapping. Experiments utilize the TREC-CAR public dataset (1.032 million academic documents) and a self-built enterprise office dataset (528,000 multi-format documents). DSMR is compared with four algorithms, including BM25 and BERT+IR. Results show that DSMR achieves a Precision@1 of 92.3% on the TREC-CAR dataset, a 22.1% improvement over BM25, and a Recall@50 of 89.5%, with an average retrieval time of 0.32 seconds. On a self-built dataset, Precision@1 reaches 90.7% and Recall@50 reaches 87.8%, both outperforming baseline algorithms. Ablation experiments show that the dynamic semantic mapping module contributes most to the F1 score of fuzzy queries (reduced by 9.4%), while the weight adaptation module improves Recall for tabular documents by 9.3%, validating the system's advantages in both accuracy and efficiency.

title: Self-repair environmentally friendly insulation material based on disulfide bond and DA reaction
authors: Bo Zhu, Bo Yang, Yu Feng, Wenliang Xu, Zhiqing Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) is widely used in high-voltage cable insulation for its good insulation and mechanical properties. However, long-term use leads to damage, breakdown, and environmental pollution due to non-recyclability and high thermal degradation. This study designs an S-S-DA cross-linker with reversible Diels-Alder (DA) and disulfide bonds using isocyanate reactions. Graft cross-linking forms XLPE-S-S-DA, and doping silane-modified SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> creates XLPE-S-S-DA/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> composite for self-repair. Tests show elongation at break increases by 17.89%, tensile yield stress by 17.26%, and breakdown field strength by 34.4% compared to XLPE. It self-repairs mechanical/electrical damages (three mechanical repairs restore strain to 91.26%, yield stress to 82.65%, strength to 92.37%; three breakdowns restore field strength to 76.04%). Three recycling retains 68.89% strain, 71.59% strength (yield stress increases 6.46%), and 79.57% breakdown strength. Lower thermal degradation helps recycling. Dynamic cross-linking and doping create self-repair, recyclable XLPE composites, improving service life and eco-friendliness for high-voltage cable insulation, providing a new design strategy.

title: Design of a Digital Twin System for a Biped Robot for Prediction of Foot-terrain Contact Forces and its Validation using a Force-sensing Foot_supp2-3677302.pdf
authors: Vyankatesh Ashtekar
abstract: This paper presents a digital twin system that facilitates robot operation and combined prediction of state and dynamics of a biped robot with imperfections such as parasitic compliance. The key contribution lies in utilising proprioceptive sensor feedback together with a contact-aware forward-dynamics (shadow) simulation, augmented by a contact-implicit inverse-dynamics controller—assuming MuJoCo's rigid-body contact formulation—to improve the replication of the robot's posture and dynamics. The speed and accuracy of the developed method is demonstrated through physical experiments on a small biped robot standing on flat, steps or inclined ground. The predicted contact forces are validated using the feedback of a force-sensing robot foot via the concept of zero-tilting moment point. For the first time, a systematic design of a robot foot using force-sensing resistors is presented to achieve repeatability, linearity, and sensitivity to small loads.

title: A Novel Data-Driven Framework Based on K-NN and PCA: Evolutionary Calibration of Game Parameters in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Xin Wu, Kai Zou, Jianyi Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The validity of conventional game models frequently finds itself constrained by their reliance on theoretical assumptions for parameters, which are devoid of empirical validation. In order to address this fundamental limitation, this paper puts forward a data-driven framework that employs an innovative and robust integration of the K-Nearest Neighbours (K-NN) algorithm and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), with a view to achieving empirical parameter calibration. The parameter estimation problem is transformed into a multiple regression task, which involves predicting payoff matrix parameters for a four-agent evolutionary game. These parameters are based on feature similarity between target scenarios and historical cases. A comprehensive data set was constructed, comprising 120 real-world event records across 28 dimensions. PCA facilitates the reduction of dimensionality and enhancement of features, thereby ensuring computational efficiency while concomitantly mitigating multicollinearity. The core framework utilises a distance-weighted K-nearest neighbour (KNN) regression model in order to calibrate cost, payoff, and incentive parameters. It is posited that parameters derived through data mining processes are to be integrated into replicator dynamic equations, with the aim of simulating strategic interactions among finitely rational agents. The efficacy of the proposed model has been substantiated by a substantial body of numerical simulations. In comparison to conventional parameterised models, the proposed model has been shown to demonstrate a 45% reduction in oscillation amplitude and an 18% enhancement in convergence rate. In addition, a sensitivity analysis of the data-derived parameters has exposed critical system thresholds and counterintuitive optimal policy ratios. These are conclusions that are unattainable through theoretical deductions alone. The central tenet of the present research lies in the establishment of a rigorous, transparent, and reproducible methodological framework. The employment of fundamental data mining techniques has been demonstrated to bridge the gap between abstract game theory and empirical reality. This enhancement of the predictive capability and practical value of evolutionary game models has been shown to facilitate more efficient analysis of complex multi-agent systems.

title: Enhancing Financial Protection using Advanced Fraud Detection Methods
authors: Krishnaveni V, V Sumalatha, Mridul Dixit, Ajay Kuamr Sahu, Deep Biswas, Jolly Sneh Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Financial fraud continues to pose a significant threat to financial institutions and individual consumers alike. Traditional rule-based systems struggle to keep pace with the evolving nature of fraudulent activities. This paper explores the potential of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) with a specific focus on binary classification for identifying fraudulent transactions in real-time. We propose a novel approach utilizing a powerful ANN architecture trained on historical labeled data, enabling effective and efficient detection of anomalous patterns indicative of fraud. Furthermore, the paper introduces the development of a comprehensive and user-friendly dashboard designed to seamlessly integrate with the ANN model. This interactive dashboard empowers stakeholders with real-time visualization of transaction activity, allowing for dynamic monitoring and management of flagged transactions. By combining the strengths of an advanced ANN model and a user-centric dashboard, this research proposes a comprehensive solution aimed at enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of financial fraud detection, ultimately safeguarding financial institutions and consumers from potential losses. This research signifies a valuable contribution to the field of financial fraud detection by demonstrating the effectiveness of ANNs coupled with an insightful dashboard, offering a more robust and comprehensive approach to combatting financial crime.

title: Blue Elixir - Real Time Water Purity Assessment and Purification Recommendation System using Isolation Tree Model and Sensors
authors: Shanmughapriya M, Uma Saras A, Sandhya A, Srivarthini P
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Water is the next big market in the world today! It is indeed a challenging task to provide good quality water for the purpose of drinking. The main objective of the proposed work is to build a customized device that can monitor the water purity at real time. The aim of this work is to design an artificially intelligent model to measure the water quality based on Total Dissolved Solids (TDS). Ion Selective Electrode is used with eight different membranes for measuring the total dissolved solids in water. The Isolation Tree Model (ITM) is built by using the customized dataset collected from the national water quality monitoring council website. The Isolation tree model is trained with the customized dataset, the data collected from the real time sample using electrodes is fed to the model via a web application, the model classifies the water quality into three groups a) For Drinking - The collected water is well within the limits in terms of TDS as recommended by monitoring council b) For Domestic use - the collected water can be used for domestic use like washing and cleaning c) Needs purification - the water collected is not suitable for use and methods of purification is recommended based on the measures of ions and chemicals. The isolation tree model's classification accuracy is 97%. The purification recommendations are based on the water council’s guidelines.

title: Resource Orchestration of Natural Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence of Things in Resilient Supply Chain Management
authors: Anubrata Dhar Chaudhuri
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This study investigates how integrating Natural Language Processing (NLP) with Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) technologies enhances supply chain resilience (SCR) and performance (SCP). While existing literature predominantly examines NLP in customer analytics contexts, this research addresses a critical gap by exploring NLP-AIoT synergies for supply chain optimization under disruption conditions. Grounded in Resource Orchestration Theory (ROT), the proposed framework conceptualizes NLP adoption as a resource-bundling mechanism where perceived usefulness of NLP-enabled AIoT (PUAIoT) and NLP-enabled supply chain management (PUSCM) drive technology implementation. The model further examines how leadership support moderates the relationship between NLP adoption and resilience outcomes. Empirical validation comes from 1,302 supply chain professionals across 24 manufacturing and logistics firms in North America and Asia. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) analysis confirms that PUAIoT <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.24, \mathrm{p}<0.01)$</tex> and PUSCM (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.31, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>) significantly drive NLP adoption, which subsequently improves SCR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.33, \mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex> and SCP (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.29, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>). Leadership support amplifies the NLP-SCR relationship (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\beta}=\mathbf{0. 2 1}, \mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 1}$</tex>), while SCR mediates 68 % of NLP's impact on performance outcomes. The model explains 71 % of SCP variance, offering theoretical advances in digital supply chain management and practical frameworks for disruption mitigation.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification Using Fundus Images
authors: Arshaq Ibrahim, Tilani Gunawardena
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of vision loss globally, necessitating early and accurate diagnosis. This paper evaluates the effectiveness of various deep learning models such as InceptionV3, DenseNet121, DenseNet169, DenseNet201, and ResNet50 in classifying DR stages using retinal fundus images and the efficacy of a custom CNN binary classification model for DR detection. Two publicly available datasets, APTOS 2019 and DR Detection 2015, were used. The study explores the impact of dataset balance, volume, and hyperparameter optimization on model performance. InceptionV3 and DenseNet201 emerged as top performers among the pretrained models used for multi-class classification, with InceptionV3 achieving 75% accuracy on APTOS and DenseNet201 matching it. Meanwhile, the custom CNN binary classification model achieved an accuracy of 91.45%. The findings demonstrate the feasibility of using transfer learning for automated DR stage screening and the efficacy of Convolutional Neural Networks in identifying Diabetic Retinopathy while highlighting the significance of dataset quality and class balance.

title: A Comparative Survey of V2V Communication Technologies: DSRC, Wi-Fi, LoRa and VLC
authors: Sudha G, Raghav G S, Saranya S, Yugenthar A P, Aakash P, Rajalakshmi B
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The proposed methodology introduces a new way to improve Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication by comparing and contrasting numerous inferred approaches from the existing technologies on the subject. This proposed study includes the solution provided using the LoRa, DSRC, Wi-Fi model of communication, and VLC with their advantages and limitations they hold in the respective areas. They each individually hold different aspects of advantages in various areas, including efficient communication, reliability, and latency rates. Overall, this article presents a comprehensive solution for vehicle communication and traffic analysis, leveraging the advantages of various technologies. By facilitating data driven traffic control and smooth communication, the suggested system might significantly improve the efficiency and safety of current transportation networks.

title: Monotonic Learning: Guaranteed Improvement in Neural Network Training
authors: Sa'eed Serwan Abdulsattar, Fayzeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Hani Al-Balasmeh, Rahmeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Ahmad AbdulKareem Jaber, Nour Abdulkareem Jaber
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents Monotonic Neural Training, a novel approach to neural network optimization that eliminates performance regressions while improving model quality. Unlike conventional methods that accept all parameter updates, our method enforces strict improvement criteria, ensuring each step advances validation performance. Traditional training often suffers from unpredictable dynamics, with sudden deteriorations requiring constant monitoring and manual intervention. Monotonic Neural Training addresses this via an “improve-or-reject” mechanism: updates are only committed if they demonstrate measurable improvement. The approach treats optimization as a quality-controlled process rather than blind descent. Candidate updates are validated, and failing updates trigger automatic retries with varied batch sampling and intelligent perturbations, forming a self-correcting training loop that guarantees monotonic progress. Experimental results on both regression and classification tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed monotonic training framework. On the diabetes regression benchmark, the method achieved a mean coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.569 compared to 0.450 for conventional training, reflecting a 26 % improvement. On the breast cancer classification task, it reached an accuracy of 0.991 versus 0.947, yielding a 4.6 % gain. While the approach requires greater computational effort due to its selective update process—accepting less than 1 % of candidate steps—it ensures strictly non-degrading performance and stable, deterministic convergence across all runs. Beyond performance gains, Monotonic Neural Training navigates complex loss landscapes autonomously, adapting to training difficulties without human supervision. Even steps requiring over thousands of retries ultimately succeed, demonstrating that patient, qualityfocused optimization outperforms rapid but unstable approaches. Ultimately, the fastest path to superior models is not accelerating updates but ensuring that every update genuinely improves performance.

title: Design and Analysis of a Power-Efficient 4-Bit ALU with XNOR Logic for Enhanced Performance
authors: K. Padmavathi, Venkatarao Tirumalasetty, K.Swetha, D.Prathyusha, B.Kaveri, M.Jhansi, K.Sridevi, A. Jayasree
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper focuses on designing a 4-bit ALU that prioritizes low power consumption and high speed of operation by utilizing XNOR logic. The ALU supports a range of arithmetic functions—such as addition, subtraction, pass-through, and two’s complement—as well as logic operations including AND, OR, XOR, and XNOR. Since the full adder is a fundamental component of the ALU, optimizing its power usage significantly affects the overall power efficiency. The designed ALU exhibits a power consumption of 2.26 mW, a propagation delay of 1.58 ns, and a power-delay product (PDP) of 3.5210 pJ. By adopting an XNOR-based full adder design, the system achieved a 50% reduction in power consumption compared to the traditional Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) full adder. The entire design was simulated using the Cadence Virtuoso platform at the 90 nm technology node, operating at a supply voltage of 1.8 V.

title: Fostering Engagement Through a Latency-Optimized LLM-Based Dialogue System for Multimodal ECA Responses
authors: Konstantin W. Kühlem, Jonathan Ehret, Torsten W. Kuhlen, Andrea Bönsch
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Interactions with Embodied Conversational Agents (ECAs) are an integral part of many social Virtual Reality (VR) applications, increasing the need for free, context-sensitive conversations characterized by latency-optimized and multimodal ECA responses. Our presented methodology consists of three interdependent steps: We first present a holistic framework driven by a Large Language Model (LLM), which integrates existing technologies into a modular and extendable system that is developer-friendly and suitable for diverse use-cases. Building on this foundation, our second step comprises streaming-based optimizations that effectively reduce measured response latency, thereby facilitating real-time conversations. Finally, we conduct a comparative analysis between our latency optimized LLM-driven ECA and a conventional button-based Wizard-of-Oz (WoZ) system to evaluate performance differences in user engagement. Our insights reveal that users perceive our LLM-driven ECA as significantly more natural, competent, and trustworthy than their WoZ counterparts, despite objective measures indicating slightly higher latency in technical performance. These findings underscore the potential of LLMs to enhance engagement in ECAs within VR environments.

title: IPO-ResNet: Improved Parrot Optimization Algorithm-Based Optimization of Residual Network for Myocardial Infarction Classification
authors: Chang Qu, Xin Zhang, Yansong Lu, Yi Wang, Chengzhi Su
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Myocardial infarction (MI) causes permanent cardiac damage, making rapid and accurate diagnosis critical. Electrocardiography (ECG) serves as a key diagnostic tool for MI, and integrating computer-aided diagnosis technology enhances diagnostic efficiency and accuracy. This paper proposes an automated MI classification method based on an improved parrot algorithm (IPO) to optimize residual networks (ResNet). The core approach involves enhancing the parrot optimization algorithm through two strategies: adaptive switching factors and hybrid Cauchy-Gaussian mutation. The improved parrot algorithm is used to find optimal hyperparameter configurations for the ResNet network, thereby constructing a high-performance MI classification model. Experiments on the PTB-XL dataset demonstrate that this method achieves an accuracy of 88.18% and a ROC-AUC of 0.9647, outperforming existing methods and exhibiting high accuracy and practicality in MI classification.

title: Optimizing Waste Classification with Ensemble Learning: Fine-Tuned CNNs for Organic and Recyclable Waste Sorting
authors: Ratnesh Kumar Choudhary, Ansh Devendra Dulewale, Dishant Sevake, Riya Ramteke, Shweta Rewatka, Bharti Mathanka, Prachi Borade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Waste management research deals with the urgent need to implement sustainable waste management strategies and considers the effectiveness of ensemble learning and refined CNNs in the classification of waste. This research experiment would be associated with the waste-classificationdata dataset (more than 25,000 images) with the help of which the models are trained and tested according to paramount performance indicators (accuracy, precision, and recall). The optimized transfer learning pre-trained architectures (VGG16, ResNet50, and InceptionV3) achieved test accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.50,96.00$</tex> and 97.00 percent respectively. To improve classification, a majority voting ensemble design was applied that combines predictions of these optimized models and achieves a final test accuracy of 98.00 which is better than the individual model. Transfer learning with ensemble methods has shown to be effective in attaining accurate classification of waste and provides a sorting solution that is both automated and scalable. The findings of this study conclude that carefully trained CNNs are highly resilient to real-life waste management issues and make an important contribution to developing sustainable recycling systems.

title:
F
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Opt: Novel Fine-Tuning and Folding Algorithms for FPGA-Based DNN Accelerators
authors: Muhammad Shakeel Akram, Bogaraju Sharatchandra Varma, Vincent Meyers, Mehdi Tahoori, Dewar Finlay
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: FPGAs, with their parallelism, low power consumption, and reconfigurability, offer an ideal solution for accelerating quantized deep neural networks (qDNNs) on resource-constrained edge devices. They enable enhanced latency, reduced energy consumption, and improved computational efficiency. However, existing frameworks to accelerate qDNN inference on FPGA face challenges in fine-tuning the deployed models on the FPGAs, as expensive re-synthesis and re-mapping of the accelerator is required. Additionally, the folding algorithm for DNN compute engines in these frameworks introduces substantial padding overheads to align with memory widths. This paper introduces a novel evolutionary algorithm-based hardware-in-the-loop (EvoHIL) framework. EvoHIL uses hardware-level weight bit-flip operations to activate neurons and improve accelerator accuracy without requiring DNN re-training and rebuilding the entire accelerator. We also propose a novel algorithmic optimization (Aopt) for folding across DNN accelerator layers. Aopt optimally aligns folding factors with memory width, eliminating excessive padding overheads and improving throughput while reducing memory and resource utilization. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of these solutions. EvoHIL optimization enhanced the accuracy of a binarized convolutional neural network (BCNN) accelerator to nearly 86%. Aopt delivered significant improvements, including up to 96.77 % padding overhead reduction, 33.2 % increased throughput, and 25 % reduced runtime compared to FINN.

title: Efficient Wireless Power Transfer Considering Superdirectivity in Transmit and Receive Arrays
authors: Brian McMinn, Thomas L. Marzetta
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We investigate wireless power transfer (WPT) systems using jointly optimized superdirective transmit and receive arrays as a possible solution to the numerically large currents of superdirective arrays. Modeling the system as a ported network and applying circuit theory and scalar Green’s function analysis, we propose and demonstrate that the optimal array gain scales approximately as M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, where M and N are the number of elements in the transmit and receive arrays, respectively. Notably, we find that current magnitudes within each array depend only on the local array size and are largely independent of the configuration of the other array. We further show that systems achieving the same array gain with paired superdirective arrays (e.g., M → M) require significantly lower currents than traditional configurations (e.g., M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> → 1).

title: SmartTicket: Chatbot-Based Ticketing System for Museums
authors: Shanmugam Sundaramurthy, Meheraab Chothia, Arshaan Iqbal, Jothi Kumar C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Conversational AI is becoming a widely used tool in end-user applications. Through advances in technology, it is now capable of interpreting intent and extracting structured information from users, making it ideal for systems such as ticket booking, where accuracy and clarity of information are essential. This paper presents the development of an LLM-powered chatbot designed to handle user interactions concerning booking, updating, or deleting museum tickets, along with general museumrelated queries. Since the application's performance depends on the accuracy of the LLM model being used, a comparison of different models is conducted in this paper. We primarily compare three instruction-tuned variants of Meta's Llama model family: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct, Llama-3.2-90B-Vision-Instruct, and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct. Additionally, we compare results from two smaller models in the same family: Llama-3.1-8BInstruct and Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct. This comparison provides insight into how smaller models perform relative to larger models on the same task. Results indicate that the larger models (70 B and 90B) naturally outperform the smaller models, demonstrating higher accuracy in entity extraction. However, in classifying user intent, returning responses in valid JSON format, and identifying missing elements, the smaller models' performance closely matches that of the larger models. Among the larger models, Llama-3.3 narrowly outperforms Llama-3.1 and Llama3.2, despite Llama-3.2 having 90 billion parameters compared to Llama-3.3's 70 billion.

title: Integrating CBC Data with Artificial Intelligence for Early Detection of Dengue Fever
authors: Ashish Reddy Kotla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Dengue fever is a major health issue that affects the world community with early detection being the way forward. The current paper presents the newest framework that integrates complete blood count data and a hybrid artificial intelligence model to identify dengue early in its progression. The system combines a set of algorithms based on the principle of the Random Forest, XG Boost and neural networks, whose work has been optimized with specific focus on features selection. Another unique Query Fragment Caching Algorithm enhances further computing performance. The accuracy of clinical validation (94.99) was high, and the sensitivity (92.35) and specificity (95.78) were higher than the traditional diagnostic methods and machine-learning methods that exist. The results obtained prove that CBC-based AI screening is a cost-efficient, scalable method of early dengue disease detection, which is especially beneficial in cost-limited settings with high disease rates.

title: Deciphering Seasonal Cropping Patterns using Sentinel-1 Time-Series Data and Machine-learning Approach
authors: Swadhina Koley
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This research aims to extract the seasonal cropping patterns using Sentinel-1 time series datasets. The Radar Vegetation Index (RVI) was calculated from the Sentinel1 data and provided as input in a random forest classifier to extract the cropping pattern. A total of $\mathbf{1, 5 0 0}$ samples (divided into 70:30 training and testing data) were used to classify the results. The classifier achieved a training accuracy of 88.81% and a testing accuracy of 74.57%. The classified map indicates that approximately 30% of the total agricultural area is utilized as single-crop lands during the kharif season only. The rabi (mono-cropping) and the zaid (mono-cropping) lands occupy about 2.57% and 2% of the agricultural fields, respectively. About 62% of the croplands are occupied by the multi-cropping lands.

title: MedXAI: A Retrieval-Augmented and Self-Verifying Framework for Knowledge-Guided Medical Image Analysis
authors: Midhat Urooj, Ayan Banerjee, Farhat Shaikh, Kuntal Thakur, Sandeep Gupta
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Accurate and interpretable image-based diagnosis remains a fundamental challenge in medical AI, particularly under domain shifts and rare-class conditions. Deep learning models often struggle with real-world distribution changes, exhibit bias against infrequent pathologies, and lack the transparency required for deployment in safety-critical clinical environments. We introduce MedXAI (An Explainable Framework for Medical Imaging Classification), a unified expert knowledge based framework that integrates deep vision models with clinician-derived expert knowledge to improve generalization, reduce rareclass bias, and provide human-understandable explanations by localizing the relevant diagnostic features rather than relying on technical post-hoc methods (e.g., Saliency Maps, LIME).We evaluate MedXAI across heterogeneous modalities on two challenging tasks: (i) Seizure Onset Zone localization from resting-state fMRI, and (ii) Diabetic Retinopathy grading. Experiments on ten multicenter datasets show consistent gains, including a 3% improvement in cross-domain generalization and a 10% improvmnet in F1 score of rare class, substantially outperforming strong deep learning baselines. Ablations confirm that the symbolic components act as effective clinical priors and regularizers, improving robustness under distribution shift. MedXAI delivers clinically aligned explanations while achieving superior in-domain and cross-domain performance, particularly for rare diseases in multimodal medical AI.

title: An Intelligent IDS/IPS Framework to Fortify Industry 4.0 Applications
authors: Samir Qurbani, Athirah Mohd Ramly, Pardha Saradhi Chakolthi, Mhd Saeed Sharif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The deployment of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices has been crucial to enable new business processes that convert traditional manufacturing to Smart Factory ecosystems, with higher levels of automation and operational efficiency. This digital transformation, however, also opens additional cybersecurity vulnerabilities to the system, including emerging threat types, such as advanced persistent threats (APTs), zero-day attacks, and insider threats. In this paper, a deep learning-based IDS/IPS based on BiLSTM with an attention mechanism is proposed. The BiLSTM model is trained on the KDDTrain+ dataset and validated on the KDDTest+ dataset, with class balancing addressed using the SMOTE technique. Hyperparameters are optimized using Bayesian Optimization with Keras Tuner. The model achieved 89.2% validation accuracy and a weighted F1-score of 0.91. Performance metrics, including confusion matrices and ROC-AUC scores, suggest effective detection across both common and rare attack classes, with a low false positive rate. Additionally, the system was integrated into a real-time environment using a Flask-based API, showcasing the practical feasibility of the proposed solution for Smart Factory cybersecurity.

title: An Intelligent Captioning System for Deaf Users Using Speech Recognition and NLP
authors: Patan Amrulla Khan, Gayatri Ketepalli, Srikanth Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Effective communication requires real-time access to spoken content, especially for those who are deaf or hard of hearing (DHH). For dynamic, real-time settings like classrooms, meetings, or conferences, conventional solutions like post-event transcription or human interpreters are inadequate. This study suggests an AI-Based Real-Time Captioning System that uses cutting-edge Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models, such as Mozilla's Deep Speech and OpenAI's Whisper. The system's features include multilingual support, high accuracy, and low latency while transcribing spoken speech into written captions. It is deployable on a variety of platforms, including as computers, tablets, and smartphones, and incorporates a user-centered interface. With the goal of offering a communication solution that is inclusive, scalable, and economical, the article describes the system design, methodology, evaluation findings, and major problems.

title: Transitive Indexing for Main-Memory: Adaptive Performance under Massive Interleaved Queries and Updates
authors: Imran Ali Mohammed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper introduces Transitive Indexing, a novel paradigm designed for efficient main-memory indexing in modern applications that demand seamless handling of massive interleaved reads and writes. Unlike traditional static indexing methods, which require high initialization costs and prior workload knowledge, or adaptive indexing methods, which struggle to maintain performance under continuous query and update workloads, transitive indexing evolves dynamically to optimize performance across varying workload stages. We present TRIMMER, a concrete implementation of transitive indexing, which begins as a collection of unordered vectors and incrementally adapts its structure in response to workload patterns. Over time, TRIMMER morphs into a trie for high-access data regions, balancing fast lookups and update absorption without any upfront initialization. Detailed experimental evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets from the astronomy domain demonstrate that TRIMMER overcomes the limitations of traditional adaptive and static indexing, providing an efficient and resilient solution for interactive data exploration under massive update scenarios.

title: SMAP and Sentinel-2 Based Vegetation Dynamics and Soil Moisture Analysis for Evaluating impact of farm Pond interventions
authors: Veeramallu Satya Sahithi, Suneetha Manne
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Water conservation activities like farm ponds and check dams are crucial for improving the agricultural resilience in drought prone regions. Developmental activities in India like Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) and IWMP aim at constructing a wide variety of water conservation activities with focus on improving the soil moisture and crop productivity. Remote sensing data from optical and radar sensors can help in analyzing the impact of these activities on surrounding. The present study reports an integrated analysis using SMAP’s L band soil moisture data and Sentinel-2 optical data-based vegetation indices for evaluating the impact of farm ponds in Prakasam District, Andhra Pradesh. The SMAP’s all-weather, high temporal soil moisture data from 2016 to 2024 complements high-resolution Sentinel-2 imagery to track the surface soil moisture along with vegetation dynamics in a period before and after the construction of farm ponds. Results show a consistent improvement in soil moisture with an increase of $0.02-0.04 \mathrm{~m}^{3} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$ and an increase in NDVI $\mathbf{1 2}-\mathbf{1 8 \%}$ in areas influenced by new pond interventions. Field visits and farmer interactions corroborate the enhanced water retention in the study area. Correlation with annual mean rainfall of the area also indicated strongly that the positive agricultural changes were related to new farm pond construction but not due to rainfall. This study demonstrates the utility of combining microwave and optical data for scalable, seasonal impact analysis of water conservation efforts, particularly where cloud cover limits conventional remote sensing.

title: Adaptive Cyber Defense for IoT: A Real-Time Threat Monitoring Approach
authors: Saswat Ranjan, Balaram Ghosal, Bikromadittya Mondal, Asim Kumar Panda, Himadri Sekhar Mondal, Ranjit Halder
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) devices has significantly expanded the attack surface of networks. In contrast, the resource constraints of IoT devices hinder the use of traditional cryptographic methods or continuous patching. To address these challenges, an adaptive cyber defence framework is proposed that combines lightweight authentication with real-time Anomaly detection and automated responses. This framework involves three main parties: the IoT device, the Gateway server, and a centralised Threat-Control Service(TCS). All parties share long- term symmetric keys and participate in a five-step nonce-based challenge-response handshake over an unsecured network. The session key is derived using hash functions, XOR, concatenation, and symmetric encryption techniques. Additionally, TCS collects handshake metadata, such as timing, nonce reuse, and integrity- check failures, to detect anomalies through a one-class machine- learning classifier. When an anomaly is detected, TCS can revoke tokens, devices or initiate firmware updates.
