Experimental results show that the framework achieves an average handshake latency of 180 ms, with 95% accuracy in anomaly detection, enhancing IoT security with a dynamic, context-aware approach.

title: Hybrid Model ARIMA-Random Forest for Energy Forecasting of Smart Energy Monitoring System
authors: Norith Chealy, Channareth Srun, Sros Nhek, Virbora Ny, Chivon Choeung, Mengseu Pheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The current contribution is a hybrid model interfacing ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average) with a Random Forest for energy consumption forecasting with a proposed smart energy monitoring system. The ARIMA is used as a predictor to catch periodic time series data in periods, while the random forest would finalize these periodic patterns in energy consumption trends. The embedding of these models within a smart energy monitoring system creates a chance of improving the forecasting accuracy and enables the facilities consumption behaviors effectively, leading to support in energy optimization as a result. We validate our methodology by applying it to actual household data and find that the performance was significantly better than standalone ARIMA or Random Forest. When the dataset size increases, the hybrid model, as discussed, gives an accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) value of 0.98 and the lowest MSE from 0.34 to 0.03. ARIMA yields fairly steady <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}(0.56-0.61)$</tex> and an increase in MSE being relatively slight (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 4 6 - 0. 4 8}$</tex>). MSE drops from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 4 4}$</tex> to 0.16, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> improves from 0.68 to 0.90 for Random Forest as sample size increases. This indicates the feasibility of how much this work is an applicable and reliable alternative that enhances the efficiency of energy administration, especially in domestic units.

title: Efficient Fog-Cloud Computing Networks: Task Placement and Binary Offloading Optimization
authors: Shweta Bansal, Pankaj Rahi, D Nagesh, Merajul Islam, Aakanksha Upadhyay, S. Naveen Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The increase in low latency and high resource end applications in the global IoT, computing through the fog-cloud network model is becoming the most emerging one. The methodology for solving APTPO and binary process offloading proposed in this paper focuses on fog + cloud solution in cloudcloud computing networks. However, when task characteristics, resource available, and connection band width are integrated into the model, it is possible to minimize computing latency and overall energy utilized. To identify jobs that can be effectively offloaded and hence improve the effectiveness of computing, simple binary decision-making procedure is employed. From the results of all the experiments, it can be determined that the anticipated technique can be used in Internet of Things applications in real world environments which lead to an increased coverage area of the system, longer time consumed in all operations, cost and total overall network throughput as well. The specificity about the technique that has been described can be advantageously applied to a variety of IoT applications that will improve the credential of the network.

title: Optimizing Organizational Performance through Machine Learning Enhanced Management Practices
authors: Ashish Mishra, Nyangam Rime, Somil Vasal, T Aswani, Pankaj Agarwal, Sandeep Tomar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The digital sphere is seeing progressive application of machine learning (ML) to change managerial decision-making and operational performance of organizations. The paper discusses the ways to streamline organizational performance by using ML to improve management using predictive analytics, adaptive process automation, and data-driven leadership approaches to management. As opposed to traditional methods, ML makes it possible to conduct real-time analysis of mega-organizational data and contribute to evidence-based decision-making regarding resource allocation and workforce productivity or customer engagement. The study underlines the importance of the intelligent models in locating the performance bottlenecks, business predictions, and the improved collaboration of employees via dynamic task management. Also, an ethical study is examined, this encompasses transparency and fairness under responsible use of AI in the management environment. This paper proved how companies may gain resilience and sustainable competitive advantage even in fast-changing environments by developing an overall framework to enable machine learning skills to support managerial functions.

title: Smart System to Monitor Noise Using Machine Learning
authors: M. Bindu Madhavi, R. Swathika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Urban sound environments present complex challenges for modern city management systems, requiring sophisticated approaches to understand and classify diverse acoustic patterns. This research proposes a comprehensive machine learning framework that combines supervised and unsupervised learning techniques to automatically recognize, classify, and analyze urban sound patterns. The system integrates deep neural networks, ensemble methods, and feature learning algorithms to process real-time acoustic data from multiple urban locations. Our approach utilizes convolutional neural networks for spectral analysis, recurrent networks for temporal pattern detection, and clustering algorithms for automated sound categorization. The methodology incorporates adaptive learning strategies that continuously improve classification accuracy through online learning mechanisms. Experimental validation across multiple urban districts demonstrates significant improvements in sound classification accuracy, achieving 94.7% precision in identifying different acoustic events and 89.3% accuracy in temporal pattern prediction. The system provides real-time processing capabilities with response times under 300 milliseconds, making it suitable for practical deployment in smart city infrastructure.

title: Multi-Task Pulmonary Nodule Detection and Segmentation in CT Scans Using Hybrid 2D-3D Deep Learning with Size-Aware Attention
authors: Harshan, K Kiran, Upasana Singh, Hemant Kumar Yadav, Bhavik Kuchipudi, Jolly Sneh Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The early identification of pulmonary nodules within CT scans serves as a crucial element for enhancing lung cancer patient outcomes. This research introduces an innovative dual deep learning system which combines two-dimensional (2D) and three-dimensional (3D) convolutional neural networks to detect and segment nodules simultaneously. The proposed architecture divides into parallel 2D and 3D feature extraction streams which merge through a size-aware attention mechanism that adjusts weights according to nodule features. The approach uses multi-task learning to optimize classification and segmentation jointly which results in better accuracy alongside improved computational efficiency. Our approach demonstrates superior performance on the LIDC-IDRI dataset by achieving an AUC of 0.92 for malignancy classification and a Dice coefficient of 0.89 for segmentation than all other state-of-the-art 2D, 3D and hybrid models. The obtained results show how adaptive hybrid architectures can be used to create robust systems for clinical lung nodule analysis.

title: Adversarial Learning for Graph-Based Deep Models with a Focus on Crafting Effective Perturbations Against GNNs
authors: Neeta Nathani, G. Durga Sowjanya, Pankaj Agarwal, Gaurav Dheer, Rajesh Kumar Verma, Guddakesh Kr. Chandan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Using Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) more often in social networks, recommendation systems and bioinformatics has made it clear that such networks are prone to adversarial attacks. Such vulnerabilities can create major problems such as incorrect information circulating or the breakdown of predictive systems. This research proposes an innovative way to attack GNNs by using both K-Means clustering and Class Activation Mapping (CAM). In contrast to FGSM, IFGSM and Carlini-Wagner (CW) techniques, the approach presented here creates perturbations that are more difficult to notice, while continuing to be highly effective in generating attacks. The methodology transforms each MNIST image into a graph node and uses the pixel patterns to guide how the graph is made. The system reaches great misclassification rates, even though the range of visible defects is minimal. Performance evaluation reveals that the method proposed here lowers classifier accuracy and is more effective when an attack is designed to exploit specific security vulnerabilities in comparison to other approaches. The fact that SVMs can handle more data than Logistic Regression models verifies that how a classifier is built has a strong effect on its vulnerabilities. It provides a useful approach to assessing and improving resistance in GNNs, making a difference in adversarial machine learning and helping to build better defenses in practical uses. The suggested K-Means clustering and CAM approach reliably achieves an overall accuracy of up to 97.5% when applied to various datasets and classifiers.

title: Verifying Closed-Loop Contractivity of Learning-Based Controllers via Partitioning
authors: Alexander Davydov
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We address the problem of verifying closed-loop contraction in nonlinear control systems whose controller and contraction metric are both parameterized by neural networks. By leveraging interval analysis and interval bound propagation, we derive a tractable and scalable sufficient condition for closed-loop contractivity that reduces to checking that the dominant eigenvalue of a symmetric Metzler matrix is nonpositive. We combine this sufficient condition with a domain partitioning strategy to integrate this sufficient condition into training. The proposed approach is validated on an inverted pendulum system, demonstrating the ability to learn neural network controllers and contraction metrics that provably satisfy the contraction condition.

title: Deep Learning-Based Fraud Detection in Digital Trade and Logistics Systems
authors: Trapty Agarwal, Madhur Grover, Madhusmita Sahu, Varun Ojha, S M Jahirul Islam, M. Saravanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The flood in digital trade and cross-supply chain logistics systems has become a challenge now where the supplying entities require the ability to detect frauds. It is hard to miss security and compliance issues that would be much less likely to occur in rule-based systems, since those cannot adapt to evolving fraud patterns. The proposed application of this study is the use of deep learning in detecting fraudulent activities based on transactional and behavioural logistic network data of high dimensions. The model applies the attention-based LSTM architecture and graph the attention-based LSTM architecture. They capture sequential patterns and relations between objects with the purpose of enhancing the accuracy of detection. With real-time processing, it is feasible to perform dynamic threat assessment and the method of feature embedding shows a superior detection of anomalies of structured and unstructured data. The system has good generalization to the various logistics forms such as the e-commerce, B2B, as well as maritime dealings. It has been confirmed in experimental outcome that better performance is achieved against fraud prediction metric compared to the traditional models in terms of the amount of load and noise present. This will greatly minimize the occurrence of the false positive and enhance early detection of frauds, enhancing confidence in digital logistics. The proposed model of a deep learning solution is scalable, has real-time adaptability and is more than viable as well as future-proof in terms of digital fraud defence logistics operations. The results of the proposed system were better in comparison with those of other methods with the highest ACR of 93.1% and LLR of 88.7%.

title: Artificial Intelligence in the Detection of Knee Joint Injuries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Baswarani Sandila, Rama Valupadasu, Manjula Sri Rayudu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Knee joint injuries are a leading cause of musculoskeletal disorders, affecting millions worldwide. Traditional diagnostic methods, such as clinical examination and radiographic imaging, often have constraints in terms of performance parameters. Past one-decade, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has shown immense potential in enhancing the performing of diagnosis of knee joint injuries. The Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models are applied to medical images from modalities viz. X-ray, MRI, and CT scans for the detection of knee disorders. Common knee injuries include knee osteoarthritis (KOA), Anterior Cruciate Ligament (ACL) tears, meniscal injuries, and fractures. This paper reviews the current state of AI applications in knee joint injury detection, AI models used, their advantages, challenges, and prospects. The findings suggest that AI can significantly enhance diagnostic performance, decrease detection time, speedup clinical decision-making, despite challenges related to data quality, interpretability, and generalization.

title: Pinpointing Radio Devices: Robust Fingerprint Localization in Industrial Environments
authors: Anton Schösser, Friedrich Burmeister, Joschua Bogner, Zhongju Li, Philipp Schulz, Gerhard Fettweis, Norman Franchi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The massive availability of wireless links will shape the factory of the future. Many envisioned wireless use cases rely on ultra-reliable low latency communication (URLLC). While providing single devices with URLLC is trivial these days, scaling URLLC networks up to many users remains a challenge. Channel-aware radio resource allocation is a method to face this challenge efficiently. However, fine-granular channel awareness is required for channel-aware radio resource allocation. A recently proposed approach to address this requirement is to reuse the channel state information (CSI) stored in a radio environment map (REM). To successfully apply the information from the REM, very accurate localization of the radio device(s) is required, which is the focus of this paper. Based on measurement data and spatially extremely fine-grained fingerprint sequences, it is demonstrated that localizing an automated guided vehicle (AGV) as a radio device is possible with sub-centimeter accuracy on its trajectory. The performance is achieved with the k-nearest neighbor (kNN) approach, and its robustness, even in a time-varying environment, is highlighted. As an enabler for channel-aware resource allocation, the presented work represents a step towards scaling up URLLC networks in industrial use cases.

title: Voicyx: An Intelligent Voice Assistant Driven by the Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B-Instruct AI Model
authors: Aswathy PB, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Vineeta Singh, Basanta Mahato, Bhavik Kuchipudi, Rohit Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) has driven the creation of intelligent voice assistants capable of deep contextual understanding and adaptive interaction. Voicyx, an advanced AI-powered voice assistant built on Meta's Llama-3.1 70B-Instruct model and optimized using NVIDIA NIM, marks a significant leap in real-time, context-aware voice communication. This paper provides an in-depth analysis of Voicyx's architecture, core features, applications, challenges, and future prospects. By integrating the Web Speech API for precise speech recognition, Django for robust backend operations, and AI-based text generation for natural, human-like conversations, Voicyx delivers an intuitive and seamless user experience. Its adaptability allows implementation across diverse fields such as personal assistance, education, healthcare, customer support, and smart home automation, establishing Voicyx as a key milestone in the advancement of intelligent, voice-driven human computer interaction systems.

title: Extracting Two-Dimensional Signal Correlation Maps via Gaussian Process Regression with Zernike Means
authors: Sahar Khosravi, Peter Jendrichovsky, Katherine Maximov, Patrick O. Kanold, Behtash Babadi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Analyzing the functional connectivity of the brain is an enormous challenge, as deciphering it requires knowledge of both functional responses and circuit structure. A promising strategy involves examining the spatial pattern of stimulus-driven activity correlations across neuronal populations. In the mouse primary auditory cortex (A1), while a global tonotopic organization exists, local frequency preferences in layer 2/3 exhibit a fractured, heterogeneous map. This raises the question of whether functional connectivity is spatially ordered or disordered. To answer this question, we developed an inference methodology based on a spatial Gaussian Processes with Zernike means (GPRZ) to estimate robust and statistically principled 2D SC maps from in vivo two-photon calcium imaging of A1 during presentation of pure tones (PTs) and harmonic two tone pairs (TTs) that are smooth and interpretable. We found that PT-driven signal correlation maps were restricted to local neurons, whereas TT signal correlations showed more global textures. Harmonic stimuli elicited spatially distinct patterns in SC maps, revealing precise functional connectivity between harmonically tuned neurons in TT presentations. Our findings suggest that while neighboring neurons in A1 exhibit diverse frequency preferences, their functional connectivity is precise and selectively aligned with harmonic relationships.

title: An Ai Powered Information Assistant for Government Schemes - Development and Comparative Analysis
authors: Nagesh H R, Ravinarayana B, Prakhyath Rai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: An AI-powered information assistant -Abhiyan is developed to streamline access to government schemes, especially for rural users who may lack technical knowledge. Its core purpose is to simplify data retrieval, enhancing information accessibility and bridging the digital divide. Utilizing advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Large Language Models (LLMs), the developed chatbot can understand user queries, including those in regional languages, to provide relevant, userfriendly responses. Abhiyan leverages a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework, enabling it to pull real-time information from structured government notices and documents. By preprocessing and organizing data on various schemes, the system ensures accurate and efficient response delivery, addressing common inquiries like eligibility criteria and application processes. This work integrates LLMs for natural language understanding with RAG for data retrieval, creating a platform that promotes digital inclusion. Abhiyan's scalable infrastructure and ability to offer real-time updates make it a powerful tool for citizens and government agencies alike, empowering users with timely access to essential information on government schemes.

title: Comparative Analysis of Shading Strategies on Solar Heat Gain Through Glass for Energy-Efficient Buildings
authors: Jay Prakash Mathiya, Ankit Goyal, Sashi Kumar Jain, Priyavrat Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of different shading strategies—no shading, fixed shading, and louver shading—on solar heat gain through glass surfaces. Solar radiation, comprising direct and diffuse components, significantly contributes to indoor heat buildup and cooling loads in buildings. Without shading, daily heat gain values reach approximately 4.4-4.5 kWh, indicating maximum solar penetration. The application of fixed shading reduces this value to about 3.3 kWh, demonstrating its ability to block a portion of incident radiation, though with limited adaptability to variations in solar angle. In contrast, louver shading proves to be the most effective strategy, lowering daily heat gain to around 2.1-2.2 kWh. This reduction, nearly 50% compared to unshaded conditions, highlights the superior performance of louvers in minimizing direct and diffuse solar transmission while maintaining controlled daylight entry. Overall, the results emphasize that dynamic shading systems such as louvers are critical for improving energy efficiency, reducing cooling demand, and enhancing thermal comfort in sustainable building design.

title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of Drought Trends in Key Towns across the Philippines using Machine Learning
authors: Dessa Louise Clemenia, Jane Charmaine Wong, Jazzie R. Jao, Edgar A. Vallar, Ibrahim Hameed
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study investigates spatiotemporal climate variability and drought dynamics in four Philippine munici-palities-Coron, Kabugao, Virac and Tagum-using a 20 -year record (2003-2023) of key meteorological variables, including Precipitable Water (PW), 2-meter Temperature (T2M), Wind Speed (WS2M), Relative Humidity (RH2M), and Surface Soil Wetness (GWETTOP, GWETPROF). Analysis reveals distinct seasonal patterns, with increasing rainfall during the wet season and declining precipitation in the dry season, alongside a consistent upward trend in surface temperatures. Vegetation decline was detected in Virac, while Coron, Kabugao and Tagum showed relative stability. The study highlights the intensifying impacts of climate change and extreme events such as El Niño on drought frequency and severity, posing significant risks to agriculture and rural livelihoods. Drought characterization was conducted using the Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI), and predictive modelling was performed using both machine learning (Linear Regression, Support Vector Machine) and deep learning (Long Short-Term Memory, Gated Recurrent Units) approaches. Working within a limited data resource, linear regression, demonstrated best predictive performance, achieving an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ of $\mathbf{7 2. 0 \%}$, RMSE of $\mathbf{0. 4 3 0}$, and MAE of $\mathbf{0. 4 4 0}$ for a stacked dataset with all four locations, having a 6 -months look-back period.

title: Invited: Analog IC Design Automation -- More than a Technical Challenge
authors: Benjamin Prautsch, Uwe Eichler
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: Chip Design in the Era of AI & Quantum: From Idea to IC, are we there yet?
authors: Leon Stok
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Symposium on Physical Design
abstract: None

title: Study on the Total Harmonic Distortion of Microspeaker by 3D Finite Element Simulation
authors: Po-Chun Ting, Tzu-Hsuan Lei, Shu-Chien Wu, Yu-Cheng Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The use of computer simulation software to assist in the development of new products and rapid optimization through its models has become a staple of most consumer electronics products. In recent years, research on acoustic simulation models has mainly focused on results in the frequency domain. However, distortion characteristics such as harmonics, which directly affect the human ear, can only be simulated and predicted by time-domain modeling. Therefore, in this paper, the 3D time domain of a cross-system microspeaker including electromagnetic, mechanical, and acoustic domains is simulated by a commercial finite-element simulation software from the basic principle of microspeaker operation. The acoustic characteristics of the microspeaker are then measured in a professional electroacoustic measurement laboratory in accordance with international standards, and finally compared with the simulation results to verify the differences. The results show that the three-dimensional simulation model proposed in this paper not only improves the average difference between the low-frequency band and the measurement by approximately 2.6% compared with the conventional 2D axisymmetric model, but also reduces the average difference between the full-frequency band simulation and the measurement to 2.9%. This represents a significant technological breakthrough in the field of electroacoustic finite element method simulation, which has long been lacking in three-dimensional time-domain simulation capabilities.

title: Adaptive SNN-PIM: RL-Driven Triple-Hybrid Architecture for Always-On Consumer AI
authors: Sridevi Navulur, Rangababu Peesapati, Subba Rao S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: The vision for Embodied AI in consumer electronics (CE) is fundamentally blocked by the Energy Wall, where data movement costs drain battery life, preventing true Always-On operation. We propose a novel Triple-Hybrid Architecture integrating traditional processors (CPU/GPU), energy-efficient Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs), and RRAM-based Processing-In-Memory (PIM). Our key innovation is the SNN-PIM Fusion Core, which performs sparse inference at the femtojoule (fJ) scale by eliminating the memory bottleneck. Orchestration is managed by an Intelligent Triple-Mode Scheduler (ITMS) enhanced with a Reinforcement Learning (RL)-Driven Adaptive Agent to dynamically route tasks based on system reliability (temperature) and battery state. Through Python simulation, high-fidelity SystemVerilog/FPGA simulations and emulation on the Intel Agilex 5 SoC, we demonstrate a 160 × power reduction in continuous sensing, validating a projected battery life extension to over 9 months on a standard coin cell. This RL-driven adaptability addresses PIM’s reliability challenges, providing a comprehensive architectural framework for sustainable, next-generation Always-On Consumer Electronics AI devices.

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT Enabled Solar Tracking System with Environmental Sustainability and Voltage Monitoring
authors: Sathish kumar Shanmugam, Abarna Sakthivel, Gobika Muthusamy, Iswarya Palanisamy, Monigashree Srinivasan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Solar energy has become one of the most valuable and promising renewable resources on the rise due to the growing global need for clean, inexpensive power. But stationary solar panels frequently don't deliver anywhere near the maximum levels of power output, as they lack built-in tracking mechanisms that let them automatically shift with the sun. In this way an IoT-based dual-axis solar tracking system is implemented to improve power conversion by keeping the solar panel facing the maximum sunlight. It uses Light Dependent Resistors (LDRs) to sense light intensity and a servo motor for panel angle adjustment to get the optimal exposure. A PIC16F877A microcontroller is used to interface the sensors and helps in motor control based on logic introduced during programming. Environmental monitoring is implemented from a DHT11 sensor, for temperature and humidity, meanwhile a thermistor measures the surface panel to avoid overheating. The automatic shed mechanism during precipitation is triggered by a rain sensor for protection. Steady operation is assured via buck-converter control of the voltage supply, and there is an LCD readout that presents real-time measurements of environmental conditions and system status. The ESP8266 Wi-Fi module provides Internet of Things functionality for remote monitoring and recording. Furthermore, the regulated stable power output is designed for charging mobile devices through multiple charger which indicates that it has potential practical applications.

title: AI-Driven Task Offloading Prediction Using SAP-DE-Optimized GRU in 6G Edge Computing
authors: Akshat Gaurav, Shin-Hung Pan, Varsha Arya, Ching-Hsien Hsu, Kwok Tai Chui, Brij B. Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Task offloading is a important issue in 6G edge computing, which required intelligent decision making. In this context, this paper proposed an AI-driven task offloading prediction model. The proposed model used differential evolution with self-adaptive Populations (SAP_DE) for feature selection and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) for classification. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 80.57%, surpassing different baseline models. Compared to baseline models, it reduces FLOPs by 98.82% and total trainable parameters by 97.26%, ensuring lightweight deployment.

title: Low-Power Edge AI Architecture for Sustainable E-Waste Classification and Management Using Raspberry Pi AI HAT with Multi-Sensor Fusion
authors: Pradeep S, Karthika A, Dharshini R, Harini T, Hariharan S, Durganandhini G
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: The exploding wave in the amount of electronic-waste (e-waste) has led to serious environmental and resource problems, which naturally foster the urgent need for the development of smart and scalable waste management systems. This manuscript showcases a Generative Artificial Intelligence - enabled Smart E - Waste Classification and Reward Kiosk that is powered by the Raspberry Pi AI HAT integrated with an Hailo-8L Neural Processor to facilitate efficient on - device inference. The system uses multi-sensor fusion (a combination of computer vision, weight, and metal detection modalities) for the accurate classification of the discarded electronic devices. A quantized convolutional neural network that has been optimized for TensorFlow Lite runs locally on the edge processor and provides classification in 5 seconds with a total accuracy of 93.2 percent. The kiosk communicates with a secure cloud environment through the use of using the Restful APIs for user reward management and traceable supply chain data exchange with retailers and manufacturers. Classification reliability and reward distribution is improved by continuous learning using Generative AI in line with user participation. Experimental validation shows low latency, high energy efficiency, and flexibility for real-time operation, thus making the proposed design a workable and sustainable solution for responsible e-waste recycling within circular economy schemes.

title: Brain Tumor Classification Using Hybrid Quantum Graph Neural Network
authors: Sowbaraniga K, N. Sasipriyaa, B. Krishnakumar, R. Ravikumar, Tharun S, Sindhu A
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Brain tumors refer to the excessive and uncontrolled development of brain cells. Multiple studies have been conducted to prognosticate the emergence of a tumor in the brain and this study offers a new way of tackling this problem. The method presented here involves the use of a ResNet18, an Autoencoder, and a Quantum Graph Neural Network (QGNN) as the basis to predict the incident of a brain tumor. ResNet18 is an extensively used deep learning model due to its residual connections which help reduce the vanishing gradient problem. In this work, ResNet18 serves as the feature extractor, capturing essential representations from the dataset. An Autoencoder compresses the dimensionality of input features while conserving the relevant data. After dimensionality reduction the features are transformed and interpreted in graph structures, as regions of the MRI have interdependencies that can be captured and represented as graphs. This representation enables the QGNN to learn the structural relationships and the feature patterns to improve on classification. The method proposed was implemented on the public Kaggle Brain MRI dataset and showed a significant advantage over the PCA based Hybrid QGNN with a performance of 71% training accuracy and 70% validation accuracy compared to 64% training accuracy and 52% validation accuracy in the previous hybrid model.

title: Milk Spoilage Analyser for Predicting Shelf Life Using Microbial Activity
authors: Ramkumar S, Mohan Raj S, Likhit S, Manishankar M, Naveensurya V, Saravanan S
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: India, the largest milk producer in the world with an annual production of approximately 239 million tonnes, suffers losses of almost 3-4 percent of milk (worth of over 25,000 crores annually) as a result of microbial contamination, poor cold-chain infrastructure and low quality of storage. This spoilage is not only bad to the economy but also it creates threats to the health of the population. Traditional milk quality measurement techniques involve use of sensory assessment and expiry dates labeling which is prone to inaccuracy and time wastage. This paper is a proposal of a Smart Milk Spoilage Analyzer that combines pH, temperature, gas, and turbidity sensors and an ESP32 microcontroller to monitor in real-time. A random forest regression model is used to process the data collected and classify the milk as being fresh, a warning, or spoiled and also predict the remaining shelf life of that milk. It was experimentally demonstrated that pH fell to 5.6 down to 6.7, turbidity rose to 1.6 NTU up to 0.2, and the model had a 96.8% classification accuracy having a 0.3-hour prediction error. The system is inexpensive (approximately 1200 rupees), mobile, and have Internet of Things (IoT) capabilities and thus suit rural and commercial dairy supply chains. It can help in early diagnosis of spoilage, minimize on wastage and increase food safety by means of remote, noninvasive monitoring.

title: Deep Super-Resolution-Aided OCR Framework for Low-Quality Environmental Imagery
authors: Thitiwat Niramol, Prattana Deeprasertkul
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (ICoIAS)
abstract: This paper presents a deep super-resolution-aided optical character recognition (OCR) framework designed to extract datetime metadata from low-quality environmental imagery. The proposed system integrates Real-ESRGAN, a state-of-the-art deep learning model for image enhancement, into the preprocessing stage of the OCR pipeline. Evaluations conducted on three datasets-high-resolution archival scans and degraded radar outputs-demonstrate that the framework significantly improves OCR accuracy, particularly for images with severe degradation, blur, or compression artifacts. The system operates effectively on CPU-only environments and supports both predefined and format-agnostic workflows. Comparative analysis against baseline methods confirms the superiority of the proposed approach in terms of recognition performance, generalizability, and resource efficiency. The framework shows strong potential for scalable deployment in hydrological data centers and similar environmental monitoring systems.

title: Application and Comparative Study of the $\alpha \beta$ Filter in Model-Based Speed Estimation and Sensorless Control of PMSMs
authors: Harikrishnan S, Arun Rahul S
abstract: In the context of speed sensorless control of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives, model-based speed estimation methods are widely employed in the mediumto high-speed range. However, these approaches typically require an auxiliary startup scheme to enable operation from a standstill. Conventional estimators rely on machine flux or back-EMF reconstruction to derive the rotor position, from which speed is subsequently obtained through mechanisms such as a phaselocked loop (PLL) or using a type 2 system (T2S). This work focuses on the incorporation of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha \beta$</tex> filter-a primitive form of the Kalman filter-in the speed estimation loop. Unlike traditional schemes, the proposed method bypasses the intermediate position estimation step and instead applies the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha \beta$</tex> filter to the raw speed estimate, effectively suppressing noise and small signal oscillation. The simulation and experimental studies conducted showed that adopting this approach has extended the applicability of modelbased estimators to the full operating range, including reliable control from standstill. In addition, experimental results showing a comparative evaluation against conventional first- and secondorder filters is also presented in this manuscript, validating the efficacy of the proposed approach.

title: Low Power and Low Complexity 0.3 V CMOS Voltage References Using Single $V_{T}$ MOSFETs
authors: J. P. Goyret, M. V. Cassani, M. Carrá, L. Caivano, M. A. Garcia Inza
abstract: In this work, we present two low-power CMOS voltage references which exploit the effect of MOSFET's length on its threshold voltage. The result of utilizing this technique are low complexity, fully subthreshold designs, which can use single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{T}$</tex> NMOS and PMOS transistors only. One of the designs provides an improvement of two orders of magnitude in line sensitivity thanks to a technique which does not require cascodes. The designs are simulated in a 180 nm technology and the layout of the highest performing design is presented. The best design generates an output of 72.6 mV at 25 °C, with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$102 \text{ppm} /{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> temperature coefficient between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and 80 °C, a supply sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.19 {\%} \mathrm{V}^{-1}$</tex>, while consuming 1.87 nW.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: As electrical engineering, automation, and artificial intelligence (AI) accelerate the digital transformation of industries and cities, their deep integration has become a core driver of technological innovation and sustainable development. To explore cutting-edge achievements and future trends in these interdisciplinary fields, the 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI 2026) was successfully held in Guangzhou, China, from January 16 to 18, 2026. Hosted by the Guangdong Industrial Software Federation and co-organized by Sun Yat-sen University, South China University of Technology, Guangdong University of Technology, and Guangzhou Xinhua University, with technical support from IEEE and IEEE Guangzhou Section, the conference emerged as a premier international academic hub. It united renowned experts, scholars, and industry practitioners worldwide to share innovative research, deepen technical exchanges, and forge collaborative partnerships, propelling progress in these dynamic domains.

title: Automated Discourse Structure Identification in Student Essays for Writing Improvement System
authors: Dilafruz Abdullayeva, Abdurahim Mannonov, Zilola Sattorova, Yorkinoy Altmisheva, Azamat Qodirov, Zilolakhon Ruzmetova, Akmal Abdurakhmanov
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: Automated discourse structure recognition in student essays is crucial in improving the writing technique through recognition and analysis of key elements of argumentative elements. It allows a customized discussion hence aiding students as well as educators in their academic writing enhancement. Current solutions usually depend on rule-based systems or sequence labeling strategies, which find it difficult to deal with long-context dependencies, non-linear structures and generalization to a variety of writing styles. The limitations decrease the precision of recognition of coherent discourse relations. The new framework uses Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to do discourse modeling in which the essay parts are represented by nodes and the rhetorical relationships between them by edges. This proves to be a more effective semantic net of complex argumentative paths, global context and semantic dependencies, in comparison with traditional models. It is applied to student essays and produces structured discourse graphs which are used to feed on targeted revisionary feedback and coherence analysis. The results have shown better results in discourse role identification, better recognition of argumentation relationships and more practical writing comments on learners.

title: Constructive Application of Prompt-Engineering Patterns to Integrate Generative AI in Higher Education
authors: Patricia Brockmann, Heidi Schuhbauer, D. Moritz Marutschke, Victor V. Kryssanov
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: University students increasingly misuse generative AI (GenAI) to cheat on their academic assignments. Instructors need to develop pedagogic strategies which encourage active, authentic learning rather than just the superficial completion of tasks. A constructivist pedagogic concept which utilizes prompt engineering patterns to purposely integrate GenAI into an undergraduate course on databases is described. Students learn to develop structured queries to GenAI, based on prompt engineering patterns. This approach teaches inquiry-based exploration of complex topics in database design and development through iterative interaction with GenAI, which thus serves as an individually personalized tutor. Students learn to use GenAI as a cognitive tool, rather than just copying the answers provided. This approach makes use of scaffolding to empower students to master complex database principles.

title: Research on a Few-Shot Reinforcement Method for Substation Equipment Question-Answering Integrating Global Semantics and Local Features
authors: Lin Li, Guanlin Si, Zuozheng Wang, Yue Zhang, Min Li, Leran Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: A dual-pathway learning framework is developed for substation equipment Question Answering (QA), leveraging knowledge graph-based global semantic encoding in conjunction with hierarchical convolutional local feature extraction. Employing meta-reinforcement learning techniques, the system achieves rapid adaptation and robust generalization with limited samples. To identify contextual clues, the local extractor processes sensor and raw image data to recognize devices and operational dependencies. The feature pyramid aggregation mechanism reduces cross-modal alignment loss, thereby enhancing multi-scale discriminative capabilities. Experimental evaluations on the PowerGrid-QA v2.1 dataset demonstrate that our method achieves higher accuracy and efficiency compared to traditional transformers, GNNs, and prototype networks, particularly under data inconsistencies and rare events. Experimental results confirm that our approach outperforms traditional transformers, GNNs, and prototype networks in terms of accuracy and efficiency on the PowerGridQA v2.1 dataset when dealing with rare events and data inconsistencies. Ablation studies confirm that both global semantic fusion and reward-driven meta-optimization are critical for system performance. Practical applications on edge hardware have demonstrated the framework’s inference stability and efficiency in substation environments. These results indicate that the integration of meta-reinforcement learning with local features and global semantics is effective for industrial-grade QA systems in smart grids.

title: Message From Principal ICTCT 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: On behalf of Acharya Institute of Graduate Studies, I extend a warm welcome to all participants of the International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT-2025), organized in association with IEEE Conference Publishing Services (CPS). This global academic gathering reflects our institution's enduring commitment to fostering excellence in research, innovation, and knowledge dissemination.

title: RadarST: Spatial Transformer Networks for Radar-Only Road-User Classification in Intelligent Intersections
authors: Mai Abourobea, Mohamed Atia, Karim Ismail
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Recent advancements in environment perception for intelligent transportation systems (ITS) and autonomous driving have largely relied on optical sensors such as cameras and LiDAR. However, these modalities degrade in low light or adverse weather, limiting their reliability for infrastructure-based perception. Radar offers a robust and cost-effective alternative capable of operating under diverse environmental conditions. Yet, radar-based classification remains underexplored due to the inherent challenges of radar point-cloud data, including sparsity, clutter, and limited spatial resolution. This paper introduces the Radar Spatial Transformer (RadarST) Network, a radar-only framework designed for road-user classification using annotated roadside radar point-cloud data. RadarST leverages spatial self-attention to model global geometric dependencies among radar reflections, capturing relationships across range, azimuth, elevation, Doppler, and radar cross section (RCS) to enhance object representation. Two training strategies are investigated: (1) an object-only model, where background points are excluded, and (2) a clutter-suppression model, where all radar points are retained and an adaptive clutter-gating mechanism learns to suppress non-informative background returns. The object-only RadarST model achieves a precision of 0.96, a recall of 0.963, and an F1-score of 0.961, while the clutter-suppression RadarST variant attains a precision of 0.81, a recall of 0.92, and an F1-score of 0.85, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed clutter-aware learning strategy. These findings highlight the suitability of radar-only Transformer architectures for smart fixed infrastructure, enabling reliable, all-weather perception for next-generation intelligent transportation systems (ITS).

title: Development of a Digital Twin Modeling System for Power Transmission and Transformation Equipment Based on Domestic Finite Element Analysis Software
authors: Haozhou Wang, Xianzhe Long, Yuran Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Several engineering challenges exist in online condition assessment of power transmission and transformation equipment, namely, the difficulty in unifying multi-physics mechanisms, the inability to conduct simulations in real-time, and the difficulty in cyclically updating models. To address these challenges, a digital twin modeling system based on domestically developed finite element analysis software was created. The system utilizes parametric geometry and mesh consistency strategies to ensure the portability of models across batches. A thermomechanical coupled finite element master model is created, and batch processing, service-oriented, and containerized calling interfaces are provided to achieve unified access with SCADA and online monitoring data. A state-parameter- observation assimilation framework is used to achieve online alignment of measured field quantities, and ROM/proxy models are used to reduce latency costs, creating a fast and accurate hierarchical inference approach. Multi-condition assessment and fault degradation verification show that the system has engineering usability in terms of consistency indicators, hotspot location, and alarm lead time, and can also meet the requirements of real-time SLA and governance.

title: Novel Feedforward and Feedback Control Strategy for Active DC-Bus Ripple Decoupling in Single-Phase PWM Rectifiers Using Bidirectional Buckxy–Boost Converter
authors: Kavitha B, Patnana Swarna Teja, Pinkymol K.P
abstract: In a single-phase PWM rectifier, second-order harmonic currents naturally appear in the DC bus. These harmonics also cause ripples in the output voltage. Traditionally a large electrolytic capacitors are used to absorb these second-order ripples, but this increases the overall system size. Moreover, using large capacitors can lead to discontinuous input current. To overcome these drawbacks, a power decoupling circuit is required to handle the second-order harmonic power with a smaller capacitor. In this work, a bidirectional buck–boost converter is used as an active decoupling circuit, and the proposed method integrates both feedforward and feedback loops to dynamically compute the current reference based on instantaneous system conditions, thereby improving the accuracy and responsiveness of the control also reducing capacitor size.

title: DDAN-RGAL: A Dual-Domain Adaptive Network With Reality-Gap Aware Learning for Field-Deployable Plant Disease Detection
authors: Syed Asif Ahmad Qadri, Nen Fu Huang, Yu-Hsiang Huang, Pin-Cheng Chan
abstract: Deep learning models achieve over 95% accuracy for plant disease detection in laboratory settings but suffer severe performance drops exceeding 30% when deployed under real agricultural conditions. This article presents dual-domain adaptive network with reality-gap aware learning (DDAN-RGAL), a unified framework that explicitly bridges this laboratory-to-field gap through three synergistic modules. The first, domain-invariant feature disentanglement, separates disease-intrinsic patterns from environmental artifacts using adversarial and mutual-information constraints. The second, cross-domain prototype learning with uncertainty, learns hierarchical laboratory and field disease prototypes while providing calibrated confidence through evidential deep learning. The third, reality-gap aware meta-classification, dynamically adapts expert classifiers based on domain characteristics inferred from each input. Experiments on PlantVillage, PlantDoc, and a real-world paddy field dataset collected using a field-deployed speed-dome camera and Raspberry Pi-based edge system demonstrate that DDAN-RGAL achieves 98.7% laboratory accuracy and 94.8% field accuracy, reducing the reality gap from 27.9% to 3.9%, a more than seven-fold improvement over existing methods. The framework provides highly reliable uncertainty estimates (expected calibration error: 0.042), inference (21.6 ms on GPU; 285 ms on Raspberry Pi 4), and strong data efficiency (85.3% accuracy with only 10% of the training data). These results establish DDAN-RGAL as a practical and field-ready solution for precision agriculture, enabling robust deployment of laboratory-trained models in real-world farming environments where both accuracy and reliability are essential.

title: Enhanced RGB-D Visual SLAM Using Photogrammetric Key-Frame Selection Method and Static Conditional Probability Model
authors: Ching-Chih Tsai, Ali Rospawan, Chi-Hsiang Li, Hung-Sheng Lin, Shih-Che Chen
abstract: The original ORB-SLAM3 has been designed with static backgrounds in mind. However, when dealing with moving obstacles in a dynamic environment, this method could lead to significant localization errors and inaccuracies in the generated maps. This paper presents an enhanced ORB-SLAM3 method to mitigate these errors caused by dynamic environments and moving obstacles. To achieve this, we integrate the known Photogrammetric Key-Frame Selection (PKS) algorithm to optimize keyframe selection, and a Static Conditional Probability Model (SCPM) for effective identification and eliminating dynamic map points in each frame. Extensive evaluation simulations demonstrate that our approach substantially reduces Absolute Trajectory Error (ATE) metrics compared to the original ORB-SLAM3, with details as follows; mean ATE by 84% on TUM datasets and 17.91% in Gazebo simulations, median ATE by 86% and 28.19%, root mean squared error (RMSE) of ATE by 80% and 29.45%, and standard deviation (STD) of ATE by 71.5% and 61.73%, respectively. This performance improvement comes with a modest computational cost increase of 12.5% on TUM datasets and 31% in Gazebo environments. Furthermore, the experimental validation with an autonomous mobile robot (AMR) in dynamic indoor environments further demonstrates that the proposed enhanced ORB-SLAM3 method achieves superior accuracy, performance, and robustness, with 51.56%, 33.49%, 27.46%, and 9.99% of improvement in terms of mean, median, RMSE and STD of ATE, making it particularly valuable for real-world robotic applications in crowded settings.

title: Unfolding High-order Correlations for Interpretable Multi-contrast MRI Super-resolution
authors: Qiangqiang Shen, Xuanqi Zhang, Peilin Chen, Zhiwei Zhong, Howard Leung, Shiqi Wang
abstract: Deep unfolding network has gained significant attention for magnetic resonance imaging super-resolution (MRI SR) due to its performance and interpretability. However, 1) existing methods predominantly focus on cross-contrast correlations while neglecting high-order correlations embedded within spatially adjacent slices in volumetric MRI data. 2) Their degradation models are optimized via the proximal gradient algorithm (PGA) that relies on manually designed hyperparameters (e.g., step size), often leading to overshooting or suboptimal solutions. To solve these limitations, we propose HocMRI, a deep unfolding multi-contrast MRI SR framework, which seamlessly integrates dual-prior modeling and hyperparameter-free PGA for enhanced reconstruction. Specifically, we first design a novel degradation model based on the dual-prior mechanism: an explicit prior based on low-rank tensor factorization to capture intra- and inter-slice dependencies, and an implicit prior leveraging a Mamba-based network with a novel 3D scanning strategy to further exploit high-order correlations across slices. Then, we derive a hyperparameter-free PGA to boost the traditional PGA, which employs a hyperbolic tangent function to dynamically control the gradient descent step, eliminating manual tuning while ensuring stable convergence with theoretical proofs. Based on the hyperparameter-free PGA, we develop an efficient iterative optimization algorithm to solve the degradation model and unfold it into a multi-stage deep network. Numerous experimental results from widely used MRI datasets demonstrate that our HocMRI achieves superior performance with enhanced efficiency compared to the state-of-the-art methods.

title: 3D Reconstruction of Vertebrae from Sparse 2D CT Data Using Conditional GANs and Transfer Learning
authors: Usman Khan, Maysoon Aldukhail
abstract: A three dimensional reconstruction of vertebrae from CT Scans is necessary for surgical planning, medical teaching and diagnosis. Traditional methods are computationally expensive, while state-of-the art deep learning techniques lack in effective handling of sparse or incomplete data.We propose a deep learning pipeline composed of transfer learning, convolutional autoencoders, and cGANs for reconstructing 3D point clouds of vertebrae from sparse 2D CT slices.The feature extractor, based on MobileNet, takes 2D input images and their augmentations and feeds them to an encoder-decoder network, producing 3D point cloud predictions. The generation of point cloud further improved under a cGAN framework with novel geometric loss functions, one of which is the peculiar shape combining cosine similarity, MSE, and Koenderink Shape Index. The proposed approach offers a 95.4% cosine similarity between predicted and ground-truth point clouds with just 64 sparse CT slices. Qualitative visualization depict anatomical plausibility and robustness against rotational artifacts. This work demonstrates that transfer learning via conditional GANs may create 3D reconstructions of the vertebral body in CT data with minimal doses, less acquisition time, and complete preservation of its clinical value. The method involves CT-machine-specific modeling and sparse-view medical image reconstruction.

title: VATS: Visual–Audio Multitask Transformer With Specialty Audio Encoder for Multimodal Deepfake Detection in CPSS
authors: Ruiqi Zha, Zhichao Lian, Qianmu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Detecting multimodal deepfakes has become a pressing concern due to the rising sophistication of generative techniques capable of creating highly convincing visual-speech synchronized deepfakes. As these manipulated contents proliferate on social media, remote education, smart meetings, smart homes, and virtual reality (VR), their increasing realism significantly impacts individuals, underscoring the urgent need for effective detection methods to curb their deceptive spread. Several research efforts have aimed to detect visual–audio deepfakes by examining inter-modal correlation and inter-class consistency. However, optimal detection results have yet to be achieved. Studies have indicated that detectors learn forged characteristics specific to particular generative methods and hide the real class in the visual modality, leading to a bias towards forgery distributions and degrading detection performance. Since audio modality data is a 2-D signal, we consider this bias in the audio modality to be more serious than in the visual modality, a conclusion we have verified through experiments. Therefore, an audio encoder that learns a universal audio distribution is indispensable for effective audio modality deepfake detection. In this work, we propose a new visual–audio deepfake detection method visual–audio multitask transformer with specialty audio encoder (VATS), designed to learn a more robust and comprehensive representation, thereby mitigating overfitting. The structure includes unimodal encoders for preliminary encoding and a dual-stream structure based on a multimodal framework for multilevel interaction and precise detection. To prevent the audio encoder from focusing excessively on specific identities or generator textures, we employ a pretrained speech encoder in the audio stream, while utilizing a linear projection encoder in the visual stream to focus on the fine-grained features of each video frame rather than the semantic information of the graphic. Our method comprises three key components: the fast temporal multimodal alignment and embedding module (FTAE), the modality interaction block (MIB), and the interpretable manipulated location module (IML). Experimental results on FakeAVCeleb demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms previous works in multimodal deepfake detection.

title: GRAFNeT: Guided Recursive Attention Fusion Network for Multimodal Medical Imaging
authors: Mamta Rani, Jyoti Yadav, Neeru Rathee, Vijay Mohan
abstract: This study introduces GRAFNeT (Guided Recursive Attention Fusion Network for Multimodal Medical Imaging), a novel multi-stage image fusion framework designed to achieve high-quality multi-modal integration with minimal computational overhead. The framework employs CNN feature extraction and attention modules, followed by an iterative refinement of fusion parameters at inference time. The methodology begins with adaptive weight normalization to dynamically balance modality contributions and redundancy reduction. Hybrid feature extraction integrates Sobel-based edge detection with ResNet-derived deep features to enhance anatomical structure representation. Present work with guided recursive attention, through metric guided optimization iteratively refine the fused features. Thus, emphasizing diagnostically significant regions. The consistency across modalities is ensured using multi-scale fusion with scale-specific refinement. Moreover, diagnostically reliable reconstruction is achieved using generalized metric integration involving SSIM, gradient preservation, and patch-wise alignment. The qualitative and quantitative evaluation were performed on three benchmark datasets, which shows GRAFNeT’s superior ability to preserve spatial fidelity and capture intricate multimodal details, proving it as a robust and clinically relevant fusion approach.

title: Enhanced Multivariate Fusion TSNN-based Dynamic Integration Error Detection for Unmanned Platforms’ Multibeam-Motion Sensor Systems in Internet of Underwater Things
authors: Jiawei Long, Jianhu Zhao, Xi Zhao, Wenbo Zhu, Yongcan Yu
abstract: A profound understanding of unique underwater terrain characteristics is crucial for the Internet of Underwater Things (IoUT). Currently, using unmanned platforms equipped with Multibeam Echosounder Systems (MBES) for underwater topographic scanning has become a key method and future trend to understand seabed topography. However, non-ideal integration between MBES transducers and motion sensors induces dynamic integration errors (time delay, motion scale, yaw misalignment, lever arm errors), which manifest as high-frequency ship-track orthogonal bathymetric undulations, severely limiting the accurate generation of high-resolution seabed topographic maps. To address this, this study innovatively proposes a multibeam dynamic error detection method based on time-series neural networks (TSNN). Its core innovations are: first, leveraging the characteristic that dynamic errors’ ship-track orthogonal bathymetric undulations are highly correlated with attitude; second, focusing on distinct manifestations of beam-angle-dependent and beam-angle-independent dynamic errors across different beam reception angles, laying a key basis for accurate error inversion. The method involves two core modules: the "MBES Bathymetric Sequence Trend Extraction Module" separates seabed terrain trends from undulations to obtain error-driven fluctuation signals; the "Dynamic Error Regression Module" realizes quantitative prediction of dynamic integration errors. Experimental results show the method can effectively eliminate high-frequency undulations in shallow-to-medium water MBES data, with processing efficiency meeting unmanned platforms’ real-time requirements—providing a new technical approach for high-resolution marine surveying and mapping to support IoUT’s reliable deployment and operation.

title: Mapping the Asymmetric Spatial Distribution of Urban Homeless Encampments Using High Resolution Satellite Imagery_supp1-3678098.docx
authors: Jiale Qian

title: An improved transformer-grid inductor magnetic integration for single-stage on-board charger
authors: Dinh Bao-Hung Nguyen, Huu-Phuc Kieu, Ba Phu Do, Yeonseo Yoon, Sewan Choi
abstract: This article proposes an improved integrated magnetic structure for the single-stage interleaved totem-pole AC-DC converter, which is applicable for on-board chargers (OBCs). The grid inductors are integrated into the high-frequency transformer, which eliminates the core and winding of grid inductors. This results in a reduction in the magnetic loss, core material volume, and total volume. The analytical comparison among three core candidates to implement the integrated magnetic structure is discussed. The four-leg integrated magnetic, whose total volume is 11% smaller than that of the prior art integration, is selected for further design. An optimized design based on the Figure of Merit (FoM) method is carried out to design the proposed magnetic. A single-phase 3.7kW single-stage OBC prototype with both discrete and integrated magnetic structures is assembled and tested. Compared to the discrete structure, the converter efficiency with the integrated one is 1% higher at light load, and 0.1% higher at full load. In addition, the integrated magnetic volume is 35% smaller than the discrete one. The designed module achieves 97.3% peak efficiency.

title: Generalizable Hierarchical Skill Learning via Object-Centric Representation_supp1-3678126.mp4
authors: Dian Wang
abstract: We present Generalizable Hierarchical Skill Learning (GSL), a novel framework for hierarchical policy learning that significantly improves policy generalization and sample efficiency in robot manipulation. One core idea of GSL is to use object-centric skills as an interface that bridges the high-level vision-language model and the low-level visual-motor policy. Specifically, GSL decomposes demonstrations into transferable and object-canonicalized skill primitives using foundation models, ensuring efficient low-level skill learning in the object frame. At test time, the skill-object pairs predicted by the high-level agent are fed to the low-level module, where the inferred canonical actions are mapped back to the world frame for execution. This structured yet flexible design leads to substantial improvements in sample efficiency and generalization of our method across unseen spatial arrangements, object appearances, and task compositions. In simulation, GSL trained with only 3 demonstrations per task outperforms baselines trained with 30 times more data by 15.5% on unseen tasks. In real-world experiments, GSL also surpasses the baseline trained with 10 times more data.

title: AI-Assisted Electronic Digital Twin for Capacitive Pressure Sensors Controlling Robotic Gripping
authors: Alessandro Massaro, Nicola Epicoco
abstract: The paper focuses on the analysis of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) classification of the pressure forces to control the gripping of a robotic hand. The goal is to structure an Electronic Digital Twin (EDT) model of capacitance sensors to classify robotic finger movements. The model simulates a robotic application of two fingers gripper (Figure (a)) by means of a circuit model based on the capacitive effect principle. The proposed approach is able to classify and apply a control signal using an AI engine trained by circuit simulations (Figure (b)) providing the theoretical hand responses to pressure forces. The AI analysis is initially performed to select the best algorithm for the classification of the gripping and its control (Figure (c)) by processing a dataset of an electronic glove characterized by different time series signals of capacitance sensors. Compared with other AI supervised algorithms, such as Random Forest (RF) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN), the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) exhibits the best performance for complex capacitive sensor systems. The CNN-EDT model is then successively applied to a system of two coupled capacitive sensors (Figure (d)) by proving the possibility to process even more complex robotic control systems, such as an electronic glove. The EDT is constructed by using LTSpice and KNIME open-source tools.

title: ContractionPPO: Certified Reinforcement Learning via Differentiable Contraction Layers
authors: Vrushabh Zinage, Narek Harutyunyan, Eric Verheyden, Fred Y. Hadaegh, Soon-Jo Chung
abstract: Legged locomotion in unstructured environments demands not only high-performance control policies but also formal guarantees to ensure robustness under perturbations. Control methods often require carefully designed reference trajectories, which are challenging to construct in high-dimensional, contact-rich systems such as quadruped robots. In contrast, Reinforcement Learning (RL) directly learns policies that implicitly generate motion, and uniquely benefits from access to privileged information, such as full state and dynamics during training, that is not available at deployment. We present ContractionPPO, a framework for certified robust planning and control of legged robots by augmenting Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) RL with a state-dependent contraction metric layer. This approach enables the policy to maximize performance while simultaneously producing a contraction metric that certifies incremental exponential stability of the simulated closed-loop system. The metric is parameterized as a Lipschitz neural network and trained jointly with the policy, either in parallel or as an auxiliary head of the PPO backbone. While the contraction metric is not deployed during real-world execution, we derive upper ContractionPPO TumblerNet RMA PPO Fig. 1: Comparison of trajectories for quadruped handstand using Con bounds on the worst-case contraction rate and show that these bounds ensure the learned contraction metric generalizes from simulation to real-world deployment. Our hardware experiments on quadruped locomotion demonstrate that ContractionPPO enables robust, certifiably stable control even under strong external perturbations. Videos of experiments are available at https://contractionppo.github.io/.

title: A Three-Stage Framework for Large Language Model–Assisted Formative Assessment and Actionable Feedback in Teacher Education
authors: Mengyi Bao, Cong Cao, Yuqian Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: The involvement of artificial intelligence in learning analytics and assessment feedback is reshaping the logical structure of traditional teaching. In particular, the application of large language models (LLMs) has accelerated the process of educational intelligence and provided new perspectives for learning assessment. However, as a crucial approach for the continuous optimisation of learning outcomes, the overall paradigm of formative assessment (FA) supported by LLMs has not yet been established. From evidence acquisition to feedback generation, functional coordination still lacks an integrated mechanism, and practical explorations remain fragmented. Therefore, this study proposes the Observe–Align–Coach Framework, which takes the stage characteristics of FA as its logical thread and constructs an integrated mechanism for LLM intervention. LLMs undertake the core tasks in the evidence acquisition and target alignment stages, after which the framework shifts to a human–AI collaborative mode to achieve a closed-loop assessment. By integrating evidence collection, target alignment, and feedback guidance, the framework realises the systematisation and traceability of the assessment process, providing a feasible approach for the intelligent transformation of FA under the involvement of LLMs.

title: Short Term Prediction of Complex Power Load Considering Power Fluctuation Characteristics
authors: Qiang Chang, Quan Cai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Clean Energy Storage and Power Engineering (CESPE)
abstract: Accurate power load forecasting is crucial for ensuring the safe and stable operation of the power system and optimizing the allocation of power resources. However, due to multiple uncertain factors such as weather, season, and social activities, short-term load fluctuations exhibit complex characteristics, making predictions challenging. To address this issue, this paper proposes a short-term load forecasting method that incorporates power fluctuation features. The approach first applies two-dimensional wavelet threshold denoising to preprocess the raw load data, mitigating noise interference. Subsequently, based on Copula theory, the nonlinear dependencies between load and multiple influencing factors are analyzed to extract fluctuation characteristics. Finally, a Least Squares Support Vector Machine (LSSVM) prediction model is constructed to achieve high-precision short-term load forecasting. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively tracks load variation trends, with the mean absolute percentage error controlled within 1.29%, significantly outperforming traditional methods in prediction accuracy. This study not only provides a novel solution for short-term load forecasting in power systems but also holds substantial practical significance for enhancing grid dispatch decision-making, improving the integration capacity of renewable energy, and ensuring the economical and reliable operation of the power system.

title: Advances in Deep Learning-Based Handwritten Devanagari Script Identification and Recognition
authors: Vinod R. Thakare, Akshit Samadhiya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Transformative Computing Technologies (ICTCT)
abstract: Recognition of handwritten script is an important task of optical character recognition (OCR) systems, especially for complex scripts, e.g., Devanagari script, used in several Indian languages such as Hindi, Marathi, and Sanskrit. The inherent challenges posed by variations in writing styles, compound characters, and intricate diacritics demand robust and intelligent recognition techniques. In recent years, deep learning has emerged as a powerful paradigm, significantly enhancing the performance of handwritten script identification and recognition systems. In this paper, review study of the various deep learning methods proposed in the literature for the identification and recognition of handwritten Devanagari script is explored. It throws light on the different Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and mixed types of models, which are used to overcome the intricacies of the Devanagari script. The paper also reviews popular datasets, preprocessing approaches, feature extraction techniques, and evaluation metrics from this field. Moreover, it discusses the main limitations, open problems, and opportunities in designing more efficient, scalable, or real-time OCR systems for Devanagari script recognition. The observations made are intended to assist researchers and practitioners in developing intelligent OCR solutions specifically for Indic scripts.

title: Gamifying Campus Life: A Project-Based Learning Initiative for Engineering Students Using AI and Serious Games
authors: Antonio Carlos Bento
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: This study explores an innovative educational framework that merges project-based learning (PBL) with gamification and artificial intelligence (AI) to address real-world challenges. In a 10 -week initiative, 30 engineering students at Monterrey Technological University were tasked with developing serious game simulators to improve campus well-being, aligning with Sustainable Development Goal 3. The methodology was grounded in Agile development, structured into four iterative sprints that guided teams from mockup design to fully functional prototypes. Students integrated a diverse technology stack, including Unity3D for game development, relational databases, and the Gemini API for AI-driven features like personalized feedback and interactive chatbots. Despite technical challenges such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> latency and integration hurdles, the initiative demonstrated high efficacy. Results showed that 70 % of students rated their experience as “Excellent,” and the project yielded six functional prototypes, such as an emergency preparedness trainer and a campus maintenance game, which were recognized at a university technology fair. This work underscores the significant potential of interdisciplinary, gamified PBL to boost student engagement, foster practical software engineering skills, and create tangible social impact, while also highlighting the need for robust foundational support in emerging technologies like AI.

title: A Study on the Multi-Agent Application Mechanism in Project-Based Learning from the Perspective of Human-Machine Collaboration
authors: Qiuchen Wang, Haiguang Fang, Zhihong Zheng, Xin Hong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Teacher Education (ICAITE)
abstract: Generative artificial intelligence, as a leading technology in the development of AI, is significantly reshaping the educational landscape. Intelligent agents, with capabilities for autonomous learning, reasoning, decision-making, and action execution, can adapt to environmental changes and achieve specific goals through iterative learning. These agents are becoming essential components of a new classroom teaching model supported by human-machine collaboration. From this perspective, this paper constructs a multi-agent application mechanism by examining the project-based learning model and integrating multi-agent workflow design, agent type classification, and the educational division of labor. Rooted in project-based learning, the mechanism introduces a collaborative working model for multi-agents. A practical application in the primary school science project-based course “How to Make a Spinning Top” demonstrates the innovative value of this mechanism, emphasizing its potential in advancing project-based education and facilitating the digital transformation of education.

title: Cryogenic to Room-Temperature Characterization of Modified Uni-Traveling-Carrier Photodiodes
authors: Dingran Gu, Qiushi Chen, Linze Li, Luyu Wang, Liqi Zhu, Zhidong Tang, Chang He, Juanjuan Lu, Xufeng Kou, Baile Chen
abstract: The development of large-scale quantum networks requires scalable interconnects operating from cryogenic to ambient temperatures. While conventional electrical interconnects pose thermal management and system complexity challenges, photonic interconnects enable efficient optical modulation and fiber transmission. Here, we present temperature-dependent characterization of a modified uni-traveling carrier photodetector (MUTC-PD) for quantum network applications. The 28 μm device exhibits stable responsivity (> 0.56 A/W), > 9 GHz bandwidth at –5 V bias, and good linearity from 5 K to 300 K.
