These robust characteristics identify the MUTC-PD as a promising candidate for scalable quantum photonic interconnects and provide design insights for next-generation cryogenic photodetectors.

title: Non-orthogonal Coding Against Information Leakage in Image Semantic Communication Systems
authors: Linlin Xiao, Buxiang Sheng, Zhiqiang Feng, Peisong Zhang, Donghong Cai, Zahid Khan
abstract: This paper focuses on the information leakage and secure encoding transmission in semantic communication systems. A two-path images feature-coupling coding transmission is proposed to improve the transmission efficiency and bandwidth utilization while ensuring the restoration quality of each image. In particular, the designed encoder first extracts the feature of each-path image and then performs superimposed encoding in the feature space. In order to separately restore the two images at the decoder without successive interference cancellation or other traditional multi-user estimation, we add the device vectors to train the encoder and decoder. Unlike embedding the device vectors into each image, we embed the device vectors in the feature space instead. Furthermore, we construct an inversion attack network based on generative adversarial networks, which can simulate a potential eavesdropper and use the received transmission signal to recover the original image. Therefore, we can evaluate the system’s defense capabilities against model inversion attacks. Moreover, we use Huawei Ascend Atlas 200I DK A2 development board to conduct hardware simulation of legal user communication with proposed feature-coupling coding and the interception of information by eavesdroppers. Experimental results show that the proposed feature-coupling coding has better Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio performance compared to the data–coupling coding and effectively resists information leakage among different noise level.

title: Dual Time-Frequency Feature Fusion Framework for Incipient ITSC Fault Diagnosis in Induction Motors
authors: Jinhan Zhou, Xilian Wang, Junfeng Gui, Jianfeng Hong, Huabin Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Existing time-frequency feature analysis based on deep-learning framework for induction motor (IMs) fault diagnosis predominantly focus on single amplitude spectrum, overlooking the potential of phase spectrum and their fusion. This study proposes a dual time-frequency fusion diagnostic method for incipient inter-turn short-circuit (ITSC) to address this gap. The method uniquely integrates features from the time and dual frequency domain (both amplitude and phase spectrum) of vibration signals. Firstly, the feasibility of utilizing dual frequency domain of vibration signals for detecting ITSC is validated through theoretical derivation. Subsequently, a multi-channel cross-attention mechanism is designed to extract and fuse time-frequency features. An adaptive gated dual-spectral fusion strategy is then employed to dynamically weight and integrate the amplitude and phase spectral features, capturing comprehensive fault signatures. Experimental investigations and data acquisition were conducted on induction motors with ITSC to evaluate this model under multiple fault scenarios. The method is demonstrated that it achieves a fault diagnosis accuracy of 99.4% even under complex operating conditions when the short-circuit degree is as low as 0.4%. The results confirm that incorporating phase information with proposed structure significantly enhances diagnostic precision and fault severity discrimination, showcasing the model's potential for reliable detection of incipient faults.

title: Simultaneous Self-Localization and Base Station Localization with Resonant Beam
authors: Guangkun Zhang, Wen Fang, Mingliang Xiong, Qingwen Liu, Mengyuan Xu, Yunfeng Bai, Mingqing Liu, Siyuan Du
abstract: High-precision positioning in GPS-denied environments is a challenging yet critical technology. Resonant beam positioning (RBP) utilizes a resonant beam with properties such as energy focusing, self-establishment, self-alignment, and passive operation, offering a promising solution for this task. However, traditional RBP algorithms require a fixed number of resonant beam base stations, which can be costly to expand coverage. To address this limitation, we propose a distributed resonant beam positioning (DRBP) system that simultaneously estimates the base station and mobile target (MT) positions. Firstly, the MT receives resonant beam samples to locate the base station in the limited field of view (FoV) region. Subsequently, it estimates its own position by tracking the apparent motion of these newly localized base stations. During moving, the DRBP system facilitates self-positioning on the MT side, enabling dynamic expansion of both the number of base stations and the coverage area. Numerical results demonstrate that DRBP achieves a positioning root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.1 m and a rotation RMSE of 2<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating the system's high accuracy.

title: Design and Simulation of a Composite Beam Structure Based on the Differential Capacitive MEMS Accelerometer
authors: Jie Xiang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: To extend the measurement range, enhance the sensitivity, and simplify the structure of a differential capacitive MEMS accelerometer, this paper designs a novel differential capacitive accelerometer with composite beam structure via theoretical modeling. The high sensitivity and large range characteristics of the proposed accelerometer are verified through finite element simulation using COMSOL Multiphysics. The composite beam structure consists of a single-crystal silicon arch beam and a silicon carbide straight beam, achieving a measurement range of 100g. Within the range of 20g, the mechanical sensitivity reaches 0.089μm/g, and the capacitive sensitivity reaches 50.58fF/g; within the range of 20g to 100g, the mechanical sensitivity is 0.0118μm/g, and the capacitive sensitivity is 6.689fF/g, with good linearity observed in each interval. This research enhances the applicability of differential capacitive MEMS accelerometers and demonstrates considerable potential for application in various fields demanding high-performance inertial sensors. It also provides a theoretical foundation for subsequent studies on composite beam accelerometers.

title: Research on Auxiliary Algorithms for 3D Human Body Modeling in Virtual Try-On Technology: A Case Study of Parametric Human Body Model Algorithms
authors: Fei Jing, Xia Wang, Li Zhu, Lingzhi Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: The rise of online shopping, particularly for clothing, has become a mainstream form of consumption among young people. The development of the metaverse and AI video technology has further propelled virtual try-on technology as a key bridge connecting online and offline experiences. 3D human body modeling serves as the core technology, with its precision directly impacting the personalization of models. This paper studies auxiliary algorithms for 3D human body modeling, introduces three mainstream methods, and explores algorithm optimization strategies tailored to the needs of virtual try-on.

title: TEGCA-DTI: Drug-Target Interaction Prediction Model Based on Transformer-ECA and GaAN and Multi-head Cross-Attention
authors: Yonggui Wang, Zhuozhuo Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: Drug–Target Interaction (DTI) prediction plays a pivotal role in multiple domains of drug development and is one of the key tasks in computer-aided drug discovery. Deep learning methods have gradually become the core approach for this task, by encoding drug molecules and protein sequences separately, followed by modeling the association between them through feature fusion modules. To effectively capture long-range dependencies in protein sequences, accurately guide the selection of key active substructures of drugs, and fully model fine-grained interactions between cross-modal data. To this end, this paper proposes a novel DTI prediction model, TEGCA-DTI. The model employs a Transformer encoder at the protein sequence terminus and introduce the Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module. At the drug molecule level, graph convolutional networks (GCNs) are employed to extract structural features, followed by the integration of gated attention networks (GaANs). Furthermore, a multi-head cross-attention mechanism was employed to achieve fine-grained alignment and information fusion between drug and protein features. Experimental results demonstrate that TEGCA-DTI achieves significant performance improvements in metrics such as AUROC and AUPR, validating the effectiveness of the proposed model in terms of expression capability and generalization performance.

title: Design of Hybrid Nonlinear Controller for Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converter Used for Hydrogen Electrolyzer Load
authors: Tejaswi M Timsani, Satish Kumar, Dharmpreet Kundlas, Animesh Kumar Sahoo
abstract: Dual Active Bridge (DAB) dc-dc converters are mostly preferable for hydrogen electrolyzers (HEs) at grid scale due to inherent galvanic isolation and high voltage ratio conversions. The controllers for this converter can be linear or non-linear. Linear controllers, especially proportional and integral controllers, are easy to implement. However, they are highly affected due to several system disturbances. This paper presents a hybrid nonlinear control strategy for the DAB DC-DC converter operating in buck mode, intended for hydrogen electrolyser load applications. It is named as robust backstepping fast integral terminal sliding mode control (RBFITSMC). It consists of an outer non-linear recursive backstepping controller (BSC) and an inner FITSMC. The dynamics of the DAB converter are derived. BSC is implemented to accurately track the output voltage and generate a virtual reference current simultaneously. FITSMC is implemented to ensure the fast convergence of input current with the help of quick-reaching law. The proposed hybrid controller, along with a 500 KW DAB converter, is modelled using MATLAB/Simulink. The HE load is modelled using its dynamic electrochemical equivalent model. The robustness of the proposed controller is tested against output voltage tracking, input voltage disturbances and changes in switching frequencies. In all the scenarios, the proposed controller provides fast and accurate response.

title: Welcome Letter
authors: N/A
abstract: It is with immense pleasure, we welcome you to the 12<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> edition of the National Power Electronics Conference (NPEC 2025), one of India's most prestigious and long-standing conferences in the field of Power Electronics. We are honoured to host this distinguished event at the National Institute of Technology Calicut, from December 14 to 16, 2025. NPEC 2025 is organized by the Department of Electrical Engineering, NIT Calicut, and technically co-sponsored by IEEE Kerala Section, IEEE IAS, IES, PELS and PES Chapters. With a legacy of over two decades, NPEC has been hosted primarily at various IITs across India. NIT Calicut is proud to host the 12th edition, reaffirming our institute's growing academic footprint and commitment to power electronics research.

title: ICCE 2026 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)

title: WBG-based Reduced Switch Self-balanced Symmetrical Capacitors 7-level MLI for Grid Applications
authors: Rajanikanta Sahoo, Anurag Tiwari, Molay Roy
abstract: This study introduces an innovative single-DC source seven-level inverter featuring eight switches, two diodes, and two capacitors, which can achieve a voltage boosting factor of three. The POD-PWM method is utilized for producing the switching signals. The work covers the complete design aspects, including circuit configuration, modulation strategy, capacitor and inductor sizing, loss evaluation, and performance comparison with existing inverter structures. The comparative outcomes indicate that the proposed topology necessitates fewer components while offering significantly greater boosting capability than numerous contemporary alternatives. The effectiveness of the seven-level inverter is verified through both simulation and experimental investigation. MATLAB/Simulink is used for simulation studies, whereas a laboratory prototype, controlled by the TMS320F28379D digital controller, validates the proposed design experimentally.

title: Advanced Planning and Scheduling Framework for Manufacturing Operation with Demand Forecasting
authors: Dohun Kim, Jinho Park, Wonjong Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS) has been adopted as a systematic decision-support tool to address global supply chain instability and increasing demand volatility. This study introduces an AI-based APS framework that integrates demand-order forecasting models and constraint-based scheduling algorithms to enhance responsiveness, efficiency, and flexibility in manufacturing operations. The system integrates forecasting, planning, scheduling, and execution feedback based on data linkage with external systems such as ERP, PLM, and MES. By applying deep learning models such as LSTM and PatchTST, the system improved demand forecasting accuracy, while the CP-based scheduler optimized production plans under resource and delivery constraints. The results demonstrate that forecast-driven scheduling enhances responsiveness and reliability in manufacturing operation.

title: Research on Prediction and Data Modeling Based on Machine Learning and Multi-Indicator Evaluation
authors: Yuxiao Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Power, Electronics and Green Energy (ICPEGE)
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical decision-making optimization model for multi-UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) collaborative scheduling in a multi-unit defense system. A three-tier decision framework, namely "Threat Assessment - Task Allocation - Parameter Optimization," is constructed. In the threat assessment layer, a quantitative evaluation model is established based on the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP). The task allocation layer implements an optimal resource allocation for UAV-unit matching through a 0-1 integer programming model. In the parameter optimization layer, a Multi-Population Particle Swarm Optimization (MPSO) algorithm is employed to address a 60-dimensional high-dimensional continuous optimization problem. The MPSO algorithm converges after 168 iterations, achieving a system-weighted total shielding duration of 13.03 seconds. The optimal deployment parameters for each UAV are detailed in result3.xlsx. This study provides a systematic approach for enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of multi-UAV collaborative operations in defense scenarios.

title: ER-NeRV: Training Error-Resilient Neural Representations for Videos
authors: Ariel Justine N. Panopio, Dara Varam, Mohamed Hefeeda, Ihab Amer
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Neural representations for videos (NeRV) are emerging as a learning-based alternative to traditional video codecs, enabling efficient and low-complexity playback across consumer and edge devices. These neural decoders increasingly operate on energy-optimized hardware where memory faults can degrade perceptual quality. In this study, we investigate the robustness of NeRV under simulated hardware-induced faults. We introduce ER-NeRV (Error-Resilient Neural Representations for Videos): a robust and efficient neural video codec to improve NeRV’s resilience to such errors while maintaining its efficiency. By exposing the models to controlled bit-flip noise, ER-NeRV improves robustness to numerical faults possible in low-power edge execution. Compared to NeRV, we demonstrate that ER-NeRV can achieve a higher peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) and multi-scale structural similarity index (MS-SSIM) under increasing bit error rates across variable bit-width regimes, while maintaining visual fidelity with negligible overhead.

title: Athena’s Light - Accessible, highly portable research combining fNIRS, EEG, & PPG
authors: Mitchell Seitz, Kinkini Monaragala, Meher Malik Patel, Christina Le, Homey Poon, Steve Mann
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Muse headbands are commercially available bio-sensing devices designed to measure human brain and related biometric data for meditation, research, and neurofeedback training using EEG, PPG, and fNIRS. This study explores the use of Muse devices with MuseCroc Mobile, a research tool currently under development for use in neuroscience research. Topics covered include preliminary validation of the data collection pipeline by observing changes in brain activity during eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions, analyzing the data stream processing behavior of MuseCroc Mobile, and the use of this software for developing an understanding of the fNIRS system of the Muse S Athena.

title: Facial Palsy Simulation Using a Partially Fine-tuned ControlNet with Contour-guided and Organ-size Deformation Conditioning
authors: Atsushi Tajima, Masataka Seo, Yen-Wei Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Facial nerve palsy causes impairment in voluntary facial movements, and its severity in Japan is typically evaluated using the 40-point Yanagihara method. However, visual inspection introduces inter-rater variability, and patient privacy limits the use of real images. In this work, we present a simulation approach that synthesizes facial palsy images using a Stable Diffusion model with a partially fine-tuned ControlNet. Fine-tuning is limited to timesteps corresponding to abstract feature representations, allowing the generation of pathological expressions while suppressing the reproduction of actual identity. Moreover, a preprocessing step modifies the size and position of facial organs and incorporates contour information from non-patient faces, thereby enhancing expression fidelity while ensuring privacy protection. This framework has potential applications in medical training and clinical research.

title: Optimization of Optical Sensors Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Akash Kumar Pradhan, Mandala Harika, Mursal Ayub Hamdani, M Laxmi Prasanna Rani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: In this work, a Machine Learning technique is employed for the inverse design of a sensor based on a one-dimensional photonic crystal structure. A dataset relating sensor reflectivity to structural parameters—such as the number of layers, refractive indices, and layer widths—is used for model training. The trained model enables rapid prediction of structural parameters without extensive numerical simulations, allowing custom-made sensors to be designed within milliseconds. This approach significantly reduces design complexity, cost, and time, while advancing thin-film optics and photonic device fabrication.

title: A Note on a Method for Repeated Classifier Update Based on Features of Normal Driving Vehicles
authors: Shoichiro Imoto, Chinami Fukui, Masahiro Yagi, Sho Takahashi, Toshio Yoshii
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Consumer Electronics (ICCE)
abstract: Recently, various methods for detecting dangerous driving vehicles from traffic videos using machine learning have been studied. However, because road environments change over time, the performance of the same classifier degrades with continued use. To address this, methods that automatically update the classifier have been studied. Nevertheless, their application to traffic videos has been limited. In this paper, based on our previous work, we propose a method for repeatedly updating the classifier using only vehicles commonly observed in daily traffic. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through experiments using traffic videos recorded on public roads.

title: AI-Enhanced MEMS-Based Weather and Environmental Condition Monitoring
authors: Sudha R, C. Padmavathy, Poonam Verma, Upkar Singh Kandhari, B. Shoban Babu, Kaustubh Kumar Shukla
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Communication, IoT and Security (ICISCoIS)
abstract: A unique collaboration of MEMS which is known as Micro Electro Mechanical Systems and Artificial Intelligence grabbing the attention of maximum researchers and scientist. The major beauty of this technology is with the help of AI diverse kind of computational intelligence can be developed and MEMS helps to analyze and optimize the step-by-step analysis. Through this research a very unique tool COMSOL Multiphysics software has been used to perform the simulation and analysis. Additionally, the several analysis parts have been done with the help of AI based different algorithms and computational intelligence to obtain the accuracy and overall optimized model. This research majorly focuses on the automatic identification of environmental patters, noise filtration and sustainable environment with the help of AI and MEMS. There are three major parts of this research paper. In first part a brief understanding about AI and MEMS has been identified. In second stage COMSOL analysis is available in which different MEMS based materials played a vital and notable role. At last, in third stage uniqueness and feature outcomes has been pointed out with the help of several practical approaches and real-time based testing.

title: Confidence-Driven Adaptive Convolution Mechanism for Sonar 3D Image Feature Extraction
authors: Xinzheng Pu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive variable-scale convolution kernel computation method based on 3D sonar image confidence, aiming to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of feature extraction in underwater target detection. Leveraging the physical characteristics of sonar imaging, the method models target confidence across spatial directions, dynamically computes the scale parameters of the 3D convolution kernel along the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>-, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$z$</tex> - axes, enabling an adaptive adjustment mechanism that employs smaller kernels in high-confidence regions and larger ones in low-confidence regions. The proposed approach effectively mitigates range-induced resolution non-uniformity, enhances spatial consistency of deep features, reduces computational redundancy, and improves real-time performance and model robustness while preserving detection accuracy. It is particularly suitable for intelligent perception modules in underwater electromechanical automation systems.

title: A New Method to Establish Smart Transformer-Based Meshed Hybrid Ac and Dc Distribution Grids
authors: Chandan Kumar, Sahil Gaurav, Somnath Meikap, Hitesh Malviya
abstract: Smart transformer (ST)-based meshed hybrid microgrids consisting of ac and dc distribution lines have recently been proposed. However, such solutions demand laying of additional distribution lines in the electric grid for forming dc grids, resulting in additional costs and space requirements. Generally, the loads are predominantly single-phase with limited three-phase loads in the low voltage (LV) ac distribution grid. Considering such load composition in LV distribution grid, this paper proposes a new method to establish ST-based meshed hybrid grid consisting of ac and dc grids. The three-phase dcac power converter of the conventional ST is replaced with a single-phase dc-ac converter, and one of the phases of existing three-phase ac distribution along with the neutral wire is used for forming single-phase ac distribution grid. The remaining two phases of the existing three-phase ac distribution grid are connected to the LVdc link of the ST to form dc distribution grid. Single-phase distributed generation (DG) converters injecting power from DG systems are utilized to form the ac-dc meshed hybrid microgrid. A three-phase converter is used to support three-phase load by taking power from the LVdc line. The simulation results have been shown to verify the performance of the proposed configuration.

title: Observational Study on Streamer Discharge Characteristics in Long Air Gaps Under High Altitude Conditions
authors: Jun Kang, Jingwen Ku, Xuanrui Zhang, Junhao Li
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Electrical Automation and Artificial Intelligence (ICEAAI)
abstract: Streamer discharge serves as the initial stage of gap breakdown and plays a decisive role in the formation of subsequent leaders. While extensive research has been conducted on short gaps, studies on streamer development in long air gaps of several m remain limited, especially under high-altitude conditions. In this study, we established a multi-dimensional synchronous observation platform to investigate the positive streamer discharge in a 3 m rod-plane gap subjected to lightning impulse voltage. Using an 8-frame ICCD camera with nanosecond-level time resolution, we captured the spatiotemporal evolution of streamer morphology and velocity. The results indicate that the streamer development exhibits three typical stages corresponding to the voltage evolution: rapid initiation, accelerated propagation during voltage saturation, and velocity decay. We observed that electrode geometry significantly influences the initial streamer morphology; specifically, hemispherical electrodes produce needle-like branched structures while conical electrodes result in diffuse patterns. Furthermore, statistical analysis reveals that the inception voltage of the first streamer is largely independent of the applied impulse peak, whereas the peak current shows a strong positive correlation. These findings provide critical data for refining discharge models and optimizing external insulation design in high-altitude power transmission engineering.

title: Adaptive Resource Optimization in Cloud Infrastructure via QDASOA and NDARBA Algorithms
authors: R Jayaraman, Naresh R
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Communication and Computers (ELEXCOM)
abstract: Task allocation and load balancing are vital issues in cloud computing, especially in the Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) model, where resources and virtual machines (VMs) are inadequate. The current paper presents a new concept of improving cloud resource management and at the same time bolstering VM resilience against the possible threats of resource significantly. The optimization of cloudlets placement in virtual machines using Quantum-Driven Adaptive Surfer Optimization Algorithm (QDASOA) is conducted based on intertwining quantum-driven principles and surfer-based methods. QDASOA is able to minimize makespan, execution time, and have cloudlet deadlines met so as to reduce attack surfaces that can occur due to resource contention and underutilization. The optimized cloudlet allocation that is produced by QDASOA is the base of the following optimization. It was based on this that the Nebula Dynamic ANFIS Resource Balancing Algorithm (NDARBA) dynamically balances the load between data centers. This strategy also deals with uncertainties and makes sure that SLA will be achieved because of adapting to dynamic workload and current situation of the system. The simulation includes the testing of a variety of cloudlet environments (with set-ups of 10 to 1000 cloudlets), and virtual machine environments (set-ups of 2 to 6 VMs), with variable task lengths and deadlines to give consideration of actual cloud scenarios. The experimental evidence indicates that a key performance indicators, such as the shortened makespan, execution time and increased use of the resources are significantly increased, and additionally, that it increases the system robustness to possible VM-specific threats in comparison to both the tradition model and the current state of the art practices

title: Adaptive Edge-AI and Digital Twin Coordination for Resilient Energy Management in Decentralized Microgrids
authors: Vanitha. C.N, Anusuya. P
abstract: The rapid adoption of renewable energy sources has accelerated the evolution of decentralized microgrids, which require intelligent and adaptive energy management strategies to handle variable generation and load dynamics. This paper presents CET-Energy: Continual Edge-AI with Cooperative Digital Twins, a framework that integrates continual learning at the edge with cooperative digital twins to enable adaptive, decentralized, and bandwidth-efficient energy management. Each microgrid is modeled as a lightweight digital twin, which forecasts load and generation, updates its model incrementally, and exchanges compressed updates with neighboring twins. Simulation results on a synthetic multi-microgrid dataset show that CET-Energy achieves an overall improvement of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}-\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> compared to state-of-the-art baselines, enhancing forecasting accuracy, system resilience, and communication efficiency. The framework offers a scalable and practical solution for real-time energy management in dynamic IoT-enabled microgrids.

title: High-Efficiency Tandem Quantum Dot Light-Emitting Diodes with Ultralow Turn-on Voltage
authors: Tian Liu, Kuibao Yu, Hao Liu, Jiahao Wang, Fushan Li, Hailong Hu
abstract: Energy level mismatch and interface defects in the charge generation layer (CGL) give rise to an increased turn-on voltage and degraded operational stability in tandem quantum dot light-emitting diodes (QLEDs). Herein, we propose a small-molecule ammonium succinate (AmSuc) for the interfacial modification of the CGL. This modification strategy passivates the surface defect states of ZnO through molecular coordination interactions and lowers its conduction band energy level, thereby substantially facilitating efficient charge separation and transport at the CGL interface. As a result, the modulated QLED achieves an ultralow turn-on voltage of merely 2.0 V and a high EQE of 44.8%. Importantly, the device exhibits remarkably improved operational stability, with an exceptional T95 lifetime (@ 1000 cd m-2) of 35,136 h, representing the longest operational lifetime reported to date for bottom-emitting tandem QLEDs.

title:
A Control Approach for Performance Improvement of MMR-Based SST in H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Electrolyzer Applications
authors: Zhengzhao Li, Reza Mirzadarani, Jarno van der Weg, Werner Heyns, Robin van der Sande, Mohamad Ghaffarian Niasar, Aditya Shekhar, Pavol Bauer, Zian Qin
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper introduces a novel control strategy for Modular Multilevel Resonant converters (MMR) in Solid-State Transformer (SST) applications, with a focus on medium-voltage conversion for hydrogen electrolyzers. The article first reviews voltage control methods in MMR, analyzing their operational principles and regulation capabilities. A continuous modulation index control method with double-step staircase waveform modulation is then proposed, simplifying the control scheme to a single control variable while maintaining robust controllability. Meanwhile, the proposed approach maintains comparable power loss and harmonic performance to existing methods under the investigated operating conditions. Simulations and experiments are conducted to verify the feasibility and practical implementation of the proposed approach.

title: MuLTEcast-Bandit: Lightweight Adaptive Multicast/Unicast Selection for Massive Event Streaming in 5G/6G Edge Networks
authors: Omar Dario Delgado Brito
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Unicast adaptive bitrate (UABR) streaming maximizes individual Quality of Experience (QoE) but wastes radio re-sources when thousands request the same content simultaneously. Conversely, static multicast saves spectrum yet degrades QoE because it ignores time-varying context. We present MuLTEcast-Bandit, a lightweight contextual bandit (LinUCB) that runs at the mobile edge and decides, per group and decision epoch, whether to transmit via multicast or unicast and at which bitrate, using features such as group size, channel quality, cell load, and recent stall events. Across massive-event scenarios, MuLTEcast-Bandit consistently approaches the QoE of pure unicast while substantially reducing spectrum consumption, and it clearly out-performs static multicast and threshold-based heuristics without manual tuning. These findings highlight a practical trade-off: near-unicast QoE at a fraction of the resource cost, enabled by a deployable, computation-light policy.

title: Early Prediction of Pregnancy Risk based on Cardiometabolic and Psychological Factors using Machine Learning
authors: Patatri Acharya, Sudipta Acharya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Early detection of high-risk pregnancies is critical for the improvement of maternal and fetal outcomes, especially in resource-constrained environments. In this study, we propose a machine learning-based method for classifying pregnancy risk levels (categorized as high or low) using a multidimensional dataset comprising cardiometabolic and psychological factors. A dataset with 1,205 observations was obtained from the Kaggle website, having features like Age, Systolic BP, Diastolic BP, Blood Sugar, Body Temperature, Previous Complications, Preexisting Diabetes, BMI, Gestational Diabetes, Heart Rate and Mental Health and Heart Rate. Data preprocessing was done using Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) for class balancing, one-hot encoding for handling categorical values and feature normalization. Twelve classification models were evaluated: Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Naïve Bayes, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, CatBoost, LightGBM, and a multi-layer perceptron (MLP). Experiments were conducted using both 80:20 and 70:30 train–test splits. This study takes into consideration mental health data of the pregnant individual along with physical health data and finally introduces a predictive model based on Gradient Boosting giving 99.13% accuracy with 80:20 split for identifying high-risk pregnancies. The proposed solution offers a scalable, interpretable, and clinically relevant tool that can aid healthcare professionals in early risk detection and intervention planning, thus helping to improve maternal care and reduce mortality rates.

title: Peak Delta Frequency Regulates the Timing of Decisions
authors: Logan Peters, Gregory Bales, Vincent Tran, Ariana Moghbel, Zhaodan Kong, Karen Moxon
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Despite extensive study on how the brain decides, the neurophysiological underpinnings of when a decision is made remain poorly understood. Previous modeling work in our lab using human intracranial local field potentials demonstrated that the time at which a decision was made was time-locked to a particular phase of the participants on-going slow wave activity (delta, .5-4Hz). To evaluate whether the on-going delta was driving the time of decision, we repeated our study but forced participants to make a decision at random times throughout their delta cycle. We hypothesized that if delta was driving the time of decision, the phase of the delta prior to decision would be well time locked to the time of decision. Alternatively, if the decision drove the phase of delta by resetting the phase at the time of choice, the time of decision would be time-locked to the phase of the on-going delta after choice. Because we are interested in this low frequency, we could use surface electroencephalogram (EEG). Results showed that participants consistently made their decision during a specific phase of their peak delta (.5-4hz) cycle, regardless of where in their delta cycle they were prompted to make their decision, consistent with our prior study using intracranial local field potentials. Moreover, time of decision was phase-locked to the on-going delta prior to choice selection. Since it has been shown that information about decision making is widely distributed across the brain, these results suggest that delta oscillations could be used to synchronize information from multiple brain regions.

title: Live Demonstration: Continuous Processing of Event-Data with Graph Convolutional Neural Networks Implemented for SoC FPGA
authors: Piotr Wzorek, Kamil Jeziorek, Marcin Kowalczyk, Krzysztof Blachut, Tomasz Kryjak, Marek Gorgon
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: For perception systems in mobile robotics - particularly in demanding, highly dynamic environments - event cameras (DVS - Dynamic Vision Sensors) are being increasingly utilised as an alternative to conventional vision sensors. The real-time processing of the registered spatio-temporal sparse point cloud must satisfy stringent requirements in terms of latency, throughput, and energy efficiency. In this demonstration, we present our method for implementing Graph Convolutional Neural Networks on a heterogeneous SoC FPGA platform, aimed at meeting these constraints. We address the challenges associated with integrating event-based sensors with reconfigurable hardware, as well as the intricate relationship between latency, performance, and hardware resource utilisation.

title: Data as Property: Reimagining Digital Rights under Contemporary Legal Systems
authors: Nalini Bhadwal, Chander Parkash Singh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Digital revolution has also challenged the conventional thinking regarding ownership thereby requiring a re-definition of digital rights in modern judicial systems. The theoretical roots of data property rights are explored in this research in terms of Locke labor theory, the theory of will by Kant and the theory of justice by Rawls. It solves the problem of duality of data, both as legal property and nonrival good, discovering the dark sides of law and jurisdiction issues when it comes to digital property. The concept of the potential of technological solutions like blockchain and differential privacy to help support Promise-Based Ownership models and protect data ownership entities are presented in the paper to protect data privacy. It also examines economic issues in terms of market failure and a conflict between incentives to innovate and safeguard the public good. Such ethical considerations as consent as well as rights of a data-subject are underlined as pivotal in the field of responsible policy-making. The study suggests a comprehensive theory of data property rights through the principle of adaptive legal framework, global collaborators, and online defence to the steady the search, the rights of person and the public eudaemonia.

title: Dynamic Pricing Scheduling and Mobile Charging Scheme for Electric Vehicles Based on Cloud and Fog Computing
authors: Cui Gu, Ke Gu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Recently, there has been a widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), aiming to fully supplant vehicles powered by internal combustion engines. However, the availability of electrical resources at charging stations and parking areas is limited, so an optimized charging scheme needs to be developed. In this paper, leveraging a cloud-fog computing architecture, we propose a dynamic pricing scheduling and mobile charging scheme for electric vehicles. In our scheme, we first develop a cloud-fog computing-based system model that assists EV users in selecting appropriate charging points and provides a foundation for unified scheduling. Second, we propose a real-time dynamic pricing scheme driven by time-of-use tariffs and congestion at charging points, which can avoid the situation where some charging points are too congested while others are empty. Third, we propose an enhanced scheduling and routing algorithm designed to minimize both time and cost expenses. This algorithm leverages fuzzy logic control and Dijkstra's method to address the complexities of scheduling and routing challenges effectively. Finally, we also propose a standby program for users whose power is too low to reach a charging point by arranging for them to be charged by a mobile charging truck. Our scheme achieves the dual objectives of minimized user cost and maximized charge cycles, as evidenced by experimental results.

title: Customized Power Grid Solutions for Mitigating IR Drop and Noise in Advanced Process Nodes
authors: Praveenkumar Jayakumar, Sudhagar G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As semiconductor technology advances into lower nodes such as 7nm and below, power integrity and power consumption have become critical concerns in digital VLSI design. One of the key contributors to power inefficiencies and reliability issues in these nodes is the degradation of the Power Grid due to increased current densities and limited routing resources. This paper presents the importance of Power Grid optimization as a fundamental step toward achieving power-efficient designs in lower technology nodes. It highlights the significant role of Power Grid enhancement during the Floor planning stage, where early architectural decisions can influence the performance and integrity of the entire power delivery network (PDN). By incorporating intelligent power strap planning, early IR-drop and electromigration (EM) analysis, and layout-aware power grid synthesis, it is possible to reduce dynamic and static power losses while improving voltage stability across the chip. The proposed methodology demonstrates measurable improvements in power delivery efficiency and design reliability, making it a crucial consideration in next-generation system-on-chip (SoC) implementations. This can be achieved by understanding the routing resources available, complexity of the design and its requirements. Power Grid improvement, shielding of the clock nets to mitigate noise and signoff checks are explored in this work.

title: Simultaneous Detection of Anomalous Nodes and Edges in Graphs
authors: Abdullah Karaaslanli, Selin Aviyente, Panagiotis A. Traganitis
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Graph anomaly detection is an important research problem that aims to identify structures in a graph that deviate significantly from the rest of the graph. Existing work proposed various approaches to detect anomalous nodes, edges or sub-graphs using tools from machine learning and statistical signal processing. Nevertheless, the majority of existing methods are limited to detecting only a single type of anomalous object, that is, they consider detection of node, edge, or subgraph anomalies as separate tasks. Real world graphs can simultaneously include multiple types of anomalous objects, motivating the development of new algorithms that can perform joint detection. This work proposes an algorithm that aims to simultaneously identify node and edge anomalies. In particular, we consider graphs whose topology includes anomalous edges and whose nodal attributes are contaminated with anomalous labels. The proposed approach uses an autoencoder to find node embeddings that are informative for identification of both types of anomalies. A sequential ensemble learning process is also put forth to train the autoencoder to reduce the effect of anomalies on training. Preliminary results showcase the potential of this novel approach.

title: Co-Design of Electric Drives and Mechanical Transmission for Enhanced Efficiency in Electric Vehicles
authors: Kbvsr Subrahmanyam, B.Satish Kumar, O. Rajeshwari, B.Nagi Reddy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The powertrain of an electric vehicle (EV) emphasizes the urgency of the electric vehicles to improve on efficiency, long range, and overall performance. Design of electric drives and mechanical transmissions has traditionally been carried out in segregated ways and often lead to less-than-optimal use of energy and higher operational losses. In this paper, the authors have met this challenge by coming up with a co-design framework that integrates the design of both electric drives and mechanical transmission system in EVs simultaneously. Based on the simulations using elaborate simulations created in MATLAB/ Simulink, the paper simulates the interaction of the motor properties, gear ratios, and the vehicle dynamics on typical road driving cycles. An optimized combination of the desirable torque-speed properties, energy consumption, and thermal limitations is gained by means of multi-objective optimization strategy. Findings indicate that the co-design approach results in the system efficiency being significantly improved and overall energy losses can be as low as 15 percent in comparison to the general practical sequential design methodologies. In addition, the optimized torque-speed characteristics and gear ratios will lead to an improvement in drive performance even though the downsizing of battery packs can be potentially performed without range loss. The results demonstrate the great value of combining electric drive and transmission design flows with great insights that can be used by manufacturers and engineers who want to create the next-generation of EV powertrain. The suggested strategy preconditions more environmentally friendly, affordable, and efficient electric vehicles.

title: Optimizing Type 2 Diabetes Management Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Buthaina Gady, Long Jun, Chen Yuedan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Managing type 2 diabetes requires a lifelong, ongoing approach involving interventions in various areas such as medication, nutrition, and physical activity. Effectively monitoring and coordinating these factors presents a complex challenge. While reinforcement learning has made significant strides in type 2 diabetes management, its application has generally been limited to one aspect of management. In this study, we proposed multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework using the collaborative QMIX algorithm to address all three areas (medication, nutrition, and exercise decisions) simultaneously, providing personalized recommendations for each within a healthcare setting with patient level reward functions, to achieve optimal glucose control. To test this approach, we used two algorithms, QMIX and MADDPG, and conducted a comparative analysis. The results indicated that the QMIX algorithm outperformed the MADDPG algorithm. The QMIX algorithm's ablation study provided a more in depth evaluation of the impact of its individual components. The results underscore QMIX's potential in managing type 2 diabetes and providing comprehensive, multidimensional recommendations.

title: An Edge-Cloud Collaborative System for AI-Enabled Project Portfolio Management with Cyber Threat Detection
authors: Sai Krishna Thota, Priyanka R. Raval, Kiran Onapakala, Jaswant Singh Taur, Amit Jain, S. Balamuralitharan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: With the new terrain of digital project governance, adding intelligent decision-making from proactive cybersecurity has become a necessity. The presented study introduces a derivation of a new Edge-Cloud Collaborative System as EC-P2M, which can be used to improve Project Portfolio Management (PPM) by using analytics and real-time threat identification with AI. The system is underpinned with a local monitoring of the project-execution metrics based on edge computing and global optimization and coordination based on cloud infrastructure. Light-weight neural models implemented on the edge predict the presence of an anomalous performance in order to trigger the subsequent orchestration actions. The orchestrator, located on the cloud, uses Multi-Criteria Reinforcement Learning to dynamically re-prioritize the projects based on the resource and risk constraints. At the same time, an Adaptive Threat Shield on the base of Graph Neural Network (GNN) maintains consistent monitoring of cyberspace within the infrastructure and implements Zero Trust measures by identifying anomalous behaviour. Roles of federated learning provide the privacy of data, and blockchain-supported logs offer indelible audit records. Such an architecture enables data integrity, scalability and resilience in distributed environments. As shown by experimental simulations, there is a better sense of project orientation with regards to strategic objectives, a short response to appearing threats, and better resources usage. The EC-P2M system takes the bar of smart, secure, and flexible project portfolio management to a different level, and it helps overcome the twofold challenge of business efficiency and cyber resilience of AI-enabled enterprise systems. The suggested Edge-Cloud Collaborative approach attained an overall accuracy of 94.1%, surpassing Heuristic Rule-Based Scheduling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{ (8 2. 3 \%) }$</tex>, Static Cloud-Centric Orchestration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 6 \%}$</tex>), Deep QLearning Scheduler (89.7%), Federated Learning with Reinforcement Learning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(91.2 \%)$</tex>, and Edge-Only Secure Task Engine (88.5%).

title: Smart Connected Healthcare System by the Role of IoT and Wearable Sensors in Patient Monitoring
authors: Nagarjuna Tanikonda, Shahbaz Khan, B Venkataramanaiah, Suman Mishra, B.Thiyaneswaran, K.Kasturi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Smart, linked healthcare systems are made possible by the fast developments in the Internet of Things (IoT), which have greatly changed healthcare. Wearable sensors combined with IoT technology let continuous real-time tracking of important physiological indicators including heart rate, blood pressure, oxygen saturation, glucose levels, and body temperature. By means of early disease identification, remote monitoring, and prompt medical interventions, these systems improve patient care and lower hospital visits, hence increasing healthcare efficiency. By sending real-time health data to cloud-based systems, IoT-driven healthcare solutions improve data-driven decision-making and let healthcare personnel evaluate patient status from a distance. Widespread adoption will depend on issues including data privacy, cyber security threats, interoperability, and energy efficiency even with all the advantages. This article investigates how IoT and wearable sensors fit into contemporary healthcare, hence affecting real-time patient monitoring, healthcare access, and general patient outcomes.

title: Efficient Methods for Modeling Shift-Varying Operators
authors: Nebiyou Yismaw, Chinmay Hegde, M. Salman Asif
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Many imaging systems require accurate characterization of their forward operators for reliable reconstruction. While shift-invariant systems admit efficient convolutional representations, many practical imaging systems are shift-varying and cannot be captured by classical convolution models. We propose the Shift-Varying Neural Operator, an efficient and expressive architecture for learning spatially varying linear operators directly from measurements. Our method builds on existing factorizations of spatially varying convolutions and expresses them in a learnable architecture. Each layer implements a spatially adaptive transformation constructed from a low-rank factorization of modulated convolutional bases. Our experiments show that our proposed method accurately recovers spatially varying point spread functions (PSFs) and learns interpretable operators. Furthermore, we show that the learned forward operator can be integrated into existing iterative inverse problem solvers.

title: Evaluating AutoML Frameworks in the Context of Autism Diagnosis: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Karpagam C, Mahadevi M, Manivasagam G
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) is a transformative paradigm that aims to leverage access to machine learning by automating the model development process. Traditional machine learning workflow requires domain expertise and substantial manual effort in tasks such as data preprocessing, feature selection, model selection, hyperparameter tuning and validation. AutoML frameworks simplify these processes in various sectors, especially in the field where limited expert knowledge or computational resources is required. AutoML enables more efficient and effective model generation than the existing machine learning approach. In the context of health informatics and particularly in autism spectrum disorder (ASD) detection, AutoML offers a promising solution for accelerating the development of diagnostic models. By automating the optimization of model performance, AutoML discovers robust predictive patterns in complex and heterogeneous data types. This paper evaluates a comparative analysis of AutoML approaches on the autism questionnaire dataset. The analysis and results generated provide valuable insights into their applicability, efficiency and generalizability in supporting early and accurate autism diagnosis. The AutoML approaches evaluated are AutoGluon, H2O, PyCaret and FLAML.

title: Drivers of E-Recruitment Adoption in Indonesia's Automated Businesses
authors: Rini Kurnia Sari, Aisyah Qanita, Marita Salandanan, Baghas Budi Wicaksono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Digital transformation in various fields has driven the implementation of information technology, including in the employee recruitment process. E-recruitment, as part of the human resource information system (HRIS), is increasingly used due to its efficiency in reaching candidates widely. In Indonesia, around 55% of companies have adopted HRIS to manage the recruitment process by 2023. However, although the implementation of e-recruitment continues to increase, challenges remain, especially in building user trust and reducing stress when using digital platforms. This study aims to analyze the factors that influence behavioral intentions in adopting erecruitment in automation-based businesses in Indonesia. Using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) approach, this study explores the role of Perceived Effectiveness (PU), Perceived Ease of Use (PEU), Vividness (VIV), and Perceived Internet Stress (PIS) on user intentions in using e-recruitment. Data were collected from 204 respondents and analyzed using the Structural Equation Modeling-Partial Least Squares (SEMPLS) method. The results showed that PEU had the greatest influence on user intention, followed by PU, indicating that the easier and more useful a system is, the higher the likelihood of users adopting it. Vividness and PIS factors also have an influence, although their impacts are smaller. The implications of this study highlight the importance of a user-friendly interface, attractive system design, and technical support features to reduce user stress in encouraging the adoption of erecruitment in Indonesia.

title: Real-Time Adaptive Camera-Attack Detection and Correction for Autonomous Vehicle Platoons in Cyber-Physical Systems via a MADRL Approach
authors: Mohamed El Jbari, Atika Revinq, Mohamed Moussaoui, Felipe A. P. de Figueiredo, Omar Bouazizi, Chaimae Azroumahli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: In autonomous vehicle platooning, members of the platoon not only use their own sensor data for making driving decisions. They also rely on data shared by other members of the platoon. This research proposes a novel framework for real-time detection and correction of camera-based attacks on platoonbased autonomous vehicles within Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) using a Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) approach. Camera systems, critical for perception in autonomous vehicles, are vulnerable to adversarial attacks such as image manipulation, occlusion, or sensor jamming, which can disrupt platoon coordination and safety. The proposed system leverages MADRL to enable adaptive, decentralized decision-making among vehicles, ensuring robust attack detection and mitigation while maintaining platoon stability. By integrating real-time camera sensor data fusion, anomaly detection, and corrective action policies, the framework enhances resilience against cyber-attacks. Experimental results demonstrate improved detection accuracy, reduced response time, and enhanced platoon performance under various attack scenarios compared to traditional methods.

title: A High-Performance and Resource-Efficient FPGA-Based Multi-Object Tracking System Using Event Cameras
authors: Wei Xiong, Jianfan Zhang, Xingzhe Zhu, Jiacheng Cao, Jian Wang, Jinmei Lai
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: As emerging visual sensors, event cameras are gaining attention for their excellent temporal resolution, low power consumption and extremely high dynamic range. Some studies have explored Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based systems for edge event-driven Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) tasks, which aim to achieve high real-time performance and tracking accuracy with strict resource and power constraints. Among these FPGA-based systems, the per-event processing systems can effectively utilize event cameras' high temporal resolution and asynchronous nature. To address the performance and resource overhead issues in per-event processing systems, this paper presents the Boundary-Movement-based Multi-Object Tracking (BMMOT) event-driven system for high-performance and resource-efficient MOT tasks while keeping reasonable power consumption and accuracy. BMMOT employs a straightforward boundary-movement-based tracking mechanism for tracker design. Additionally, a tracker lifecycle management strategy and a tracker merging strategy, based on concise motion trend criteria, are adopted to ensure the system functions properly. Effective BMMOT circuit designs are also proposed. The efficiency of BMMOT is validated by multiple academic and real-world datasets captured by different cameras. BMMOT implemented on an AMD ZYNQ FPGA has an event processing latency of 19.6-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$137.2 ~\text{ns} /$</tex> event and can process up to 51 million events per second with a power consumption of less than 2.71 W. BMMOT keeps good accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(52.1 \%-79.4 \%)$</tex> for the datasets. Under a similar tracking system configuration, higher performance is obtained while almost all block RAMs, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.4-41.0 \%$</tex> look-up tables, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64.5-78.7 \%$</tex> flip-flops can be saved compared to existing work.

title: AI-Driven Resume Analysis: Ensemble-Based Skill Extraction and Semantic Job-Candidate Matching
authors: Nandan P Vernekar, Sanath P Suvarna, Kawshik A, S Pranava
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Traditional Applicant Tracking Systems rely on rigid keyword matching that fails to understand semantic context, resulting in qualified candidates being overlooked. Large Language Model-based solutions offer better semantic understanding but suffer from high computational costs, and inconsistent outputs due to lack of domain-specific training for technical skill extraction. We propose an AI-Powered Platform using a custom-trained Named Entity Recognition model with an ensemble extraction pipeline that combines entity recognition, fuzzy matching, TF-IDF analysis, and semantic embeddings. Our locally-deployed system achieves 99.72 % accuracy and 81.48% precision in skill extraction across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 0 0 0}$</tex> resumes with 1.084 seconds average processing time, ensuring both data privacy and cost efficiency. The platform generates intelligent job-candidate compatibility scores, provides personalized Career Path recommendations, and includes an AI-powered Mock Interview System with real-time feedback. This solution eliminates manual screening overhead while overcoming the limitations of existing ATS and LLM-based recruitment approaches.

title: Decoding Risky Decision-Making from Nonlinear Neural Latent Dynamics
authors: Angela Zhou, Logan Peters, Karen A. Moxon
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Decision-making is a complex cognitive process often disrupted in neuropsychiatric disorders, making it essential to understand the underlying neural dynamics of deliberation. To investigate, we examined high frequency activity (70–200 Hz) recorded from intracranial EEG (iEEG) across multiple brain regions as human subjects performed a dual-option neuroeconomic decision-making task involving risk-taking. In this task, subjects had to choose between a safe bet for a fixed reward or to gamble for a potentially higher reward or no reward. We applied DFINE, a nonlinear dynamical systems model, to learn low-dimensional latent representations of neural activity. The model was first trained in an unsupervised manner, optimizing neural reconstruction and k-step-ahead prediction objectives to produce a set of latent variables. After training, the latent variables were used as inputs to both linear and nonlinear classifiers to classify trials as either safe or gamble trials. Next, we modified the DFINE model to include training of the classifier during the DFINE model training in a supervised manner. We compared 5 different modes of behavioral supervision: an artificial sigmoid trajectory, a simple mean-pool perceptron classifier, a flattened latent feature perceptron, a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN).

title: Evaluating the Reliability of Natural Ground Control Points and Check Points Compared with Artificial Targets for UAV Photogrammetry in Urban Areas
authors: Kittipong Prapakan, Chayanon Penyalai, Jurawan Nontapon, Siwa Kaewplang
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Improving UAV photogrammetric accuracy in urban areas is essential for civil engineering, planning, and public-safety applications, yet deploying artificial ground control points can be difficult in constrained environments. This study evaluates whether natural ground control points and natural check points can reliably replace artificial targets across eight UAV flight configurations combining RTK and non-RTK positioning. Independent checkpoints and RMSE-based metrics at the 95% confidence level were used for accuracy assessment. Results show that while RTK improves horizontal accuracy, non-RTK flights with well-distributed control points achieve comparable sub-five-centimeters performance. Uncontrolled flights produced $\sim \mathbf{2} \mathbf{m}$ vertical error, whereas configurations using artificial or natural control achieved mapping-grade vertical accuracy of $\mathbf{0. 0 4 - 0. 0 9 ~ m}$. Carefully selected natural features matched or exceeded the accuracy of artificial targets while reducing field effort. These findings demonstrate that NGCP/NCP workflows offer a practical and reliable alternative for high-precision UAV mapping in urban environments.

title: Real-Time Fall Detection and Alerting System Using NodeMCU and MPU6050 with Threshold-Based Analysis
authors: Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Pradeep Kumar, Kothawala Yogishwar Sai, Pendam Tanvi, V. Sai Bharath
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Falls are a major cause of serious injuries (to the head and/or neck) in older adults. This paper describes a real-time fall detection and protection system that can reduce or eliminate severe head and/or neck injuries through the deployment of a neck-mounted airbag, which will deploy when a fall is detected. The system is built on an Arduino platform, using an MPU6050 inertial sensor as the basis of its fall detection algorithm, which includes a lightweight three-stage threshold-based approach to monitor motion and detect fall events. If a fall is detected, the solenoid valve activates to release compressed CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> gas, filling the airbag within seconds after the event. The key to this design is converting passive fall detection to active protection against serious injury, with an emphasis on protecting the vulnerable upper body areas that have not received sufficient attention from existing products. In contrast to existing systems using machine learning or cameras to detect falls, our design is inexpensive, does not invade the owner's privacy, and is computationally efficient; therefore, it has great potential for use in the home and within elder care facilities. Experimental results demonstrate a reliable detection rate, an average response time of 2.1 seconds, and minimal false alarms in diverse test scenarios. This work contributes a practical and innovative solution that combines accurate sensing with immediate protective action to enhance elderly safety.

title: A Unified Testing Framework for ML-Based Fraud Detection in Healthcare Claims
authors: Gayathri Surianarayanan, Srikanth Singireddy, Abinaya Mettupatti Sivagnanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The role of Machine Learning (ML) models has grown substantially in the detection of fraud and abuse in healthcare claims data. However, the lack of a unified testing and validation framework complicates the problems of reliability, transparency, fairness, and regulatory compliance. This paper develops a Unified Testing Framework (UTF) designed specifically for validating ML-based fraud detection systems in healthcare claims processing. The framework includes modules for model performance evaluation, integrity of data validation, adversarial robustness, explainability, and compliance verification. Using a hybrid approach consisting of statistical testing, adversarial simulation, and explainable AI (XAI) metrics, we develop a rigorous methodology by which to assess whether a model is sufficiently mature to be deployed in real-world payer environments. Experimental results from simulated healthcare claims datasets show that the UTF improves accuracy by 8-12% while reducing bias and false positives compared to conventional ad hoc validation methods. The proposed framework is well aligned with industry standards such as HIPAA, GDPR, and the CMS program integrity guidelines for ethical deployment of AI.

title: Transformer and Recurrent Neural Network-based Spatio-temporal Modeling of Mangrove Vegetation Health Based NDVI and MVI Features
authors: Jonathan Marcelino, HosheaSophia Tañedo, Ronnie Concepcion, Edwin Sybingco, Giolo Rei Mababangloob
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study explores transformer-based modeling for spatiotemporal analysis of mangrove vegetation health in the Mandaue Mangrove Eco-Park, Cebu City. Sentinel-2 multispectral images from 2017 to 2025 were used to derive the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and Mangrove Vegetation Index (MVI) as indicators of canopy vigor and ecosystem condition. Four deep learning models were implemented and compared: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT), TabTransformer, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). Traditional spatiotemporal models such as LSTM and GRU often face challenges in capturing long-range temporal dependencies, managing multivariate inputs, and providing interpretability, which limit their accuracy in complex environmental datasets. Transformer-based architectures, particularly the TFT, overcome these limitations through attention and gating mechanisms that enhance feature selection, interpret variable importance, and support multi-horizon forecasting. The models were trained using data from 2017 to 2023 and evaluated on data from 2024 to 2025 for both forecasting and vegetation-health classification. Model performance was assessed using MAE, RMSE, $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, and Cohen’s Kappa. Results showed that the TFT provided the highest NDVI forecasting accuracy, while the GRU achieved the best MVI prediction and classification performance. Both models effectively identified Low, Normal, and High vegetation-health categories, demonstrating their ability to capture temporal variations in mangrove canopy dynamics. The findings highlight the potential of transformer and recurrent architectures as reliable tools for mangrove monitoring and recommend exploring additional vegetation indices and transformer variants in future research.

title: AI Driven Speech-Based Asthma Detection using Feature Optimization for Early Diagnosis
authors: Md.Asim Iqbal, Dr.K.Devarajan, Syed Musthak Ahmed, M. Sadanandam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Asthma is a common respiratory disorder affecting individuals across various age groups, often going undetected until symptoms become severe. Traditional diagnostic methods are time-consuming and require clinical intervention, which limits the possibility of real-time, early diagnosis. To address this issue, speech-based analysis has gained attention as a non-invasive and efficient diagnostic tool, leveraging the impact of asthma on voice characteristics. This work presents a real-time asthma detection and classification model using speech signals, integrating an AI-driven model. The proposed model uses an improved social spider optimization combined with genetic algorithm (ISSO-G) for optimal feature extraction, feature optimization, followed by a convolutional neural network (CNN) for accurate classification of asthmatic and non-asthmatic conditions. The model is trained and validated using the COSWARA dataset, which contains respiratory-induced voice samples across different health categories. The ESSO-G algorithm ensures effective selection of discriminative speech features, while CNN enables robust classification performance. Experimental results show that the proposed ESSO-G-CNN model delivers superior detection accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques, demonstrating its potential for early-stage asthma diagnosis through speech in real-time environments.

title: Design and Development of Cost-Effective Unmanned Aerial Vehicle for Crop Field Monitoring
authors: R.S.W. Madanayaka, C.V. Kumarasiri, Chathurika S. Silva
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a low-cost unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) designed specifically for field-based agricultural monitoring. The UAV platform incorporates a custom-designed flight controller, offering an affordable alternative to commercial autopilot systems. Essential sensors, including an inertial measurement unit, barometric pressure sensor, magnetometer and GPS module are integrated to achieve flight stabilization using a PID control algorithm. The flight controller was engineered using in-house PCB fabrication techniques to maximize cost reduction and modularity. Supporting modules include a remote-control system and 3D-printed components for structural and environmental protection. The system was tested under field conditions for stability, power efficiency and responsiveness. The results demonstrate that the custom-built, cost-effective UAV is a viable tool for agricultural remote sensing. Its utility is validated by successfully processing captured RGB imagery to generate standard Vegetation Indices for crop analysis, enabling more accessible and scalable monitoring approaches.

title: Block-sparsity Through Bayesian Hyper-parameter Learning for Generalized Wideband DOA Estimation
authors: Hitesh D. Khunti, Bhaskar D. Rao
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This work presents a Bayesian framework for generalized wideband Direction-of-Arrival (DOA) estimation that simultaneously exploits block sparsity in spatial and spectral domains to resolve closely spaced sources with unknown bandwidths and spatial spreads. We uniquely extend the well-known Sparse Bayesian Learning (SBL) algorithm for wideband DOA estimation. A key contribution is the introduction of a flexible regularization framework on the hyperparameter space for enforcing spatial and spectral block sparsity which supports a broad class of penalty functions. This allows the algorithm to dynamically adapt to sources of varying bandwidths and spatial spreads. Furthermore, we utilized MM and ADMM techniques to decompose the large-scale MAP optimization into a collection of simple and highly parallelized scalar optimization problems while efficiently handling both smooth and non-smooth regularizers. Numerical simulations demonstrate significant improvement in resolution and accuracy over established methods.

title: Modeling Willingness to Pay with Cultural Integrity Signals in AI Mediated Markets
authors: Lall Apoorva, Soma Mukhareejee, Jyoti, Gule Ganesh, Dabi Das Mukherjee
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This study investigates how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming digital communication and consumer behavior, with a focus on marketing, advertising, and visual communication. By integrating personalization, predictive analytics, dynamic content generation, chatbots, and immersive technologies such as AR/VR and the metaverse, AI reshapes how consumers interact with brands. Empirical results show measurable improvements: click-through rates rose by 41%, conversions by 31%, average order value by 12%, and repeat purchases by 5 percentage points, while return rates dropped by 1.8 points. Similarly, recommender systems across Netflix, Amazon, and Booking demonstrated uplift in engagement and transactions ranging from 14.9% to 42.9%. Survey evidence from 300 Indian participants revealed that 50% were highly familiar with AI, 70% supported AI-generated art, and 65% endorsed its creative benefits. Collectively, these findings highlight AI’s capacity to deliver efficiency, personalization, and emotional resonance, while also raising ethical challenges of privacy, bias, and fairness in global digital ecosystems.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approaches for Agronomic Price Prediction Using Multi-Source Data
authors: Shanmugam Sundaramurthy, Aditya Raj Singh, Shail Shah, C Jothi Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: In the agricultural sector, farmers can greatly benefit from accurate price forecasting, which enables wellinformed decision making. However, forecasting agronomic commodity prices presents significant challenges, as prices depend on multiple factors including historical trends, weather patterns, economic indicators, and satellite imagery. This paper proposes a hybrid machine learning framework that uses ensemble learning to integrate specialized models, each trained on a specific data modality. Ridge regression models economic trends, LightGBM captures weather-related impacts, and LSTM networks identify temporal patterns in historical prices. A CatBoost meta-learner combines these predictions through decision-level fusion. Experiments on multi-source Indian agricultural datasets demonstrate that this hybrid approach outperforms traditional single-model methods, achieving superior performance across RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> metrics. The results confirm that the multi-model ensemble is more effective and robust, successfully accounting for the multidimensional factors influencing agricultural commodity prices.

title: Ride-Hailing Trip Distribution and Its Relationship with Road Network Density and Public Transport Accessibility in Jakarta using Gravity Model
authors: Roland Febrian, Shierly Anastastya Lie, Miranda Setiawati, Edy Irwansyah
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This research investigates how road network concentration, accessibility to public transportation, and the distribution of ride-hailing trips are spatially interconnected within Jakarta’s urban villages. While gravity models are widely used in transportation studies, this study introduces a novel integration of road network density, ride-hailing trip distribution, and public transport accessibility within a single analytical framework.
