title: GPU-Accelerated Syndrome Decoding for Quantum LDPC Codes below the 63 μs Latency Threshold
authors: Oscar Ferraz, Bruno Coutinho, Gabriel Falcao, Marco Gomes, Francisco A. Monteiro, Vitor Silva
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents a GPU-accelerated decoder for quantum low-density parity-check (QLDPC) codes that achieves sub-63 μs latency, below the surface code decoder’s real-time threshold demonstrated on Google’s Willow quantum processor. While surface codes have demonstrated below-threshold performance, the encoding rates approach zero as code distances increase, posing challenges for scalability. Recently proposed QLDPC codes, such as those by Panteleev and Kalachev, offer constant-rate encoding and asymptotic goodness but introduce higher decoding complexity. To address such limitation, this work presents a parallelized belief propagation decoder leveraging syndrome information on commodity GPU hardware. Parallelism was exploited to maximize performance within the limits of target latency, allowing decoding latencies under 26 μs for [[784, 24, 24]] codes and as low as 16.9 μs for smaller codes, meeting the tight timing constraints of superconducting qubit cycles. These results show that real-time, scalable decoding of asymptotically good quantum codes is achievable using widely available commodity hardware, advancing the feasibility of fault-tolerant quantum computation beyond surface codes.

title: A Unified Framework for Text Classification: Evaluating Feature Extraction Techniques and Classifiers from BoW to BERT
authors: Savitha Ramesh, Devarashetty Nandini, Dhanya Sri Kokku, Mandadapu Pooja
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Text classification is one of the applications in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and it can incorporate applications such as spam detection, topic classification, etc. In this paper, we have developed a comprehensive and unified framework to help measure and evaluate the performance of classical, deep learning and transformer-based approaches in text classification. We analyze and compare the different feature extraction methods—BoW, TF-IDF and BERT—with classification approaches, such as Naive Bayes, LSTM and finetuned BERT. We evaluate representation methods, classifiers and investigate how they interact with classifiers through thorough testing on benchmark datasets, highlighting some important trade-offs in terms of accuracy, efficiency, and computational cost. This research is meant to support researchers and endusers in making informed approaches to solving a variety of text classification problems using the methods presented in this paper.

title: Evaluating IoT-Enabled Waste Management Readiness in Kenyan Urban Slums: A Systematic Review Towards Achieving SDG 6 and 11
authors: Ogeto Treza Nyanchama, Reny Nadlifatin, Apol Pribadi Subriadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study examines the potential of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies to improve waste management in Kenya's informal settlements. Despite the promise of smart bins, RFID, and low-power networks, their implementation in areas like Kibera and Mathare often falls short of addressing local challenges. Reporting followed the PRISMA 2020 guideline and checklist to ensure transparency and completeness of the systematic literature review of 36 peer-reviewed studies from 2019 to 2025. This research reveals key barriers such as unreliable infrastructure, digital exclusion, and policy gaps that hinder sustainable adoption. Addressing a gap in existing literature, the study proposes a holistic evaluation framework integrating technical readiness, community trust, and policy alignment to assess the feasibility of IoT systems in slum contexts. This is done while borrowing from the TAM and DOI theories. The approach aims to help policymakers, technologists, and urban planners design context-sensitive, inclusive solutions that advance Sustainable Development Goals 6 and 11. Although Kenya serves as our main focus, we triangulate our findings using similar data from Sub-Saharan Africa and other low- and middle-income contexts to improve generalizability and underscore contextspecific variations.

title: Digital Transformation in Auditing: Key Drivers of Information Technology Adoption by Government External Auditors
authors: Anita Carolina, Nur Aini Rakhmawati, Bambang Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of information technology competency, accounting information system complexity, organizational support, and external pressure on information technology adoption in auditing, drawing on the concepts of task-technology fit theory. A survey of external government auditors in Indonesia was conducted. Partial least squares structural equation modeling was employed to investigate the associations among the variables. The findings revealed that information technology competency, accounting information system complexity, organizational support, and external pressure influence technology adoption. This study extends the task-technology fit theory by highlighting the internal and external factors of technology adoption in a high-risk, regulated profession. Practical recommendations are provided to help organizations enhance their information technology adoption mechanisms, thereby improving the audit process's efficacy and efficiency, which ultimately results in a higher-quality audit report.

title: Social Media Signal Analysis for Predicting Influencer Impact on E-commerce Consumer Behavior
authors: Kiran Kakade, Reena Mahapatra Lenka, Mangesh Manohar Sanap, Priyadarshini Daga, Shilpa C. Shinde, Shailesh Kediya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The great quantity of social networking sites has had a significant impact on behaviour, particularly with relation to online buying. One of the most crucial elements influencing consumers' purchases now is social media celebrity presence. They may convert people's opinions and boost revenue. This study investigates how social media signal analysis may be used to project influencer behaviour on online shopping. Important indicators of an influencer's efficacy include social media cues like likes, shares, comments, and interactions amongst followers. Using data analytics and machine learning, this research investigates how much influencers really matter and how one may use these indicators to predict what people would purchase. This research looks for relationships between social media signals and activities like clicks, sales, and purchase intentions by means of predictive modelling. We also examine other types of media, including advertising postings, reviews, and unboxing videos, to see how these influence behaviour. The research findings provide us with insightful knowledge on how businesses may cooperate with powerful individuals to make their marketing initiatives more successful and better target the appropriate target clients.

title: Environmental Remote Sensing with Passive Ambient Noise Radar
authors: Michael Lexa, Ethan S. Miller, Phillip M. Corbell, Stephen R. Kaeppler, Yi-San Lai, Wesley C. Leong, Charles A. Mohr
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The high-frequency (HF; 3–30 MHz) band enables beyond line-of-sight communications and surveillance via ionospheric skywave and oceanic surface wave propagation modes. Passive ambient noise radar (PANR) is a new HF surveillance paradigm that exploits ambient noise fields regularly present in the HF spectrum. This paper derives a generalized likelihood ratio detector for PANR. The detector is based on the predicted phenomenon that the presence of targets alter spatiotemporal correlations measured by two (or more) geographically separated sensors. We propose an SNR-like quantity called the "scattered-plus-direct to direct-noise ratio" (SDDNR) as the driving quantity in detection performance. We also present two examples and show preliminary evidence that some of the model assumptions hold in a real HF radar system.

title: Reversible Data Hiding in Hyperspectral Images Using Pixel-Frequency-Based Band Selection with Minimal Overflow and Without Preprocessing
authors: Sai Pragna Koritala, Kavya Gudiseva, Sai Naren Polavarapu, Bhavya Sri Vangapandu, V. M. Manikandan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper proposes a reversible data hiding (RDH) technique designed for hyperspectral images (HSI) in ENVI format, enabling lossless embedding of binary data while preserving spectral integrity. The hyperspectral dataset used in this study was acquired using a Specim IQ camera, covering a spectral range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400-1000$</tex> nanometers, providing high-resolution imagery across 204 contiguous spectral bands. The method identifies an optimal band for embedding by selecting one with minimal overflow risk and maximum pixel repetition. In our study, Band 186 with pixel value 246 was chosen, allowing precise control during embedding. Binary data is embedded by incrementing the frequent pixel (246) to 247 for bit ‘ 1 ’ and leaving it unchanged for bit ‘ 0 ’. To prevent pixel collision, values above 246 are preshifted by +1. The extraction process restores both the embedded data and original image by traversing the band in raster order and reversing the modifications. Experimental validation includes pixel-level matrix comparisons, difference heatmaps, and 3D visualizations, confirming complete recovery of both data and image. The approach demonstrates 100% reversibility and zero distortion, making it ideal for secure and sensitive applications in remote sensing and surveillance.

title: Design and Deployment of GSM-Based IoT System for Real-Time Monitoring of Water Quality in Rural Areas
authors: Seven Siren, Rothna Pec, Sreynou Chin, Chhengiy Mao, Angkeaprek Yonchem, Channareth Srun, Chivon Choeung, Khov Veng Chhay, Sokheang Eang
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: The Tonle Sap Lake and the Mekong River are the main water sources in Cambodia. Recently, industrial activities have led to growth in Cambodia’s economy. The dumping of factory waste and garbage into water bodies causes water pollution. This paper introduces Water Quality Monitoring Based on Cellular Communication, integrated with the Internet of Things (IoT), which provides real-time data on water quality over long distances, and enhancing water management. The system incorporates cellular communication and crucial parameters such as potential of Hydrogen (pH), Total Dissolved Solids (TDS), Turbidity, and Temperature. These parameters are combined in each node, which inspects the water quality and transmits the information via the Global System for Mobile Communications (GSM) system to the main system. The data are sent from Main to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to be stored and allow users to access AWS to request information. Thus, users obtain the data through smartphone applications, providing easy access to the information.

title: Reliable Connectivity Optimization in Wireless Networks with Dependent Link Failures
authors: Zheng Ge, Lara Jüschke, Lars Wolf, Eduard A. Jorswieck
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Resilient network design is a mandatory feature for future wireless network infrastructure. When links in mesh networks fail, multi-connectivity and multi-path transmissions can establish the required end-to-end availability. In this work, we investigate a general wireless network graph with dependent link failures. We consider both packet duplication and load balancing strategies, as well as resource allocation across various network topologies with different levels of multi-connectivity. Our approach is based on analytical lower and upper bounds on network connectivity to compute the overall reliability, complemented by numerical simulations to illustrate the results. By modeling the entire resilience cycle from the event, through survival, to the recovery phase, different tradeoffs are characterized: redundancy versus efficiency, dependencies, data rates, and network topologies. Finally, we present a method for identifying the optimal link configuration to achieve the desired network performance.

title: Automotive 4D Imaging MIMO Radar Online Channel Imbalance Estimation via Bilinear NLMS
authors: Esmaeil Kavousi Ghafi, Matthias Wagner, Alexander Melzer, Mario Huemer
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In this work, a channel imbalance estimation method for automotive 4D imaging MIMO radars is proposed. The imbalances are estimated iteratively using a bilinear normalized least mean squares (BLNLMS) algorithm. Our method features low computational complexity, and does not require any prior knowledge of the targets in the environment. This is because it jointly estimates the targets’ parameters and imbalances in the signal processing chain of the radar. In addition, our method is not restricted to specific scenarios. In contrast, the majority of works in the literature are limited to the evaluation of measurements from a single target in the angular spectrum. Due to these advantages, our approach is highly qualified for a fast and reliable online channel calibration and/or fault detection tasks. We will demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method with the help of simulation results.

title: Implementation of the YOLOv11 and MobileNetV3 in a Real-Time System for Detecting and Estimating the Gender and Age of Visitors
authors: Muhammad Farid Hakim, Faisal Rahutomo, Joko Hariyono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Understanding visitor demographics in real-time is crucial for optimizing services and strategic decision-making in various sectors. However, existing data collection methods are often manual, inefficient, and limited in scope. This study proposes a fully automated system for real-time visitor detection and demographic analysis (gender and age) by leveraging existing CCTV infrastructure. The system employs a two-stage deep learning pipeline integrating YOLOv11 for efficient detection of visitors and faces, followed by a fine-tuned MobileNetV3-Small model trained on the FairFace dataset for simultaneous gender classification and age group estimation. The YOLOv11n-face model achieved an mAP@50-95 of 0.55 for face detection, while the MobileNetV3 model reached 86.6% accuracy for gender classification and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5. 8 \%}$</tex> for age estimation. Integrated system tests demonstrated real-time operation at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim$</tex> 6.5 FPS on limited hardware, with a counting error rate of 9.52 % due to occlusion. These results validate the feasibility and efficiency of combining YOLOv11 and MobileNetV3 for realtime visitor demographic analytics.

title: A Study on Deep Fake Detection and Content Suspension Mechanisms from Creation to Control
authors: Sreekanth Rallapalli, Jeeru Sowmya, Dileep M R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: In today's digital world, deepfake tech has turned into a big headache. This type of AI creates fake real-looking text, sound, video, or pictures that can fool or misdirect people. This research looks at various ways to spot deepfakes how well they work, and steps to reduce their impact. It digs into forensic methods, AI-powered detection tools, and cutting-edge machine learning systems.Deepfakes are a real problem for privacy, democracy, and how much we can trust what we see and hear. They mess around with video and sound using smart tech like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). Trying to spot these fakes gets tougher as the tricks to make them get more advanced. So, we need to make better tools to catch them and keep online info legit. This study also shows that it’s super important for governments, businesses that make tech, and schools to work together. They need to spread the word to everyone, improve the technology, and set up rules to block those deepfakes from fooling us all.

title: Analysis on intelligent DevOps by using AI-Powered automation for cloud application management
authors: G Divya, Kasarla Priyanka, L Chandra Sekhar Reddy, Shaik Munawar, M.Mohammed Ibrahim, Balaji Kannan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The combination of greater cloud computing pace and DevOps adoption allowed software deployment and monitoring as well as scaling to undergo automated enhancement. The current DevOps approaches using human intervention rules during automation process cause management inefficiencies in complex cloud-native systems. The research explores the integration of Machine Learning with Artificial Intelligence features into DevOps methodologies to develop autonomous healing capabilities through combining predictive diagnostic information with automated problem-fixing systems. When AI technologies are integrated under the AIOps framework companies acquire automatic anomaly detection tools and predictive resources management features and system failure assessment tools which improve their CI and CD deployment processes. The implementation of artificial intelligence-based monitoring systems enables organizations to receive genuine performance data and security protection functionality and cloud application scaling features. Automated correction systems initiated through programming helped identify sources of problems while reducing operational costs and system uptime losses.A DevOps platform created through AI automation enables deployment management and monitoring in addition to incident resolution as cloud-native functions. The analysis presents relevant research data and experimental evidence to prove how deployment operations benefit from AI and system reliability increases alongside the reduction of operational costs. Administrative roles experience substantial performance improvement through DevOps automation that employs AI because the system removes manual work and generates consistent reliable operations. The results indicate that cloud-native application control will utilize AI-driven DevOps that permits autonomous management along with self-adaptive operation of cloud platforms.

title: Sub-Problem Reduction Using Hypervolume Difference for Decomposition-Based Many-Objective Optimization
authors: Makoto Ohki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper proposes novel algorithms to solve a many-objective optimization problem (MaOP) by decomposing MaOP into two- or three- objective optimization problems (MOPs) and reducing the number of these sub-problems. First, all combinations of two or three objectives are enumerated from all objectives, and each objective subset is defined as a subproblem. Next, for an external non-dominated solution set, each sub-problem performs the non-dominated selection and obtains a sub-problem non-dominated solution set. The conventional method employs the size of the sub-problem non-dominated solution set as the contribution of the sub-problem, repeatedly select a sub-problem with great contribution in descending order of the contribution based on the set covering model until when the non-dominated solution set corresponding to the selected sub-problems sufficiently covers the original non-dominated solution set. When certain non-dominated solutions are unevenly distributed in the objective space, those concentrated solutions are associated with specific sub-problems, and the sub-problems are necessarily not ranked properly. In other words, the domain of the objective space covered by the surviving sub-problems that are not removed loses diversity. In order to reduce as many sub-problems as possible while maintaining as much diversity as possible in the objective space, this paper proposes to employ the sum of hypervolume differences (HVDs) of the non-dominated solutions of each sub-problem as the sub-problem contribution. Furthermore, the following two termination conditions are prepared, which are the coverage rate of the non-dominated solution set of the selected sub-problems as in the conventional method and the HVD rate of the selected sub-problems. The proposed algorithm, the conventional method, and NSGA-III, which is considered effective for solving MaOPs, are applied to test problems to verify their effectiveness.

title: Knowledge-Augmented Multimodal Reasoning for Automated Bridge Crack Diagnosis
authors: Yang Fang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in structural health monitoring often suffer from hallucinations and superficial reasoning. To address this, a professionalgrade framework integrating Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (MRAG) with few-shot Multimodal Chain-of-Thought (MCoT) is proposed. Built on a domainenhanced Qwen2.5-VL backbone, the system utilizes MRAG to retrieve expert exemplars, effectively anchoring the model's latent space. Subsequently, MCoT calibrates diagnostics through a rigorous four-stage sequence: Visual Deciphering, Analogy Mapping, Deductive Synthesis, and Adaptive Remediation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed configuration achieves a composite score of 91.0, outperforming the zero-shot baseline by 25.4 points. This approach effectively mitigates logical gaps, offering a robust, auditable solution for automated bridge maintenance.

title: Mapping Seagrass Cover Percentage and Estimation of Aboveground Carbon Stock using PlanetScope SuperDove Imagery and Random Forest Algorithm in Bontang, East Kalimantan
authors: Diki Akhyar Amanatulloh
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Seagrass meadows are a vital component of blue carbon ecosystems, playing a significant role in carbon sequestration and storage, and contributing effectively to climate change mitigation. However, the sustainability of seagrass ecosystems is increasingly threatened by climate-induced degradation and anthropogenic disturbances. Therefore, spatial inventory of seagrass meadows including aboveground carbon stock (AGCseagrass) estimation is essential for conservation and climate action. High-resolution remote sensing imagery from PlanetScope SuperDove, integrated with field survey data, offers an optimal approach for mapping benthic habitats, seagrass percentage cover (PCv), and AGCseagrass. This method is particularly effective and efficient for covering extensive areas with diverse terrain conditions. The aim of this research is to map the biophysical properties of seagrass meadows including benthic habitats, seagrass percentage cover, and aboveground carbon stock using PlanetScope SuperDove imagery with a spatial resolution of 3 meters. The Random Forest algorithm was employed for both classification and regression, integrating remote sensing data with field observations. The results indicate the presence of five benthic habitat classification classes, with an overall accuracy (OA) of 96.44% and a kappa accuracy of 95.16%. For seagrass percent cover modeling, the Random Forest algorithm yielded an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ of $\mathbf{0. 6 6}$ ($\mathbf{R M S E}=\mathbf{2 1. 0 9}$), and an average seagrass percent cover of 67.06%. The estimated total aboveground carbon stock (AGCseagrass) in Bontang reached 984,915 tons C, with an average value of $274.05 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$, calculated across a spatial extent of $32.35 \mathrm{~km}^{2}$. The AGC modeling also resulted in an $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ of 0.66 (RMSE = 9.25).

title: DEFA: Design Space Exploration for FPGA Overlay Accelerators Through Frequency Prediction and Bayesian Optimization
authors: Qilong Zhu, Yunfei Dai, Shiyan Bi, Huizhen Kuang, Dylan Wang, Wenbo Yin, Lingli Wang
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: In edge AI inference, FPGAs demonstrate superiority in performance-area balance. FPGA Overlay Accelerators (FOAs) are programmable accelerators implemented on FPGAs, typically highly parameterized to enable flexible hardware realization. These parameters, varying across a wide design space, have a significant impact on performance and require efficient Design Space Exploration (DSE). However, current frameworks struggle to accurately predict performance metrics like maximum frequency and fail to fully explore the design space, limiting DSE's effectiveness. In this paper, we propose a DSE framework for FOA (DEFA) based on Bayesian optimization, providing more effective and comprehensive DSE. To address complex parameter interdependencies in FOA, a dependency-aware design space modeling approach (DAM) is proposed. This approach applies fine-grained pruning to the parameter space while addressing dependency constraints. Based on this pruned parameter space, we develop a custom regression predictor (CREP) for maximum frequency using LightGBM, significantly enhancing performance estimation accuracy. Furthermore, the search efficiency is improved through enhanced Latin hypercube sampling and the Tree-Structured Parzen Estimator. We use the proposed framework to optimize an FOA template, Intel FPGA AI Suite. The Pearson correlation coefficient of CREP's predictions regarding the maximum frequency of accelerator instances achieves 0.87. In the throughput optimization experiment, the proposed DSE framework improves 30.16 % compared to the architecture optimization functionality provided by Intel FPGA AI Suite across the given 10 benchmarks on average. In the areathroughput trade-off optimization experiment, compared with FPGA AI Suite, the proposed DSE framework improves 5.01 % in frequency, 18.48 % in throughput and 21.60 % in area.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance for Industrial Robotics Using IoT Sensor Fusion
authors: Kapil Rajput, Anvar Absamatov, Shakir Yusupov, Odilbek Matsapayev, Etibor Sariyeva, Mukhammad Khabibullaev
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Further predictive maintenance of industrial robotics based on advanced AI with sensor data fusion with IoT is a mere game changer towards reducing downtimes and enhancing efficiency in smart factories to produce goods. After analyzing data about various sensors, vibration, temperature and current sensors, as well as with the help of machine learning algorithms, such as deep neural net and random forests, the framework deduces liabilities and equipment degradations prior to failure occurrences. Edge or cloud computing will manage the real-time data stream of IoT-based robots and be used to monitor them and make decisions. Some of the techniques that enhance predictive models include time-series analysis, anomaly detection, and RUL estimation. The analysis of historical data is a necessity. Create digital twins, which can be considered as a reflection of a connected system, in which it is possible to contemplate and experiment with the scenario to validate it. Handling high-dimensional, noisy sensor data, low latency inference and being able to generalize across a diverse array of robotic systems are major challenges. More recent advances are federated learning to support privacy-aware collaboration between factories, and explainable AI (XAI) to support interpretable fault diagnostics. The addition of 5G and edge AI will also make the real-time feedback faster. This kind of paradigm is a major reduction in the cost of maintenance and extends the life of robotic assets, as a part of the Industry 4.0 objectives. The future research directions include the development of hybrid physics-informed AI models, label-efficient training, and scalable deployment schemes. Lastly, AI predictive maintenance with IoT support will revolutionize the world of industrial robots with predictive and data-driven reliability management.

title: Energy-Aware Big Data Analytics for Sustainable Computing
authors: Abdul Hadi, Rahul Kumar Konduru, Geetha Krishna Sangam, Sai Raghavendra Varanasi, Sesha Sai Sravanthi Valiveti, Uday Kumar Reddy Gangula
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The dynamic increase in data-driven applications and massive cloud deployments has only aggravated the energy demand of digital infrastructures, and it has become a strong challenge to sustainability. The Energy-Aware Big Data Analytics has become a radical concept that will incorporate energy optimization methods with smart data processing to attain eco-friendly computation. This paper introduces a holistic framework, which makes use of the energy-adaptive scheduling, workload aware resource provisioning and machine learningbased power prediction models to reduce energy overheads at the expense of high analytical performance. The suggested architecture incorporates the heterogeneous big data platforms, such as edge, fog, and cloud systems, with the help of dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS), renewable energy forecasting, and carbon-conscious workload orchestration. Experimental analysis shows reduction in energy use, as much as 35 percent, increases in processing throughput, and huge saving of costs without affecting service level guarantees. The proposed approach stands out as superior in terms of sustainability goals when the comparison is made with the state-of-the-art approaches. The work provides a scalable, smart and green computing infrastructures solution that is environmentally responsible.
